raw_text: ABERDEEN, 7TH EARL OF, creat. 1682; VISCT. FROMARTINE, BARON HADDO, METHLIC, TARVES, and KELLIE, 1682; Bart., 1642 (Scot.); VISCT. GORDON OF ABERDEEN, 1814 (U.K.), under which title he sits in the House of Lords; P.C. (1886); G.C.M.G. (1895); JOHN CAMPBELL HAMILTON-GORDON, 3rd son of the 5th Earl.—B. 1847; succeeded his brother, 1870; lord-lieut. of Aberdeenshire, 1880; high comsnr. to gen. assem., Church of Scotland, 1881-6; lord-lieut. of Ireland, Feb. to Aug., 1886; gov.-gen. of Canada, 1893 to 1898; representative of Canada on Pacific Cable Board, 1899 to 1900; lord-lieut. of Ireland, Dec., 1905.

ADAMS, P. TARGETT.—Ed. at Epsom Medical Coll., 1874 to 1879, Guy's Hosp. and Durham Univ., 1881 to 1886; res. med. offr., Kent County Ophthalmic Hosp. (9 years), and a further 3 years as hon. full surgeon; for 10 years asst. med. offr. of health of Borough of Maidstone and deputy M.O.H. and asst. pub. analyst for County of Kent; med. offr. G.P.O., London, 1897; asst. M.O.H., Bombay, 1898-1900; returned to med. dept., G.P.O., London, 1900; govt. analyst and bacteriologist, O.R.C., June, 1903; and deputy M.O.H.
ADDISON, GLENTWORTH WALSH FRASER.—Asst. gold. comsnr., northern dist., N.S. Wales, 1858; pol. mag., Tenterfield, 1869; pol. mag., Maitland, 1875; stip. mag., Sydney, 1882.

ADDISON, R. H.—Cik. and interp. to R.M., Umlati, Natal, 1876; to R.M., Lion's River, 1882; lieut. Zulu Carbineers, 1883; asst. comsnr. and R.M. Ndwandwe dist., Zululand, 1887; ditto, Nqutu dist., 1889; mag., Estcourt, 1899.
ADOLPHUS, G. A.—Supervisor of customs Gold Coast Col., 1890; asst. treas., July 1896; ag. on several occasions as dist. comsnr., Axim and C. C. Castle; asst. treas., Northern Nigeria, July, 1900.

ADRIAN, F.O., C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1836; Entered C.O. Sept. 1863; asst. clk., 2nd class, 28th Dec., 1870; 1st class asst. clk., 26th Jan., 1877; supt. of the correspondence branch, gen. dep., and clk. for legal instruments, 1st Oct., 1880; apptd. offr. of arms of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, May, 1882; ret. 1901.

AGBEHI, DENYI.—Asst. warder, Lagos prison, 1885; 4th clk., treas., 1886; examg. offr., customs, 3rd Aug., 1893; paymr., Ibadan detchmt., Hausa Force, 15th May, 1897; in conjunction, postmtr. and clk. to res., Ibadan, 14th Dec., 1901, to Mar., 1903; audit clk., June, 1903.

AGBEHI, E. M. E., B.A., Durham Univ., 1903 (Arabic prizeman).—Ed. at Educational Inst., Sierra Leone, and Fourah Bay Coll.; 2nd on list, S. Leone civ. serv. exam., Nov., 1899; tutor, S. Leone gram. schl., 1904; audit clk., Lagos, 1905.

AGOSTINI, LOUIS EDGAR, K.C.—Ed. at Stonyhurst Coll., Downside Coll., and Univ. Coll., London; matric., London Univ., 1870; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1874; solr.-gen., Trinidad, 1st Oct., 1903; atty.-gen., Trinidad, 10th Sept., 1904.
AIREY, Hon. Peter.—Served in dept. of pub. instr., Queensland, for several years; M.L.A. for Flinders, 1901; sec. for mines and pub. wks.; home sec., Queensland, since Apr., 1904.
AITKEN, C. COLQUHOUN.—3rd class clk., col. sec.'s office, Jamaica, Feb., 1878; 2nd class clk., gen. register office, 1878; 1st class clk., 1880.

AKITOYE, DANIEL.—Ed. C.M.S. Coll. Institute, Lagos; copyist to Queen's Advocate, Lagos, Aug. 1886; was fourteen years regis. of instruments affecting land; dep. regis. of births, deaths, marriages, and aliens, Jan. 1895; ag. regis. at intervals; clk. to comsnr. of lands, Apl., 1901.

ALBURY, JOSEPH BENSON, M.D.—Mem. House of Assem., Bahamas, 1869; J.P., 1873; public vaccinator, 1881; and public physician, 1883; comsnr. Col. and Ind. Exhib., 1885; mem. legis. coun., 1889; med. insp. of col., 1900.

ALDWORTH, JOHN RICHARD OLIVER.—Ed. at Cheltenham College; second asst. collector and magistrate, Klang, 23rd March, 1889; passed in Malay, 11th July, 1889; asst. coll. and mag., Rawang, 11th July, 1889; first asst. coll. and mag., Klang, 1st December, 1889; asst. dist. officer, Kuala Selangor, 23rd December, 1889; ag. dist. officer, Kuala Selangor, 14th August, 1890; ag. treasurer of Pahang, 12th July to 12th November, 1892; dist. officer, Klang, 3rd July, 1893; dist. officer, Ulu Langat, 1st December, 1895; ag. collector of land revenue, Seremban, 17th Sept., 1896, to 30th Sept., 1897; examiner in Malay, Negri Sembilan, 1896; ag. collector of land revenue, Kuala Lumpur, and registrar of titles, Selangor, 15th February, 1898; collector of land revenue, Seremban, and registrar of titles, Negri Sembilan, 15th June, 1900; sec. to High Commissioner, Fed. Malay States, 25th Sept., 1901; dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Aug., 1903; ditto, K. Lipis, Feb., 1906.
ALEXANDER, THOMAS.—Sub-inspr., Jamaica constab., Jan., 1872; 3rd class inspr., Oct., 1873; 2nd class inspr., Nov., 1876, now 1st cl. inspr.

ALLARDYCE, W. L., C.M.G. (1902)—B. 1861; clk. and interp. Provincial Dept. Fiji, 1879; transf'd. to Rotumah, 1882; stip. mag., July, 1882; asst. native comsnr. and stip. mag. and inspr. of native taxes, Kadavu, Jan., 1890; mem. exec. coun., Nov., 1893; native lands comsnr. and mem. legis. coun., 1894; native comsnr., 1895; asst. col. sec. and receiv.-gen., Dec., 1898; deputy gov., 14-20 Feb., 1901; administrd. govt., 18th July, 1901, to 9th Sept., 1902; col. sec. and receiv.-gen., 18th July, 1902; gov., Falkland Is., 23rd May, 1904; editor of native newspaper "Na Mata," 1890-99; author of a digest of Native Reguls., 1877-1900.

ALLDER, W. H.—5th clk., C.S.O., Barbados, 1892; 4th do., 1898; 3rd do., 1900; 2nd cls. clk., P.O., 1904; ag. clk. exec. coun. and comtee., Apr. to Oct., 1900, Apl. to July, 1902, Jan. to Oct., 1904; sec. to Barbados quarantine comsnr., 1903; also to W.I. quar. conference, 1904.
ALLDRIDGE, T. E. LESLIE.—Ed. at Rugby and Weimar, Germany; priv. sec. to Sir W. Roberts-Austen, K.C.B., Royal Mint, London, 1897 to 1899; 2nd-cl. supervisor of customs, Gold Coast Col., 1899.

ALLDRIDGE, THOMAS J., F.R.G.S., F.Z.S., &c.—Ag. U.S. consular agt. at S. Leone, 1871-2; travelling comsnr. for col.; hon. inspr. of pol., and J.P., 1889; pioneer to remote interior and concluded numerous treaties between British Govt. and paramount chiefs in Mendi Land; dist. comm. Sherbro, 1893; comm. of Court of Requests; accompanied H.E. Sir. F. Cardew, K.C.M.G., upon his first interior tour of 617 legs. in 1894 as astronomical observer and topographer; medal and clasp for Mendi rising, Sherbro, 1898, mentioned in despatches; hon. corr. sec. Roy. Col. Inst.; awarded the Cuthbert Peek grant, 1900, by the R.G.S. for geographical work done during the past ten years in the hinterland of the colony; author of "The Sherbro and its Hinterland" (Macmillan), 1901.
ALLEN, GEORGE JAMES.—Entered C.O. Oct., 1895; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. 2nd div. Civil Ser. and re-assigned to C.O. Oct., 1898.
ALLEN, H. T.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser. and assigned to the C.O. Mar., 1898.

ALLEN, RAYMOND CECIL, F. R. G. S., A.M.I.C.E., F.S.I.—Ed. at Roy. Univ. of Ireland and Queen's Coll., Cork; dipl. in engnr., Queen's Coll.; asst. engnr. water supply and main drainage, London Cty. Coun., 1st Aug., 1895; resign. 1st Nov., 1900; chief survr., Uganda Prot., 30th Nov., 1900.

ALLMAN, ROBT., C.M.G. (1901), L.R.C.S.E., L.R.C.P.I.—Prin. med. offr., Oil Rivers Prot. (now So. Nigeria), Sept., 1891, and organized med. dept.; prin. med. offr., Cross, Eket, and Okrika expdsns., 1895-1896; prin. med. offr., Prot. troops, Benin City expdn., 1897 (desps., medal and clasp and recd. thanks of S. of S.); prin. med. offr., Oron., 1897; Ekuri, 1897-8 (desps.); Ubium and Ishan expdsns., 1899; Aro expdn., 1901-2 (medal and clasp) complimented by S. of S. (late Marquis of Salisbury) on efficient organization of med. dept., 1897; mem. of comtee. at C.O. for re-orgniztn. of W. African med. staff, Oct., 1901, and recd. thanks of S. of S.; ret. 1905.

ALLNUTT, A. C., B.A. Oxon.—Ed. at Charter house, 1888-1893; at Brasencote Coll., Oxon., 1893-7; apptd. to Ceylon civ. ser., 1st Dec., 1898; attached to secretariat, 24th Dec., 1898; on spec. duty with Boer prisoners of war, 6th Aug., 1900; dist. judge, Tangalle, 18th Aug., 1902; ag. asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, 13th Jan., 1903.

ALLIT, ALLEN BRAVO.—Ch. clk. bond. w'house, cust. dept. Brit. Guiana, Sept., 1894; 4th clk. offr. Jan. 1886; 3rd, May, 1886; 2nd Oct., 1893; sub. comptlr. Berbice, harmr. and regis. of shipping, July, 1897; ch. clk. May, 1898; ag. comptlr. of cust. June to July, 1899.

ALLWOOD, JAMES, C.M.G. (1903).—2nd clk., gov.'s sec.'s off., Jamaica, July, 1862; ag. 2nd clk., exec. comtee. off., Feb., 1865 to May, 1866; clk., immigrtn. dept., May to Oct., 1866; 1st class clk., finance off., Oct., 1866 to Dec., 1869; supervisor of dist. post offices, Oct., 1871, to Mar., 1876; 1st class clk., col. sec.'s off., Nov., 1885, afterwards asst. col. sec. to 1897; collr.-gen., 1877-1904; ret. 1904.

AMIRAYAN, G. G.—Called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1894; clk. and translator, comsnr.'s office, Larncsa, Cyprus, Oct., 1878; translator, high ct. of justice and ct. of Temiyz, 1881; regis., sup. ct., 1883; was sec. to coman. apptd. in 1888 to inquire into organiztn. of Cyprus pol.; was examr. in Turkish, 1886; sec. to legal bd., and examnr., 1901; asst. King's Advocate, 16th Apl., 1905; wrote Turkish translation of draft penal code for Cyprus, 1897, and English translation of Ottoman commercial code, 1906.
AMORY, H. W.—Extra rev. offr., St. Kitts, 1893; rev. offr., quarantine offr., and clk. for bd. of health, Nevis, 1895; cashier, treasy., St. Kitts, 1897.

AMPTHILL, 2nd Baron, creat. 1881, Oliver Arthur Villiers Russell.—Son of the 1st Baron; born 1869; succeeded his father, 1884; G.C.S.I. (1904), G.C.I.E., 1900. Ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxon. Rowed in the Oxford eight, 1889-91; pres. of the Oxford Union, 1891; B.A., 1891; M.A., 1900; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Sec. of State for Cols., June, 1895; priv. sec., June, 1897; one of the British delegates to the Internat. Sugar Conference, Brussels, May and June, 1898; lieut. Roy. 1st Devon Yeomanry Cavalry; capt. 3rd batt. Bedfordshire Regt.; J.P.; and prov. grand master of Freemasons, Bedfordshire; governor of Madras, Oct., 1900-1905; ag. Viceroy of India in 1904.

ANDERSON, CHARLES OSBORNE.—Indentured clk., pub. hosp., Bahamas, May, 1885; 2nd clk., G.P.O., Apr., 1888; ch. clk., July, 1890; ag. postmnr., 1892; elected mem. legis. assem. 1895; re-elected 1896 for the septennial term; ag. res. justice and collr. of rev. dist. of Inagua, from Oct., 1896, to Feb., 1898; J.P. for Col., 1903.

ANDERSON, CHAS. WILGRESS.—4th Govt. survr., B. Guiana, 1887; govt. offr. (mining regns.), and J.P., 1890; ditto, dept. of mines, in charge No. 2 dist., 1893; govt. survr. in charge Kaieteur Conglomerate prospection expdn., May, 1895 (published map); 1st cls. govt. survr., 1896; ag. asst. crown survr., Nov., 1900; govt. survr. atchd. to B. Guiana boundary comsn., Sept., 1901; on geol. survey with Prof. J. B. Harrison, C.M.G., Nov., 1902; junior comsnr. (to act with H. I. Perkins, I.S.O.) for delimitation of B. Guiana boundary, Sep., 1903.

ANDERSON, Sir John, K.C.M.G. (1901), C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1858; M.A., Aberdeen, 1877; 1st class honours in mathematics; gold medal as most distinguished graduate of year; 2nd class elk., C.O., 30th June, 1879; Bacon scholar, Gray's Inn, 1887; Inns of Court studentship, 1888; joint comsnr. with the late Sir J. F. Dickson, Nov. and Dec., 1891, to inquire into certain matters connected with the registry of the sup. court of Gibraltar; priv. sec. to Sir R. Meade, 17th Aug., 1892; attached to the staff of the Br. agent for the Behring Sea Arbitration in London and Paris, 1892-93; 1st cls. clk., 11th Mar., 1896; prin. clk., 3rd June, 1897; sec. to the conference between Mr. Chamberlain and the Colonial Premiers, June and July, 1897; visited Gibraltar to inquire into rates of pay in the civ. serv., May, 1899; apptd. C. O. representative on staff of H.R.H. the Duke of Cornwall and York, during his visit to the Colonies, 1901; sec. to Colonial Conference, 1902; received thanks of Canadian Govt. and Confederation Med. for services in connection with Alaska Bound., 1903; govr., Straits Settlements, 1st Feb., 1904; also of Labuan and its dependencies, 1st Jan., 1906, and high comsnr. for Brunei, Dec., 1906.

ANDERSON, JOHN.—Ed. at George Watson's Coll., Edin., Edin. Univ. and Univ. of Leipzig; 11 class medals, Van's Dunlop scholarship in chemistry, 1903; M.A., 1st hons. maths., Apr., and B.Sc., hons. maths., nat. phil. and chem., July, 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd class clk., C.O., 23rd Oct., 1903.

ANDERSON, Capt. P. W.—Ed. privately and at St. Andrews University; entered the army as 2nd lieut. Derbyshire regt., 1888; lieut., 1890; capt., 1893; retired with gratuity, 1902; Lushai expdn., 1890 (medal with clasp); expdn. to Bida, 1901 (medal with clasp); ass't inspr. of signalling in Bengal and the Punjab, 1892-94; adjutant of aux. forces, 1895-1900; brigade maj., W.A.F.F., Aug., 1900, to Feb., 1902; apptd. 2nd cls. res., N. Nig., June, 1902; ag. sec. to the adminstr., Aug., 1902.

ANDERSON, Sir William John, Kt. Bach. (1896).—B. 1846; ed. at Pembroke Coll., Oxford; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1869; judge of the sup. ct., Turks Islands, Feb., 1874; dist. judge, Jamaica, 1886; R.M., Kingston and St. Andrews, 1888; ch. justice, Br. Honduras, 1890; ch. justice, Trinidad, 1900; ret., 1903.

ANDERSON, RALF WILLIAM.—Ed. at Marlborough and in Germany. Asst. examr. of accounts, audr.-gen.'s off., O.R.C., Sept., 1901; ch. clk. and senior examr. of acts., and-gen.'s office, 1st July, 1902; asst.-auditor, 1st Dec., 1902.

ANDREWS, MARTINDALE STEWART.—Ed. at Merchant Taylors' schl.; Eastern Tel. Co.'s staff, Alexandria, 1882; African dir. tel. co., Gambia, June, 1886; supt., Accra, Feb., 1887; dir. of tels., G. Coast, June, 1891; offl. mem. and vice-chrmn., Accra town coun., 1898; dir. of tels. with Ashanti Field Force, June to Dec., 1900 (medal, ment. in desps.); ag. P.M.G., Jan. to June, 1901, also from Apr. to Oct., 1904; title changed to asst. P.M.G., Jan., 1905.

ANGERS, Hon. Auguste Réal.—Solr.-gen., Quebec, 1874-6; atty.-gen., 1876-8; leader of upper house for three years; then puisne judge, super. ct., Quebec; lieut.-gov., Quebec, 1887; senator and min. of agricult., Canada, Dec., 1892, to 1895.
ANGOVE, THOMAS.—Probation clk., audit office, W. Australia, July, 1880; 2nd clk., Treasy., 1886.
ANGUS, WM.—B.Sc., Prof. of agric. and sec. to min. of agric., S. Australia, 1904; dir. of agric. and intelligence, 1906.

ANSELL, WILLIAM JAMES DAVID.—Asst. supt. of port, Larnaca, Cyprus, March, 1880; ag. supt. of port, Limassol, June to Sept., 1880, Apr. to Dec., 1881; tile surv. and dep. harbrmr., Dec., 1881; passed exam. in Modern Greek, 1885; govt. and municipal inspr. of weights and measures, Apr., 1892, to Aug., 1898; 2nd div. clk. and asst. collr. of customs and excise, Mar., 1894; acted collr. of customs and excise, July to Nov., 1894; May to Oct., 1895, Nov., 1896, to Nov., 1897; asst. comsnr., Nov., 1896, June, 1897, Nov., 1897; collr. of customs and excise, May, 1898; is also supt. of port, receiver of wreck, and registr. of shipping; acted as comsnr., coroner, and pres. of mun. comsn., Kyrenia, 23rd Sept. to 24th Dec., 1903; acted as comsnr. and coroner, Famagusta, 7th July to 8th Nov., 1906.

ANSON, Major-General Sir Archibald Edward Harbord, R.A., C.M.G. (1876), K.C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1826; entered the royal mil. acad., Woolwich, 1841; presented with sword for exemplary conduct; 2nd lieut. R.A., June, 1844; 1st lieut., 1st Apr., 1846; 2nd capt., 9th July, 1852; 1st capt., 1st Sept., 1855; brevet major, 21st July, 1864; lieut.-col., 12th May, 1836; brevet col., 12th May, 1874; col., 23rd Dec., 1875; ret. with the hon. rank of maj.-gen., 26th August, 1879; served at the siege of Sebastopol in 1855; Crimean medal and clasp, 5th class Medjidie, and Turkish medal; inspr.-gen. of pol., Mauritius, Sept., 1858; was employed in 1862 as the responsible mem. of the Br. mission to the court of Radama II. of Madagascar, on the occasion of his coronation; lieut. gov. of Prince of Wales Is., Feb., 1867; admstd. govt. Str. Sttlmnts. 1871, 1877, and 1879; conducted the successful military operations for repelling the invasion of the state of Sungei Ujong, 1875-76 (medal and clasp); ret. 1882; J.P. for Sussex.

ANSON, E. R.—Asst. comsry., of taxation, Br. Guiana, 1831; dep-comsry., and inspr. of weights and measures, 1834; stip. mag. N.W. dist. 1890; ag. govt. agt. N.W. dist., Sept. 1898; stip. mag., Corentyne coast dist., Feb., 1901; passed govt. exam. in Hindi, May, 1903.

ANSON, FRANK CHARLES MONTRESOR.—B. 1857; ed. Haileybury Coll.; Asst. comsry. of taxn., Br. Guiana, May, 1881; dist. comsry. and inspr. of weights and measures, June, 1884; sec. to road arrears coms., 1889; mem. of coms. to inquire into and assess damages caused by riots, 1890; J.P., 1892; chairman bd. of examsr. under "weighers and gaugers ordinance, 1883"; mem. cent. bd. of health and excise bd., and ag. ch. comsry., 1892-3, 1893-4, 1894-5; ag. stip. mag. Centl. Demerara judicial dist., Br. Guiana, Aug., 1898; capt. Br. Guiana Militia, 1896, and obtained certif. for drill and musketry from schls. of instruc., Chelsea Bks., Lon., and Hythe; Oct., 1898, ag. stip. mag. North-West Dist., Br. Guiana; May, 1900, ag. stip. mag. Sth Ess. River Jud. Dist., and pres. P.L.Bd., Wakenaam, Br. Guiana; Feb., 1901, treasurer and comptroller of customs, St. Lucia, ex officio member of exec. coun. and legislature; chrmn. quarantine authority, and registrar of shipping; treas. and mem. of ex. and leg. couns., Lagos, May, 1903.

ANTHONISZ, JAMES OLIVER.—Cadet, S. Sttlemta., Oct., 1883; B.A., St. John's Coll., Camb. (Senior Optime); asst. Indian immigr. agt., Singapore, May, 1886; 3rd mag., Singapore, 1888; 2nd ditto, July, 1892; ag. official assignee and regis. of deeds, Singapore, Oct., 1894; and Apr., 1897; ag. 1st mag., July, 1898; offl. assignee and regis. of deeds, May, 1897; prn. munic. comsrs. Sing., Jan., 1901; comsrs., court of requests, Sing., 1st Jan., 1902; 1st magis., Sing., Aug., 1902, but continued to act as pres. of the munic. comsrs.; seconded for 1 year as pres. munic. coms., 1st Jan., 1903; ag. treas., Sept., 1904.

ANTHONISZ, RICH. GERALD.—Served in educ. dept. and regisr.-gen.l's dept., Ceylon; asst. regisr.-gen.l., 23rd Feb., 1892; seconded for serv. as examiner of Dutch records, 15th July, 1899; archivist and librarian, 1st Jan., 1902.

ANTON, GEORGE.—Clt., audit office, Grenada, Nov., 1872; 2nd clk. col. sec.'s office, Apr., 1878; ag. ch. clk., Apr. to Sept., 1879, and again in 1880; treasy. acctnt., St. Vincent, Jan., 1881; ag. auditor, Aug., 1888, to end of 1889; regisr. sup. ct., Dec., 1891; J.P., 30th July, 1892; regisr.-gen.l., 1st Jan., 1898; ag. treas. and paymaster, and mem. ex. and leg. coun., 10th June to 14th Nov., 1899; offl. mem., leg. coun., 8th Jan., 1901; coroner, 1st dist., 1st Apr., 1902; mag., 1st dist., 10th June, 1902.

ANTROBUS, REGINALD L., C.B. (1898).—B. 1853; ed. at Winchester and at New Coll., Oxford; scholar of New Coll., 1872; 1st class in classical mode, 1874; 2nd class in final classical school, 1876; B.A., 1876; appttd., after an open compet. exam., to be a clk. in the C.O., May 3rd, 1877; asst. priv. sec. to the Earl of Kimberley, 5th Dec., 1880, to Dec., 1882; priv. sec. to the Earl of Derby, 16th Dec., 1882, to 24th June, 1885; to Col. the Right Hon. F. A. Stanley, M.P. (now Earl of Derby), 24th June, 1885, to 6th Feb., 1886; and to Earl Granville, 6th Feb. to 3rd Aug., 1886; administd. the govt. of St. Helena, 5th Nov., 1889, to 8th June, 1890; priv. sec. to Mr. Meade, 1st Feb., 1890, and to Mr. S. Buxton, M.P., 17th Aug., 1892; sen. clk., Jan., 1894; princ. clk., 1896; asst. under sec. of state, Oct., 1898.

APPLEYARD, CHARLES W.—Apptd., after compet. exam., to be a clk., 2nd div., civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., Mar. 14th, 1893; matric., Lond., 1895; temporary staff officer in accts. branch, 1900; ag. col. secretary, etc., Falklands, Apl., 1900, to Feb., 1901; returned to C.O., Mar., 1901; assist. acctnt. to high commissioner for South Africa, Nov. 30th, 1901.

APTHORP, MAJOR K.P.—Ed. at Charterhouse; lieut., Royal Irish, 1881; capt., 1888; major, 1889; Nile expdn., 1885 (medal, with clasp and Khedive's star); Black Mountain expdn., 1888 (medal with clasp); S. African war, 1900-1 (medal with two clasps, King's medal, ment. in desps.); A.D.C. to lieut.-gov., Punjab, 1889-1891; S.S.O., Lucknow, 1892-1894; adjutant, Oudh Light Horse, 1894-1899; dist. comsnnr., O.R.C., 1900; S.A. constab., 1901; sec., land settm't. dept., O.R.C., 1st Oct., 1901; dir., land settm't. dept., 23rd Jan., 1905.

ARBUCKLE, SIR WM.—Kt. bach. (1902); B. 1839; mayor of Durban, Natal, 5 times; mem. of leg. coun., 1893; col. treas., Natal, 1897-1902; pres. of leg. coun., 1902-4; J.P.; agent-gen.l. for the colony, in London, 1904.

ARCHER, F. BISSET.—Prin. clk. col. sec.'s office, G. Coast, Mar., 1894; ch. clk., Jan., 1896; ag. asst. col. sec., Aug., 1896; elk. of coun., Sept., 1896; lieut. G. Coast rifle volrs., 1895; ag. adjt., June to Oct., 1896; F.O. certif., Wellington Barracks, 1897; asst. col. sec. Lagos, June, 1897; compiled Lagos offl. handbk.; in charge of the secretariat on many occasions, 1898-1902; ag. treas., Jan. to May, 1901; and mem. of ex. and legis. couns.; treas., man. savings bank, and postmr., Gambia, Jan., 1903; mem. of ex. and legis. couns., and J.P. of the colony; ag. collr. of cust., and chmn. navigation and pilotage bd., in addition to substantive apptmts., Feb. to July, 1906; ag. col. sec., Feb. to Aug., 1903, June, 1904, to May, 1905, and July to Dec., 1906; also as dep. gov., Mar.-Apr., 1905. Author of "Handbook of the Colony of the Gambia and its Protectorate."

ARCHER, F. L. H.—Ent. pub. service corrspcll. branch, col. sec.'s off., Barbados, 1883; promoted gen. post-office, 1884; customs, 1890; 2nd cls. supervisor, G. Coast, June, 1902; ag. ch. regisr. and sheriff, G. Coast, 11th Dec., 1902, to 8th June, 1903, and 30th July, to 25th Dec., 1904; 1st cls. supervisor, 27th Sept., 1904; asst. collr., Lagos, 26th Dec., 1904; ag. collr. of cust., 29th Oct., 1905, to 1st May, 1906; postmr.-gen.l., S. Nigeria, 14th May, 1906.

ARGYLL, DUKE OF, P.C., K.T., G.C.M.G., G.C.V.O., MARQUIS OF LORNE, THE RT. HON. JOHN DOUGLAS SUTHERLAND CAMPBELL.—Born 1845; married, 1871, H.R.H. Princess Louise Caroline Alberta, 4th daughter of H.M. the late Queen; sat in the House of Commons for Argyllshire from Feb., 1868, to Sept., 1873; priv. sec. to his father, the Duke of Argyll, at the India Office, Dec., 1868; is author of poetical and other works, including "Trip to the Tropics"; gov.-gen. of Canada, 1878 to 1883; M.P. for S. Manchester, 1895; succeeded his father, 1900; chancellor of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1905.

ARMBRISTER, PERCY W. D.—Ag. registr., prothonotary, and elk. of Crown, Bahamas, 1884; ag. ch. clk., col. sec's. dept., 1886; clk., pol. ct. Nassau, 1887; J.P., 1890; res. mag. and collr. of rev., Abaco, 1890, Eleutherae, 1894, Harbour Is., 1895, Inagua, 1896; ag. stip. and circuit mag., Bahamas, and judge of ct. of com. pleas, 1896 and 1897; registr. of cts., prothonotary, and clk. of Crown, Jan., 1897; chairman of comte of prisons; presides in cases of marine casualties.
ARMBRUSTER, Hubert.—B.A., Ed. at Cnus Coll., Camb.; asst. collr., B.C.A. Prot., Aug., 1899; ag. dist. mag., Marimba dist., Apl., 1905; dist. res., 2nd cls., Apl., 1906.
ARNOLD, W. J. J.—Royal Univ., Ireland; B.A., 1887, M.B., 1894; civ. surgeon attached to R.A.M.C., St. Helena and S. Africa, 1900 to 1903; apptd. col. surgeon, St. Helena, Apl., 1903.

AREOWSMITH, W. C. G.—Apptd. 8th clk. G.P.O., Jamaica, May, 1866; 6th clk., audit off., Aug., 1869; promoted to 1st cls., Nov., 1870; acted as ch. clk. in aud. off., Feb., 1874, to Apl., 1875, and Mar. to July, 1877; apptd. ch. clk. June, 1884; deputed for spec. work of inspecting parochial accts., Oct., 1888; acted as aud. gen. on sev. occasions.
ARTHUR, J. S. W.—Cadet, S. Sttlmta., Jan., 1905.

ARUNACHALAM, P.—Ed. Acad. Coll., Colombo, Ceylon, and Christ's Coll., Camb., of which he was a scholar; B.A. 1875; M.A. 1879; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1875; writer Ceylon civ. serv., Apr., 1875; comsnr. of requests, pol. mag., 1876; dist. judge, June, 1887; comsnr. of requests, Colombo, 1892; dist. judge, Kurunegala, Sept., 1897; regisr.-gen., Jan., 1898; supt. of census, Mar., 1900; officer of class I, dist. judge, Kurunegala, 23rd June, 1903; regisr.-gen., Feb., 1905.

ASHBURNHAM, John Anchitel.—Ck. to admstr., Brit. Bechuanaland, Oct., 1885; sec. to Bech. admstr., Feb., 1888; accompanied high comsnr. to conference with pres., S.A.R., at Blignaut's Pont, Mar., 1890, and at Colesberg, Apl., 1893; sec. to concessions comsnr. for Bech. Prot., May, 1893; C.C. and R.M. at Taungs, Brit. Bech., Feb., 1894; C.C. and R.M. at Gordonia, June, 1894; asst. comsnr. for Bech. Prot., Nov., 1895; R.M., Bloemfontein, Sep., 1901; chrmn., land laws enquiry coman., O.R.C., Oct., 1901; ag. col. sec., O.R.C., Apl., 1904, and Oct., 1906.

ASHLEY, Rt. Hon. Anthony Evelyn Melbourne, P.C. (1896).—Son of the 7th Earl of Shaftesbury; born 1836; ed. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Camb.; M.A., 1858; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 1863; was for some time sec. to the late Lord Palmerston; is a mag. for Dorset, and was treas. of county cts. of Dorset from 1863 to 1874; is 2nd church estates comsnr.; parly. sec. to the Board of Trade, Apr., 1880; parly. under-sec. of state for the cols., 12th May, 1882, to 24th June, 1885; sat in the House of Commons for Poole from May, 1873, to Mar., 1880, and for the Isle of Wight from Apr., 1880, to Nov., 1885; is author of a Life of Lord Palmerston.
ASHTON, Hon. James.—B. 1864; M.L.A. for Hay, New S. Wales, 1894; ditto for Goulburn, 1898; min. for lands, New S. Wales, Aug., 1904.

ATCHLEY, C., L.S.O. (1902).—B. 1850; entered the C.O., Aug., 1868; served in ch. clk's, acctnt.'s, and gen. depts.; asst. clk., 2nd class, 1875; supt. of the library, 1st Oct., 1880; mem. libr. comtee., Imp. Instit., 1891; corresp. mem. of Instit. Colonial Universal, 1892; librarian, C.O., 1900; edited "Lucas' Historical Geography of the British Colonies," vol. ii., West Indies, second edition, 1906.

ATHILL, S. L., Junr.—Ag. clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Sept., 1901; ag. 2nd clk., registr.'s off., Antigua, Nov., 1902, to Feb., 1903; ag. jnr. audit clk., Dec., 1903; acctnt., St. John's savings bank, Dec., 1903.
ATKINSON, F. A.—Apptd. inspr. of wks., Cyprus, 1st Feb., 1900; 2nd div. engr., 2nd Jan., 1904; ag. 1st div. engr., May, 1905.

ATKINSON, John Mitford, M.B. (Lond.), 1881; M.R.C.S. (Eng.) and L.S.A., 1878, D.P.H. (Camb.), 1894.—Honours at Pret. Sc. M.B., 1896; prizeman at London Med. Coll., 1896-1897; res. med. offr. St. Mary Abbot's Infirmary, Kensington, 1878-1885; med. offr. No. 3 dist. St. Mary Abbott's, Kensington, 1885-1887; supt. govt. civil hospital, Hong Kong; med. offr. to small-pox hospital and to the govt. lunatic asylum, 1887; J.P. 1890; ag. col. surg., 1896; prin. civ. med. offr., 1897; received thanks of S. of S. for services during plague, 1898; pres. of the Sanitary Board 1897-1900, and again from August, 1901.

ATKINSON, Thos. Henry.—Cadet, destitute poor dept., S. Aust., 1875; clk., 1878; 2nd visiting offr., 1884; 1st ditto, 1886; dep. supt. of dest. asylum, and visiting offr., 1889; supt. of dest. asylum, and sec. to bd., 1897; chrmn. of dest. bd., 1904.

ATTRILL, GEORGE.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of lower (now 2nd) div. of civ. ser., and assigned to C.O. June, 1884; asst. col. sec., G. Coast, Oct., 1893; ag. ch. assist. col. sec. Mar. to Oct., 1896, Dec., 1897, to June, 1898, Dec., 1898, to June, 1899; clk. of coun. Feb. to Sept., 1896, Nov., 1897, to June, 1898; comptroller of cust., Sept., 1899; acted as col. sec., and mem. of ex. and leg. councils, July, 1899; Sept. to Dec., 1899; Sept. to Dec., 1900; and July to Dec., 1901.

AUBER, Steven J.—Temporary outdoor offr. customs, S. Leone, June, 1878; tide waiter, Aug., 1879; landing waiter, Sep., 1880; sen. ag. measuring survr. of shipping, Aug., 1882; clk. of customs, Gambia, 1883; ch. clk. and cashier, 1892.
AUCKLAND, Bishop of, Right Rev M. R. Neligan.—Ed. Reading schl. and Trin. Coll., Dub.; B.A., 1884; deacon, 1886; priest, 1887; formerly Vicar of St. Stephen's, Westbourne Park.

AUSTIN, Henry Boase.—Ed. at Grey Coll., Bloem., O.R.C.; entered late O.F.S. serv. as clk. to surv. gen., 11th Oct., 1889; ch. clk., deeds regisy. off., 28th Dec., 1897; apptd. military regisr. of deeds, O.R.C., 15th Nov., 1900; asst. regisr. of deeds, 20th Dec., 1901; ag. regisr. of deeds, July to Dec., 1901; regisr. of deeds, O.R.C., 1st July, 1905.

AUSTIN, J.—Ck., educn. off., Natal, 1st Jan., 1897; 2nd cls. clk., audit dept., 1st Aug., 1900; re-transferred to educn. dept., 1st June, 1901; examining officer and statistical clerk, 1st July, 1902; acting officer, 8th Aug. to 30th Sept., 1903; accountant and statistical officer, 1st Jan., 1904.

AUSTIN, REV. PRESTON BRUCE.—1st division Lond. matric. and deacon (Durh.), 1888; priest, 1890; assistant master Huddersfield Coll., Yorks, 1884-88; curate Georgetown Cathedral, Br. Guiana, 1888, and of Demerara River dist. 1892; assistant master, Queen's Coll., Georgetown, 1889; J.P. for the Col., 1893; superintendent of missions in Demerara and Essequibo river dists., 1897 to 1904; headmaster, Cape Coast govt. schl., G. Coast, May, 1904; assistant col. chaplain, 1904.

AYLIFFE, GEORGE HAMILTON.—Pol. trooper, S. Austral., Feb., 1859; clk. in G.P.O., Apr., 1860; clk. in pol. commr.'s office, May, 1866; inspector under cent. bd. of health, May, 1873; sec. cent. bd. of health, May, 1883, to Aug., 1904; retired off. under Electoral Act (Dist. E. Adelaide), Dec., 1884, to Aug., 1903; registrar-gen. of births, deaths and marriages, Dec., 1888; is a J.P.; retired off. for the state, and retired off. for central dist. for leg. coun., Aug., 1903; also vaccination offr. and instructor of anatomy, Aug., 1904.
AYTON, A. L.—Appointed, after compet. exam., clk. 2nd division civil ser. and assigned to science and art dept., Mar., 1899; to C.O. July, 1899.

AZOPARDI, VINCENZO FREndo.—LL.D., Malta Univ.; advocate for the poor, 1895; professor of law, Malta Univ., 1899; mag. of judicial police, 1900; assistant crown advocate, 1903; crown advocate and government legal adviser, with seat in exec. coun. and coun. of govt., ex officio, 1905.
BACKHOUSE, ALFRED P.—Crown prosecutor, N.W. dist., N.S. Wales, Oct., 1878; ditto, S. dist., Jan., 1881; et al. district judge, N.W. dist., 1884.

BADELEY, FRANCIS JOSEPH.—Ed. Clergy Orphan School, Canterbury, and Jesus Coll., Camb.; sen. opt. math. tripods, 1889; Hong Kong cadet, Oct., 1890; passed cadet, Jan., 1893; assistant deputy superintendent of police, June to Dec., 1893; assistant postmaster-general, May to July, 1894; assistant registrar-general, July, 1894; J.P., 1894; deputy superintendent of police and assistant superintendence fire brigade, July, 1895; captain of police, superintendence fire brigade, and superintendence Victoria gaol, Apl., 1902; member of leg. council, 1902.

BAILEY, ARTHUR CECIL.—Acted 5th clerk, C.S.O., 10th Dec., 1901, to 3rd Mar., 1902, and 23rd Aug. to 17th Sept., 1902; 3rd clerk, savings bank, 18th Sept., 1902; 5th clerk, C.S.O., 3rd Jan., 1903; assistant clerk, C.S.O., 15th Sept. to 23rd Nov., 1903; 4th clerk, C.S.O., 10th Mar., 1904; assistant 3rd clerk, C.S.O., 14th Sept. to 31st Dec., 1904.

BAILEY, ARTHUR W.—Ed. Dulwich Coll. and St. Cath. Coll., Camb. (scholar); 2nd sen. opt. math. tripods; B.A. 1895; cadet S.S., Nov., 1896; assistant professor of Chinese, Penang, Feb., 1899; passed final exam. in Chinese, June, 1899; assistant professor of Chinese, Sing., Mar., 1901; 4th magistrate, Singapore, May, 1901; 3rd magistrate, Penang, Jan., 1902; assistant professor of Chinese, Penang, Feb., 1902; assistant professor of Chinese, Sing., Aug., 1902, and June, 1904; district officer, Xmas. Is., 1st June, 1904.

BAILEY, DEMETRIUS J. S., B.A. (Camb.).—Served in Methuen's Horse; cadet Sarawak Service, Mar., 1888; assistant res., May, 1891; res., 2nd class, 2nd division, Jan., 1894; assistant res., May, 1891; res., 2nd class, Jan., 1894.

BAILEY, EDWARD S.—2nd clerk, col. treas., Barbados, 1880; chief clerk, 1892; acted as clerk to assistant clerk of appeal in 1892 and other occasions; assistant secretary emigration commission, Oct., 1893; assistant col. treas., 1894.

BAILEY, WILLIAM H., I.S.O. (1905).—Entered the service June, 1875, as 3rd clerk customs dept., Barbados; promoted 2nd clerk, Nov., 1876; chief clerk, col. sec.'s office, and clerk of legislative council, May, 1882; acted for five months in 1892 as governor, Glendairy Prison; ag. audit-general, June, 1893, to April, 1894, Aug. to Dec., 1895, and July to Nov., 1896; ag. audit-general, May to Oct., 1897, and from June, 1898; registrar, Jan., 1898; colonel postmaster, 1900.

BAKER, SIR RICHARD C., K.C.M.G. (1895); C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1842; educated at Eton and Trinity College, Cambridge; B.A. 1864; M.A. 1870; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1864; M.H.A., South Australia, 1868; attorney-general, 1870-1; M.L.C., since 1887; minister of education and leader of council, 1884-5; special envoy from Australian colonies to United Kingdom to negotiate Postal Union, 1885-6; member of Australian federal convention, Sydney, 1891, and chairman of Australian federal convention, Adelaide, Sydney, and Melbourne, 1896-7; president, L.C., 1893 to 1901; member of senate, Australian Parliament, 1901; president, 1901; retired, 1906.

BALL, JAMES DYER, M.R.A.S.—Ed. King's College, London; employed in registrar-general's office, Hong Kong, Feb., 1875; in magistrates' court, Mar., 1875; in education department, Mar., 1876; passed in Chinese; assistant interpreter of Chinese, supreme court, Oct., 1878; chief interpreter, 1881; deputy sheriff, Aug., and sheriff, Sept., 1881; also marshal, Sept., 1881; deputy marshal, V.A. court, 1881 and 1882; assistant, aforesaid, 1882; commissioner of oaths, 1881; employed in consular court, Canton, in connection with Logan case, 1883; 1st clerk of court and judge's clerk, supreme court, Mar., 1890; assistant deputy registrar, supreme court, Nov., 1894; registrar-general, registrar of marriages, and member of sanitary board, Sept., 1898; member of examiners, Sept., 1898; assistant registrar-general and deputy registrar of marriages, Feb., 1899; assistant inspector of schools, Aug., 1900; assistant registrar-general, Aug., 1905; member and secretary, board of inspectors, Aug., 1905; author of "Things Chinese," "The Cantonese Made Easy" series, "Hakka Made Easy," and other books on Chinese languages, literature, religion, and customs.
BALLANTINE, DAVID.—Subordinate collector, Br. N. Guinea, July, 1889; magistrate for native affairs, Nov., 1890; collector of customs, postmaster, and treasurer, Dec., 1893.
BALLARD, H., C.M.G. (1901).—Captain of the port and shipping master, Durban, Natal; retired, 1914.

BANBURY, GEORGE ALEXANDER LETHBRIDGE.—Appointed, to Legacy and Succession Duty Office, London, May, 1873 (upper grade), after competitive examination; assistant collector and treasurer, S. Leone, May, 1880; acted as postmaster from 1880 to 1882; assistant collector of customs, 1882; assistant collector of customs, 1883; member of executive and legislative councils, 1883 to 1884; assistant collector and treasurer, St. Helena, 1884; assistant collector and member of executive council, Mar., 1885; collector of dues and taxes, Seychelles, Sept., 1886; collector, treasurer, and manager of savings bank, 1889; registrar-general of Mauritius, Sept., 1899; provisional nominee, member of government, June, 1900; assistant collector, 19th December, 1904.

BAPTISTE, GEORGE ALBERT CLARENCE.—Ed. City School, Patna, and Patna College; matriculated, Calcutta University, Dec., 1867; assistant commissioner, Patna division, Bengal; sub-deputy magistrate and collector, Tirhoot, 1872; in 1873 passed the test in Hindustani, surveying and engineering, and law; assistant supervisor, Champaran, and special deputy collector for irrigation purposes, Patna, Shahabad, and Gaya, 1873; in 1874 passed the higher examination in law and Hindustani; 1875, deputy magistrate and collector, Patna, but continued on special duty; 1877, on special duty for the... acquisition of land for the Tirhoot State Rly.; stip. mag., Mauritius, July, 1877.

BARBADOS, BISHOP OF (founded 1825), RIGHT REV. WILLIAM PROCTOR SWABY.—Late Barry schlr. and Dur. Exhibr., Hatf. Hall, Durham; B.A. 1873, M.A. 1876, B.D. 1887, D.D. 1890; Cons. Lord Bish. of Guiana, 1893; transf. to Barbados, 1899; V. of Castletown, co. Durham, 1874-84; V. of St. Mark, Millfield, co. Dur. 1884-93.

BARKER, ALFRED J. GLANVILLE, M.R.C.S. Eng., 1884; certif. medico-psychol. assoc.—Ed. Univ. Coll. and Hosp.; 1st M.B., Lon.; late res. clin. asst. E. Lond. Hosp. for Children; house-surg., Tiverton Infir., Cent. Lond. Ophthalmic Hosp.; res. clin. asst., Bethlehem Royal Hosp.; temp. asst. med. offr., Surrey Co. Asyl., Tooting; ag. col. surg. and res., Penang, S. Stltlts., 1895; J.P. and coroner, Penang, 1896; prin. med. offr., Sarawak, May, 1897.

BARKER, GENERAL SIR G. DIGBY, K.C.B. (1900), C.B. (1888).—B. 1833; Ensign 78th Highlanders, 1835; adj. 1858; col. in the army, 1887; maj.-gen., 1887; lieut.-gen., 1895; gen., 1900; served in Persian war, 1857 (medal with clasp); in Indian Mutiny, 1857-8, including battle of Cawnpore; relief, defence, and capture of Lucknow (as D.A.Q.M.G.) (mentioned in despatches, medal with two clasps, brevet maj., and a year's service); graduated Staff Coll., 1866; special service at army headqrs., 1867-8; D.A.Q.M.G., S. dist., 1867-73; prof. of mil. art. and history at the Staff Coll., 1874-6; asst. dir. of mil. educn. at army headqrs., 1877-83; A.A.G. and A.Q.M.G., western dist., 1884-87; commanded the troops in China and Hong Kong, 1890-95; ag. gov. of H. Kong, May to Dec., 1891; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Bermuda, 1896-1901.

BARLOW, HON. ANDREW HENRY.—B. 1837; M.L.A. for Ipswich, Queensland, 1888-96; sec. for pub. lands and agric., 1893-6; min. without portfolio, 1896-9; sec. for pub. instr. since Sept., 1903, and govt. representative in Council.
BARNARD, JAMES.—Ent. govt. service, 1864; sec. for customs, Tasmania, 1894.

BARNES, JOHN FREDERICK EVELYN, C.M.G. (1901), M.I.C.E.; M.I.C.E. Ire.—Asst. county survr., Antrim, 1872; on Abercorn Estates, 1873-9; govt. survr., Natal, 1880; Durban boro. engr., 1882; asst. col. engrnr. and surv. gen. Natal, 1888; ag. col. engrnr., with seats in exec. and legis. couns., 1889-90; engrnr., P.W. dept., 1894; ch. engrnr. of P.W., 1897.

BARNES, WALTER STANLEY, M.D., M.R.C.S., L.S.A.—Asst. med. sup. Lunatic Asyl., Berbice, 1888; ag. med. sup. 1890; med. sup. Lepor Asylums, 1891; asst. res. surg., Georgetown Hosp., 1893; res. surg., 1896.

BARNES, WARREN DELABERE, B.A.—Ed. King's Coll. Sch. and Pemb. Coll., Camb., cadet, S.S., Nov., 1888; passed final exam. in Chinese, Nov., 1891; 3rd mag., Penang, 1893; ag. prot. of Chinese, Perak, Oct., 1893; warden of mines, Perak, Jan., 1896; warden of mines, prot. of Chinese and sen. mag., Pahang, Jan., 1890; asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, Dec., 1901; ag. comsnr. of lands and mines, F.M.S., Aug., 1903; ag. Brit. res., Pahang, 4th Nov., 1903; ag. prot. of Chinese, S.S., Mar., 1904; sec. for Chinese affairs, S.S. and F.M.S., 1st May, 1904.

BARNETT, BERTRAM LEEDS THOMAS, M.A. (Honors in Nat. Sci. Tripos, 1896) Camb.; M.B.; B.C. Camb.; M.R.C.S. Eng.; L.R.C.P.; D.P.H. Lond.—Ed. Malvern Coll., St. John's Coll., Camb., and St. George's Hosp., Lond.; late senior house surg., Cheltenham hosp.; civ. surg., H.M. brigade of guards; asst. med. registr., St. George's hosp.; asst. med. offr. of health, H. Kong, 1903.

BARNETT-CLARKE, H. P.—Temp. clk. H. of Assm., Cape, 1885-7; ass't lib., joint library of parlmt., 1887-97; ass't clk. of coun., clk. of the papers, and shorthand writer, 1897; clk. of coun. and offr. in charge of books, 1st Jan., 1904.

BARRETT, S.—Supt. of natives, Grahamstown, Cape, Oct., 1875; also inspr. of locations, Albany, 1876; commanded a Fingo levy in Gaika war, 1878 (medal); J.P., Albany, etc.; R.M., Quthing, and J.P. for Basutoland, June, 1882; asst. comsnr., Quthing, 1884; transf. to Leibe, Nov., 1886; Basutoland comsnr. on boundary delineation between Basutoland and O.F.S., Sept., 1891; transf. to Quthing dist., 1893; bndry. comsn. between Basutoland and Cape Colony, 1896; organised and commd. force of Basuto on Drakensberg in Boer war during operations of Gen. French in Barkly East, Cape Colony (medal); asst. comsnr., Mafeteng, July, 1905.

BARTON, THE RIGHT HON. SIR EDMUND, P.C. (1901), G.C.M.G. (1902).—Mem. legis. assem., N.S. Wales; atty.-gen. in Dibbs' ministry, 1891-3; mem. Fedn. Convention, 1897-8; leader of Opposition, N.S.W., 1898-9; chief Austr. del. to England in connection with passing of Federation Act, 1900; first Prime Minister of the Commonwealth of Australia and min. of state for External Affs., 1st Jan., 1901; judge of high court, Sept., 1903.
BARTON, FRANCIS RICKMAN, C.M.G. (1905).—Formerly a capt. in W. India Regt.; priv. sec. to lieut.-govr. of B.N. Guinea, 1898; res. mag. of B.N.G., 1903; admstr. of govt. of B.N.G., 6th Apr., 1904.

BATESON, EDWARD.—Ed. at Rugby and King's Coll., Camb.; exhibnr., 1888; schlr., 1889; sen. in the historical tripos, 1890; editor of vols. i. and ii. of a "History of Northumberland," issued under the direction of a comte., 1891-4; called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), 1896; dist. and stip. mag. in Mauritius, 1901; att.-gen., St. Lucia, 1903.
BATHFIELD, WM.—4th clk., regisy., sup. ct., Mauritius, 6th Sep., 1873; ch. clk., master's off., 9th Oct., 1896; ag. acctnt. in bankruptcy, 1898-99.
BATHURST, W. DUNDAS.—Ed. Lancing Coll.; agt. of Congo Free State, 1883-6; apptd. to col. audit branch exchequer and audit dep., Jan., 1889; local audr., Gibraltar, under C. and A. G., Jan., 1891.

BATTERBEE, HARRY F.—Ed. at Grammar Schl., Faversham, and at Oxford; classical scholar, Hertford Coll., 1899-1904; 1st cls. class. mods., 1901; 1st cls. math. mods., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1904; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., exchequer and audit dept., Jan., 1905; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., May, 1905.

BAUERLE, WALTER, B.A., St. John's Coll., Camb. (class. tripos, 1899); apptd. clk., col. audit. branch of E. and A. Dept., 4th Nov., 1901; asst. auditor, S. Nigeria, 4th Sept., 1902; ag. asst. treas., Oct., 1902; ag. local auditor in 1903, 1904, 1905 and 1906.

BAUMGARTNER, G. A.—Writer Ceylon Civil Service, July, 1871; Class V., Feb., 1874; Class IV., Sept., 1876; Class III., Feb., 1883; Class II., Dec., 1894; govt. agt., prov. of Uva, Apl., 1897; dist. judge, Kurunegala, Oct., 1901; Class I., Jan. 1902; ag. dist. judge, Kandy, Apl., 1902; dist. judge, Galle, Apl., 1903.
BAUMGARTNER, H. V.—Cik., land settm't. dept., O.R.C., 16th Oct., 1902; cik., audit dept., 2nd Dec., 1902.

BAXENDALE, F. R. S.—Ed. at Charterhouse, cadet, Fiji civil service, 1884; passed cadet, 1887; European offr. armed native constab., 1886; 2nd cik. and interpr. prov. dept., 1888; stip. mag., 1889; res. comsnr. Cakaudrove Prov., 1892; ag. sup't. of pol. and dep.-commdt. armed native constab., sheriff, and marshal of the admiralty division of the supreme court, 1896; stip. mag., Levuka, and ag. stip. mag., Tailevu, 1897; also comsnr., Lomaiviti, 1899; ag. nat. comsnr., mem. exec. coun., 1903, and mem. of nat. regs. revision comttee.; M.L.C., 1905.
BAYER, CHAS. ALBERT.—Asst. survr., P.W.D. (Waterworks branch), S. Aust., 1882; sanitary engrn., 1888; hyd. engrn., 1902.

BAYLY, MAJOR GEORGE C.—(5th Batt. Rifle Brigade).—Passed school of instruction, Mar., 1879; 1st class extra certif., Hythe, 1886; and instructor of musketry, 1886; asst. inspr. G.C.C., Dec., 1883; dist. comsnr., Lagos and Badagry, 1884; ditto, Cape Coast, 1885; sub.-inspr., Br. Honduras constab., Dec., 1888; mag., Orange Walk, Nov., 1889, to Nov., 1890; aide-de-camp to Sir A. Moloney, Sept., 1891, to Jan., 1897; inspr., 1893; ag. inspr. commandant, 1894; ch. of police and excise, Grenada, Feb., 1898; M.L.C., Mar., 1899; mem. of St. Vincent police comsnr., 1903, recd. thanks of S. of S.; local comdt. mil. police, and asst. comsnr., Cyprus, Dec., 1903.
BAYLY, LIEUT.-COL. Z. S., C.M.G. (1880).—Comdg. right wing, Cape Mounted Rifles; and ag. commdt.-gen. of Cape col. forces; commdt.-gen., 1883; ret., 1892.

BAYNES, EDWARD WILLIAM.—Ag. cik. comsnr.'s office, Montserrat, and cik. couns. Jan. and Feb., 1896, and May, 1897; ag. 4th cik. col. sec.'s office, Antigua, 1896, 1897, and 1898; ag. ch. cik. audit office and federal acctnt., Dec., 1897; ag. treas'y. office, Montserrat, Dec., 1898; 2nd cik., G.P.O., Antigua, Jan., 1899; ag. ch. cik. G.P.O., Apl., 1899, to Feb., 1900; ag. cik. to mag. dist. A, Feb. to May, and June to Dec., 1900; cik. to comsnr., Virgin Islds., Jan., 1901, also cik. to treas'y., clk. legis. coun., and registr. of births, etc., dist. D; survr. of ships, June, 1901.

BEARD, CHARLES HALMAN.—Called to the bar, Leeward Is., Mar., 1875; awarded equity prize by coun. of legal educn., Lond., 1881; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Trin., 1882; ag. puisne judge, Leeward Is., on various occasions, 1886-1893; ag. solr.-gen., on various occasions, 1887-1889; ag. atty.-gen. (9 times), 1888-1898; apptd. solr.-gen., 15th Oct., 1889; res. mag., St. Mary, Jamaica, 28th Jan., 1898; regisr. friendly societies, Antigua, Apl., 1889, to Oct., 1893; mem. leg. coun., Antigua, 1899, 1890, 1896, and 1897; J.P., Antigua, Sept., 1889; visiting justice of gaol, Antigua, Sept., 1889 to 1897; res. mag., St. Mary, Jamaica, 1898.

BEATTY, DAVID.—Cadet, S. Sttnmts., Nov., 1898; passed final exam. in Chinese, June, 1901; ag. 3rd magis., Sing., Nov., 1901; ag. dist. offr., (Christmas Is.), July, 1903; 3rd mag., Penang, Oct., 1903; but cont. to act as dist. offr.; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., May, 1904; ag. 2nd asts. prot. of Chinese, Sing., June, 1904; ag. sheriff and dep. regist., Penang, Dec., 1905.

BEAUCHAMP, 7th EARL (creat. 1815), VISCT. ELMLEY (1815), BARON BEAUCHAMP (1806, U.K.); K.C.M.G. (1899); WILLIAM LYON; B. 1872; succeeded his father, 1891.—Ed. Eton and Chr. Ch., Oxford; D.L. Worcestershire; Mayor of Worcester, 1895-6; mem. London schl. bd., 1897-9; gov. N.S.W., 1899 to 1900; capt. of hon. corps of Gentlemen-at-Arms, 1906.

BEAUMONT, W. H.—Ensign 75th (Stirlingshire) Regt., Aug., 1870; lieut. Oct., 1871; served on the "Langalibalele Expedition" in 1873; ret. Aug., 1878; priv. sec. to Colonel Milles (administering the govt. of Natal), May, 1873; to Sir Benjamin Pine (lieut.-gov.), and cik. to the exec. coun., July, 1873; ag. R.M., Umzazi Div., Aug., 1874; cik., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1875; ag. gov.'s cik. and cik. to the exec. coun., Oct., 1875; R.M., Newcastle division, Feb., 1878; col. comdt. of Dist. No. 1, Natal, at the commencement of the Zulu War, Jan., 1879, during which time he raised levies called the "Newcastle Scouts"; R.M., Inanda, May, 1887; R.M., City div., Pietermaritzburg, 1890; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., for various periods, 1st Feb., 1895, to Oct., 1902; judge of special treason et al., Oct., 1901; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1st Nov., 1902.

BEDFORD, ADMIRAL SIR FREDERICK GEORGE DENHAM, G.C.B. (1902); K.C.B. (1895).—B. 1838; entered R.N., 1852; lieut., 1859; commander, 1871; capt., 1876; commander-in-chief at the Cape and West Coast of Africa, 1892-95; Lord of the Admiralty, 1889-92, and 1895-99; commander-in-chief on N. American and W. Indian station, 1899; gov. of W. Australia, 16th Jan., 1903.

BEDWELL, HORACE, B.A., Jesus Coll., Camb.—Apptd. asst. dist. comsnr. Niger (now S. Nigeria) Protec., June, 1896; wounded while on duty Qua Ibo Riv., Sept., 1896; ag. dist. comsnr. Old Calabar, Dec. 1896-June, 1897; apptd. dist. comsnr. Warri dist., Jan., 1898; sent up Niger to take over Asaba dist. from Roy. Niger Co., Dec., 1899; dist. comsnr. Asaba, Dec., 1899-Sep., 1900; ag. divsnl. comsnr. Cent. (Niger) div. S. Nigeria Protec., June, 1901, to Dec., 1901; transfd. to secretariat as ch. asst. sec., Feb., 1902; ag. sec. to govt., Mar.-Sept., 1902; ag. divsnl. comsnr. and ag. sec. to the govt., 8th Mar., 1902, to 24th Sept., 1902, 17th Apr., 1903, to 5th Aug., 1904, and on various occasions from 3rd Apr., 1905 to Mar., 1906; dep. high comsnr., Apr., 1906; prov. comsnr., 1st May, 1906.

BELFIELD, HENRY CONWAY.—Ed. Rugby and Oriel Coll., Oxon.; B.A., 1877; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1880; went W. circuit; mag., collr. of land rev., and inspr. of schls., Selangor, 1884; ch. mag. and comsnr. of lands, 1888; ag. Br. res., June and July, 1889; sen. mag., Perak, 1891; ag. sec. to govt., Perak, July, 1891, to Jan. 1893; comsnr. of lands, Federated Malay States, July, 1896; ag. British res., Selangor, on various occasions from Mar. 1897 to Oct. 1899, and since Jan. 1, 1900; British res., Negri Sembilan, Apl., 1901; British res., Selangor, Aug., 1902; J.P. for Devonshire.

BELL, ARCHIBALD G., M.I.C.E.—Ed. at Felstead and Uppingham; employed on surveys Jamaicra rlyw. extensions, 1887-8, and by Jamaica rlyw. co. after sale of line, 1889-90; asst. to W. Shelford, Esq., M.I.C.E., Apr. to Oct., 1890; asst. to Messrs. Hawkshaw and Hayter, 1890-1891; asst. engrn. P.W.D., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1891; asst. col. civil engrn., Oct., 1900; col. civil engrn., Aug., 1901; apptd. official member et al. of policy, Feb., 1901.

BELL, EDWARD.—Served in the R.I.C. from Dec., 1885, to Aug., 1896; Hythe certif. musketry, 1894; ag. inspr., L. Islds. police, 1898; drill instr., St. Kitts-Nevis defence force, and inspr. L. Islds. police, Sept., 1898; capt. and adjut. Antigua defence force, ag. inspr.-gen., L. Islds. police, and ag. inspecting offr. defence forces of the colony, Apl. Nov., 1903; chmn., St. Kitts telephone board, 1902 and 1904; ag. inspr.-gen., L. Islds. police, 1st Apl., 1905; J.P. for Antigua and St. Kitts-Nevis.

BELL, GEORGE CECIL.—Ed. Bedford grammar and modern schl.; midshipman, R.N.R., 1898-1901; supernumerary sub-insp., Jamaica constab., Mar. to Nov., 1901; 3rd cls. clk., revenue dept., Jamaica, Dec., 1901, to Mar., 1902; asst. supt. of pol., B. Hond., Apl., 1902; asst. comsnr. of pol., G. Coast, 14th July, 1906.

BELL, GEORGE GERALD.—Apptd. to Imperial civ. serv. after compet. exam., Nov., 1894; bookkeeper, P.W.D., Lagos, Nov., 1897; clk. and acctnt., marine dept., So. Nigeria, Nov., 1902; asst. sec., So. Nigeria, May, 1904.

BELL, HARRY CHARLES PURVIS.—Ed. at Cheltenham Coll.; writer Ceylon civil service, July, 1873; pol. mag., Matala, Mar., 1876, landing and tide survr., Galle, Jan., 1878; office asst. Colombo, to the govt. agt.; W. Prov., Feb., 1878; 1st landing survr., customs, Colombo, May, 1883; ast. collr., customs, Galle, Apr., 1884; ast. agt. and additional dist. judge, Kegalla, June, 1885; ag. 2nd ast. to col. sec., May, 1886; ag. landing survr., Colombo, 1887; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Nov. 1888; ditto Kegalle, July, 1889; archaeological comsnr., Dec., 1895; officer of Class I, but to continue as arch. comsnr., 23rd June, 1903.

BELL, H. HESKETH, C.M.G. (1903).—B. 1865; Ed. in Paris; 3rd cls. gov.-in-chief's office, Barbados, May, 1882; transferred to treasy. dept., Grenada, 1883; supervisor of customs, G. Coast, Jan., 1890; sen. ast. treas., G. Coast, Aug., 1891; ag. dist. comsnr. and sheriff, Accra, 1892; ag. col. treas., 1893; rec. gen., Bahamas, Dec., 1894; represented Harbour Isld. in House of Assem., 1895-6; ag. col. sec., July to Oct., 1897, Apl. to Nov. 1898; admnstr. of Dominica, Aug., 1899; ag. gov., Leeward Is., Aug., 1904, and from June, 1905; comsnr. and comdr-in-chief, Uganda Prt., 31st Jan., 1906; author of "Geography of the Gold Coast," "Obeah," &c.
BELL, DR. J.—Ast. surgeon govt. med. dept., Hong Kong, May, 1896; ag. supt. govt. civil hos., 1896; ag. prin. civil med. offr., 1900-1; supt., govt. civ. hosp., 1903.
BELL, THE HON. JAMES.—Min. without portfolio, Victoria, Feb., 1886; min. of defence, 1888-90.

BELL, HON. JOSHUA THOS.—B. 1863; called to the bar, Inner Tem.; ditto, Queensland; priv. sec. to Sir Samuel Griffith, 1890; M.L.A. for Dalby, Queensland, since 1893; chmn. of comtees, 1902-3; sec. for pub. lands, Sept., 1903.
BELL, VALENTINE G., C.M.G. (1903), B. 1839; M.I.C.E.—Ch. res. engnr., Jamaica Govt. rly., 1890-86; dir. of pub. wks., Mar., 1887; ex-officio mem. legis. coun., mem. of priv. coun., Aug., 1886.

BELL, W. G., M.A., Glasgow.—Cadet, S. Settlements, Oct., 1897; passed fin. exam. in Chinese, May, 1900; supintndt. army order branch and govt. sav. banks, Singapore, July, 1901; 2nd ast. prot. of Chinese, Penang, May, 1902; ag. ast. postmr.-gen., Penang, May, 1903; ag. postmr.-gen., S.S., Oct., 1903; asst. postmr.-gen., Singapore, Oct., 1905.

BELLAIRS, Lieut.-Gen. Sir William, K.C.M.G. (1882), C.B. (1878).—B. 1828; entered the army, 1846; adj., 1851; capt., 1854; major, 1855; lieut.-col., 1865; col., 1873; brig.-gen., 1881. Served with the 49th Regt., and on the staff of the army throughout the Crimean campaign, being present at the siege and fall of Sebastopol; medal with three clasps, Turkish medal, 5th class of Medjidie, knight of legion of honour; served throughout the Kaffir campaign, 1877-8, when he commanded the combined forces on the E. frontier; also throughout the Zulu campaign, 1879. Has also acted as inspr.-gen. of pol., Barbados, 1897-8; local inspr. of army schls., 1868-73; col. sec., Gibraltar, Aug. to Nov. 1872; admnstr. of Natal, Apr. to May, 1880; mem. of exec. coun., Transvaal, 1880-1; comdr. of the forces at Pretoria in the Transvaal, 1880-81; admnstr. of Transvaal, Apr. to June, 1881.

BELLAMY, CHAS. VINCENT, M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., M.I.E.E., F.G.S., F.R.Met.Soc.—Ed. Plymouth Coll.; Queen's Prize man, S. Kensington, 1885; ast. to borough and water engrn., Plymouth, 1887; dist. engrn. P.W.D., Ceylon, May, 1889; 2nd financial ast., Colombo, 1889; dist. engrn., Pallai, Jan., 1890; dist. engrn., Vavoniva, Vilankulam, Apr., 1890; dist. engrn., Jaffna, Jan., 1891; exec. engrn., Victoria Bridge, Colombo, Sept., 1893; col. engrn. and survr.-gen., Dominica, W.I., Nov., 1895; also comsnr. ordnance property, July, 1897; nom. mem. Roseau Town Board, July, 1896; on special service at Antigua, Aug., 1896; and at Montserrat, Jan. and Feb., 1897; dir. pub. wks., Cyprus, May, 1898; also dir. of irrigtn., July, 1900; on spec. serv. in Egypt, Nov., 1902; dir. pub. wks. and dir. govt. telegraphs, Lagos, Jan., 1904; received thanks of S. of S., April, 1898, for a report on the pub. wks. of Dominica, and elaborated a scheme (since adopted) for the development of the island by means of roads; reed. thanks of govt., Jan., 1904, for services in Cyprus; author of "Rainfall of Dominica"; "Climate of Cyprus"; "Rainfall in relation to Irrigation, Cyprus"; "Victoria Bridge, Colombo"; "Economic Construction of Roads"; "Monograph of the Main Roads of Cyprus"; "The Salt Lake of Larnaca, Cyprus"; compiled geological map of Cyprus, with key; joint author with Mr. A. J. Jukes Browne of "The Geology of Cyprus"; author of "A West African Smelting House," &c., &c.

BELMORE, EARL OF, P.C. (Ireland, 1867), G.C.M.G. (1890), K.C.M.G. (1872), Somerset Richard Lowry Corry, fourth Earl and Viscount Belmore of the co. Fermanagh, Belmore, Viscount and Baron Belmore, of Castle Coole, co. Fermanagh, in the peerage of Ireland, of which he is a representative peer.—B. 1835; graduated M.A. (hon.) at Cambridge, 1856; elected a representative peer for Ireland, Jan. 1857; was under sec. of state for home dept. in Lord Derby's 3rd adminstrn., July, 1866; gov. of N. S. Wales, Aug., 1867-1872; served as one of the Lords Justices of Ireland, 1885; again 1886.

BENKA-COKER, AMBROSE PETERSON.—Ed. Wesleyan High schl. and Educl. Inst., Freetown, S. Leone; pioneer offr. and offr. of customs (3rd cl.) of Southern Nigeria—then the Oil River—Protectorate, July, 1891; attached to the preventive service and stationed at Idu, Sep. to Dec., 1892; 2nd cl. offr., Mar., 1893; 1st cl. Apl., 1897; has been successively prin. customs offr. of Bonny, Warri, Old Calabar and New Calabar; transf'd to Bathurst, Gambia, as chief landing waiter, etc., 1st Aug., 1900; actd. as tide survr., warehouse keeper, and wharfmaster, 1st Nov., 1901, to 2nd Mar., 1902.

BENNET, JOHN LIGHT.—Ed. Bourton Acad., near Bath, Somerset; joined London pol., Scotland Yard, 1879; wt. offr., Southwark pol. ct., 1883; sergt. in charge of Holloway pol. statn., 1887; ag. inspr., 1888; sen. inspr. pol., Gibraltar, 1889; ch. of pol., 1895; sent to S. Africa on spec. serv., 1902.

BENNETT, C. KAYE.—Tempy. outdoor offr., S. Leone, July, 1887; recd. thanks of S. of S. with gratuity, 1889; warehouseman, 1890; ch. clk. of customs, Niger Coast Protectorate, 1891; supervisor of customs, Southern Nigeria, Jan., 1906.

BENNETT, HENRY JAMES LEE.—M.R.C.S., Eng., 1878; L.R.C.P. and L.M., Edin., 1878; med. offr., Grenada, 1882; late house-surg., gen. infirmary, Dewsbury, Yorks.; jun. res. med. offr., Royal Albert hospital and eye infirmary, Devonport, Devon; mem. Br. Med. Assoc.; J.P., 1890.

BENNETT, WILLIAM HART.—Entered C.O. Nov., 1878; ch. clk. ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, Jan., 1884; ag. asst. to ch. sec., Aug., 1886; acted as ch. sec., July to Oct., 1893; ag. comsrr. Papho, May to July, 1894; asst. sec. to govt., June, 1895; ag. ch. sec. and mem. exec. coun., July, 1895, to Jan., 1896, Jan. to April, 1898, July to Dec., 1899; col. sec., Falklands, 1900; admnted. govt., May to Nov., 1902, and June to Sept., 1904; col. sec., Bahamas, 1905; admnted. govt., Sept.-Oct., 1906.

BENNETT, WM. HENRY.—Clk. and acctnt. educn. dept., Natal, 1st Mar., 1895; acctng. offr., educn. dept., 1st July, 1898; govt. nominee mem. of Council of Natal Soc'y., 1st Aug., 1901; sec., educn. dept., 1st July, 1902.

BENSON, J. HAWTREY, A.B., M.D., F.R.C.P. Irel.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; 1st hors. maths., graduated, 1864; consulting physician to Royal City of Dublin Hosp., and to the Monkstown Hosp.; Fellow of Acad. of Med., Ireland; late censor and examr., Royal Coll. of Phys., Ireland, and late external examr. in med., Dub. Univ.; is now med. adver. for Ireland to C.O.; author of numerous papers and articles on technical med. subjects.

BENT, HON. THOMAS.—M.L.A. for Brighton, Victoria, 1873; comsrr. of pub. wks., 1880; comsrr. for rlyws. and vice-pres. of board of land and wks., 1881-3; speaker, 1892-4; vice-pres. of board of land and wks., 1902-4; comsrr. of pub. wks., min. of health, and min. for rlyws., 1903-4; premier and treas., Victoria, Feb., 1904.

BERESFORD, M. H. DE LA POER, I.S.O. (1904).—Rev. offr., Leeward dist., St. Vincent, Oct., 1883; acted as pol. mag. and coroner, Windward dist., Dec., 1884; confidential clk. and clk. of couns., Grenada, Jan., 1885, and in conjunction therewith priv. sec. to gov., Sir Walter J. Sendall, K.C.M.G., Nov., 1886, to Nov., 1889; ag. inspr. of prisons, Jan. to Mar., 1889; clk. of couns., and ch. clk. to gov., Windward Is., Nov., 1889; priv. sec. to Sir A. Moloney, K.C.M.G., in conjunction with appts. of clk. of couns., and ch. clk., gov.'s office, Nov., 1897, to May, 1900; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 11th July, 1900; asst. sec. to admtn., 1st Sept., 1900; asst. sec. to admtn., Sept., 1900, to Jan., 1901, Jan. to Aug., 1902, May to June, 1903; apptd. sec. to admtn., 1st June, 1903.

BERKELEY, SIR HENRY SPENCER, KT. BACH. (1896).—B. 1851; called to the bar, Inner Tem., June, 1873; ag. atty.-gen. of the Leeward Is., Aug., 1877; solr.-gen., Leeward Is., June, 1878; col. sec., Leeward Is. (ag.), 1883; atty.-gen., Fiji, 1888; ag. ch. justice and ch. judicial comsrr. for W. Pacific, May, 1887, to July, 1888; ag. high comsrr. for W. P., Dec., 1887, to Feb., 1888; ch. justice of Fiji and ch. judicial comsrr. for W. P., Feb., 1889. Has admnted. the govt. of Fiji and acted as high comsrr. W. P. on several occasions; recd. thanks of S. of S. for desp. relating to native affairs, 1894; atty.-gen., Hong Kong, May, 1902; ag. ch. just., Apl. to June, 1903, and Aug., 1904, to May ; 1905 ; ret., Oct. 1906.

BERKELEY, HUBERT.—Midshipman, R.N.R., 1881; to Perak, 1884; pol., 1886; supt. of Penghulus, 1889; ag. collr. and mag., Upper Perak, 1891; asst. mag., Gopeng, 1894; ag. dist. mag., Kuala Kangsar, 1896; dist. mag., Upper Perak, 1889; dist. offr., Klang, 1903; ditto, U. Perak, Sep., 1904.

BERKELEY, MAURICE J.—Ed. at Cheltenham and priv. tuttn.; barrister-at-law (Lincoln's Inn), 1896; ag. pol. mag., Tobago, 1882; St. Vincent, 1884; Barbados, 1883-1892; inland rev. offr., Barbados, 1883; J.P., 1884; pol. mag. and petty debt ct. judge, Dist. "D," Barbados, 1892; Bridgetown, Jan., 1899; ag. ct. of appl. judge, 1900-01; S.J.P., Port of Spain, 1902.

BERNAYS, LEWIS ADOLPHUS, C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1831; F.L.S., F.R.G.S., and corpdg. mem. of various scientific societies.—Ed. at King's Coll. and St. Thomas's Hosp., and was an offr. of Parl. in N.S. Wales, 1853-9; clk. of the legia. assem., Queensland, 1859; is sec. of the Brisbane Water Supply, and local dir. of various public instns.

BERRINGTON, ARTHUR T. D.—Ed. Clifton Coll. and Chr. Ch., Oxford; B.A., 1878; called Inner Tem., 1879; Oxford circuit; J.P. and D.L. for Monmouthshire; priv. sec. to Sir H. Bulwer in Cyprus, Oct., 1888, and to Sir Frederick Dickson in the S. Sttlmsts., May, 1890; ch. mag., Sélängor, 1891; sen. mag. Perak, July, 1896; legal adviser, F.M.S., Nov., 1902; sen. judl. comsrr., Jan., 1906.

BERTEAU, F. C.—Ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey, 1865-72; ent. Newfoundland civ. ser. as clk. H.M. Customs, 1878; collr. of cust., Labrador, 1881; clk. in col. sec.'s offr., 1889; ch. clk., 1890; J.P. for col., 1893; mission to Ottawa to inquire into Canadian fin. system, with view of applying same to Newfld., May, 1898; audr.-gen. Newfld., July, 1898.

BERTRAM, ANTON.—Ed. at City of London sch., and Camb.; scholar of Caius Coll., 1887; Fellow, 1891; 1st cl. class. trip., part I., 1890; ditto, part II., 1891; Powis medal for Latin verse; Chancellor's medal for classics, 1891; B.A., 1890; M.A., 1892; president, Union Soc., 1891; Tancred law studentship, Lincoln's Inn, 1890; called, 1893; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1902; elected to H. of Assem., June, 1903; ag. col. sec., May to Sept., 1903; admnted. govt., Sept. to Oct., 1905; ag. ch. justice, Sep.-Nov., 1906; chrmn. of comsrr. on the Out Is., 1906; puisne judge, Cyprus, 1906; joint author, "Handbook to Workmen's Compensation Acts, 1897 and 1904."

BERTRAM, LOUIS J., C.M.G. (1904).—Ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey, 1870-7; apptd. to exchequer and audit dept., Mar., 1878; asst. auditor, G. Coast Col., Oct., 1884; ag. auditor, 1885, 1886, 1887; auditor, 1888. Prepared scheme of store accounts introduced into G. Coast and Lagos, 1886; auditor of Windward Is., 1893; ditto, Leeward Is., 1896; ditto Jamaica, 1897.

BEST, T. A. V.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; Taylorian Univ. scholar; gold medallist of French Ministry of pub. instruction; asst., B. Cent. Africa adminstr., 1896; 2nd cls. asst., July, 1898; judicial offr., Mar., 1898; ag. ch. judicial offr., Nov., 1899, to Mar., 1900; ag. vice-consul, Chinde, Aug., 1902, to Apl., 1903; ag. dep. comsrr., Apl. to Dec., 1903, and June, 1905 to Jan. 1906; dist. res., 1st cls., Apr., 1906; ag. asst. dep. comsnr., Jan. to Aug., 1906.

BETHELL, ARNOLD TALBOT.—Ed. Ardingley Coll., Sussex, and Ay. Hall, Camb.; asst. instr. Beckford and Smith's foundation school, Spanish Town, Jamaica, 1899; ent. civ. ser. Bahamas, July, 1900; asst. resident justice, Watling's; 3rd clk. G.P.O., May, 1901.
BEVAN, J. F.—Apptd. spec. auditor for repatriation and transfd. from E. and A. dept., 29th November, 1902; asst. col. treas., O.R.C., 1st June, 1903; ag. aud.-gen., Aug., 1905, to Feb., 1906.

BEVERLEY, WM. HENRY.—Ed. Bishop's high schl., Poona, India; F.R.G.S.; 2nd lieut., 4th Norfolk regt., 1889; lieut., 3rd Essex regt., 1890; capt., ditto, 1899; sub-commdt., R. Niger Co.'s constab., and govr., Asaba Gaol, 1899; Wing offr., N.C. Prot. Force (afterwards S. Nigeria regt.), 1900; intell. offr., S. Nig. regt., 1903; operations in Niger Co.'s territories, 1899 (medal and clasp); Obium expdn., 1901; Oran expdn., 1901; Aro expdn. (medal and clasp), 1901; Asaba hinterland, 1902; Uri Omonoha hinterland, 193.
BICKLE, W. H.—Apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div., and assigned to C.O., 17th Mar., 1905.
BICKNELL, W. A.—Ch. clk. col. sec.'s office, Singapore, Sep., 1885; auditor, Penang, July, 1888.

BIDDLE, FREDERICK WM.—Copyist in col. sec.'s off., Br. Honduras, Feb., 1894; clk. to admnsmr., Apl. to Dec., 1895, and Jan. to Feb., 1897; 3rd clk. col. sec.'s off., June, 1898; clk. to audr., Jan., 1899, to June, 1900; ag. auditor, Sep., 1900; 4th clk., treasy. and cust. and cl. to comsners. of currency, 1900; 3rd clk., treasy. and cust. dept., and clk. to comsners. of currency, 27th Dec., 1901; ag. 2nd clk., treasy. and cust. dept., and clk. to quarantine bd., 1st Dec., 1902; ag. 2nd clk., col. sec.'s dept., 16th Dec., 1904; 1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s dept., 1st Feb., 1906; priv. sec. to O.A.G., 1905-6.

BIDDULPH, GEN. SIR ROBERT, R.A., G.C.B., G.C.M.G. (1886), K.C.M.G. (1880).—B. 1835; apptd. to Roy. Artillery, 1853; served in Crimean campaign, 1854-56; Ind. Mutiny campaign, 1856-57; China war, 1860. Staff services: Dep. ast. adj.-gen. in India, 1858-60; mil. sec. in China, 1860-61; mil. sec., Madras, 1861-65; dep. ast. qtrmr.-gen., Woolwich, 1868-71; priv. sec. to Mr. Cardwell when sec. of state for war, 1871-73; ast. adj.-gen., War Office, 1873-78; special service, Cyprus, 1878; commanding the troops, Cyprus, 1879; was an ast. boundary comnrs. for the Parly. Reform Act of 1867; was apptd., 1879, H.M.'s comsnr. for arranging the payment to be made to the Porte under the annex to the Convention of 4th June, 1878; and high comsnr. for Cyprus, 1879; inspr.-gen. of recruiting, 1886; dir.-gen. of mil. educn., 1st Mar., 1888; qtrmr.-gen., Jan., 1893; gov. and comdrr.-in-chief, Gibraltar, Oct., 1893, to 1900.

BIDEN, ARTHUR GERALD.—Ed. at Radley Coll. and Trin. Coll., Oxon.; B.A. (honour jurisprudence), 1902; student, Inner Temple, 1902; clk., col. audit branch of E. and A. dept., Feb., 1903; ast. audr., G. Coast, Apr., 1904; local audr., Gambia, Sept., 1905.
BIGGS, JOHN W. FORBES.—Lieut. 109th regt., 1871; enlisted in Natal mounted pol. 1879; 2nd clk. to R.M., Weenen County, Natal, May, 1882; clk. to inspr. of prisons, 1893; clk. to R.M., Estcourt, 1894.

BINNIE, THOS. I.—Supervisor of roads, B. Cent. Africa Prot., 15th Mar., 1897; ast. survr., survey dept., 15th Mar., 1898; survr. to Anglo-Portuguese provisional boundary delimitation, 1899 and 1900; chief survr., 1st Dec., 1900; dir. pub. wks., Dec., 1904.

BIRBECK, R. J.—Ed. at Clifton Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb., B.A.; ast. mast., Northdown Hill Schil., Margate, 1895-1902; ditto, Hillside, Brighton, 1902-03; jur. ast. mast., Mrs. Young's Coll., Hong Kong, 1903.

BIRCH, SIR ARTHUR N., K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1875).—B. 1837; clk. in the C.O., Feb., 1855; promoted to 3rd class, Oct., 1859; ast. priv. sec. to Sir E. Bulwer Lytton, 1858; priv. sec. to Mr. Chichester Fortescue, from Sept., 1859, until Feb., 1864, when he was permitted, while still remaining on the establishment of the C.O., to accept the apptntn. of col. sec. of Br. Columbia; was sen. mem. of the exec. coun., and ex officio clk. of the same; also pres. of the legis. coun.; was admnstr. of the govt. of the col. from Sept., 1865, to Nov., 1866; promoted to ast. sen. clk., C.O., July 5, 1866; resumed his duties in C.O. in Sept., 1867; ag. licut.-gov. of Penang and Province of Wellesley, Feb., 1871, returned to England, July, 1872; 1st class clk. C.O., Sept., 1872; col. sec., Ceylon, June, 1873; administered the govt. during the spring and summer of 1874 and 1875-6; lieut.-gov. Ceylon, 1876; resig., June, 1878; is agt. of the W. branch of the Bank of England, Burlington Gardens; exec. comnrs. for Ceylon at the Col. and Ind. Exhibtn., 1886.

BIRCH, ERNEST WOODFORD, C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1857; ed. Harrow; cadet, S. Sttlmnts., Jan., 1876; employed in the C.O., Downing-street, to July, 1878; sec. to the pol. comsnr., 1879; passed final exam. in Malay, Aug., 1880; J.P. and a mag., Malacca; took charge Malacca land office, Feb., 1881; ag. collr. of land rev., Singapore, 1881, 2nd ast. col. sec., 1882; on special mission in H.M.S. "Esquire," to report on the Cocos-Keeling Is., 1885; mag. and collr. of land rev., Malacca, 1888; ag. Br. res., Selangor, May, 1892, to Jan., 1893; sec. to govt., Perak, July, 1893; ag. res., Perak, Sept., 1895, to July, 1896; Br. Res. Negri-Sembilan, June, 1897; govrn. and commander-in-chief of the col. of Labuan and of B.N. Borneo, Dec., 1900; Br. res., Perak, 10th Feb., 1904.

BIRCH, JAMES KORTRIGHT.—Cadet, S. Sttlmnts., 1872; passed final exam. in Malay, Dec., 1873; ast. mag., coroner, and collr. land rev., P.W., Sept., 1874; collr. land rev., Penang and P.W., and Sttlmnt. offr., Trans Krian, Sept., 1877; collr. of land rev., Singapore, June, 1880; ag. pol. mag. and comnrs., ct. of requests, P.W., May, 1882; sen. dist. offr., P.W., 1888; 1st mag. and comnrs., ct. of requests, and supr. of prisons, Penang, June, 1890; ag. treas., S. Sttlmnts., Feb., 1894; 1st mag. Singapore, Apr., 1895, but continued to act as treas.; treas., Apr., 1897; ag. res. councillor, Penang, May, 1897, to Aug., 1898, and from Apr., 1901; res. councillor, Malacca, 1898; ag. res. coun., Penang, Oct., 1903; confirmed, July, 1904.

BIRD, HON. B. STAFFORD.—Has represented Franklin Dist. in House of Assem., Tasmania, since 1882; mem. of comsns. on educn. and on lunatic asyls., 1883; treas. of the col. in the Fysh Govt., 1887-92; and treas. of the col. in the Lewis Ministry, 1899-1900.

BIRD, CHRISTOPHER, C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1856; clk., engnr.'s dept., Natal, Jan., 1874; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1880; 1st clk., Aug., 1881; clk., exec. coun., Apr., 1883, to July, 1885; J.P., 1887; ast. col. sec., Jan., 1888; apptd. prin. under-secr. on estabnmt. of responsible govt., Oct., 1893; mem. of civ. ser. bd., 1894; chrmn., civ. serv. bd., 1901; permitt. head of civ. serv., 1903.

BIRD, R. E. O.—M.A., All Souls' Coll., Oxford, honours in classics; head mast., Armenian Coll., Calcutta, 1892; asst. mast., King's Coll., Bangkok, 1901; junr. asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1902; senr. asst. mast., ditto, 1906; passed in Cantonese (written and colloquial), 1906.

BISHOP, WM. HOWARD.—Asst. clk., Georgetown prison, B. Guiana, 15th Oct., 1885; clk., ditto, 12th Aug., 1889; clk. H.M. penal settm., Massaruni, 2nd June, 1891; clk., Bartica pol. mag. court, 27th Oct., 1891; issuer, penal settm., Massaruni, 30th May, 1892; clk., ditto, 18th Aug., 1892; clk. and issuer, Georgetown prison, 12th June, 1894.

BLACK, G. G.—Ed. High Schl., Glasgow; Univ. Coll. Schl.; London and Glasgow Univ.; clk., audit dept., W. Australia, 1892; examt. examr., 1894; ch. clk., P. W. Dept., 1896; ag. under sec., P.W., Jan. to Dec., 1902; is a J.P.

BLACKMORE, EDWIN GORDON, C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1887; ed. at King Edward VI. Gram. Schl., Bath; served with the Taranaki Rifle Volrs. in the New Zealand war, 1863-64, and was present, in reserve, at the action of Poutoko, 2nd Oct., 1863, and at the storming and capture of the rebel Maori strongholds at Ahauhu and Kaitake, Mar., 1864 (medal); parly. libr. to the legis. of S. Austral., Oct., 1864; clk. asst. and serj.-at-arms, house of assem., Dec., 1869; clk. of the house of assem., May, 1886; clk. leg. coun. and clk. of parlt., May, 1887; clk. of Austral. convention which met in Adelaide and Sydney, 1897, Melbourne, 1898, to agree to a bill for the federation of Australia; clk. of the constitutional comttee. to draft the bill; reed. thanks of conven.; clk. of sen. and clk. of parliaments, Austral. Commwth., 1901; several years examr. and lecturer in hist. for Univ. of Adelaide; organising sec. of movement to raise a Bushmen's corps for service in S. Africa, and mem. of sub-comttee. to fully equip the corps; author of "The Decisions of Mr. Speaker Denison and Mr. Speaker Brand on Points of Order, Rules of Debate, and the Gen. Practice of the House of Commons, 1857-84"; "The Decisions of Mr. Speaker Peel, 1884-95"; "Manual of the Practice, Procedure, and Usage of the House of Assembly of S. Australia"; "Manual of the Practice, Procedure, and Usage of the Legis. Coun. of S. Australia"; and the "Law of the Constitution of S. Australia."

BLACKWELL, LIONEL NORTON.—2nd lieut. 4th Batt. King's Shropshire L.I., Nov., 1888; lieut., Sept., 1889; transferred to Thames Mil. Div., R.E., Mar., 1894; instructor of musketry, Apr., 1895; asst. inspr., Lagos Housa Force, Nov., 1895; ag. dist. comsrr., Oct., 1896; captain, July, 1898; received reward (50l.) after examination in the Yoruba language, 1898; Lagos representative—delimitation of the boundary Lagos; Northern Nigeria, July to August, 1901; appointed District Commissioner of the Colony and Acting Railway Commissioner, August, 1901.

BLACKWOOD, LORD BASIL TEMPLE.—Ed. at Harrow and Balliol Coll., Oxford; called to the Bar (In Temp.), 1897; dep. judge advoc., S. Africa, Mar., 1900, to June, 1901; sec. to gov., O.R.C., 1901 to Mar., 1903; ag. col. sec., O.R.C., May to Sept., 1902; asst. ditto, 1903; ag. ditto, Apr. to Aug., 1903, Dec., 1903, to Sept., 1904, and Mar., 1905, to Jan., 1906.

BLADEN, JAMES BUXTON.—Appd. clk., store and timekeeper, harbour wks., Antigua, Jan., 1896; ag. 2nd clk., P.O., Oct. to Dec., 1896; ag. 1st clk., 1897; acct. of savings bank, Jan. to Oct., 1898; cashier of ditto, Oct., 1898 to Mar., 1902; ag. 1st indoor treasy. offr., Aug., 1901; ag. 2nd indoor ditto, on sev. occasions, 1899, 1900 and 1902; ag. 3rd outdoor ditto (or cashier), 1901 and 1902; ag. 3rd outdoor ditto, and excise offr., in 1899 to 1902; jun. clk., audit dept., May, 1902; appd. 2nd treasy. offr., and quarantine offr., Montserrat, Apl., 1903.
BLAINE, SIR CHARLES FREDERICK, KNT. BACH. (1889).—Deleg. for Cape Colony to S. African Customs Union Conf., 1882.

BLAINE, HERBERT FRANCIS.—Ed. at Harrow and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; called to Bar (In Temp.), 1881; admitted advoc., sup. ct. of Cape, 1881; M.A., 1889; twice ag. sol.-gen. East. dists. ct.; att.-gen., O.R.C., May, 1901; ag. atty.-gen., Transval, 1905; mem. exec. and legis. couns.
BLAIR, HON. A. G.—Mem. for York in New Brunswick House of Assem.; premier and atty.-gen. from 1883 to 1896; min. of rlys. and canals in Mr. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.

BLAIR, ALEXANDER HAMILTON.—B. 1872; ed. at Oxford Mil. Coll.; 2nd lieut., 4th batt., Norfolk rgt., 1892; lieut., 1893; resigned, 1894; re-appd. lieut., 4th batt., Norfolk rgt., 1898; capt., 1898; Hythe certif., 1898; Shoeburyness, 1900; asst. inspr., Lagos Hausa force, 1899; on active serv., Ashanti, 1900, relief of Kumasi (medal and clasp, ment. in desps.); spec. serv., E. Coast of Africa, 1900-01; dist. comsrr., Lagos, 1904; spec. serv., Jebu Ode, 1904.
BLAIR, HON. JAS., WM.—B. 1871; called to the bar, Queensland, 1894; M.L.A. for Ipswich, Queensland, since Mar., 1902; atty.-gen. since Sept., 1903; and atty.-gen. and sec. for mines since Apr., 1904.

BLAKE, THE HON. EDWARD, K.C., M.P.—B. 1833; called to the bar of Upper Canada, 1856; created Q.C., 1864; entered Canadian parl., 1867, and has also sat in Ontario assem.; was prime min. of Ontario from 20th Dec., 1871, until 25th Oct., 1872, when he resign.; sworn of the priv. coun. 7th Nov., 1873, but did not hold office; re-entered the cabinet, 19th May, 1875, as min. of justice, a position he exchanged in Sept., 1877, for that of pres. of the coun.; ret. from the cabinet, 31st Jan., 1878; declined the chancellorship of Ontario Dec., 1862, also the ch. justiceship of the sup. ct. of the Dominion, May, 1875; is chancellor of the Univ. of Toronto, and Pres. of the Law Soc. of Upper Canada; leader of Liberal party in Canada, 1880-7; has sat in the Parlmt. of U.K. since 1892; mem. of S. Afr. Comttee., 1897.

BLAKE, SIR ERNEST EDWARD, K.C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1845; appd. to a clerkship in the C.O., 4th July, 1863, after a compet. exam.; 1st jun. class, 20th Nov., 1869; 2nd class clk., 30th Sept., 1872; asst. priv. sec. to the Earl of Kimberley, 1st Oct., 1872; priv. sec., 27th Jan., 1874; 1st class clk. and head of the gen. dep., 1st May, 1879; crown agt. for the cols., 1st Jan., 1881.

BLAKE, SIR HENRY ARTHUR, G.C.M.G. (1897), K.C.M.G. (1888), C.M.G. (1887), F.R.G.S.—B. 1840; cadet, Roy. Ir. Constaby., Feb., 1859; sub-inspr., Mar., 1859; appd. res. mag., Feb., 1876; in Jan., 1882, was one of the five special res. mags. selected by Govt. to concert and carry out measures for the pacification of a large portion of Ireland; appd. gov. of the Bahamas, Jan., 1884; Newfoundland, 1887; Queensland, Nov., 1888, but did not take up appmt.; gov., Jamaica, Dec., 1888; gov., H. Kong, 1898; gov., Ceylon, 1903.
BLAKELY, DONALD QUENTIN.—Clk., atty.-gen.'s off., B. Honduras, Apl., 1898.

BLAKELY, JAMES FRANCIS.—3rd clk., col. sec.'s off., B. Honduras, Feb., 1894; ag. 2nd clk. Apl., 1895; clk. to survr.-gen., Nov., 1896; asst. keeper, King's warehouse, May, 1897; clk. to col. engr., Jan., 1899; ag. 2nd grade cust. and ex. offr., Mar., 1904; 1st cls. clk., treasy. and cust., Feb., 1906.

BLAKENEY, Capt. John Edwd. Chas.—Ed. at St. Peter's Coll., Camb.; lieut. 4th Batt. Essex Regt., Sept., 1886; capt., Feb., 1895; qualified for comsn. in the line, Sept., 1889; Hythe certificate, 1894; Aldershot and Shoeburyness, 1895; tactics and mil. topography, 1894; asst. inspr. S. Leone frontier pol., Sept., 1895; now inspr. of pol.; is a J.P. for the col.; attached to Anglo-French bndry. comsn., 1895-6; commd. detachment of frontier pol. H.M. Diamond Jubilee, 1897; ag. dist. comsnr. Panguma dist., Aug., 1897, to Jan., 1899; commanded expdn. against Lopas and Bandis, 1898; recd. thanks of Govr. and S. of S.; subsequently commdng. the frontier pol. during the defence of Panguma against insurgents for two months until relieved by a column under Capt. Fairthought, D.S.O.; afterwards attacked and destroyed several Mendi strongholds; served on the Liberian frontier, Dec., 1898; West African med. and clsp. 1898-9; ag. dist. comsnr., Falabu, Sept., 1899; adjt. frontier pol., Dec., 1899; ag. inspr.-gen., Feb., 1900.

BLANCHARD, Victor P.—Clk., govt. office, Roseau, Dominica, 1889; clk. to valuation comsnrs. and comsnrs. of assessed taxes, Feb., 1895; ag. ch. outdoor offr., treaty and quarantine offr., July, 1896, to 1896; ag. sec. quarantine bd., 1896.
BLANCHET, J., K.C.—Prov. sec. and registr. Quebec; puisne judge, King's Bench div., sup. ct., Quebec, 1891.

BLAND, Robert Norman.—Ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Chelt. Coll.; B.A. Trin. Coll., Dub.; Cadet, S. Settlmts., Oct., 1882; passed in Malay, May, 1884; collr. and mag. Sri Menanti, June 1886; dist. offr. southern dist., P.W., 1888; collr. of land rev., Penang, 1889; ditto, Singapore, 1890; offr. in charge, Sungei Ujong, Dec., 1894; ditto, Negri Sembilan, Jan. to Apr., 1895; ag. offr. assignee of deeds, Singapore, June, 1896; inspr. of prisons, S. Settlmts., Mar., 1897; sen. dist. offr., prov. Wellesley, Apr., 1897; inspr. of prisons, July, 1897; ag. res. councillor, Malacca, April to Sept., 1900, Nov., 1901, to June, 1903, and Oct., 1903; treas., S.S., July, 1904, but continued to act as res. coun., Malacca; confirmed, Jan., 1905.

BLAYNEY, J. Albert, I.R.C.P., Edin.; I.R.C.S., Edin.; L.F.P.S., Glas.—Govt. med. offr. Br. New Guinea; res. mag. central dist., 1895; mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1896; ag. regist.-gen., and collr. of customs, Oct., 1898, to Jan., 1899; ch. med. offr., Oct., 1899.
BLENKIRON, J. E.—3rd acctnt., B. Cent. Africa Prot., Dec., 1893; 1st asst. treas., 1901; ag. treas., Apl. to Dec., 1902, and Nov., 1904, to June, 1905.
