BLISSETT, Henry Frederick, C.M.G. (1874)—B. 1847; clk., Roy. Ordnance Factories at Portsmouth, Jan., 1867; asst. comsry., African commissariat, Aug., 1869; priv. sec., 1870-1, to the admnr. of the G. Coast, and clk. of coun., 1872; served throughout the Ashanti Expedn. of 1873-4, as control offr. with Capt. Glover's force; mentioned in despatches, and medal; acted on several occasions as auditor-gen. of the W. A. Sttlmts. from 1875 to 1881; comsnr. to examine the accts. of the Falkland Is., 1882; sent on special service to the G. Coast as comsnr. of accts., 1884.
BLOUNT, The Rev. Chas. K., M.A.—Assistant minister, Falkland Is., 1901.

BLOW, Horatio John Hooper.—Arrived, N. Zealand, 1871; entered govt. service (P.W. dept.), 1873; record clk., 1878; ag. asst. under sec., Jan., 1885; asst. under sec., Oct., 1885; ag. under sec., June, 1891; under sec., Oct., 1891; under sec. for rly. (in addition to P.W.), Jan., 1895.
BOAK, The Hon. Sir Robert, Kt. Bach. (1902).—B. 1822; Pres. of legis. coun., Nova Scotia; resigned, 1904.

BOAST, Alfred.—Clk. in the mag.'s office, Umgeni Div., Natal, Jan., 1879; clk. and interp. to R.M., Umvoti, Nov., 1880; ag. admstr. of native law and border agent, Lower Tugela, Aug. to Nov., 1883; R.M., Zululand, 1890; mag., Eshowe divn., 1901.

BOAST, Henry W.—Clk. and Zulu interp., Greytown, Natal, July, 1876; clk. to res. mag., Ladysmith, May, 1878; clk. and Zulu interp., Estcourt, Dec., 1880; ditto, Ixopo, 1886; registr., native high ct., Apr., 1887; admstr. of native law, Tugela Valley, Jan., 1890; mag., Krantzkop, 1894; mag. Polela div., Oct., 1899.
BOLTON, John George Elliott, M.R.C.S.—Eng. Govt. med. offr. and comsnr. in lunacy, Mauritius, 21st Jan., 1870; sanitary warden, 15th Nov., 1895; ag. med. inspr. in 1900 and 1902.

BOLTON, Wm. Ferrall.—Apptd. to crown agent's dept. 20th Nov., 1900; on spec. serv. under F.O. as ag. treas. of Somaliland, 22nd Nov., 1902, to 19th July, 1903; African gen. serv. medal and clasp for operations against Somali Mullah, 1902-4; asst. treas. East Africa Prot., 11th Jan., 1904; asst. gov't. emigtn. agt. at Calcutta for Trinidad, Jamaica, Mauritius, and Fiji, 21st Sept., 1904; passed exam. in Hindustani, June, 1905; ag. emigtn. agt., 29th Mar., 1906.
BOLUS, Percy Reginald, M.B. (Lond.).—Asst. col. surgeon, Falkland Is., 1904.

BOND, The Rt. Hon. Sir Robert, P.C. (1902), K.C.M.G. (1901), Hon., LL.D., 1902.—B. 1857; Speaker, Newfoundland house of assm., 1884; mem. of exec. coun. and col. sec., 1889 to 1894; official deleg. to London on fisheries question, 1890, and to Washington on reciprocity question in the same year; again col. sec. 1895 to 1897; deleg. to Ottawa in connection with the confederation negotiations, 1895, and subsequently negotiated a loan for the col. in London in that year; prime min. and col. sec., 1900; specially invited by H.M.'s govt. to attend confce. in relation to French treaties question, 1900; negotiated Hay-Bond treaty with U.S.A., 1902.

BONSER, Rt. Hon. Sir J. W., P.C. (1901), Kt. Bach. (1894), M.A.—B. 1847; formerly fellow of Christ's Coll., Camb., sen. classic in 1870; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 18th Nov., 1872; att.-gen., S. Sttlmts., 1883; ch. justice, 1893; ch. justice, Ceylon, 1893; mem. jud. comtce., priv. coun., 1901.

BOOTH, Leonard Wm.—Writer, Ceylon service, 1878; pol. mag., Avisawella, 1880; office asst. to gov't. agt., Uva, 1886; ditto, Manaar, 1891; pol. mag., Kandy, 1895; ag. dist. judge, Kalutara, Oct., 1895; resumed duties as forest sttlmt. offr., Dec., 1895; ag. asst. gov't. agt., Kegalla, Mar. to Oct., 1896; special offr. under Ordinance No. 1 of 1897; gov't. agt. Sabaragamuna, June, 1899; gov't. agt. N.C. Province, Feb., 1900; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Nov., 1906; gov't. agt., Uva, Dec., 1908; prin. collr. of customs, May, 1906.
BOOTH, Robt. Malcolm.—Cadet, Fiji, Nov., 1883; attached to col. sec. office, Feb., 1884; ditto stip. mag. office, Tai Levu, July, 1884; at Navua,
BOULTON, J. F., Assoc. M.I.C.E. (1884).—Exec. engineer, p.w. dept., H. Kong, Apl., 1889; ag. asst. dir. p.w., July, 1902, to May, 1903, Apl., 1904, to Mar., 1905, and July to Sept., 1905.

BOURKE, SOUTHWELL G. J.—Ent. Royal Navy as cadet, 1864; served in "Aurora," Canada, during Fenian raid (medal); in "Orontes" during Egyptian campaign, 1882 (medal, Kheide's star); called to bar (Mid. Tem.), 1887; stip. mag., Br. Guiana, 1897; interpr. in French (1st class, Navy); ag. pol. mag., Georgetown, Oct., 1900.

BOURNE, HUGH CLARENCE, C.M.G. (1906), M.A. (Balliol Coll., Oxford).—Asst. sec., charity organization soc., 1881-84; called to the bar (Inner Tem.), 1884; registr. gen. and examnr. of titles, Trinidad, 1893; acted as judge of sup. ct., 1896 and 1898; audr.-gen. Trinidad and Tobago, 1898; ag. col. sec., 1899, 1900, 1901, and 1902; admstd. govt., 10th to 13th Nov., 1901; ag. 1st class clk., C.O., Oct., 1902; col. sec., Jamaica, June, 1904; admstd. govt., Sept., 1904.

BOVELL, SIR HENRY ALLEYNE, KT. BACH. (1902).—B. 1854; Gilchrist scholar (London Univ.), Jan., 1873; LL.B. (Lond.), Jan., 1877, with 2nd class honours in jurisprudence and Roman law; called to the bar, Linc. Inn, Nov., 1876; solr.-gen., and exchequer-gen. of Barbados, and J.P., Mar., 1882; M.L.C., July, 1883; mem. of comsn. to revise laws of Barbados, Mar., 1886; chancellor of the diocese, 1884; atty.-gen., Oct., 1886; atty.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1896; ag. legal asst., C.O., Dec., 1900 and 1901; ch. just., Br. Guiana, 1902.

BOVELL, JOHN R., F.L.S., F.C.S.—Parochial treas. and collr. of rates, parish of St. John, Barbados, Apr., 1882; sup't. reformatory and industrial schl., Apl., 1883; mem. local comtee., Col. and Ind. exhibn., 1885, and other local exhibn. comtees.; supt. Botan. Stn., 1886; went to Antigua, 1890, to start Skerratt's training schl.; vice-pres., Windward dist. agric. soc., 1890; mem. of comsn. to inquire into sugar cane borers, Jan., 1893; J.P., Mar., 1893; chairman emigr. comtee., Apr., 1895; seconded from sup't. of reformatory to act as agric. supt. for sugar cane experiments, Barbados, Oct., 1898; went to Antigua and St. Kitts to start sugar cane experiments, Jan., 1899.

BOVILL, ALFRED KARSLAKE.—Clk. of wks., pub. wks. dept., Cyprus, 1882-5; asst. to dir. of survey and principal forest officer, 1886-1895; instr. of agric. industries, 1892-96; prin. forest offr., 1895.

BOWELL, THE HON. SIR MACKENZIE, K.C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1823; emigrated from Suffolk to Canada in 1833; well known in Canada as a journalist; has sat in Canadian House of Commons since 1867; sworn of the privy coun., 19th Oct., 1878, and apptd. min. of customs; min. of militia and defence, 1892; min. of trade and commerce, Dec., 1892; on special mission to Australia in connection with the development of inter-col. trade, 1893; pres. of col. confce. at Ottawa, 1894; prem. of Canada and vice-president of council, 1894-96.

BOWEN, ALFRED SYDNEY.—Ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, and Newton Coll., S. Devon; 2nd clk. crown lands office, July, 1879; 4th clk. rec. gen.'s office, July, 1881; branch savings bk. clk., Jan., 1883; ch. clk., surg.-gen.'s office, Jan., 1885; ch. clk., G.P.O., May, 1885; wardens St. Ann's and Diego Martin, Dec., 1889; J.P., 1890; ag. priv. sec. to Sir F. Broome, Sept., 1896; instr. of prisons, 1903.

BOWEN, ERNEST F. S., F.S.I., A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; clk. audit office from Feb., 1882, to Apr., 1889; clk. post office from May, 1889, to Sep., 1890; ch. clk. and draughtsman, pub. wks. office from Oct., 1890, to Jan., 1895; supt. pub. wks., Barbados, Feb., 1895.

BOWER, SIR GRAHAM JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1892), C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1848; ret. comdr., R.N.; entered navy, 1861, and served on Mediterranean, E. coast of Africa, E. Indian, Home, and Australian stations, until 1880, when he was apptd. priv. sec. to Sir Hercules Robinson, gov. Cape of Good Hope, and high comsnr. for S. Africa; imperial sec. to the high comsnr., 1884-97; colonial sec., Mauritius, 1898; admnstd. gov't., July, 1900, to 1901, and Nov., 1903, to Aug., 1904.

BOWES, FREDERICK, B.A. (Oxon).—Exhibtnr., Wadham Coll., Oxford; cadet Ceylon civ. serv., 9th Oct., 1891; attached to Badulla Kachcheri, 1891; additional pol. mag., Badulla, 1892; ag. pol. mag., Haldumullia, 1892; ag. office astt. to gov't. agt., N. Prov., 1893; ditto, N.C. Prov., 1894; pol. mag., Point Pedro and Chavakachcheri, 1895; office astt. to gov't. agt., Uva, 1895; ag. ditto, Cent. Prov., and ag. fiscal, Cent. Prov., 1897; ag. supt. of pol., 1898; officer of class IV., 1899; landing survr., customs, Colombo, 1899; officer of class III., 1901; astt. gov't. agt., Matara, 1901; pol. mag., Kandy, 1902; ditto, Colombo, and addit. dist. charge, Colombo, 1903; pol. mag., Kandy, 1903; astt. gov't. agt., Puttalam, 1st May, 1904.
BOWIE, ROBT.—P.C. certif., 1892; M.A., Edin., 1893; Normal Coll., Cape Town, 1896-1900; vice-principal, Normal Schl., Bloemfontein, 1st Jan., 1901; inspr. of schls., O.R.C., 1st July, 1904.
BOWLEY, FRANCIS BULMER LYON—SOLR. of sup. ct., England, 1890; solr. of sup. ct., Hong Kong, 1893; notary public, 1895; ag. Crown solr., May, 1899; Crown solr. and Queen's Proctor, 1900.

BOWRING, WALTER ANDREW.—Ed. at Eton; clk., col. audit branch, 1894; served in Cyprus, 1896; astt. auditor, East Africa Prot., 13th Feb., 1899; ag. local auditor, Uganda Prot., 1899; local auditor, ditto, 31st May, 1902.

BOXWELL, W.—B.A., Trin. Coll., Dublin, 1872; ensign, 20th regt. of foot, 1872; lieut., 51st L.I., 1873-78; adjt., Bord. horse, S. Africa, during Sekukuni campaign (medal with clasp), 1879-80; Natal mtd. pol., 1881-86; sub-inspr., Basutoland mtd. pol., 1886-1901; astt. comsnr., Basutoland, 1901.

BOYCE, ERNEST PEDDER.—Supernumerary clk., G.P.O., Barbados, June, 1878; notarial clk. col. sec.'s office, Apr., 1879; sec. to comsners. to inquire into the working of the G.P.O., 1879; ch. clk., pub. library, 1880; ag. libr., Sept., 1882; astt. clk., petty debt ct. of St. Michael's, Nov., 1882; ag. ch. clk., May, 1883, to June, 1884; inland rev. offr., 1887; clk., astt. ct. of appeal, 1901.

BOYD, A. D.—Teacher, preparatory dept., Dominica Grammar Schl., Sept., 1894; junv. gov't. off. treas., Jan., 1895; clk. gov't. off., Portsmouth dist., Jan., 1896; gov't. off. treas., Roseau, Sept., 1896; quar. off., Oct., 1897.
BOYD, A. H.—Entered C.O. after exam., Apr., 1887; astt. clk., June, 1892; supplementary clk. in registry, Mar., 1900.

BOYLE, ALEXANDER GEORGE.—Ed. at Charterhouse, Clifton Coll. and R.I.E.C., Cooper's Hill; priv. sec. to senior puisne judge, S. Sttlmsts., 1893; resig., 1895; astt. treas., Uganda Prot., 18th Sept., 1895; ag. sec. to H.M. Comsnr., Uganda Prot., Feb., 1896, to Apr., 1898; ag. treas., Mar., 1899, to Feb., 1900; dep. treas., 1st Apr., 1900; ag. sec. to admstn., May, 1901, to Jan., 1902; apptd. collr. with seniority of 18th Sep., 1895, 1st April, 1902; astt. sec., 1st Apr., 1902.

BOYLE, SIR CAVESENDISH, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1849; ed. at Charterhouse; dist. mag., Leeward Is., Dec., 1879; col. sec. of Bermuda, and registr.-gen., 1882; col. sec., Gibraltar, 1888; gov't. sec., Br. Guiana, 1894; admstn. gov't. at various times, 1894-1900; was also chairman of the Br. Guiana bk., Apr., 1897, to Nov., 1898; gov'nr. of Newfoundland, Jan., 1901; gov'nr., Mauritius, 15th Apr., 1904.
BOYSE, V. H.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of 2nd div., and assigned to C.O., 17th July, 1902.

BRABANT, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR EDWARD YEUD, K.C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1839; Mem. of the legis. assm. for E. London, Cape of Good Hope; retired from Cape mounted rifles, as captain, 1870; field comdt. of col. forces, 1878; col., Cape yeomanry, 1879; in command of local forces in the S. African war, 1899-1901; ret. 1904; M.L.A. for East London, 1904.

BRACE, GEORGE KNIGHT KIRKWOOD.—Dispenser of medicines and gen. astt., N. P. asylum, Bahamas, 1885 to 1890; clk., registr. of records off., 1890 to 1896; 3rd clk., recvr.-genl.'s and treas.'s dept., 31st Dec., 1896; ag. registr. of records, 26th Aug. to 10th Nov., 1901; ag. 2nd clk. and book-keeper, recvr.-genl.'s and treas.'s dept., Nov., 1903, to March, 1904, and from 5th May, 1904.

BRACE, T. E. D.—Dispenser N. P. Asylum, Bahamas, Sept., 1882; clk. pol. office, Aug., 1883; 2nd clk. col. sec.'s office, Sept., 1887; clk. to bd. of pub. wks., New Providence, Mar., 1900; ch. clk., col. sec.'s dept., Mar., 1904; ag. col. sec., Sept.-Dec., 1905, and Sept.-Oct., 1906.
BRADFORD, M. G.—Astt., P.W.D., Sarawak, May, 1895; supt., July, 1901; ag. comsnr., May, 1902.
BRADLEY, JOHN THOMAS, L.R.C.P. and S., Edin.—Astt. med. offr., Seychelles, 26th Aug., 1901; J.P., and chairman, local bd. of health, South Mahe; supt., lunatic asylum, 1906.

BRADY, ALFRED BARTON, M.I.C.E.—Pupil and afterwards ch. draughtsman, ch. engrnr.'s dept. L. and Y. Rly., Manchester, Jan., 1872; astt. to G. W. Stevenson, M.I.C.E., 1879; survr. Docking Union and Hunstanton urban sanitary authority, Mar., 1881; engrnr. and survr., Maldon sanitary dist., Essex, Oct., 1882; astt. engrnr., rly. dept., Brisbane, Qnsld., Jan., 1885; astt. engrnr. of bridges (rlys.), July, 1887; engrnr. for bridges (main roads), P.W.D., June, 1889; gov't. architect and engrnr. for bridges combined, Sept. 1891; under-secretary for public works, gov't. architect and engrnr. for bridges combined, Feb., 1901.
BRADY, JOHN BANKS.—Head mast., Grey Coll. Schl., 1st Dec., 1901; inspr. of schls., O.R.C., 1st Feb., 1904.

BRAITHWAITE, H. W.—Petroleum lockerman and astt. excise locker, San Fernando, Trinidad, 1873; ch. clk. sub-treasy., 1874; supt. registr. of births, 1878; J.P.; elected mun. coun., 1888; now ch. clk. med. dept.; sec. quarantine, and sec. bd. o health.

BRAMSTON, SIR JOHN, G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.M.G. (1897), D.C.L., C.B. (1886).—B. 1832; graduated B.A. at Balliol Coll., Oxford, 1854; elected fellow of All Souls, 1855; D.C.L., 1863; barrister, Mid. Tem., 1857; went to Queensland, 1859, as priv. sec. to Sir George Bowen; resig., 1861; was mem. of the legis. coun. of Queensland, 1863 to 1869, and of the exec. coun. 1863 to 1866. While in England in 1867, was asst. boundary commr. for Devon and Cornwall under the Reform Act of that year. Returned to Queensland, 1868, and became atty.-gen., 3rd May, 1870; resig., 31st Dec., 1873; represented the Burnett district in the legis. assem. of Queensland, Apr., 1871, to Dec., 1873; apptd. atty.-gen. of Hong Kong, 1873; was ex officio mem. of the legis. and exec. couns.; acted as judge of the sup. ct. of Hong Kong, Feb. to May, 1874; apptd. an asst. under-sec. of state, C.O., 30th June, 1876; employed on a mission to Berlin in connection with the Angra Pequena negotiations, July 1886; regis. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, Feb., 1892; ret. 14th Nov., 1897; sent out as roy. comsnr., in conjunction with Admiral Sir James Erskine, K.C.B., to inquire into matters relating to French treaty rights in Newfoundland, 1898; mem. roy. comsnr. for Paris Exhibn. of 1900.

BRANCH, CHRISTIAN WM.—Ed. at St. Kitt's high school and Antigua gram. school, Edin. Univ., M.B., C.M., 1893; Leeward Is. med. offr. in charge, Virgin Is., 1894; M.L.C., Virgin Is., 1894; ag. comsnr., V.I., 1894; med. offr. dist. 4, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1895; mem. bd. of health, St. Kitts, 1895; ag. supt. Cunn. Hosp., ag. surg., gaol, and ag. analyser, vital statistics, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1896; ag. med. offr., dista. 7 and 8, 1897; lieut., St. Kitts-Nevis volrs., 1897; ag. asst. col. surg., St. Lucia, 1899; pub. phys., Inagua, Bahamas, 1900; ag. res. just., Inagua, 1911; med. offr., Kingstown dist., med. offr., col. hosp., and supt., pauper asylum, St. Vincent, 1902; also ag. med. offr., div. I., surg., gaol, and health offr., 1902; J.P., 1902; ag. mem., legis. and exec. couns., 1903; mem. bd. of educ., 1904; del. to W. I. Quarantine Confe., 1904.

BRANCH, EDMUND RALPH.—M.B.Ch.B. Edin.; dist. med. offr., Dominica, 1902; ditto, Dieppe Bay, St. Kitts, 1902; med. suptdt., Cunningham hosp., and surgeon of the gaol, St. Kitts, Feb., 1903; lieut., St. Kitts-Nevis def. force, 1904; capt., 1905.

BRANCH, E. ST. J., B.A., B.C.L., Durham Univ.—Called to the bar, Gray's Inn, May, 1892; ag. chief mag., St. Vincent, 1895 to Jan., 1898; asst. to atty.-gen., Leeward Islds., May, 1898; ag. atty.-gen., July to Nov., 1899; mem. legis. coun. and exec. coun., Dominica; mem. general legis. coun., Leeward Is., Dec., 1899; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., 12 Sept., 1902; ag. col. sec., 10 Oct., 1902.

BRANCH, E. W. R., M.B., C.M. (Edin.), B.A. (Durham).—Med. offr., Virgin Is., Oct., 1889; acted as comsnr., Virgin Is.; J.P. and M.L.C., Virgin Is.; med. offr., Montserrat, 1890; M.L.C., Montserrat; med. offr., St. Kitts, 1894; dist. med. offr., Antigua, Jan. 1905.
BRANDON, THE VERY REV'D. DEAN, M.A.—Colonial chaplain, Falkland Is., 1877.

BRASSEY, LORD, 1ST BARON (CREATED 1886), SIR THOMAS BRASSEY, G.C.B. (1906), K.C.B., D.C.L. (HON. AND M.A. (OXON.).—B. 1836; called to the bar Linc. Inn, 1866; M.P. for Devonport, 1865, and for Hastings, 1868 to 1886; lieut. comdg. roy. naval art. volrs., 1873; hon. comdr., 1880; col. 2nd Cinque Ports art. volrs., 1891; dep. lieut. and J.P. for Sussex; was civil lord of the admty., 1880 to 1884, and sec. to the admty., 1884-5; pres. of the statistical soc., 1879-80; comdr. of the legion of honour, 1889; chairman of the roy. comsnr. on opium traffic, 1894-5; lord-in-waiting, 1893-5; gov. of Victoria, 1895-1900.

BRATT, J. HICKS DAVSON.—5TH CL. CLK., AUDIT DEPT., BRITISH GUIANA, 1889; 4TH, 1892; 3RD, 1895; 2ND, 1897; SEC. TO COMTIE. OF INQUIRY INTO TRAVELLING EXPENSES, 1893; SEC. TO COMTIE. ON PENSIONS, 1898; ASST. LOC. CLK., NIGERIA, FEB., 1901; LOC. CLK., SEPT., 1901; CH. ASST. TREAS., JAN., 1903; AG. TREAS., FEB., 1903.
BRAYN, RICHARD FYSHER, B.A., CANTAB.—CLK., COL. AUD. BRANCH, E. AND A. DEPT., 5TH JUNE, 1905; ASST. AUD., HONG KONG, 14TH APRIL, 1906.

BREARLEY, L. J.—PASSED LOND. MATRIC. AND OXFORD SEN. LOCAL EXAMS., JUNE, 1899; APPTD., AFTER COMPET. EXAM., CLK. OF THE 2ND DIV. AND ASSIGNED TO LD. CHAMBERLAIN'S OFFICE, FEB., 1900; TRANSFERRED TO C.O., 18TH FEB., 1900.
BRETT, F. W.—ENT. SERVICE OF UNITEL AFRICAN CO. (AFTERWARDS ROY. NIGER CO.), MAR., 1882; PRIV. SEC. TO SIR G. GOLDIE, 1883 TO 1890; TRANSFERRED TO C.O. AS SUPPLEMENTARY CLK., JAN., 1900.
BREW, SAM. H.—ED. WESLEYAN HIGH SCHL., CAPE COAST; CLERICAL ASST., GOV. OFFICE, G. COAST, APR., 1887; JUN. CLK., JAN., 1891; 3RD CLK., JAN., 1893; NOW 2ND CLK.

BREWIN, ARTHUR WINBOLT.—ED. AT WINCHESTER; CADET, HONG KONG, 1888; PASSED CADET, DEC., 1890; AG. ASST. REGISTR. GEN., MAY, 1891, TO MAR., 1894, AND AGAIN 1895; J.P., 1891; INSPI. OF SCHOOLS, 1897; REGISTR. GEN., 1901; M.L.C., 1903.

BREWSTER, EDWARD JOHN.—ED. AT BREWOOD AND CHELT.; ASST. DIST. OFFR., MATANG, PERAK, 1878; ASST. SUPT., LOWER PERAK, 1878; ASST. MAG., KRIAN, 1879; DITTO, SALAMA, 1880; AG. DIST. OFFR., KRIAN, 1883-4; DITTO, LARUT, 1885; DITTO, KINTA, 1888; DIST. OFFR., KRIAN, 1889; MAG., KRIAN, 1889; STATE COMSNR. OF LANDS, AND REGISTR. OF MINES, JAN., 1892; AG. STATE AUDITOR, APR., 1893; DIST. MAG., LOWER PERAK, AUG., 1893; DITTO, KINTA, SEPT., 1904.

BRIDGES, W. F.—SUB ADMSTR. GEN., BERBICE, BR. GUIANA, FEB., 1885; AG. ADMSTR.-GEN., FEB., 1886, TO JAN., 1887; COMSNR. TO INQUIRE INTO ROAD ARREARS UNDER RD. ORDINANCE, MAY, 1889; AND INTO LAND TITLES, OCT., 1890; ADMSTR.-GEN., BR. G., APR., 1899.
BRIDGMAN, ALFRED H.—ENTERED C.O. AFTER EXAM., OCT., 1880; APPTD. ASST. CLK., MAY, 1892; SHORTHAND WRITER TO C.O., SEPT., 1896-1900; ASST. SUPT. OF PRINTING, MAR., 1900.

BRIGHT, CHARLES E., C.M.G. (1883).—B. 1829; A LEADING MERCHANT IN VICTORIA; HAS BEEN CHAIRMAN OF THE HARBOUR TRUST AND COMSNR. FOR THE MELBOURNE EXHIBN.; HAS ALSO BEEN COMSNR. FOR VICTORIA AT EXHIBNS. IN EUROPE.
BRILL, JOHANNES.—LIT.D., Utrecht Univ.; RECTOR OF GREY COLL., O.R.C., 6TH MAR., 1873.

BRINTON, ALFRED JOHN—CLK., COL. SEC.'S OFF., CAPE COLONY, MAY, 1884; 3RD CLS. CLK., JULY, 1885; 2ND CLS. CLK., JULY, 1892; PRIN. CLK., STATISTICAL BRANCH, JULY, 1897; AG. REGISTR. GEN. OF STATISTICS, JULY TO DEC., 1904, AND APR. TO JUNE, 1905; REGISTR. GEN. OF STATISTICS, JULY, 1905.

BRISBANE, LORD ARCHBISHOP OF MOST REV. ST. CLAIR GEORGE ALFRED DONALDSON.—LATE SCHOLAR OF TRIN. COLL., CAMB.; B.A. (1ST CLS. TRIP.), 1885; 1ST CLS. THEOL. TRIP., 1887; M.A., 1889; HON. D.D., 1904; WELLS COLL., 1887; DEACON, 1888; PRIEST, 1889; CANT.; CONSECRATED LORD BISHOP OF BRISBANE, 28TH OCT., 1904; ARCHBISHOP, 1905.
BRISTOW, CHAR. EDWIN.—GOVT. PRINTER, S. AUSTRALIA, AUG., 1890.

BRISTOWE, LEONARD SYER, M.A. (OXON.).—CALLED TO THE BAR, IN. TEMP., 1882; PUISNE JUDGE, SUP. CT., TRANSVAAL, 1903; JOINT AUTHOR OF "LAW OF CHARITIES AND MORTMAIN"; AUTHOR OF "THE MORTMAIN AND CHARITABLE USES ACT, 1891," AND OF "The Hospital Legal Handbook"; joint editor of 2nd edn. of "MacSwiney on Mines."

BRISTOWE, LINDSAY W.—Clt. survr.-gen.'s office, Br. Honduras, 1880; in treas. and cust. dept., 1883-4; sec. crown lands bd., 1884; qualified land survr., clk., regis.'s record office, clk. of the petty debt ct., July, 1884; clk. of coun., 1885; registr. V.A. court, 1886; provost-marshal, 1887; registr. and keeper of records, 1887-1890; offl. admstr., 1890; dist. comsnr. Toledo dist., 1892; Belize dist., 1893; notary and comsnr. of the sup. ct.; editor Br. Honduras handbook, 1888-93; dist. comsnr. G. Coast, 1893; assigned to treasy., Apr., 1894; ag. ch. asst. treas., Aug., 1894, to Jan., 1895; dist. comsnr., Acora, 1894; Elmina, 1895-96; chief registr. and sheriff, 1897; dis. comsnr., Chama, Sekondi, 1898; Salt Pond, 1899; Winnebahal, 1900; 3rd asst. col. sec., Oct., 1901; 2nd ditto, 1902; 1st ditto, 1903; clk. ex. and leg. couns., Mar., 1904; ag. ch. asst., 9th Feb. to 3rd Mar., 15th July to 2nd Dec., 1904, 20th June to 12th Nov., 1905, 3rd to 31st Dec., 1905, and 1st Jan. to 30th June, 1906.

BRITTEN, HENRY.—Apptd. probationer, Dec., 1895; 3rd cl. clk., magistrate's office, Ixopo, June, 1896; clk. and Zulu interpreter and sub-distributor of stamps, Ixopo, Apr., 1897; ag. clk. of the court on several occasions; served through the Boer War, 1899-1900, with the Border mounted rifles, including the siege of Ladysmith.
BRITTER, CHAS. ALFRED.—Master, Royal Coll., Mauritius, 15th Nov., 1876; sec., ditto, 17th Feb., 1893; sec., college comtees., 12th May, 1896; inspr. and sec. to comtees., 18th Oct., 1903.

BROADRICK, EDW. GEO.—Ed. at Sherborne schl.; clk. lower divn. H.M.'s office of works, Nov., 1884; cadet S.S., 1887; dist. offr., Nebong Tebal, P.W., June, 1890; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1895; ag. 2nd ast. prot. of imigrts., May, 1896; 2nd mag. in sole charge of dept., Nov., 1897; dist. offr., Dindings, Apr., 1897; ag. collr. land rev.; offr. in charge of treasy., Malacca, May, 1897; ag. sen. dist. offr., P.W., Sept., 1898; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of coun., Dec., 1898, to Nov., 1900; collr. of land rev. and offr. in charge of treasy., Malacca, Dec., 1901; ag. inspr. of prisons, S.S., Feb., 1902; capt. Singapore volr. rifles, July, 1900; p.s. cert., schl. of instruction, Chelsea barracks, July, 1901; major and ag. comdt. Singapore volr. corps, May, 1902; sen. dist. offr., Province Wellesley, Aug., 1902; mag. for, and visited Cocos Is., Nov., 1902; ag. 1st mag., Sing., May to Aug., 1903; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Sept., 1903; ag. commdt., Singapore vol. corps, 6th Nov., 1903; pres., mun. comsnrs., 1st Juné, 1904; comdt., Singapore vol. corps, 1st Mar., 1905.

BROCKMAN, EDW. LEWIS.—Cadet, S. Stiltns, 1886; passed in Malay, 1888; ag. supt. of educn., Penang, 1888; 3rd mag. Penang, June, 1890; ag. col. of land rev., Penang, May, 1889; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec., Feb. to Oct., 1892; dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Mar., 1892; ag. collr. land rev., Penang, July, 1895; 2nd asts. col. sec., June, 1896; also ag. collr. land rev., Singapore, June, 1896; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of coun. Aug., 1897; collr. land rev., Malacca, 1898; ag. 1st mag., Singapore, Feb., 1899; comsnr., court of requests, Singapore, Aug., 1902; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Sep., 1903; ag. col. sec., Apl., 1905.
BROCKMAN, FREDERICK SLADE.—Survr., lands and survey dept., W. Aust., 1884; survr., P.W. dept., 1887; survr., lands and survey dept., 1890; inspecting survr., ditto, 1891; chief ditto, ditto, 1894.

BRODHURST, HENRY WM. FRED. COTTINGHAM.—Ed. at Chelt. Coll.; writer, Ceylon service, 1877; pol. mag., Matale, 1880; asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Provs., 1885; ditto, Kegalla, 1891; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, 1891; dist. judge, Tangalla, Oct., 1898; Matara, Mar., 1899; asst. govt. agt., Puttalam, Dec., 1899; ag. govt. agt. and fiscal, S. Prov., Feb., 1902; dist. judge, Kalutara, Sept., 1904; offr. of cls. I., Jan., 1905; ag. govt. agt., W. Province, Apr., 1905.
BRODIE, D. E.—Ed. at Winchester; entd. serv. of British South Africa Company, 1897; asst. sec., 1902; sec., Aug., 1905.
BRODIE, H. M.—Ed. Roy. Coll., Maur.; sworn ld. survr., Maur., Apr., 1900; asst. survr., Seychelles, May, 1901; town survr., 23rd Dec., 1901; land survr., Fiji, Feb., 1906.

BROOK, HERBERT ARTHUR, I.S.O. (1906).—Ag. clk., pol. office, Nassau, May to Oct., 1874; librn., Nassau pub. liby., reading room and museum, Oct., 1875, to Oct., 1879; clk., pol. office, Oct., 1879; ag. ch. clk. col. sec. dept., May to Nov., 1881, and Oct., 1882; ag. priv. sec. to gov., Mar. to Apr., 1883; ch. clk. col. sec. dept., June, 1883; clk. bd. of pub. works and regist. of records, Sept., 1887; sup't. Bahamas census, 1891; now regist. of records; is a J.P. and mem. of bd. of educn.; ag. col. sec., Aug. to Oct., 1899.
BROOKE, HIS HIGHNESS SIR CHARLES JOHNSON, G.C.M.G. (1888).—B. 1829; Rajah of Sarawak.

BROOKE, GILBERT E.—Ed. Penn. Coll., Cam., and Lond. Hosp.; B.A., 1894; M.A., 1901; L.R.C.S. and L.R.C.P. Edin., L.E.P.S. Glas.; Fell. Roy. Inst. Pub. Health; Fell. Med. Soc., Lond., F.R.G.S.; late surg. Allan Line and Furness S.S. Co.; govt. med. offr. and med. offr. of health, Cockburn Harbour, Turks and Caicos Islds., ag. G.M.O. and M.O.H., Turks Isld., Aug., 1899; J.P. for the col., June, 1899; ag. asst. comsnr., Cockburn Harbour, Dec., 1899; May, 1900; dist. comsnr., Caicos Is., June, 1901; port health offr., Singapore, Jan., 1902; ag. govt. analyst, Mar. to Oct., 1905.

BROOKS, G. L.—Joined Lond. and Metropol. pol. force, 1885; inspr. Sierra Leone, Oct., 1894; supt., sheriff and provost-marshal, July, 1896; ag. pol. mag., coroner and judge of ct. of requests for Freetown, July to Dec., 1897.

BROOME, WILLIAM.—Entered Natal civil service, 1875; clk. exec. coun. and sec. defence comtees., 1878; ch. clk., atty-gen.'s office, 1879; advoc., sup. ct., 1882; sec. coun. of educn., 1885; R.M., Newcastle div., 1889; acted several times as asst. col. sec., 1886-88; J.P. for the col. (1889); mem. of coun. of educn., 1891; master and regist. sup. ct., 1889 offices of master and regist. separated 1897, retain in that of master; mag. Durban, 1898; judge of spec. treas., ct., 1900; resumed duty as mag., Durban divn., 1902; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., 1904; 3rd puisne judge, Dec., 1904.
BROWN, SIR CHARLES GAGE, M.D., K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1889), Hon. LL.D.—B. 1826; was for many years med. adviser to the Col. Office; retired 1897.
BROWN, H. Y. L.—Govt. geologist, S. Australia, Dec. 1882.
BROWN, JOHN FRANK.—Imperial service, 10th Sep., 1873; P.M.G., Natal, 14th Sep., 1900; lent to Transvaal govt., 1st Oct., 1902; P.M.G., Transvaal, 3rd April, 1903; M.L.C.

BROWN, JOHN JAMES.—Ed. Liverpool Collegiate Institn. and Royal Coll., Mauritius; volr., col. sec.'s office, Mauritius, Apr., 1863; 2nd clk. to sen. stip. mag., Aug., 1863; clk. to the ch. judge, May, 1864; curator of vacant estates, Oct., 1874; govt. agent before the forest lands purchase comsn. in conjunction with his office of curator, Nov., 1881, till Dec., 1882, and alone from Dec., 1882, to Feb., 1884; is also marshal of the vice-admiralty ct. since Feb., 1873; asst. rec.-gen., Dec., 1888; ag. collr. of customs, May to Nov., 1891; collr. of customs, Jan., 1895; rec.-gen., June, 1897; rec.-gen., Jan., 1897; ag. col. sec., Sept., 1901.

BROWN, JONATHAN RICHARD.—Prison offr., B. Guiana, 1st Feb., 1878; promoted 1st cls. and served at penal settm., Massaruni, 18th Nov., 1884, to 30th Dec., 1887; ag. keeper, Fellowship pris., West Coast, Demerara, 3rd May to 3rd Nov., 1890; principal warden, penal settm., Massaruni, 19th June, 1894, confirmed; ag. chief warden, Georgetown pris., 4th July to 8th Dec., 1895, 22nd Dec., 1895, to 30th Mar., 1896, and 3rd Nov., 1897, to 12th July, 1898; ag. keeper, New Amsterdam prison, Berbice, 9th to 21st Dec., 1895; ag. chief warden and asst. supt. at penal settm., Massaruni, 15th June to 14th Sep., 1897, and 12th Mar., to 16th June, 1900; keeper, Suddie pris., Essequibo, 1st Aug., 1900, to 31 Aug., 1903 (confirmed 1902); ch. warden, Georgetown pris., June, 1903, (confirmed, Mar., 1904); ag. supt., Georgetown prison, 14th Nov., 1903, to 13th Apr., 1904, ditto, Dec., 1904, to Apr., 1905; ag. asst. supt. and ch. warden (combined), Massaruni penal settm., May to Aug., 1905.

BROWN, RICHARD MYLES.—Ed. Liverpool Coll. Instn., and Royal Coll., Mauritius (1st English scholarship); called to the bar, Mid. Tem., June, 1869; counsel to govt., Seychelles, 1872 to 1877; practised before sup. ct., Mauritius, 1877 to 1884; mem. of comsn. on laws and rules affecting civil jurisdiction and procedure of dist. cts. in Mauritius, Apr., 1882; dist. judge, Seychelles, Nov., 1884; judge of Seychelles, and sen. mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1889; adminstd. govt., Seychelles, Nov., 1891, to May, 1892; judge sup. ct., Mauritius, Aug., 1900.
BROWN, W.—Clk. of wks. for post and telegraph extension wks., Bloemfontein, O.R.C., 1st Sept., 1902.
BROWNE, ALFRED.—Apptd. asst. harbour-master, Barbados, 1890.

BROWNE, FRANK.—Student in Pharmacy, Soc.'s Schl. in 1887; medallist in chemistry; elected research schlr. in 1888; apptd. a demonstrator in practical chemistry labtories, 1889 to 1893; apptd. asst. apothecary and analyst, Hong Kong, 1893; govt. analyst, 1898; sec. to liquor comsn., 1898; is Ph.C. and F.C.S.; J.P., 1903; author of several scientific papers.
BROWNE, ROBERT.—Gen. Post Office, Bar-... 1890; customs, 1894; 2nd cls. supervisor of customs, Gold Coast, 1900; ag. asst. treasnr., Gold Coast, July to Sept., 1900; 1st cls. supervisor, May, 1902.

BROWNING, JOHN GRANT, A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Catford Schl.; res. engrnr. Eastwood and Greasley sewage scheme; exec. dist. engrnr., P.W.D., Selangor, Malay States, Feb., 1896; engaged upon Pahang Trunk rly. construction.

BRUCE, SIR CHARLES, G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1889), C.M.G. (1881).—B. 1837; ed. at Harrow; author of "Die Geschichte von Nala und Damayant" (Sanscrit text, published by the Imp. Acad. of St. Petersburg), 1862; poems, 1866, and other wks.; asst. librn., Br. Museum, 1863; prof. of Sanscrit, King's Coll., 1865; rector Royal Coll., Mauritius, 1868; dir. of pub. instruction, Ceylon, 1878; col. sec., Mauritius, 1882; adminstd. the govt. in 1883; lieut.-gov. and govt. sec., B. Guiana, 1885; gov., Windward Is., 1893; gov. of Mauritius, 1897; ret., 1903.

BRUCE, ROBERT R.—Clk., Saint Helena, Mar., 1880; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1894; dep. regisr. of births and deaths, Sept., 1886; J.P., Sept., 1896; dep. harb. mast., Oct., 1898; collr. of dues, 1887 to 1901; col. treas. and harb. mast., Jan., 1902; shipping mast., Jan., 1906.
BRUCE, THOS. R.—Postmaster, St. Helena, Apr., 1898.

BRUMELL, JOHN.—Admitted to Mid. Tem., Oct., 1872; called to the bar, June, 1875; practised his profession in B. Guiana until Apr., 1882, when he was apptd. a stip. justice of the peace, stip. justice for New Amsterdam, and sheriff for the county of Berbice, 1883; acted on several occasions as pub. prosecutor, on two occasions as judge of the inf. crim. ct.

BRUNKER, HOWARD M.—2nd lieut., Donegal Artillery (militia), 1890; lieut., 1891 (passed schl. of instrn., Woolwich, 1893); resig., 1892, to join Bechuanaland border pol.; served in ditto, Oct., 1892, to Nov., 1895; served through Matabele campaign, 1893-4 (medal); apptd. sub-inspr. of pol., B. Guiana, 12th Feb., 1896; promoted dist. inspr. 6th April, 1900; acted adjutant and musketry instr. to B. G. militia and police, April, 1901, to April, 1902; apptd. capt. in Donegal Artillery (militia), 18th June, 1902; passed schl. of musk., Hythe, 1902; promoted county inspr. of pol., B. G., 19th Nov., 1903.

BRYAN, HERBERT, C.M.G. (1906).—2nd lieut., Line regt., 18th June, 1892; lieut., 16th Jan., 1894; capt., Manchester regt., 26th July, 1899; brev. major, 29th Nov., 1900; served in W. Africa, 1897-8; employed in Lagos Hinterland; also in operations on the Niger, including expdttn. to Bassema (ment. in desp., medal with clasp); served in N. Nigeria, 1900, slightly wounded (ment. in desp., clasp); served in operations in Ashanti, 1900; on the staff, D.A.A.G. (ment. twice in desp., medal); chief staff offr., Gambia expdn., 1901 (ment. in desp., medal with clasp); staff offr., W.A.F.F., 16th Oct., 1901; employed at C.O., 8th May, 1902, to 14th Nov., 1903; ag. col. sec., G. Coast, 29th Nov., 1903; ag. govr., 9th Feb., 1904, to 3rd Mar., 1904; apptd. col. sec., G. Coast, 14th Feb., 1904.
BRYAN, HUGH, M.A.—Provl. asst., Pietermaritzburg Coll., Natal, 9th Dec., 1902; asst. mst., 1st Jan., 1903; inspr. of schls., 11th Nov., 1904.

BRYANT, ALFRED THOMAS.—Ed. Tonbridge schl. and Wad. Coll., Oxon; cadet, S. Sttlmte., 1883; ag. collr. land rev., Penang, July, 1886; dist. offr., Malacca, 1887; ag. ditto, P.W., 1889; dist. offr. Dindings, June, 1890; ag. collr. of land rev. and offr. in charge of treasy., Malacca, Mar., 1894; inspr. of schls., S. Sttlmts., Apr., 1897; 1st mag., Penang, 1898; ag. 1st mag., Sing., Aug., 1905; ag. aud.-gen., June, 1906.

BUCHANAN, ALEXANDER.—Ed. St. Peter's, Adelaide, S. Australia, and Hofirigal and Glasgow; admitted to S. Australian bar, 1884; stip. mag. Port Adelaide, 1891; master sup. ct., regisr. in admty., regisr. of companies, industrial and provident societies, and trades unions, 1891; industrial regisr. under Conciliation Act, 1895; also regisr. of probates, Aug., 1903.

BUCHANAN, SIR E. J., KNT. BACH. (1901).—B. 1844; called to the bar, Inner Temp., Jan., 1873; advoc. of sup. ct. of Cape Col., of high ct. of prov. of Griqualand West, and of vice-admiralty ct., Cape Col.; elected mem. for Worcester in house of assem., 1877; ag. atty.-gen. of Griqualand West, Nov., 1879; apptd. a puisne judge of the sup. ct. of the Cape Col., Apr., 1880; assigned to the ct. of the eastern dist., May, 1880, and to sup. ct., June, 1887.
BUCKELL, CHARLES PHILIP.—Govt.'s messenger and telegraph clk., Portland prison, 1st Mar., 1877; warder, Singapore pris., S. Sttlmts., 29th Mar., 1883; supt. of telegraphs, Penang, 23rd Dec., 1892.

BUCKNILL, JOHN ALEXANDER STRACHEY, M.A. OXON.; J.P.—Barrister-at-law, Inner Temp., 1896; mid. circuit; advocate of sup. ct., Transvaal, 1902; comsmr. of patents, regisr. of trade marks and regisr. of companies, Transvaal, 5th June, 1902; M.L.C., 1904.
BUDGE, ALEX. CAMPBELL, J.P.—Entered the civ. ser. of N. S. Wales, Nov., 1858; clk., exec. coun., Oct., 1863.

BUKO, T. B.—Ed. at C.M.S. gram. school, Lagos; entd. civ. serv., Feb., 1888; clk. to ch. regisr., sup. ct., Jan., 1890; transf. to customs dept., Apr., 1891; dep. regisr. and interpreter, Leckie, east dist., May, 1892; clk. to atty.-gen., Mar., 1901; 3rd cl. clk., col. sec.'s off., May, 1901.
BULMER, J. A.—I.S.O. (1903); Postmr., Cyprus, 27th July, 1878; postmr.-gen., Trinidad, 1883; ret., 1903.

BULWER, SIR HENRY ERNEST GASCOYNE, G.C.M.G. (1883), K.C.M.G. (1874).—B. 1836; was one of the offl. residents under the lord high comsmr. in Ionian Is., from Oct., 1860, until the withdrawal of the Br. protectorate in June, 1864; apptd. treas. and rec.-gen. for the Is. of Trinidad, in Feb., 1866; adminstd. the govt. of Dominica, Leeward Is., from Mar., 1867, to Jan., 1869; apptd. gov. of Labuan, and H.M. consul-gen. for Borneo, in Aug., 1871; lieut.-gov. of Natal, Aug., 1875, to 1880; gov. of Natal and special comsmr. for Zulu affairs, Dec., 1881; high comsmr. Cyprus, 1885; ret., 1892.
BULYEA, GEORGE HEDELEY VICARS.—Lieut.-gov. of prov. of Alberta, Canada, 1st Sept., 1905.

BUNDEY, THE HON. SIR WILLIAM HENRY, KT. BACH. (1904), K.C.—B. 1838; atty.-gen. of S. Australia, Sept., 1878; entered parlmt. in 1871; held office as min. of justice and educn., July, 1874, to Mar., 1875; 3rd judge of the sup. ct., 1884; also pres. of state bd. of conciliation, Jan., 1893, to Apr., 1896; ret., 1904.

BURBIDGE, HON. G. W., LL.D., K.C.—Called to the bar, New Brunswick, 1871; sec. to comsmr. to consolidate statutes of New Brunswick, 1876-7; dep. min. of justice, Canada, and solr. for Indian affairs, May, 1882; one of the comsmrs. to revise the consolidated statutes of Canada, June, 1883, to June, 1885; judge, exchequer ct. of Canada, Oct., 1888.

BURCHELL, H. C.—Mem. Can. Soc. C.E.; mem. Am. Inst. M.E.; Mount Allison and McGill univs.; asst. engnr. to prov. engnr. of Nova Scotia, 1875-8; gov't. engnr., Newfndld., 1884; manager govt. telegraphs, 1893; man. Newfndld. rly., 1897; dep. min. and ch. engnr. P.W., and chmn. St. John's municipal coun., 1898; supt., pub. wks., Br. Hond., 31st July, 1905, but did not take up apptnt.

BURKE, HENRY LARDNER, K.C.—Ed. at Lincoln Coll., Oxford, 2nd cl. classical mods., 1871; B.A., 1873; M.A., 1901; LL.B., of Cape univ., 1880; admitted as an advocate of east dist. ct., 1880, and of sup. ct., 1886; examiner in literature at Cape univ., 1888-1890; ag. solicitor-gen., 25th June to 25th July, 1895, 22nd Dec., 1896, to 28th Mar., 1897, and from 8th May to 12th June, 1897; apptd. crown prosecutor, Griqualand West, 14th June, 1897; apptd. Q.C., 13th May, 1898.

BURLEY, E. B.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. 2nd divn. civ. ser., and assigned to regisr.-gen.'s office, 21st Jan., 1896; transf. to C.O. 20th June, 1896; special service in office of high comsmr., South Africa, July, 1900, to Jan., 1902; minor staff offr., Feb., 1903.

BURMESTER, CHARLES MANSEL.—Ed. at Dulwich Coll.; ent. serv. of Norddeutscher Lloyd SS. Co., Bremen, April, 1898; res. Dec., 1899; joined 58th Co. (Berks) Imp. Yeom., Jan. 29th, 1900; served in S. Afr. camp, 1900-1901; clk. to sec. O. R. C. Admn., Mar. 20th, 1901; clk. to col. sec., June, 1902; ag. ch. clk., Mar., 1905, to Jan., 1906, and from Sept., 1906.
BURNS, ALAN C.—Revenue offr., St. Kitts, Leeward Is., Mar., 1905.

BURNS, R. E.—2nd clk., registr.'s off., St. Kitts, June, 1898; ag. clk., town comsmrs., Bassteerre, June to Dec., 1899; clk., P.O., Oct., 1901; extra rev. offr., treasy., Dec., 1899; ag. clk., treasy., May, 1902, to Mar., 1903; ag. govt. clk., clk. ex. and leg. couns., clk., bd. of health, Apl., 1903, to Mar., 1904, June, 1904; ag. clk., treasy., St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1904; tariff clk., treasy., St. K.-N., Oct., 1904; ag. audit clk., May, 1905.

BURNSIDE, SIR BRUCE LOCKHART, KT. BACH. (1884)—B. 1833; called to the bar, Linc.'s Inn, Apr., 1856; solr.-gen., Bahamas, May, 1864; was ag. atty.-gen. in 1865, 1866, 1869, and 1875; mem. of exec. coun. in 1866; and of legis. coun. in 1872; mem. of the local legislature from 1859; and was speaker of the House of Assem. in 1866; made Q.C. in 1874; atty.-gen., Sept., 1875; Queen's advce, Ceylon, 1879; ch. justice, 1883; ret., 1893.

BURNSIDE, NIGEL BRUCE.—New Providence, Bahamas, 3rd clk., surv.-gen.'s dept., 1879 and 1881; ag. clk. bd. of pub. wks., 1880 to 1882; clk. legis. coun., 1881 to 1888; 2nd clk. surv.-gen.'s dept., 1882 to 1883; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s dept., 1883 to 1887; priv. sec. to Admstr. E. B. A. Taylor, C.M.G., 1886; clk., col. sec.'s dept., and clk., bd. of pub. wks., 1887 (still holding same); ag. col. sec., July to Nov., 1889, July to Oct., 1890, and in 1891; ag. recvr.-gen., Jan. to Mar., 1898, May to Oct., 1898, and May to Dec., 1899; ag. col. sec., Feb. to Mar., 1899.
BURNSIDE, R.B.—Called to the bar, Linc.'s Inn, 1884; admtd. to W. Australian bar, July, 1884; usher of blackwood, legis. coun., Dec., 1890; crown solr., July, 1894; 3rd puisne judge, Dec., 1902.

BURROWS, STEPHEN MONTAGU, M.A.—Scholar of Eton, scholar of Exeter Coll., Oxford, Jan., 1876; graduated in classical hon., 1879; writer, Ceylon service, Oct., 1880; priv. sec. to Lieut.-Gov. Douglas, June, 1881; J.P. and additional coroner for Colombo, Nov., 1881; attached to the Queen's advoc. dept., June, 1882; J.P. and additional pol. mag., Galle, Aug., 1882; ag. office asst. to the govt. agt., N.W. prov., Apr., 1884; extra office asst. to the govt. agt., central prov., May, 1884; ag. office asst. to the govt. agt., north-central prov., and additional dist. judge, comsnr. of requests and pol. mag., Anuradhpura, Aug., 1884; ag. office asst., Badulla, July, 1886; ag. asst-agt., Matale, June, 1887; ag. dist. judge, Kegalle, 1892; ag. fiscal agt. C.P., 1892; ag. asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, 1893; do., Nuwara Eliya, 1896; dir. of pub. instruction, 1899; govt. agent, N.W.P., 1902; author of "The Visitor's Guide to Kandy and Nuwara Eliya," and "The Buried Cities of Ceylon."

BURSLEM, W.—Ed. Manchester gram. schl. and Pemb. Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1879; M.A., 1882; asst. mast., Harrison Coll., Barbados, 1889; 2nd mast., Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, 1891; head master, Lodge schl., Barbados, 1892; principal, Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, 1895.

BURT, ALFRED EARLE.—Clk. to ch. justice, W. Australia, June, 1871; clk. col. sec.'s office, Dec., 1873; to gov., 1874; in audit office, Jan., 1875; draughtsman, marine survey dept., 1875; and in crown lands dept., Sept., 1877; acted as regis. of deeds and titles, Jan., 1880, to Mar., 1881; regis. of titles and deeds, June, 1890.

BURT, OCTAVUS, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1849; clk. in gov.'s office, W. Australia, May, 1872; Jan., 1874, clk. to exec. coun. and priv. sec. to ag. gov.; Apr., 1874, to Dec., 1874, priv. sec. to Gov. Weld; May, 1875, clk. to exec. coun. and priv. sec. to Gov. Sir Wm. Robinson, K.C.M.G.; Sept., 1877, ch. clk. and keeper of records in survey office; res. mag., Newcastle, Apr., 1880; ditto, York, Apr., 1887; asst. col. sec., Apr., 1887; ag. col. sec., 1890, with seat in exec. and legis. couns., under sec. for W. Australia under responsible govt., 1891; ag. sec. to agent-gen., Apr. to Oct., 1898; under-sec., May, 1899; sheriff, W.A., 1901.

BURY, FRED. W.—Junior clk., regis.'s off., R. Guiana, Feb. to Aug., 1887; libr., govt. secretariat, Sept., 1887, to Feb., 1888; asst. clk., recvr.gen. & off., Aug., 1888, to Mar., 1889; 5th cls. clk., Ap', 1889, to May, 1892; 4th cls. clk., June, 1892, to May, 1897; 3rd cls. clk., June, 1897; ag. 1st cls. clk., Oct., 1900, to Mar., 1901; ag. asst. recvr. gen. and senr. administr., Berbice, Feb., 1904, to 23rd Dec., 1904.
BURY, ROBERT MAXWELL.—5th class clk., postal dept., R. Guiana, 1st Dec., 1887; 4th class clk., ditto, 1st Aug., 1898; 3rd cls. offr., customs dept., 29th Jan., 1903.

RUSHE, ROBERT GERVASE.—Ed. at King's Col., Camb., where he obtained an exhibn. on entrance; elected to a foundation scholarship, 1874; was 27th wrangler in the math. tripos, 1875; grad. B.A., 1875; 2nd master Queen's Roy. coll., Trinidad, 1878; inspr. of schools, 1890; auditor-general, 1903.

BUSHE, R. J. SCOTT.—A.M.I.C.E., Ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, Malvern Coll., and Lancing Coll.; ag. and special draftsman, P.W. Dept., Trinidad, various occasions, 1882-6; draftsman light rly., Feb., 1880; survr. of loan wks., Grenada, Jan., 1899; ag. dir. pub. wks., Mar., 1891; engaged on loan wks., Trinidad, 1893; and in charge of S. div., P.W.D., 1894-6; draftsman, 1897; dist. offr., 1st grade, 1899; asst. col.-civ. engnr., B. Guiana, 1902.

BUTLER, ERNEST ORMOND.—Served in Bech. Border police and Bech. mounted pol., 1891-1897; Matabele war, 1893-4; served in B.S.A. police, Bech. Prot. div., 1898-1902; S. African war, 1899-1902 (medal and clasp, defence of Mafeking); ch. clk., headquarter off., Lt.-Gen. Baden Powell's force, 1900; customs offr., 1902-1903; asst. commr.'s clk., Bech. Prot., 1902; J.P., ditto, 1903.

BUTLER, F. G. A.—Exhibitioner of Trin. Coll., Oxford, 1892; 1st class classical mods., 1894; 1st class final classical schools, 1896; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in Admiralty, Oct., 1896; 2nd class clk. in C.O., Apl., 1897; sec. to W. Africa currency comtee., 1899; 1st class clk, 1st Jan., 1907.

BUTLER, CAPT. P. J. H.—Late 3rd Batt. E. Lancs. Regt.; entered Natal civ. ser., Jan., 1890, customs dept.; sergt.-at-arms, House of Assem., Natal, Nov., 1895; volunt. for spec. serv., and as lieut. proceeded, Oct., 1899, with Hotchkiss gun detach. to Ladysmith, present during siege, medal and clasp.
BUTLER, RICHARD, M.L.A., S. Aust. (1890).—Min. of ed. and agric., 1898-9; treas., 1901; treas. and comsnr. of crown lands and immigtn., 1902; also premier, 1st Mar. to 26th July, 1905.

BUTLER, CAPT. VERE ALBAN.—Late R.M.L.I. and 7th Roy. Lanc. Militia; 2nd class inspr. of pol., Mauritius, June, 1880; pol. offr., with magisterial powers, Diego Garcia, Sept., 1885; reverted to former post on abolition of latter, Mar., 1889; ag. 1st class inspr. of pol., Jan., 1892; inspr., Sept., 1894; ag. ch. offr. of pol. on several occasions for Port Louis.

BUTLER, MAJ.-GEN. SIR WM. FRANCIS, G.C.B. (1906), K.C.B. (1886), C.B. (1874); B. 1838; served in Canada in Red River expedit., 1870; in Ashanti, 1874; Zulu War, 1878-9; Egypt, 1882; with Gordon Relief exped., 1884-5; comdg. troops S. Africa, 1898; admstd. govt. Cape, Nov., 1898, to Feb., 1899; commanding troops W. Dist. England, 1899; do., Aldershott, 1900.

BUTTERWORTH, CAPTAIN ARCHIBALD WILLIAM.—Govt. storekeeper, Br. N. Guinea, Oct., 1890; headqrs. offr. armed constab., Feb., 1891; also port and harbour., and native mag., promoted commandant constab., and res. mag., Oct., 1894; active serv., Tugere expedit., 1896 (medal and clasp); volunt. for serv. in S. Afr., and joined 4th Q'land conting., Mar., 1900, as sen. lieut.; prom. capt. at Pretoria, Nov., 1900; ment. by Lord Roberts in cablegram to gov. of Q'land for splendid behaviour, 2nd Dec., 1900; resig. contig. and Br. N. Guinea govt. serv., Sept., 1901; 2nd lieut., Royal Lanc. Regt., Sept., 1901; capt. and company comdr., Lagos batt. W.A.F.F., Sept., 1901; active serv., Aro expeditn., S. Nigeria, 1901-2 (medal and clasp); capt. 4th batt. Royal Lanc. Regt., Aug., 1904; dist. comsnr., Lagos, Oct., 1904.

BUXTON, SYDNEY CHARLES, M.P.—Ed. at Clifton Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; was mem. of the London schl. bd. from 1876 to 1882; hon. sec. to Mr. Tuke's Irish emigrn. fund, 1882-4; author of the "Handbook to Political Questions," the "Political Manual," "Finance and Politics," "An Historical Study, 1783-1883," &c., and editor of the imp. parlmt. series; M.P. for Peterborough from June, 1883, to Nov., 1885, when he was unsuccessful; contested Croydon unsuccessfully in Jan., 1886; elected July, 1886, and re-elected in 1892, 1895, and 1900, for Poplar (Tower Hamlets), for which he still sits; parly. under-sec. of state for the cols., 17th Aug., 1892, to 28th June, 1895; postmr. gen., Dec., 1905.

BUXTON, SIR THOMAS FOWELL, BART., G.C.M.G. (1899), K.C.M.G. (1895).—3rd Bart.; M.A., Camb.; B. 1837; dep. lieut. and J.P. for Essex and Norfolk; sheriff, 1875; M.P. for King's Lynn, 1865-68; lieut.-col., 2nd Tower Hamlets rifle vol. brigade, 1864; hon. col. since 1884; gov. of S. Australia, 1895-9.

BYATT, HORACE A.—Class. exhibtr., Lincoln Coll., Oxford, 1894; B.A. (honours), 1898; asst. collr., B.C.A. Prot., Nov., 1899; Africa general serv. medal, 1899-1900; asst. polit. offr., Somaliland, Aug., 1903; asst. dist. offr., Nov., 1905; sec. to adminstr., Somaliland, Aug., 1906.

BYNOE, A. D.—Cik., G.P.O., Barbados, 1874; rev. offr., Jan., 1879; landing waiter, May, 1879; 2nd cik., customs, June, 1887; ch. cik., customs, Dec., 1890; adm. compt. of cust., Apr., 1902, to Feb., 1903, and on other occasions; admeasurer of shipping, Sept., 1904.
BYRE, M.—Sec. to law dept., Victoria, Oct., 1896; formerly ch. cik. in same dept.

CADE, CHARLES SHERWOOD.—Capt. in the W. Cork artillery militia; served through the Zulu war, 1878-9; present at the battle of Ulundi; asst. instr., G.C.C., Mar., 1880; instr., 11th Dec., 1882; adjt. and musketry instructor of the G.C.C., Sept., 1882, to May, 1883; local comdt. of pol., Cyprus, Sept., 1883; also asst. to comsrr., Nicosia, and gov., central prison, Oct., 1884; comsrr., Nicosia, 17th Oct., 1905.

CADMAN, JOHN, M.Sc., M.I.M.E., F.G.S.—B. 1877; ed. High Schl., Newcastle-under-Lyme, and Durham Univ. Coll. Sc.; Staff, C.C. scholar, 1895; B.Sc. (Dunelm.), hon., 1899; M.Sc., 1902; pres., Union soc., 1898; certif. colliery man., 1900; asst. gen. man., Silverdale coal and iron works, Staffs., 1900; asst. agt., Walter Scott, Ltd., Collieries, Durham, 1901; H.M. insp. of mines (H.O.) East Scotland, 1902; ditto, Staffordshire, 1903; govt. mining engr., Trinidad and Tobago, 1904; author of prize papers on "Iron Ore Deposit" before Inst. of Mining Engrs.

CADOGAN, 5TH EARL (Great Britain), GEORGE HENRY CADOGAN.—B. 1840; eldest son of the 4th earl, succeeded his father in 1873; was M.P. for Bath for a few months previous to his succession to the title; parly. under sec. for war, May, 1875, to 2nd March, 1878; parly. under sec. for the col., 2nd Mar., 1878, to 28th Apr., 1880; lord privy seal in Lord Salisbury's 1st and 2nd ministries; lord-lieut. of Ireland, 28th June, 1895-1902.
CALDER, CHARLES MACLEAR.—Admitted solr., sup. ct., Jamaica, 1881; clk. of cts., parish of Portland, Apr., 1888; ditto, parish of St. Mary's, Feb., 1889; res. mag., Trelawney, Jan., 1897.

CALVERT, JOHN J., I.S.O. (1904).—B. 1830; Cik., col. sec.'s office, N. S. Wales, 1853; clk. of select comtees, legis. assem., May, 1856; 1st clk. legis. coun., Apr., 1859; clk. asst., Jan., 1860; clk. of the parlmts., Apr., 1871.

CAMBRON, ALLAN DUNCAN.—Ed., Ireland; cler. asst., instr. of villages and sec., bd. of health, B.G., 14th Apr., 1890; 6th cik. cls., instr. of pris., Oct., 1893; 5th ditto, 9th Feb., 1896; govt. offr., dep. of mines, Bartika, 8th Dec., 1896; acted as navg. offr. for Michael McTurk, C.M.G., 2nd Mar., 1897, to 8th Dec., 1898; ag. magst.'s clk., Bartika, 1897-98; transf., same appt., to Koriabe station, 19th Oct., 1899; J.P., 30th Oct., 1899; magst.'s clk., Corentyne jud. dist., 1st May, 1900; 3rd cls. clk., pol. dep., 1st Nov., 1901; commanded B. Guiana militia as lieut., 21st Nov., 1901; recommended by H.E. Sir A. J. Swettenham for appt. as hon. A.D.C. with rank of capt., Aug., 1902.

CAMERON, DONALD CHARLES.—Ed. at Rathmines School, Dublin; entd. Br. Guiana civ. ser., 1890; 5th class clk., secretariat, 1891; 4th ditto, 1895; 3rd class and despatch clk., 1895; 2nd class, 1899; priv. sec. to ag. gov't., 1896, 1897, 1898 and 1901; ag. asst. gov't. sec. and clk. of councils, 1900-1901, principal clk., secretariat, 1901; priv. sec. to gov'r. of Newfoundland (with permission of S. of S.) whilst on leave, 1902; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, July, 1904; ag. col. sec., 19th Dec., 1904, to 26th Oct., 1905, and 14th Apl., to 13th Sept., 1906.

CAMERON, MAJ.-GEN. DONALD RODERICK, C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1834; entered R.A. 1856; capt., 1866, maj., 1875, lieut.-col. 1882, col. 1886; ret. 1888; instr. in gunnery, 1859-62, 15th Bde. R.A.; served throughout Bhootan campaign, 1864-66, as adjt. and as staff offr. of commg. R.A., Door Field Force (medal with clasp, thrice mentioned in despatches); adjt. 1st Bde. R.A., 1867-8; accompanied the Hon. W. McDougall, C.B., to Fort Garry, as a mem. of the exec. coun., N.W. Territories, 1869; was comsrr. internat. bindry. comsrr. and suptd. the expdh. which marked the internat. bindry. from the Lake of the Woods to the summit of the Rocky Mountains, 1872-6; reported on the Br.-Alaskan bindry., 1874; sec. to Canadian delegation at the Paris internat. confce., 1883, for the protection of submarine cables; reported on the E. bindry. of Br. Columbia, 1884; and again on the Alaskan bindry. in 1886; priv. sec. to Sir C. Tupper, Canadian plenip. to the fishery confce., 1887; principal, Roy. mil. coll., Kingston, Canada, 1888. Ret. 1896.

CAMERON, EDWARD J., C.M.G. (1905).—Ed. at Shrewsbury schl., Clifton Coll., and Merton Coll., Oxon; priv. sec. to Sir C. C. Lees, gov', Bahamas, Jan., 1882; and as gov' of the Leeward Is., Jan., 1884; asst. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, Nov., 1884; J.P. for the Stltnt., and visiting justice, Freetown Gaol; ag. col. sec. and treas., Dec., 1885, to July, 1886; pres., Virgin Is., Feb., 1887; mem. Leeward Is. legis. coun., 1887, and exec. coun., 1888; comsrr., Turks Is., Mar., 1893; ag. judge sup. ct., May to Dec., 1893, Jan. to June, 1895, and Apl. to July, 1898; admstr., St. Vincent, May, 1901.

CAMERON, HON. EWEN HUGH.—Mem. of Shire coun., Victoria, since 1863; has been mem. and chmn. of rlyws. standing comtee.; M.L.A. for Evelyn, Victoria, 1874; min. for mines and water supply, 1902-4; comsrr. of pub. wks. and min. of pub. health, Feb., 1904.

CAMERON, MAJOR MAURICE ALEXANDER, C.M.G. (1900). R.E.—B. 1855; first comsrr. in Roy. engrs., Aug., 1874; dept. col. engrnr., S. Stltnt., Dec., 1883, to Apr., 1892; during which he acted for about two years and a half as col. engrnr. and survr.-gen., and was a mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; apptd. one of the crown agts. for the colonies, July, 1895.

CAMPBELL, ARCHIBALD NEIL, F.R.G.S.—Capt. reserve of offrs., late R.A.; South Africa, 1897-1903; on spec. serv., 1st July, 1899, to 31st Dec., 1900, including spec. apptmt. at H.B.M. consulate-gen.'s, Delagoa Bay, May to Dec., 1900; afterwards on intell. staff, army headqrs.; operations in Natal, 1899-1900, including actions at Colenso and Spionkop, and relief of Ladysmith (twice ment. in desps.; Queen's medal, 4 clasps; King's medal, 2 clasps); Anglo-Portuguese boundary delimit. coman., East Africa, 1904-5.

CAMPBELL, DOUGLAS GRAHAM.—2nd Surveyor, P.W.D., Selangor, S. Stltnt., 1883; ditto, land office, 1885; asst. dist. offr., Rawang, 1887; sec. to res., 1888; dist. offr., Kuala Langat, 1890; ditto, Ulu Selangor, 1893; ditto, Klang, 1897; ditto and ast. Indian immigrn. agt., 1898; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, 1899; sec. to res., 1901; res. of Negri Sembilan, 1904; has acted as res. on several occasions, and also as sec. to gov't.

CAMPBELL, H. C., I.S.O. (1903).—Second clk. in the deeds registry office, Natal, Apr., 1861; 2nd clk. audit office, Jan., 1863; 1st clk. R.M. ct., co. Pietermaritzburg, Mar., 1868; R.M., div. of Ixopo, Mar., 1876; ag. R.M. div. of Umgeni, Nov., 1876; R.M., Inanda, and J.P. for col., 1880; master and registr., sup. ct., 1882; audr. audr., Apr., 1889; acted as judge native high ct., and as 2nd puisne judge and atty.-gen.; audit-gen., 1894; chmn. civ. ser. bd.; judge president, native high ct., 1899; judge, spec. treas. ct., 1900; resumed duty as judge pres., nat. high ct., 1901.

CAMPBELL, J. CATHEY.—Capt., R.Irish Rifles; served in traffic dept., Ceylon govt. rlyws., 1890; Ceylon pris. dept., 1891; 1st class inspr., Ceylon harbr. pol., 1893; transf'd. to land pol., 1897; ast. comsnr., pol. and pris., N. Nigeria, 1901; sheriff and keeper of gaols, 1903; comit. of volrs., customs offr. and san. inspr., Falkland Is., 1906.

CAMPBELL, W. TELFER.—Ed. at Rossall; passed entrance exam., Roy. Mil. Coll., Sandhurst, 1882; served with Methuen's Horse, Bechuana-land field force, 1884-85; reg.-gen.'s dept., Queensland, 1889; ast. res. mag., Eastern div., B.N. Guinea, 9th Oct., 1890; res. mag., S.E. div., ditto, Feb., 1891; res. mag. and comsnr. of sup. et., B.N. Guinea, 1893; res. comsnr., Gilbert and Ellise Is. Prots., and dep. comsnr., Western Pacific High Comsn., 29th Nov., 1905.
CAPPER, THOMAS.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.Sc. (Lond.), 1876; B.A., Camb. (Math. and Nat. Sc. Triposes), 1879; inspr. of schools, Jamaica, Oct., 1880; off. mem. of legis. coun., 1882.

CARBONE, SIR JOSEPH, LL.D. (1862), C.M.G. (1887), K.C.M.G. (1891), G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.V.O. (1903).—B. 1839; received at the Malta Bar, 1863; reporter, ct. of commerce, 1868; crown advoc. and govt. legal adviser, 1880; mem. of exec. and legis. coun., ex officio; ch. justice, Malta, 1895; vice-pres. legis. coun., 1898.

CARDEN, J.—Shorthand writer in office of ast. sec. (for India) to Roy. comsn. Col. and Indian Exhib., 1886; diploma for services; entered service of Roy. Niger Company, Jan., 1887; priv. sec. to Sir G. Goldie, Jan., 1887, to Dec., 1899; transf'd. to C.O. as supplementary clk., Jan., 1900.

CARDEW, CLAUDE AMBROSE.—Ast. agt., Chinde, B. Cent. Africa Prot., June, 1893; collr., South Nyasa dist., 1894; 2nd class ast., Apr., 1902; dist. mag., Upper Shire, 1902; dist. res., 1st class, Apr., 1906.

CARDEW, COL. SIR FRED., K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1839; engaged in N.W. Frontier of India campaign, 1863, and Zulu and Transvaal campaigns, 1879-81; D.A.Q.M.G. in S. Africa, 1879-80; ast. mil. sec. China, 1882-3; sub-comsnr., Zululand, 1884-6; A.A.G., S. Africa, 1890; res. comsnr. Zululand, 1890; gov. S. Leone, 1894 to 1900.

CARGILL, JASPER FARMER.—B.A., LL.B., Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Tem., Jan., 1890; ag. ast. to atty.-gen., and clk. to legis. coun., Jamaica, 1895; law examr. to solr.'s comtee., 1897; referes of titles, 1900; sec. to Montego Bay Riots comsn., 1902; res. mag., Jamaica, Aug., 1904; ag. solr.-gen., and nom. M.L.C. on various occasions, 1896-1904.

CARLILE, EDWARD, K.C.—Gold medallist in law, Melbourne Univ., 1868; clk., census office, Victoria, May, 1861; regar.-gen.'s office, Jan., 1862; crown law offices, Mar., 1865; called to the bar, Victoria, 1871; partly. and professional ast. to crown law offrs., 1873; partly. draftsman, July, 1879; clk.-asst., legis. assem., Apr., 1882; re-apptd. partly. draftsman with higher status, Apr., 1889; author of "Notes on Victoria Constitution," "Victorian Year Book, 1883-4."

CARLING, THE HON. SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1828; mem. for London, Ontario, 1857-74; elected to Dominion house of commons on confederation, 1867; rec. gen. of Canada, 1862; min. of wks. and agricul., Ontario, 1867-71; again elected for London, 1878; sworn of the privy coun. and made postm. gen. in Sir J. Macdonald's govt., 1882; min. of agricul., 1885 to 1892; mem. of cabinet without portfolio, 1892; ret., 1894.

CARLSON, KNUT ALEXANDER.—Forest probationer in the Knysna conservancy, Cape Colony, 5th Feb., 1888; probationary ast. to conservator of forests, Knysna, Sep., 1889; dist. offr., Kokstad, Transkeian conservancy, May, 1892; passed through course of forestry at R.I.E. Coll., Cooper's Hill, during 1894-5, and was awarded a diploma; dist. forest offr., Butterworth, Aug., 1895; transf'd. to O.R.C. as chief of forestry div., 1st Dec., 1903.

CARMODY, P.—Entered Imp. service after open competn., 1876; inland rev. laboratory scholarship, 1880; diploma (1st class) Roy. Schl. of Mines; ast. analyst and subsequently analyst, Somerset House, 1881-90; govt. analyst, Trinidad, and prof. of chemistry, Queen's Roy. Coll. and Coll. of the Immaculate Conception, June, 1890; mem. central agricul. bd., 1890; mem. bd. of health, 1891; is F.I.C., F.C.S., mem. of soc. of chem. industry, and author of prize paper on "Recent Progress of Invention and Science," and other published papers on scientific subjects.
CARON, L. B.—Puisne judge, superior ct., Quebec, 1874.

CARON, THE HON. SIR J. P. R. A., K.C.M.G. (1885), K.C.—B. 1842; called to the bar of Lower Canada (now Province of Quebec) 1865; created a Q.C., 1876; entered the Canadian House of Commons, 1873, where he still sits; min. of militia and defence for the Dominion of Canada, 8th Nov., 1880; postm.-gen., 1892 to 1896; one of representatives of Canada at col. confe., Ottawa, 1894.
CARPMAEL, ERNEST.—Ed. at Dulwich and Trinity Hall, Camb.; scholar, 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., cadet, Weihaiei, 1904.

CARRENA, ALBERT EMANUEL E.O.—Ed. at St. Gregory's R.C. gram. schl., Lagos; ast. clk. har. dept., 1894; clk. and storekeeper, 1895; messenger and copyist, Queen's advocate's off., 1896; 2nd clk. gen. registry, 1897; Queen's advoc.'s clk., 1897; assisted as clk. in the trade comsn., 1898; sec. to the comsn. of inquiry on the conduct of Dr. D. J. Jones, 1899; promoted 3rd class clk., govr.'s office, 1901; sec. to the comsn. of inquiry on the railway accident at Owowo, 1901; sec. to the comsn. of inquiry on the customs dept., 1901; acted as regisr. of corres., secretariat, 1902; on spec. serv. to Ekiti countries, 1903; ag. conf. clk., clk. of ex. and leg. couns., 1904.

CARRINGTON, EARL (created 1895), VISCOUNT WENDOVER (U.K.), BARON CARRINGTON (Great Britain, 1797, and Ireland, 1796). Charles R. W. CARRINGTON, P.C. (1881), G.C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1843; ed. at Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb., B.A. 1863; late capt. Roy. Horse Guards, lieut.-col. 3rd batt. Oxford Light Infantry; capt. Queen's Body Guard, 1881 to 1885; gov. of N. S. Wales, 1885-90; lord chamberlain, 1892-95; pres., bd. of agric., Dec., 1908; joint hered. lord great chamberlain of England.

CARRINGTON, MAJOR-GEN. Sir Frederick, K.C.B. (1897), K.C.M.G. (1887), C.M.G. (1880).—B. 1844; ed. Chelt. Coll.; 24th regt.; comdr. of the F. L. H. in the Transkei war, S. Africa, 1877-78; commanded Transvaal volks against Sikukuni, 1878-79; commanded left wing C.M.R., 1879 to 1883; commanded col. forces in Basuto war, severely wounded; commanded 2nd mounted rifles, Bechuanaland field force, 1884; comdt. of pol., Br. Bechuanaland, 1885; comdt. native forces, Zululand, in 1888; again in command Bechuanaland pol., Dec., 1888-93; and again on special service, Nov., 1893, in connection with Matabele war; comdg. brigade at Gibraltar, 1895; commanded forces in Matabele war, 1896; resumed command at Gibraltar, 1897; commanded Rhodesian Field Force during S. Afr. war, 1900.

CARRINGTON, Sir John Worrell, Knt., (1897), C.M.G. (1888), K.C., F.R.G.S., D.C.L., LL.D.—B. 1847; ed. Codrington Coll., Barbados (S.C.C., 1866), and Line Coll., Oxon (B.A., 1872; M.A., 1891); called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, June, 1872; acted as judge, asst. ct. of appeal, Barbados, 1874-5; J.P., 1874; mem. house of assem., 1874-8, and again 1881; M.L.C., 1878-81; solr.-gen., 1878; again acted as judge, asst. ct. of appeal, 1879; and as atty.-gen., 1880; hon. D.C.L., Durham, 1879; hon. LL.D., St. Andrews, 1894. Was pres. of the educn. bd., and of the bd. of health, and received the thanks of govt. for services in connection with educn.; ch. justice, St. Lucia and Tobago, 1882; on special duty as ag. col. sec., Grenada, 1882; admnstd. govt. of Tobago, 1883 to 1885; prepared revised edition Tobago laws; ag. ch. justice of Grenada, 1886; in 1887-9 prepared a new edition of the laws of St. Lucia; atty.-gen. Br. Guiana, Dec., 1888; chancellor of the diocese, 1890; engaged, 1891-2, in drafting for the govt. of Br. Guiana bills relating to the cts., criminal law and procedure, etc., of that col.; chmn. central bd. of health, 1889, of the opening-up-the-country comsn., 1891-2, and of the spirits ordinance comsn., 1893-4; maj. in the Br. Guiana militia, 1893. In 1894 prepared a new and revised edition of the laws of Br. Guiana; ch. justice, Hong Kong, 1896; ret. 1902.
CARROLL, Hon. James.—Native min. and comsnr. of stamp duties, New Zealand.

CARRUTHERS, J. B., F.L.S.—Ed. Dulwich Coll., Royal Schl. of Mines, London, and Griefswald Univ., Prussia; demonstrator of botany, Royal Vet. Coll., London, 1892; prof. of botany, Coll. of Agric., Doniton, Wilts., 1894; proceeded to Ceylon to investigate cacao disease under joint auspices of govt. and Ceylon planters' assoc., 1898; mycologist to govt. of Ceylon and asst. dir., Royal Bot. Gardens, 1900; dir. of agric. and govt. botanist, F.M.S., 1905.

CARRUTHERS, Hon. Joseph Hector McNeil—B. 1857; called to the bar, 1879; M.L.A. for Canterbury, 1887-1894, and since for reformed dist. of St. George, N.S. Wales; min. for pub. instr., 1889-91; min. for lands, 1894; col. treas., 1899; leader of opposition, 1902-4; premier and treas., New S. Wales, Aug., 1904.

CARTER, Sir Gilbert T., K.C.M.G. (1893), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1848; entered navy 1864; paymr. of col. steamer "Sherbro," S. Leone, Aug., 1870; served on the G. Coast during a part of the Ashanti campaign; was one of the comsrs. for valuing the ordnance and stores left behind by the Dutch at the transfer of Elmina; priv. sec. to gov. Leeward Is., Sept., 1875; collr. of customs and treas., G. Coast, Aug., 1879; treas. and postmtr., Gambia, Oct., 1882; admnstd. the govt. several times; admnstd. of Gambia on separation from S. Leone, Nov., 1888; gov., Lagos, 1890-96; gov., Bahamas, 1898; gov., Barbados, 23rd July, 1904.

CARTER, William Stuart.—After compet. exam., apptd. copyist to the treas., cust., and post office, Br. Honduras, Feb., 1894; ag. 5th clk. of same, Sep. to Dec., 1895; 4th clk. Mar. to June, 1898; ag. clk. to registr.-gen., June to Sep., 1898, Mar. to Oct., 1899, and Mar. to Oct., 1900; ag. clk. to col. engnr., Oct. to Feb., 1900.

CARTWRIGHT, The Right Hon. Sir Richard John, P.C. (1902), G.C.M.G. (1897), K.C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1835; entered Canadian parlmt., 1863; sworn of the privy coun., and was min. of finance, 7th Nov., 1873, until the resig. of the Mackenzie cabinet, Oct., 1878; min. of trade and commerce in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.

CARUANA XICLUNA, Giuseppe.—M.D., Malta Univ.; analyt. chemist, Malta, 1881; san. inspr., 1890; sen. med. offr. of health, 1901; ch. govt. med. offr. and supt. of pub. health, with seat in exec. coun. and coun. of govt., 1905.
CASALI, Sir L. N., KT. Bach. (1894).—B. 1822; ch. justice super. ct., Quebec, 1894.

CASEY, The Hon. Jas. Joseph, C.M.G. (1878).—B. 1831; barrister-at-law; M.L.A., Victoria, 1861-1879; mem. exec. coun., 1868; min. of justice, 1868; solr.-gen., 1869; pres. of bd. of land and wks. and min. of agricul., 1872 to 1875; pres. of Victoria comsn. to Paris Exhibn., 1878; exec. vice-pres. of Melbourne internat. exhibn., 1880; pres. of fed. bk. of Australia; is now judge of insolveney ct. and chmn. gen. sessions; judge of cts. of mines and co. cts.; is knight offr. of the legion of honour and of the crown of Italy.

CASOLANI, Edward Robert.—Apptd. clk., Sep., 1878, and higher div. clk., Mar., 1883, on the high comsnr's estab., Cyprus; transf. to the ch. sec.'s estab., Mar., 1884; ag. loc. comdt. of pol. and asst. comsnr., Nicosia, July to Sep., 1898; ch. clk. and sub. loc. comdt. of pol., Jan., 1899; again ag. loc. comdt., Nicosia, May to Oct., 1902.

CASOLANI, William.—Clk. port dept., Malta, May, 1863; cust. dept., Jan., 1865; 2nd class clk., ch. sec.'s office, June, 1880; 1st class clk. and asst. clk. to the coun., Jan., 1886; dep. asst. sec. to govt. and ch. clk., July, 1891; asst. sec. to govt. and clk. to exec. coun. and coun. of govt., July, 1894; rec. gen. and director of contracts, 1902; mem. of ex. coun. and coun. of govt.
CASSERLY, James Michael.—3rd class clk., col. sec.'s office, Jamaica, 1878; 2nd class clk., Nov., 1885.

CASSON, Joseph Charles.—Priv. sec. to Sir H. H. Johnston, G.C.M.G., K.C.B., H.M.'s comsnr., B. Cent. Africa Prot., 1894-1895; clk. in ch. acctnt's off., Feb., 1896; asst. sec., B. C. A. admnstr., Apr., 1901; supt. of native affairs, Aug., 1903.
CASTOR, C. F., M.B., C.M., F.R.G.S.—Asst. col. surg., G. Coast Col., Feb., 1879; med. offr., Br. Guiana, 1885; med. supt. leper asylum, 1887; is J.P. and coroner.

CATT, THE HON. A.—Mem. house of assem., S. Australia, since 1881; comsnr. of crown lands, S. Australia, June, 1881, to June, 1884; comsnr. of pub. wks., June, 1887, to June, 1889; chmn. of contees., legis. assem., 1890-1905.

CAVENDISH, A., B.A. Oxon.—Cadet, S.S., Jan., 1901; ag. 3rd mag., Penang; ag. 4th mag., Singapore; passed final exam. in Malay, May, 1903; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Oct., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, 23rd Jan., 1904, ag. supt. of educ., Penang, Apl., 1906.

CAYLEY, SIR RICHARD (KNT., 1882).—B. 1833; M.A. and scholar of St. John's Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, June, 1862; advoc. sup. ct. of Ceylon, June, 1863; dep. Queen's advoc., May, 1868; puisne judge of the sup. ct., 1873; Queen's advoc., 1876; ch. justice, 1879; resig., 1883; is a mag. for Rutland.

CELESTIN, LOUIS ALBERT.—Ed. govt. normal schl., Mauritius; teacher in edn. dept., 1874; ag. inspr. of schls., Nov., 1886, to Aug., 1889; dist. cashier, Dec., 1889, to Nov., 1893; ch. inspr. of distilleries, Nov., 1893; ag. supt. of distilleries, Apr. to Oct., 1895; dist. cashier, Piacoq, June, 1898; Rose Hill, Jan., 1901; 2nd cls. clk., treasy., June, 1903.

CHADWICK, JOHN COURTENAY CHASMAN.—Clk. and interp. to the R.M., Inanda div., Natal, Jan., 1868; clk. of the ct., Oct., 1870; acted as R.M. and admstr. of native law on various occasions, 1872-7, at Inanda; J.P. for the Inanda div., Aug., 1875; regis. of the native high ct., and sec. to native admstn. bd., Nov., 1876; admitted an atty. of the sup. ct., 1878; first class interp. on the staff of Maj.-Gen. Newdigate during Zulu War of 1879 (medal and clasp); admstr. of native law and border agt., Upper Tugela, 1880; R.M., Lions River div., and J.P. for the col., 1882; R.M. and admstr. of native law, Ixopo, Sep., 1886; mag., Lions River div., 1897-8; Umgeni divn., 1898; judge, native high ct., July, 1905.

CHADWICK, OSBERT, C.E., C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1884; has been employed by the C.O. to report on the drainage and water supply of various cols.; was consult. engr. for the Malta water wks. and Hong Kong water supply and drainage.

CHALMERS, A. J., M.D., Ch.B. (Victoria Univ., Eng.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—Asst. col. surg., G. Coast, 1897; ag. ch. med. offr., 1900, and during siege of Kumassi, 1900 (ment. in desp., medal and clasp); registr., med. coll., Ceylon, 22nd Feb., 1901.

CHAMBERLAIN, THE RT. HON. JOSEPH, P.C. (1880), M.P., Hon. LL.D. (Camb.), Hon. D.C.L. (Oxford).—Eldest son of the late Mr. Joseph Chamberlain; born in London, 1836, and ed. at University Coll. schl., after which he was engaged in business in Birmingham till 1874, when he retired. In 1870 he was elected a mem. of the Birmingham town coun., and in the same year a mem. of the schl. bd., of which he became chmn. in 1873. In each of the three following years, 1874-5-6, he was elected mayor of the borough, and in June, 1876, on the retirement of Mr. Dixon, one of the three representatives of Birmingham, he was returned to Parlmt. At the general election of 1880 he was again returned with the late Mr. John Bright and Mr. Philip Muntz as his colleagues, and on the formation of Mr. Gladstone's admstn. he was apptd. pres. of the bd. of trade, with a seat in the cabinet. While at the bd. of trade he prepared and succeeded in passing through Parlmt. the Bankruptcy Act, which is now in force, the Patents Act of 1883, and other measures. After the redistribution of seats in 1885 he was returned as mem. for W. Birmingham, and joined Mr. Gladstone's 3rd admstn. in 1886, as pres. of the local govt. bd., but resigned in consequence of his disagreement with the premier's home rule policy. At the general election of 1886 he was returned unopposed for W. Birmingham, and at the end of 1887 proceeded to the U. States as plenipotentiary to negotiate, in conjunction with H.M.'s min. there and Sir Charles Tupper, a treaty for the settlement of the Atlantic fisheries question. The result of these negotiations was the convention of 1888, and though it was rejected by the U.S. senate, the modus vivendi established by it still regulates the fisheries. On the defeat of Lord Rosebery's govt. in June, 1895, Mr. Chamberlain joined Lord Salisbury's govt. as sec. of state for the col., and continued to hold that office in Mr. Balfour's cabinet, 1902. At the general elections of 1895 and 1900, in both of which he took a very prominent part, he was again returned for W. Birmingham. At the end of 1902 he visited S. Africa, to deal with questions connected with the settlement of the country; Lord Rector, Glasgow Univ., 1896; Chancellor, Birmingham Univ., 1901; resigned office in connection with the question of a new fiscal policy, Oct., 1903.

CHAMBERLAYNE, MAJOR TANKERVILLE JAMES.—Ed. Chelt. Coll.; ensign, Ceylon rifles, June, 1868; transfd. to 80th regt., Oct., 1873; fort adjt., Hong Kong, June, 1875, to Mar., 1877; ag. brig.-maj., Nov., 1875, to Feb., 1876; officiated twice as asst. mil. sec., China command; served in Transvaal and throughout Zulu war, 1878-79; present at Ulundi (medal and clasp, 1878-79); A.D.C. to gov. of Natal (Sir H. Bulwer, G.C.M.G.), 3rd Feb., 1882; priv. sec., Mar., 1882; also ag. clk. exec. coun., July, 1885; priv. sec. to high comnr. of Cyprus, and clk., exec. coun., Mar., 1886; local comdt. of pol., 1892; col. of customs and excise and asst. to comsnr., Larnaca, 1894; ag. comsnr., Larnaca, June-Oct., 1895; Kyrenia, Nov., 1896-Apr., 1897; Nicosia, June-Nov., 1897; Kyrenia, Apr., 1898; comsnr. and local comdt. of pol., Kyrenia, 1899; ag. comsnr., Larnaca, June-Nov., 1900; do. Nicosia, Jan. to May, 1900, Nov., 1900, to Oct., 1901, and Aug., 1902, to Nov., 1903.

CHAMBERS, CECIL H.—Cashier, St. John's savings bank, Antigua, 1893; accountant, ditto, 1894; 4th out-door offr., treasy. and cust. dept., 1895; 3rd in-door offr., treasy., Antigua, 1897; audit clk., Dominica, 1900; audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1901; ag. chief audit clk. and federal acctnt., Antigua, May, 1902, to Mar., 1903, and May to Nov., 1905; ag. 1st treasy. offr., Montserrat; M.L.C.; comsnr. for oaths, Nov., 1903, to Mar., 1904.

CHAMBERS, JOHN RATCLIFFE.—2nd clk. to pres. St. Kitts, May, 1875; 3rd landing waiter, treasy., Dec., 1878; 1st clk. to registr. and provost-marshal, Apr., 1881; sec. of the comsn. to inquire into the general condition of Dominica, Nov., 1881; priv. sec. to gov. Leeward Is., 1882 to 1883; clk. to mag. dist. C, St. Kitts, July, 1883; dep. coroner, May, 1885; qtrmr. with St. Kitts militia, June, 1886; J.P., and visiting justice of the gaol; ag. mag., dist. C, 1899, 1902, 1903 and 1904; escheator-gen., 1905.

CHANCELLOR, J. R., D.S.O.—Capt. R.E.; served with expdn. to Dongola, 1896, with force at Suakin (medal and Egyptian medal); in operations on N.W. frontier of India, 1897-8, with Tirah expd. force, action of Dargai, (ment. in desps.); sec. to col. def. comtee., Oct., 1906.

CHANDLER, WILLIAM KELLMAN, C.M.G. (1902), B.A., LL.D.—B. 1857; St. John's Coll., Camb. (1875-79); law tripes, 1879-80; called to the bar, Inner Tem., June, 1879; ag. escheator-gen. of Barbados, 1880-81; J.P., 1880; apptd. comsrr. of probates, Dec., 1880; ag. pol. mag. of Bridgetown, Aug. to Nov., 1880; ag. solr.-gen., Nov., 1880, to Mar., 1881; ag. judge of asst. ct. of appeal, Mar., 1881, to June, 1883; master-in-chancery, May, 1882; judge of asst. ct. of appeal, June, 1883; mem. of house of assem., Mar., 1881; represented parish of St. Peter until Nov., 1884; M.L.C., 1884; represented Barbados in negotiations at Washington in connection with McKinley Tariff Act, 1891; ag. ch. just., Nov., 1901, to Apr., 1902; ag. col. sec., July, 1902, to Feb., 1903, and Apr. to Oct., 1903.

CHAPMAN, ARTHUR.—Assessor of taxes, Hong Kong, 1889; ag. asst. supt. fire brigade, 1891-92; J.P., 1894; lieut. Hong Kong volunteer corps, 1893; capt., 1897; major, 2nd in command, 1899; ag. comdt. on several occasions; thanks and medal for services during plague, 1894; mem. of comttee. on volunteer corps, 1898; commanded volunteers during disturbances in New Territory, 1899; commanded Hong Kong and China Coronation contingent, 1902.

CHAPMAN, HON. AUSTIN.—B. 1864; M.L.A. of N. S. Wales for Braidwood, 1891-1901; M.P. for Eden-Monaro, 1901; retd. to 1st House of Rep., C. of A., 1901; re-elected, 1903; 1st govt. whip in fed. parlmt., 1901-3; min. for defence, 1903-4; postm.-gen., 5th July, 1905.
CHAPMAN, FRANK.—3rd class clk., audit dept., Natal, Feb., 1898; 2nd class clk., Jan., 1900; 1st class clerk, Jan., 1901; asst. inspr., audit dept., July, 1901.

CHAPMAN, THOS. HOWARD, A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Cheveley Hall schl., Plymouth; asst. in Boro' water engrnr.'s off., Plymouth, 1887; dist. engrnr., P.W.D., Ceylon, 1888; Batuilla, 1889; Galle, 1896; seconded to irrigtn. wks., 1898; dist. engrnr., Dinillulla, 1899; Korlanda, 1901; Kandy, 1903; prov. engrnr., Eastern Province, 1904.

CHATHAM, WILLIAM, M.I.C.E.—Ed. Roy. High Schl. and Univ., Edin.; asst. to Messrs. Thos. Meik and Son, C.E., Edin., 1880; res. engrnr., Bo'ness drainage works, 1883; asst. engrnr. dock engrnr.'s office, Bristol, 1885; exec. engrnr. P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1890; ag. dir. of pub. wks., 1893-94-97-1901; mem. mems. coun., 1897; asst. dir. P.W., 1898; director, 1901; mem. exec. coun., 1901; pres. of san. bd., 1901.
CHELMISFORD, 3rd BARON (U.K.), creat. 1858.

FREDERIC JOHN NAPEL TESIGER, K.C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1868; ed. at Winchester and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1st cls. law) 1891; M.A., 1894; Fellow of All Souls, 1892; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1893; mem. of London schl. bd., 1901-4; mem. of L.C.C., 1904; gov. of Queensland, 8th Aug., 1905.

CHERMSIDE, SIR HERBERT CHARLES, R.E., G.C.M.G. (1899), K.C.M.G. (1896), C.B.—B. 1850; Roy. Eng., 1870; during Russo-Turkish war, 1876-78, accompd. Turkish troops as mil. attache; served in Egypt camp, 1882; and was attached to Egypt army, 1883 to 1888; in 1896 became Brit. mil. comsrr. and commander of Brit. troops in Crete, and for services there was made K.C.M.G.; appointed to the Curragh district in 1899, and has since served in S. Afr. in commd. of 3rd infant. div.; gov. of Queensland, 1901; resigned 1904.

CHERRY, JAS. WM.—Ed. at Bedford Modern schl.; apptd. asst. ch. clk. and acctnt., P.W.D., G. Coast, 9th July, 1898; ch. clk. and acctnt., 1st Jan., 1900; offl. mem. Accra town coun., 1898, 1899, 1900 and 1901; chrmn. streets and roads comttee.; apptd. 2nd lieut. G. C. rifle volunteers, 16th Nov., 1898; lieut. 17th Aug., 1900; acted adjutant, 3rd May to 30th June, 1899, and 26th June, 1900, to 1st June, 1901; apptd. capt., 12th Oct., 1901; ag. offr. commanding, Dec., 1901, to July, 1902; certificate, army form, E 511, Chelsea barracks, Oct., 1901.

CHICHESTER, ATHOL AUGUSTUS.—Joined H.M.S. "Worcester" (training-ship), 1876; served in H.M. hospital ship, "Ganges," during Egyptian campaign, 1886; temp. trav. inspr. of bd. of agri. under cont. diseases acts, 1890 to 1892; apptd. asst. dist. comsrr. Niger Coast Prot., May, 1897; dist. comsrr. Sapeli, 1900-02; transfd. to Asaba, Aug., 1902; trav. comsrr., 17th Jan., 1903; ag. divisional comsrr., Central Division, Apr. to Sept., 1903, and again in 1904.

CHICHESTER, CHARLES RENFRIC.—Ed. Stonyhurst Coll. and Dublin Univ.; B.A. Dub.; L.R.C.P. and L.R.C.S., Ireland; diplomate in public health; licentiate in midwifery, Rotunda, Dub.; dipl. in pub. vaccination; asst. col. surg., Gambia, Mar., 1896; ag. col. surg., Apr. to Nov., 1896, May to Dec., 1898; pres. of bd. of health whilst ag. col. surg.; mem. quarantine bd.; sen. med. offr., So. Nigeria, 1904.

CHILD, LIEUT. HERBERT A.—Entered H.M. navy as cadet, 15th July, 1883; specly. promoted to midshipman, 23rd July, 1885; served in H.M. ships, "Conquest," "Triumph," "Calypso," "Minotaur," "Duke of Wellington," "Pilot," and "Thrush"; specly. promoted to lieut., July, 1891; landed with nav. brig. in Gambia expdn., 1891-92 (West African medal and clasp); resigned comsrr., Feb., 1894, to take up appt. as asst. supt. marine in N. C. P., 4th May, 1894; took part in naval operations against Chief Nana of Benin river, 1894 (mentioned in desps., 2nd clasp); took part in naval operations against Brass chiefs, 1895 (3rd clasp); sup. of marine, S. Nig., 15th Jan., 1897; took part in naval operations against King of Benin, 1897 (mentioned in desps., 4th clasp); took part in Aro exped., 1901-2, having organisation and charge of marine transport arrangements (mentioned in desps., African gen. serv. medal and clasp); comsrr. as lieut. R.N. restored 21st Aug., 1902, on joining emergency list of retired officers, R.N.
