CHIPPENDALL, GEORGE HERBERT.—Sub-lieut. 3rd foot, 1874; lieut. East Kent regt., 1874; capt. Nov., 1883; maj., W. Riding regt., Dec., 1893; lieut.-col., Nov., 1898; comdt. defence force, W. Australia, Dec., 1898; transfd. to commonwealth govt., 1901.

CHITTY, LOUIS OGILVY.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dub.; asst. mr., Harrison Coll., Barbados, 1883-91; headmr. gram. schl., St. Vincent, Sept., 1891, to Dec., 1893; inspr. of schls., St. Vincent, Jan., 1894, to Dec., 1896; inspr. of schls., Jamaica, Jan., 1897, to Mar., 1898; audr. and inspr. of schls., Seychelles, Apr., 1898; audr. only, 1902.
CHURCH, THE HON. C. E.—Mem. for Lunenburg in Nova Scotian legis. assem.; comsrr. for pub. wks. and mines.

CHURCHILL, JOHN SPENCER, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1835; Ed. at Winchester; ensign, 46th regt., 1854; served at siege of Sebastopol (medal and clasp); capt. 36th regt., 1866; A.D.C. to Lord Lisgar, when lord high comsrr. of the Ionian Is., 1857; pres. of Virgin Is., 1879; ag. pres., Nevis, 1879. and of Dominica, 1882 to 1887; prese., Montserrat, 1857; comsnr. of St. Kitts-Nevis, 1888; col. sec., Bahamas, 1896; ret., 1905.

CHURCHILL, WINSTON LEONARD SPENCER.—M.P. for Oldham, 1900-6; for N.W. Cheshire, 1906; late lieut. 4th Queen's Own Hussars; ed. Harrow, Sandhurst; entd. army, 1895; served with Spanish forces in Cuba, 1895 (1st cls. (Spanish) Order of Military Merit); served, attached 31st Punjab Infantry, with Malakand field force, 1897; present at operations in Bajaur, including actions of 16th and 30th Sept. (desp., medal with clasp); served as orderly offr. to Sir W. Lockhart with Tirah exped. force, 1898 (clasp); served, attached 21st Lancers, with Nile exped. force, 1898; present at battle of Khartoum (medal with clasp); contested Oldham (C.) 1899; served as lieut. South Africa Light Horse; acted as correspdt., "Morning Post," South Africa, 1899-1900; taken prisoner, action 15th Nov., but escaped 12th Dec.; present at actions of Acton Homes, Venter's Spruit, Hussar Hill, Cingolo, Monte Cristo, and at battles of Spion Kop, Vaal Krantz, and Pieters; also operations round Dewetsdorp, April, 1900; passage of Sand River, 15th May; engagements of Johannesburg and Diamond Hill, and capture of Pretoria (medal with six clasps); partly under-sec. of state for the Colonies, Dec., 1905; author of "The Story of the Malakand Field Force"; "The River War"; "Savrola"; "London to Ladysmith via Pretoria"; "Ian Hamilton's March."

CLANLIS, J. B.—Chief clk., Port dept., Gibraltar; supplementary clk., Port dept., Sept., 1880; 3rd cl. clk. and clk. to regis. of shipping, Feb., 1883; 2nd cl. clk., Jan., 1892; 1st cl. clk. and clk. to shipping mr., Jan., 1898.

CLARE, HENRY LEWIS.—Ed. Rathmines schl. and Trin. Coll., Dub.; B.A. (1879), M.B., and B. Ch. (1880); M.D., 1897; D.P.H.; jun. res. med. offr., pub. hosp., Kingston, Jamaica, Apl., 1881; dist. med. offr., Dry River dist. and hosp., July, 1882; transf'd to Vere dist., 1883; to Charleton, 1888; to Spanish Town, 1892; surg. to St. Catherine dist. prison, and to Middlesex and Surrey cty. gaol; dist. med. offr., Kingston, June, 1896; mem. cent. bd. of health.

CLARENCE, BEVERLEY CHARLES.—Clk. to regis., sup. ct., Natal, Apl., 1875; asst. clk. of the peace, and prosecutor of the native high ct., Natal, Apl., 1880; clk. of the peace, 1896; mag., Pietermaritzburg, 1905.
CLARK, ALFRED ALLEN.—Forester, Ceylon, 1st May, 1875; asst. conservator of forests, 1st Jan., 1889; ag. conservator, Jan., 1901, to June, 1902.
CLARK, ANDREW INGLIS.—Atty.-gen. of Tasmania in the Fysh govt., 29th Mar., 1887-92; mem. of the fed. coun. of Australasia, Jan., 1888; and of fed. convention, 1891; puisne judge, 1898.

CLARK, FRANCIS W., M.D. (Durham), 1900; M.B., 1892; D.P.H. (Camb.), 1891; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1886.—Ed. St. Paul's schl., and St. Barts. and Middlesex hosps.; mem. of Hon. Soc. of Mid. Temple; med. offr. of health and supt. fever hosp., Lowestoft, 1893; med. offr. of health and supt. of statistics, Hong Kong, 1895; Dean and lect. on forens. med., coll. of med. for Chinese; mem. sanitary board, 1895; president, 1905; J.P., 1896; mem. legis. coun., 1902; mem. exec. coun., 1905.

CLARK, GEORGE HARVEY.—Ed. Brixton gram. schl., London, and Potebam schl., Jamaica; copyist, col. sec.'s office, Jamaica, Mar., 1890, to July, 1891 (resigned); admitted sofr., sup. ct., Jamaica, 17th Sept., 1897; clk. of cts., St. Mary, Jamaica, 2nd Nov., 1903; super. res. mag., Dec., 1903, to May, 1904; ag. res. mag., St. Mary, 1st Aug. to 29th Nov., 1904; ag. res. mag., Clarendon, Aug. to Sept., 1905; admitted sofr., sup. ct., England, 12th Aug., 1905.
CLARK, THOMAS.—Apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of civ. serv., and assigned to C.O., Apl., 1902.
CLARK, WM. MORTIMER, K.C.—Called to the bar, Ontario, 1861; Q.C., 1887; senator of Toronto univ.; chrmn. of bd. of management of Knox Coll., Toronto, 1880; lieut.-gov., Ontario, Apl., 1903.

CLARKE, SIR CHAS. MANSFIELD, Bart., G.C.B. (1901); G.C.V.O. (1903).—B. 1838; entd. Army, 1856; served in N. Zealand, 1861-66 (medal); Zulu war (promoted brevet-col., C.B., medal and clasp), 1879; Basutoland, 1880-81; commdt.-gen. of col. forces at Cape of Good Hope, 1880-82; A.A.G., War Office, 1884; D.A.G., Ireland, 1886-88; maj.-gen. commanding 3rd infantry brig., Aldershott, 1889-92; D.A.G. at headquarters, 1892-93; com.-in-chief and lieut.-gen. commanding the Forces, Madras, 1893-98; quartermaster-gen. to the Forces, 1899; gov., Malta, 2nd Mar., 1903.
CLARKE, ERNEST D.—Dep.-regis., sup. ct., Trinidad.

CLARKE, SIR FIELDING, KT. BACH. (1894), LL.B.—B. 1851; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Easter, 1876; N.E. circuit; atty.-gen. of Fiji, 1881; ag. ch. just. and ch. judicial comsnr. for the W. Pacific, 1882 to 1883, and again 1884; confirmed, 1885; puisne judge, Hong Kong, 1888; ch. just., 1891; ch. just., Jamaica, 1896.
CLARKE, GEORGE H.—Asst. res. just., Nicoll's Town, Andros Is., Bahamas, Dec., 1902.

CLARKE, Lieut.-Col. SIR GEORGE S., R.E., G.C.M.G. (1905), K.C.M.G. (1893), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1848; entered R.E., 1868; capt., 1880; major, 1887; served in Egypt, 1882 (medal and star); in Suakin expdn., 1885, mentioned in despatches (clasp); sec. col. defence comtee., 1885-92; supt. of carriage factory, Woolwich, 1893; gov. Victoria, 1901-1904; apptd. as one of a comtee. to advise as to the creation of a Board for the administrative business of the War Office, Nov., 1903; sec. to Imperial Defence comtee., 1904.

CLARKE, Lieut.-Col. SIR MARSHAL JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1880), (late R.A.).—B. 1841; res. mag., co. Pietermaritzburg, Natal, 1874; A.D.C. to Sir T. Shepstone, H.M.'s special comsnr., Lydenburg, 1877; served in Transvaal campaign 1880-1, in command of Landdrost's office, Potchefstroom; mentioned in despatches; res. mag. Quithin, Basutoland, 1881; comsnr., Cape pol., King William's Town, 1882; col. comdg. Turkish regt. Egyptian gendarmerie, 1882; 3rd class order of Medjidie; ret. from the army, with rank of lieut.-col., 1883; res. comsnr. Basutoland, 1884; res. comsnr. and ch. mag., Zululand, 1893; res. comsnr., Rhodesia, 1898.

CLARKE, WILLIAM JAMES.—Joined Natal Mounted Police, 1878; served through Zulu war, 1879 (medal with clasp); accomp. ex-Empress Eugenie on her tour through Natal and Zululand, 1880; served in Basutoland war, 1880 (medal with clasp); served in Transvaal campaign, 1881, present at battle of Leig's Neck; accomp. mission to Pondoland, 1887, with mission to Tongoland, under Col. Sir R. Martin, in 1888, and with the Portuguese-Swazi bindry. comsnr., 1888; ag. res. mag. and admtr. of Native law for Ixopo div., 1889-90; apptd. crim. investgn. offr., 1890; commanded Natal police field force on S. border during disturbances in E. Griqualand, 1897, and in Zululand, 1898; took part in Boer war, 1899-1902; commanded N.P. field force, in operations round Ladysmith, and during the siege; present at capture of Boer guns on Gun hill, and at battle of Caesar's Camp, on 6th Jan., 1900; with cav. brig. in attack on Botha's Pass, 1900; attached to the F.I.D.; was intell. offr. with Gen. Dartnell's column in the operations under Gen. French in E. Transvaal, and was sent by the latter gen. on spec. mis. to Zululand, 1901; joined Gen. Bullock's col. as intell. offr. in the E. Transvaal, 1901; ag. intell. offr. with General Dartnell's col. in O.R.C., 1901. In charge of sec. serv. dept. in Natal, 1902; sent by Gen. Bruce Hamilton on spec. mis. to Zululand, and was afterwards intell. offr. on that gen.'s staff, in the operations in S.E. Transvaal (medal with 4 clasps); accompd. the Prime Min. of Natal to England, in the capacity of sec., to attend the coronation, 1902; asst. comsnr., Natal pol., 1st Dec., 1904; ag. ch. comsnr., 1st Apr., 1905.

CLAUSON, MAJOR J. E., R.E., C.M.G. (1904).—B. 1866; Ed. at Clifton and R.M.A., Woolwich; 1st cls. hons., B.A., Lond., 1887; admitted to Inner Temple, 1897; lieut., R.E., 1885; designed pontoon equipment of army, 1889; passed staff coll. (1st) 1893; employed at army headqrs., 1895; sec. to army rly. council, 1897; sec. to mobilization comte., 1898; sec. to col. def. comte. (acting 1899) 1900; chief sec. to gov't. of Cyprus, Oct., 1906.
CLAXTON, T. FOLKES, F.R.A.S.—Joined mag. and met. dept. R. Obser., Greenwich, Feb., 1890; appd. asst. dir. Royal Alfred obser., Mauritius, Jan., 1896; dir., Dec., 1896; is also sec. met. soc., Maur.

CLAYTON, A. G.—Ed. Marlbro' Coll.; 3rd class clk., col. sec. office, Jamaica, Sept., 1886; ch. clk., col. sec. office, Br. Honduras, July, 1892; clk. of councils, Jan., 1898; priv. sec. to gov' of Br. Honduras, Jan., 1897, to Oct., 1898; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Ceylon, 1900; addtl. asst. col. sec., 1901.

CLAYTON, L. H.—B.A., Camb.; cadet, S. Stllmts., Nov., 1895; ag. ass't prot. of Chinese at Singapore, May, 1897; also of Penang, Aug., 1897; passed final exam. in Chinese, Mar., 1899; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Feb., 1899; confirmed 4th mag., Aug., 1899; ag. ass't protector of Chinese, Singapore, Oct., 1899; dist. offr., Christmas I., May, 1901; ag. ass't prot. of Chinese, Penang, Apr., 1904; asst. ditto, Singapore, May, 1904, but cont. to act in Penang.

CLAYTON, REGINALD JOHN BYARD.—Cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1898; attached to land off., Batang Padang, July, 1899; in charge Kuala Kangsar treas'y., Dec., 1899; passed final law exam., Feb., 1900; ag. regiser., Taiping, Apr., 1900; ag. mag., Larut, May, 1900; ag. collr., land rev., Matang, Aug., 1900; in charge of Matang dist., Sept., 1900; passed final language exam., Nov., 1900; ag. collr. of land rev., Matang, Jan., 1901; ditto, Batang Padang, Feb., 1901; 1st cls. mag., ditto, Dec., 1901; asst. dist. offr., ditto, Jan., 1902; ag. asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Feb., 1904; ditto, Kinta, June, 1904; 2nd asst. dist. offr., Batang Padang, 1905.
CLEARE, W. T.—Resident just. and collr. of ctn., Biminis, Bahamas, Nov., 1899; ditto, Long Island, Mar., 1901.

CLELAND, W. L., B.M., A.M., Edin. (1876).—Asst. med. offr. Derby county asyl., Eng., 1877; res. med. offr. Parkside lun. asyl., S. Australia, and asst. col. surg., 1878; res. med. offr., Adelaide and Parkside lun. asyls., and col. surg., S. Australia, 1896; ceased to be res. med. offr., Adelaide Lun. Asyl., which was closed in 1903.

CLEMENTI, C.—Ed. St. Paul's schl. and Magdalcn Coll., Oxford (demy, 1894; B.A., 1898; M.A., 1901), hon. mention Hertford schl., 1895; 1st cl. mods., 1896; hon. mention Ireland and Craven schl., 1896; Boden Sanskrit scholar, 1897; prox. acc. Gaisford (Greek Prose), 1897; 2nd cl. hum., 1898; prox. acc. Chancellor's Latin essay, 1899; cadet, Hong Kong, 1899; passed cadet, 1900; ag. asst. col. sec., and ag. clk. of councils, Dec., 1900, to Oct., 1901; asst. regist.-gen., Aug., 1901; mem. bd. of exams. in Chinese, Apr., 1902; seconded for spec. serv. under gov't. of India, May, 1902; J.F., June, 1902; ag. asst. col. sec., and clk. of councils, Sept., 1902, to June, 1903; sec. interpretation bd., Dec., 1902; seconded for famine relief work in Kwang Si, April to June, 1903; mem. of land ct., New Territory, Dec., 1903; asst. land offr. and pol. mag., New Territory, May, 1905, to Sept., 1906.

CLEVELAND, ROBT. ACHILLES.—Ed. Univ. Coll. (Lond.); M.R.C.S. (England); L.R.C.P. (Lond.); D.T.M.H. (Camb.); med. offr., Cayou dist., St. Kitts, 1890; ditto, Georgetown, Saint Vincent, 1892; dist. med. offr., Larnaca, Cyprus, 1894; ditto, Nicosia, 1895; ag. ch. med. offr., Cyprus, 1895 and 1900; mem. of municipal coms'n., Nicosia; passed colloquial standard exam. in mod. Greek.

CLIFFORD, HUGH CHARLES, C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1886; joined Perak service, 1883; passed in Malay, Feb., 1885; collr. land rev., Kuala Kangsar, Mar., 1885; on special service to Pahang, 1887; ag. gov't. there, 1887-8; supt., Ulu Pahang, 1889; ag. br. res. Pahang, Sept., 1890, to Mar., 1891, Apr. to Dec., 1891, and from Feb., 1893; took a leading part in suppression of Pahang rebellion, 1892; sec. to gov't., Selangor, Dec., 1894, but continued to act as res. of Pahang; led armed exp. into native states of Trénggánu and Kelantan to effect capture of fugitive rebel leaders, Mar.-June, 1895; res., Pahang, July, 1896; gov' of Br. North Borneo and Labuan, 1899 to 1901; br. res., Pahang, 1901; ag. col. sec., Trinidad and Tobago, Sept., 1903, confirmed, 14th Sept., 1904; adms'd. govt., 26th Mar. to 30th Aug., 1904, and from 1st Apr. to 23rd Oct., 1906.

CLIFT, HON. J. AUGUSTUS, K.C.—Called to the Bar, 1884; M.H.A. Newfoundland, 1889; speaker of assembly, 1891; clk. of assembly, 1893-97; electe to assembly, representing Twillingate dist., 1900; re-elected, 1904; min. of agric. and mines and mem. of cabinet, 1904.

CLIFTON, ROBERT CECIL, I.S.O. (1904).—B. 1854; landing waiter and postmr., Bunbury, W. Australia, 1873; 2nd clk., lands and survey dept., 1874; 2nd clk., customs, 1876; ch. clk., land and survey dept., Apr., 1880; under sec. for lands, 1891.

CLINCKETT, R. J.—Entered provost marshal's office, Barbados, as jun. clk. in 1869; acentnat. in 1870; acted as provost marshal from Feb. to Sept., 1879; again in 1880; resig. 1880; apptd. official assignee, 1st Oct., 1882; served as mem. of health and quarantine bds., from 10th Oct., 1899, to 6th May, 1901; acted as mast. in chancery, from Nov., 1901, to Apr., 1902; mem. of eduen. bd.; gov' of "Harrison" and "Queen" Colls. and Combermere schl.
CLUTSAM, S. H. O.—Called to bar, Bahamas, July, 1868; ag. sec. to bd. of educn., 1882; ag. stip. and circuit mag. in 1887; asst. clk. and sergt.-at-arms, house of assem., 1866; ch. clk., 1890.

COATES, ARTHUR ROBERT.—Ck., immigrn. dept., Fiji, 1889; inspr. ind. immigrn.; inspr. nat. lab., 1892; stip. mag., Savu Savu and Taviuni, 1900; provl. inspr., Macuata and Bua, 1901; agt. gen., immigrn. and supt. poly. depot, 1904.

COBHAM, C. DELAVAL, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1842; ed. Rugby, and Univ. Coll., Oxon; B.A. (honours), 1866; B.C.L., M.A., 1869; M.R.A.S. asst. comsnr., Larnaca, Cyprus, Oct. 1, 1878; comsnr. of Larnaca, Mar. 18, 1879; ag. ch. sec. to govt., on three occasions; ag. ch. collr. of customs, 1886; has edited a Bibliography of Cyprus, and "Excerpta Cypria," and translated Mariti's "Travels in Cyprus," the story of "Omm Haram," and Graziani's narrative of the siege of Nicosia and Famagusta.

COBLEY, WALTER HENRY, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1850; A.M.I.C.E.; Asst. engrnr., Natal rly., Feb., 1876; ag. maintenance engrnr., Jan., 1880, to 1881; maintenance engrnr., 1882; dist. engrnr., 1883; suptg. engrnr. of surveys and construction, 1897.

COCHRANE, C. S., M.I.C.E.—Grad. at Trin. Coll., Dub., 1868; was employed on rly. wks. in S. America, 1871-74; asst. engrnr. on the Cape Govt. rly., 1874; ag. dist. engrnr., 1877; engrnr. in the pub. wks. dept., Trinidad, 1878; engrnr. in charge of surveys, 1879.

COCHRANE, HON. THOMAS, H.A.E., M.P.—Ed. at Eton; served in 93rd Highlanders and Scots Guards; hon. major, 4th Batt. Argyll and Sutherland Highlanders; J.P.; Fife; D.L. for Renfrewshire; M.P. for North Ayrshire; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, June, 1895, to Feb., 1901; on active service in the S. African War, 1900; U.S. of S., Home Office, 1902.

COCKBURN, THE HON. SIR JOHN ALEX., K.C.M.G. (1900); M.D. (Lond.).—B. 1850; mem. of house of assem., S. Australia, 1884-1898; min. of educn., 1885, to June, 1887; premier and ch. sec., 1889-90; again min. of educn. and agricult., 1893; agt.-gen. for S. Australia in London, 1898-1901.

CODRINGTON, STEWART.—Cadet, S. Stlmta., Nov., 1898; ag. sub.-inspr. of schls., Malacca, May, 1900; ag. supt. Malay coll., Mar., 1900; ag. distr. offr., Malacca, Oct., 1900; distr. offr., Penang, Aug., 1902; ag. dist. offr., B. Mertajam, June, 1904.

COGHLAN, T. A., I.S.O. (1903).—B.1856; public service comsnr. of New S. Wales; ed. Sydney gram. schl.; apptd. to pub. wks. dept., 1873; assoc. mem. Inst. of Civil Engrns., 1882; asst. engrnr. harbours and rivers dept., 1884; govt. statistician, 1886; registr. of friendly societies, 1892; honry. fellow roy. statis. soc., 1893; mem. of pub. ser. bd., 1896; justice of the peace, 1896; roy. comsnr. to inquire into the working of the marine bd., 1896; roy. comsnr. to enquire into working of Sydney water supply and sewerage bds., 1897; supervised the N. S. Wales census of 1891; pres. of the confere. of Australian statisticians to determine the population of the colonies, and to arrange for a uniform census of Australasia, 1900; ag. agt.-gen. for N. S. Wales in London, Feb., 1905; author of "Wealth and Progress of N. S. Wales," "The Seven Colonies of Australasia," "Childbirth, a Study in Statistics," "Notes on the Financial Aspect of Australian Federation," "Report on the Eleventh Census of N. S. W.," "Treatise on the Deaths of Women in Childbirth," &c.
COHEN, HON. H. E.—Col. treas., N. S. Wales, Dec., 1877, to Dec., 1878; judge, metropolitan ct., May, 1881; min. of jus., Jan., 1883, to 1885; puisne judge, 1896.
COHEN, W. S.—Ed. at Clifton Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; 2nd class tripos, 1893; M.A.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; sec., immigrn. (temporary), O.R.C., 1902.

COKER, S. D. A.—Asst. clk., treasy., Gambia, May, 1873; 2nd landing waiter, customs, Aug., 1875; ast. clk., treasy., Feb., 1879; ch. clk., treasy., and P.O., Mar., 1883; ag. gov.'s clk.; clk. to leg. comr. and dep. coroner, June to Nov., 1889; cashier, Jan., 1892; in charge of the treasy., June to Nov., 1892.

COLE, FRED. EDWARD.—Ed. at Marlbro' Coll. and King's Coll., Lond.; called to the bar, Mid. Term., Feb., 1886; dist. comsnr., G. Coast Col., May, 1888; clk. of cts., St. Elizabeth, Jamaica, Nov., 1890; ag. res. mag., Apr., 1891; apptd. res. mag., St. Thomas, Jamaica, 1902.
COLE, G.—Inspr. of pub. schls., Bahamas, and sec. to the educn. bd., and J.P., Sept., 1882.

COLENBRANDER, HERMAN JAMES.—Ck. for immigrn., Zulu border agency, Lower Tugela div., Natal, Nov., 1881; dep. clk. of peace, Umsinga, Nov., 1887; clk. and interp. to R.M., Umvoti, Aug., 1889; clk. of the ct., Klip River div., 1895; ch. clk., Durban magistracy, 1901; second astt. mag., Durban div., 1902; J.P. for colony, 1904; astt. mag., Umlazi div. (Pinetown), 1905.

COLES, THE HON. SIR JENKIN, K.C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1842; Mem. of house of assem., S. Australia, 1875-8, and since 1881; comsnr. cr. lds., June, 1884, to Feb., 1885; June, 1887, to June, 1889; comsnr. P.W., Feb. to June, 1886; speaker house of assem., S. Australia, 1890.

COLL, ANTHONY MICHAEL.—Ed. Haileybury, and Exeter Coll., Oxon; M.A. and B.C.L., 1888; called to the bar, Inner Tem., Jan., 1887; pol. mag., Gibraltar, 1892; atty.-gen., 1901; K.C., 1902; ag. ch. just., and ag. col. sec., on various occasions.

COLLARD, WILLIAM ALFRED.—Ck. treasury, Oct., 1882; promoted to customs outdoor dept. as 6th examining officer, Cape Town, Sept., 1888; second class clk. and astt. acct. to paymaster-gen., treasury, Jan., 1889; priv. sec. to Hon. J. X. Merriman, treasurer, July, 1890; priv. sec. to the Hon. Sir J. Gordon Sprigg, treasurer, May, 1893; and prin. clk., treasury, Oct., 1893; astt. stamping comsnr., Nov., 1895, to June, 1896; priv. sec. to the Hon. Sir J. Gordon Sprigg, prime minister, Jan. 1896; chief clk., treasury, July, 1896; accounting officer, 1st July, 1898; deputy astt. treasurer, 1st July, 1900; astt. treas., Nov., 1903.

COLLENS, JAMES HENRY, V.D. (1902).—Principal, govt. training school, Port of Spain, Trinidad, 1878, and govt. statist., 1900; lieut. Trinidad volunteers, 1880; capt., 1885; major, 1902; elected on first bd. of directors, civil service widows' and orphans' fund, 1900; author of "Guide to Trinidad," and other works; ag. supt. govt. printing dept., Dec., 1891; ag. postmr.-gen., 1893, 1895, 1897, 1898; ag. inspr. of schls., 1899, 1900, 1901, 1902; inspr. of schls., Aug., 1903; commanded the Trinidad lt. inf. contingent at His Majesty's coronation, 1902.

COLLET, WILFRED, C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1856; ed. at Univ. Coll. schl., Lond.; LL.B., Lond., 1889; 1st cls. intermed., 1889, and final, 1903; provisional 2nd clk., native dept., Fiji, Jan., 1881; inspr. of immigrn. and astt. native comsnr., Jan., 1883; sec. W. Pacific high comsnr., Oct., 1884; dist. comsnr. Cyprus, 1897; also British deleg. of Evqaf, 1901; ag. rec.-gen., 1900-01, and in 1902 and 1904 col. sec., Br. Hond., 18th Oct., 1905; admstg. govt., 18th Oct., 1905, to 12th Aug., 1906.
COLLETT, S.—Apptd. after compet. exam., elk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to C.O., 14th Feb., 1906.
COLLIE, A.—Clt., accts. branch, repatriation dept., O.R.C., 14th July, 1902; examr. of accts. audit dept., 1st Dec., 1902; dist. examr. of accts., 1st July, 1905.

COLLIER, FREDERICK WILLIAM.—Third elk., gov't sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, Nov., 1869; 2nd elk., Dec., 1870; record elk., Jan., 1877; ch. elk. and sec. to the quarantine bd., Dec., 1879; has acted as asst. gov't sec., and elk. to the ct. of policy and the combined ct. on several occasions since July, 1880; postmr.-gen., 1888.

COLLINS, A. E. E.—Ed. at the City of Lond. schl. and Camb.; L. Rothschild scholar and foundation scholar of Trin. Coll., 1890; prizeman, 1891-2; 1st class classical tripus, 1893; B.A., 1893; M.A., 1899; apptd., after compet. exam., a elk. in office of S. of S. forCols., June, 1894; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir Edward) Wingfield, Mar., 1897; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Feb., 1898; 1st class elk., Nov., 1898; sec. of S.S. currency comtee., 1902-3.

COLLINS, HON. GEORGE THOMAS.—B. 1839; called to the bar, 1861; M.L.C. for Tamar, Tasmania, since 1895; chief sec., min. of defence (until federation) and min. of agric., 1899-1903; pres. of cent. bd. of health, 1900-3; col. and late comdg. offr. of Launceston Art'y., V.D.

COLLINS, JAMES ALLISON.—Asst. regsr., high cts., O.F.S., 21st June, 1879; representative to orphan master, 9th Feb., 1885; landdrost, Ladybrand, 19th Nov., 1887; ditto, Winburg, 9th Mar., 1894; under gov't sec., 23rd July, 1897; res. mag., Bloem., O.R.C., 17th Mar., 1900; asst. sec. to O.R.C. admstn. (afterwards asst. col. sec.), 1st Sept., 1901; master of the high ct., 20th Feb., 1903.

COLLISSON, ROBT. MUHRFEED, C.M.G. (1904).—B. 1852; retired lieut. R.N.; entered R.N., 1866; served in flying squadrons in 1868 and 1872; sub-lieut., June, 1872; served on Channel and Australian stations to 1876; lieut., June, 1876; apptd. to Victorian naval forces (Australia), Dec., 1878; comdr. Victorian naval forces, 1884; sec. for defence for Victoria, 1888; sec. to dept. of defence, Commonwealth of Aust., 1st Mar., 1901; now in England representing Commonwealth govt.
COLLISSON, P. L.—Ed. at King's Coll., London; 2nd div. elk., E. and A. dept., 1901; examr., col. and branch, Oct., 1905; examr. of accts., audit dept., O.R.C., Jan., 1906.
COLLS, ARTHUR STANLEY.—Ed. Rugby; asst. engineer, Midland Rly., 1883 to 1886; dist. engrnr., P.W.D., Ceylon, 1886; dist. engrnr., Pretoria dist., Transvaal, 1892; ch. engrnr. of buildings, 1905.

COLLYMORE, H. W.—Supernum. G.P.O., Barbados, Oct., 1876; ag. 3rd elk., 1878; 3rd elk., record branch, col. sec.'s office, Mar., 1879; 3rd elk., correspond. branch, Jan., 1881; ag. 2nd elk., 1883; ag. pub. librnr., 1884; 2nd elk. correspondence, branch, col. sec.'s office, July, 1884; ag. ch. elk., June, 1893, to Apr., 1894; Aug. to Dec., 1895, and July to Nov., 1896; sec. highways comsn., 1893-6; ch. elk., col. sec.'s office, and elk. of legis. coun., Jan., 1898.
COLMAN, EUGENE ERNEST.—B.A. Cantab.; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1902; ag. 4th mag., Sing., Oct., 1903; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1904; ag. 3rd mag., Sing., Mar., 1906.

COLMER, JOSEPH G., C.M.G. (1888).—B. 1856; priv. sec. to first high comsnr. for Canada, and sec. of emigr. dept. of Canadian govt. offices, Lond., 1880; apptd. sec. to office of high comsnr., 1881; acted as interim sec. to colonization bd. since its formation, Dec., 1888, and formally apptd. as such Nov., 1891; res. Mar., 1903.

COLOMB, SIR JOHN C. R., M.P., K.C.M.G. (1888), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1838; was M.P. for Bow and Bromley, 1886 to 1892; formerly capt., R.M.A., in which corps he served 1854-69; has been variously employed with naval, military, and auxiliary forces; author of many works and lectures on imperial defence and protection of commerce in war; received the thanks of gov. of Br. Columbia, 1877.
COLOMBO, 4th BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. REGINALD STEPHEN COPLESTON, D.D.—Formerly fellow and tutor, St. John's Coll., Oxford. Consec. Dec., 1875.
COLUMBIA, BRITISH, 2nd BISHOP, RIGHT REV. WILLIAM WILCOX PERRIN.—Trin. Coll., Oxon, B.A., 1870; M.A., 1873; vicar of S. Luke's, Southampton, 1881-93. Consec. 1893.
COLVILLE, J. W.—Clt., lands dept., Victoria, apptd. 1863; head of registry of that dept., Jan., 1878; sec. cent. bd. of health, May, 1885.

COMMISSIONG, TYRELL MILDMAY.—Ed. at S.E. College, Ramsgate; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., June, 1895; joined Grenada bar, Oct., 1895; ag. pol. mag. on several occasions, 1898 to 1905; pol. mag., W. Dist., 1st Aug., 1905.
COMMISSIONG, W. S., K.C.—Admitted to the bar, Grenada, 1867; escheator-gen., July, 1872; has acted on various occasions as atty.-gen. and as ch. just.; is an unofficial member of legis. coun.

CONDELL, CLAUDE F., B.es.L., F.R.G.S.—Ed. Edinburgh Acad., Stonyhurst Coll. and Paris Univ., honours, London Univ., matric.; certificat d'aptitude à l'enseignement des langues vivantes, Paris; cert. of regsn., column B, teachers' registn. coun.; lecturer on mod. languages and English literature, Paris, 1895; ed. dept. Malta, 1900; examr. in French and German, Malta R.A. exam.; mem. of bd. of examrs. for Malta civ. ser.; inspr. of schls., St. Lucia, 1905.
CONRAN, JOSEPH GEORGE.—Emigration agent at Madras, for Mauritius, 1st Jan., 1899.
CONROY, J. G., K.C.—Cent. dist. ct. judge, Newfoundland, 1880.
COOK, ALEXR.—Auditor Br. N. Borneo, Oct., 1880; treas. and aud.-gen., Aug., 1881; mem. of coun., June, 1883.
COOK, HENRY AUSTIN.—Clt. of wks., P.W.D., O.R.C., June, 1903.

COOK, WALLACE HAYNES.—Clt. Penal Settlement, Massaruni, Br. Guiana, Feb., 1881; steward, July, 1883; ag. sec. to poor law comsnrs. and super. of girls reformatory, Apr., 1890, to Jan., 1891, and May, 1895, to Feb., 1896; elk. of supplies prison dept. and sec. to bd. of prisons, Nov., 1890; supt. almshouse, Georgetown, June, 1892; amalgamated offices of supt. almshouse, and sec. to poor law comsnrs., May, 1899.

COOKE, EBENEZER.—Accountant (afterwards manager), English & Australian Copper Co., S. Australia, 1862-82; represented Flinders in Parlmt., 1875-82; J.P., 1875; mem. of various select comtees.; chmn., roy. comsn. on finance, 1880-2; mem. roy. comsn. on educn., 1882-3; 1st comsnr. of audit, 1882.

COOKE, JOHN HAMILTON, B.A., M.B., B.Ch.—Trin. Coll., Dub., honours in classics, 1882-3; in anatomy, 1886-6; sen. house surg. Western gen. dispensary, Marylebone, Lon., 1890-1; med. offr. dist. C., Dominica, 1891; dist. St. Kitts and Nevis, 1892; Yaws hosp., 1892-7; mem. bd. of health, 1893; surg.-cap. of local forces, 1898; dist. med. offr., Antigua, Jan., 1905.
COOKE, WILLIAM ERNEST, M.A., F.R.A.S.—Cadet, Adelaide observatory, 1878; B.A. Adel. Univ., 1883; asst. astronomer, Adel. obser., 1884; M.A., Adel. Univ., 1889; govt. astronr., W.A., 1896.

COOKMAN, NATHANIEL GEORGE, L.R.C.S.I., L.R.C.P.I.—Med. offr., Portsmouth dist., Dominica, and surg. hosp., 1892-6; comsnr., regir., mag., sen. med. offr., Virgin Is., 1896; mem. gen. leg. coun., Leewards; med. offr. and mag., dist. D., Dominica.
COOM, JOHN.—M.I.C.E., engnr., N. Zealand, P.W.D., 1876 to 1881; res. engnr. working rlys., 1881 to 1897; inspecting engnr. for rlys., 1897 to 1899; ch. engnr., 1899.
COOMB, EPHRAIM HENRY.—Mem. House of Assembly, S. Australia, 1901; chmn. of comtees., 1905.

COOMBS, W. H., F.R.G.S., F.R.A.S.—Comdr. Roy. Navy; assoc. inst. C.E.; entered R.N. 1866; served on the Mediterranean, N. Amer., W. Indies, China, and other stations; lieut., 1873; served under Indian govt. 1876 to 1884 as asst. surr. marine surveys, chiefly on harbours, Madras, Colombo, Chittagong, etc.; passed as interp. in Hindi, Urdu, and Persian, 1877-8; ret. as comdr. R.N. 1894; apptd. by S. of S. for Colts. prot. of imigrts., Trinidad, Feb., 1896; M.L.C. and J.P. for the isld.

COOPER, FRANCIS ALFRED, C.M.G. (1901), M.I.C.E.—B. 1860; fellow Sanitary Inst., Gt. Britain; ed. at Rossall and Loughborough gram. schl., asst. to Mr. James Mansergh, C.E. Westminster, 1879; sanit. survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1887 to 1890; survr. to sanitary bd., 1888 to 1892; inspr. of buildings, P.W.D., 1888 to 1890; ag. asst. engnr., 1889; ag. survr.-gen., 1889; ag. asst. survr.-gen., 1889 to 1890; res. engnr. in charge of water and drainage dept., 1890 to 1892; dir. of pub. wks., Oct., 1891; mem. exec. and legis. couns., Mar., 1892; dir. pub. wks., Ceylon, 1897; pres. of sanitary bd., Hong Kong, 1895-7; reed. thanks of govt. for services in settlement of claims under the Taipingsham resumption ordnec. 1895; and from S. of S. for Colts. for services during the plague, 1896; M.L.C.; comsnr. of rds., waterwks., engnr., and mem. of harb. bd. and mun. coun., Colombo, 1897.
COOPER, SIR POPE ALEXANDER, K.T. (1904).—Atty.-gen. Queensland, Dec., 1880; northern judge, 5th Jan., 1883; 1st puisne judge, sup. ct.; ch. justice, 1903.
COPELAND, THE HON. H.—Mem. legis. assem., N.S. Wales; sec. for lands in Dibbs' ministry, 1891-4; agt.-gen. for N.S.W. in London, 1900.

CORBET, FREDERICK H. M.—Hon. cor. philos. soc., etc.; priv. sec. to Mr. Justice Lawrie at various periods from 1885 to 1888; libr. Colombo museum, June, 1886; hon. sec. to gen. comtee., Ceylon, for Paris Exposn., 1889; ag. sec., cent. irrigation bd., June, 1890, to May, 1891; resig. libr.ship., Dec., 1893; has represented col. at Imp. Inst. as hon. exec. offr., since July, 1893; called to the bar Gray's Inn, Jan., 1897; reed. jub. gold med. of Ceylon, 1897.

CORDEAUX, CAPT. HARRY EDWARD SPILLER, C.B. (1904), C.M.G. (1902).—Ed. at Cheltenham Coll., and St. John's Coll., Camb.; minor scholarship, 1889; class. tripso, 1892; passed in Hindustani and Persian, 1895; Somali, 1899; B.A., 1892; entd. Indian Army, 1894; probationer, Bombay polit. dept. as 4th astt. polit. res., Aden, Apr., 1898; asst. res., Berbera, July, 1898; vice-consul, Berbera, Oct., 1898; consul, Nov., 1902; admstd. govt. of Somaliland Prot., Nov., 1900, to Mar., 1902; Oct., 1902, to Aug., 1903; Mar. to June, 1904; served in operations against the Somali Mullah, 1902-04, as polit. offr. (ment. in despa., medal and 3 clasps); dep. comsnr., Somaliland Prot., 1904; admstd. govt., June, 1905; comsnr. and comdr.-in-chief, 15th May, 1906.

CORK, PHILIP CLARKE, C.M.G. (1904).—B. 1884; clk. imigrn. dept., Jamaica, May, 1875; Hindust. interpreter to the agt.-gen., 1876; inspr. of imigrts., 1877; prot. of imigrts., Grenada, 1881; ditto, Jamaica, 1884; comsnr. under the Kingston Improv't. Laws, 1894; nomtd. mem. of legis. coun., 1896; asst. col. sec., 1897; col. sec., Br. Hond., 1901; admstd. the govt. in 1903 and 1905; admstd., St. Lucia, Oct., 1905.

CORK, WM.—Clk. and locker, Jamaica, Aug., 1868; 3rd class asst. collr. of taxes, Jan., 1869; 3rd class collr. of taxes and parochial treas., 1870; 2nd class collr. of taxes, 1879; now 1st class collr.

CORNEY, BOLTON GLANVILL, I.S.O. (19.4).—B. 1851; ed. at Fontainebleau, in London, and at Schwerin; St. Thomas's Hosp., 1870; M.R.C.S., Eng., 1874; entered the col. service in 1877 as govt. med. offr., Fiji; also health offr., Suva, 1877; med. offr. for the imigrn. dept., 1881; agt.-gen. of imigrn., Jan., 1885, to Mar., 1887; M.L.C. and of native regulatory bd., 1885; ch. med. offr., June, 1887; M.L.C., June, 1888; ag. sup. lun. asyl., 1887; ag. rec-gen., Jan. to Mar., 1892; comsnr. to inquire into decrease of native populn., 1893; comsnr. to inquire into misgov't. of Prov. Taiyevu, May, 1895; in 1897 rec. thanks of coral comtee. of roy. soc. for assistance to the geolog. expdn. to Funafuti, Ellice Islds.; then from the roy. soc. for renewed services to expdn., 1898; ag. agt. gen. for imigrn., Dec., 1902, to Mar., 1903.

CORNWALL, C.F.—Graduated B.A. Magdalen Coll., Camb., 1857; called to bar of Inner Temp., 1861; many years mem. of legislature of col. of Br. Columbia; on confederation of that prov. with Canada in 1871, summoned by H.M. to the Senate of the Dominion; lieut.-gov. of Br. Columbia, 1881-6.
CORNWELL, G. F.—Called to the bar (Line's Inn), 1859; one of H.M.'s counsel for Gibr., 1885; coroner and J.P., 1895; inspr. of schools, 1902.
COSTIGAN, THE HON. JOHN.—Mem. of Canadian house of commons and of privy coun.; min. of inland rev., 1885; sec. of state for Canada, Dec., 1892; min. of marine and fisheries, 1894 to 1896.
COTTLE, H. C.—2nd astt. govt. printer, Ceylon, Nov., 1885; astt. govt. printer, 1888; agt.-govt. printer on several occasions, 1890-1904; agt.-contr. of govt. stores, 1904; govt. printer, 1906.

COULL, W.—Enrolled law student, Leeward Is., 1875; clk. col. sec.'s office, Leeward Is., Mar., 1878; acted on occasions as ch. clk. and as clk. of the Antigua and federal exec. and legis. couns.; dep. coroner for district A, Oct., 1880; ag. mag., dist. B, Antigua, Jan. to Apl., 1882; mag. and coroner for district E, Roseau, and regist.-gen. of births, marriages, and deaths Dominica, Aug., 1882; mem. of the bd. of poor law guardians; a visiting justice of the gool, and nominated M.L.A., 1883; escheator-gen., 1885; mem. exec. coun., 1895; offl. mem. legis. coun., 1895; in charge of out-door poor relief, 1898.
COULTER, ROBERT MILLER, M.D.—Deputy of postmr.-gen. of Canada, Aug., 1897.
COUNSEL, E. A.—Secretary for lands and surveyor-gen., Tasmania, 1889.

COURTNEY, JOHN MORTIMER, C.M.G. (1897), I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1838; asst. sec. and accntnt. to the treasry., Canada, 1869; dep. min. of finance, dep. rec.-gen., and sec. to treasry., 1878; mem. of comsn. of inquiry into the civ. ser. of Canada, 1891.

COURTNEY OF PENWITH, 1st Baron (created 1906), Rt. HON. LEONARD H. COURTNEY, P.C. (1889).—B. 1832; ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A. (2nd wrangler) 1855; called to the bar; Linc.'s Inn, 1858; repsntd. Liskeard, Dec., 1876, to Dec., 1885, when he was returned for the co. div. in which that borough was merged until 1900; parly. under-sec. for the home dept., Dec., 1880; parly. under-sec. for the cols., Aug., 1881; sec. to the treasry., May, 1882; resig., 1885; chmn. of ways and means, 1886-92.
COUZENS, CHARLES.—Apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. messenger C.O., Jan., 1889; promoted 2nd cl. mess., Nov., 1899; King's mess., 1st Feb., 1902.
COVEY, ARTHUR.—Solr., 1892; dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 1900; mentioned in Ashanti despatches; asst. col. sec., 1902; invalided, 1903; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1904.

COWAN, JAMES.—Clk. to res. mag., York, W. Australia, 1864; clk. to pol. mag., Perth, 1870; clk. to legis. coun., 1874; regis. and master sup. ct., &c., Nov., 1878; also curator of intestate estates, Nov., 1883; R.M., Guildford, Mar., 1886; govt. res., N. dist., 1889; pol. mag., Perth, 1890; mag., Perth local ct., 1897.

COWPER, SYDNEY, C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1854; clk. in office of comsnrs. exhibn. of 1851, in 1871, in educn. dept. S. Kensington, 1875; 3rd class clk. in col. sec.'s office, Capetown, 1879; priv. sec. to premier, 1880-1; 2nd class clk., 1881; priv. sec. to premier, 1884-5; 1st class clk., 1885; sec. to prime min., 1887; ret. on abolition of office, 1891; prin. clk., dept. of agricult., 1892; sec. to premier (office re-established), 1897; ret., 1904; J.P. for Cape and Wynberg; civ. ser. examiner (1890-1901), and art trustee; Basuto med. (1880); major, 2nd in commd., 4th batt., Cape Peninsula regt. (1901); major, P.A.O., Cape Field Artillery, 1903.

COX, CHARLES T., C.M.G. (1903).—B. 1858; Govt. c.'s office, Br. Guiana, July 1874; sen. clk., 1883; ch.clk. and sec. to the quarantin bd., 1888; sec. to the royal comsn. on adminstr.-gen.'s dept. of B. Guiana, 1882; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1899; compiled "Index to Laws of Br. Guiana," 1887 (2nd edition, 1891), also "Some Notes for Govt. Officers," 1894; asst. govt. sec., and clk. to ct. of policy and combined ct., 1889; also clk. of exec. coun., 1892; ag. govt. sec., in 1893, 1894, 1895, and 1898, with seat in exec. coun. and legislature; chmn. of comtee. on pensions, 1898; ag. audr.-gen. on several occasions; admnstr. of St. Kitts and Nevis, 1899-1904; ag. gov., Leeward Is., July to Oct., 1903, and May to Sept., 1904; govt. sec. and ag. gov. B. Guiana, Sept., 1904, and Apr. to Oct., 1906.

COX, E. A. W.—Cadet, Sarawak civ. ser., Mar., 1885; asst. res., May, 1891; of Lunder, June, 1896; res., 2nd class, Mar., 1898, of Trusan; again May, 1899; in charge of Lawas, 16th Apl., 1905, in addition to Trusan.

COX, HUGH BERTRAM, C.B. (1902).—B. 1861; ed. at Westminster schl. and Chr. Ch., Oxford; 1st class classical mods., 1881; 1st class final class. schl., 1883; M.A. and B.C.L., 1884; called to the bar, 1885, and joined S. Wales circuit; assisted the atty.-gen. (Sir R. Webster) in parly. and offl. work, 1886-1897; jun. counsel to the treasry. in peergage cases, 1892, and to H.M. Customs, 1896; employed in connection with the Br. Guiana bndry. question, and as one of the jun. counsel in the preparations of the Br. case in the arbitrn.; legal asst. under-sec., C.O., Nov., 1897.

COX, SIR WILLIAM HENRY LIONEL, KT. BACH. (1896).—B. 1844; substitute procureur and advoc.-gen., Mauritius, Feb., 1880; puisne judge of the sup. ct., Aug., 1880; procureur and advoc.-gen., Aug., 1886; ch. justice, S. Sttlmts, 1893; ret., 1906.

CRAIGIE-HALKETT, MONTAGU C. — Ag. priv. sec. to gov. Falkland Islds., Mar., 1897; writer, col. sec.'s off., Apl., 1897; priv. sec. to admnstr., Apl., 1897; clk., col. sec.'s off., Oct., 1897; ag. priv. sec. to gov., Nov., 1898; treasy. clk., 1899.

CRAWFORD, HENRY LEIGHTON, C.M.G. (1906).—Ed. at Clifton and Corp. Christi Coll., Camb. (scholar); writer, Ceylon service, 1877; pol. mag., Kalpitiya 1883; ditto, Gampola, 1883; office asst. to govt. agt., W.Prov.,1884; 2nd asst., col. sec. and clk.of legis. coun., 1890; prin. asst. col. sec., Apr., 1896; comsnr. under "The Buddhist Temporalities Ordee," 1889," Nov., 1900; ag. govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Nov., 1901; ag. prin. collr. of cust., 10th Nov., 1902; offr. of class I., 4th May, 1903; ag. treas., June, 1904; govt. agt., S. Prov., Sept., 1904; ditto W. Prov., Dec., 1905; ag. col. sec., May, 1906; govt. agt., W. Prov., Aug., 1906.

CRAWFORD, WALLACE.—4th clk., immgrn. dept., Br. Guiana, Sept., 1880; 3rd clk., Aug., 1884; 2nd clk., Oct., 1885; acted as sub-agt. of immgrn., June, 1883, to June, 1884, and Oct., 1885, to Jan., 1886; Sept., 1886, to July, 1888; July, 1889, to Mar., 1890; ag. emigrn. agt., Barbados, Oct., 1890, to Nov., 1891; ag. sen. sub-agt., July, 1892; awarded 1st certif. for proficiency in Hindustani, Mar., 1885.

CREAGH, C. V., C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1842; barrister-at-law (Mid. Tem., called May, 1892); was asst. dist. supt. of the 1st class in the Punjaub pol.; in 1866, passed the highest pol. examn., and the 2nd standard in Oriental languages; in 1867 raised the Sikh pol. for the Hong Kong govt.; dep. supt., 1867; J.P. in 1868; sheriff in 1874; received thanks of govt. for services in typhoon, Sept., 1874; acted as supt. of pol. in 1869-70 and 1877-78; ag. A.D.C. in 1878; supt. of fire brigade, 1878; acted as pol. mag. and coroner, 1878 to 1880; passed with credit the six exams. in Chinese colloquial prescribed by the govt.; in 1879, mem. of comtees. of public gambling, and on the defence of Hong Kong; arbitrator under the opium ordinance (7 of 1879); asst. Br. res., judge, and mem. of state coun., Perak, Mar., 1883; gov. and comr.-in-ch. and ch. judicialoffr., Br. N. Borneo, Mar., 1888, to 1895; also gov. Labuan, 1889 to 1895, when he ret.

CREASY, LEONARD, M.I.C.E.—Pioneer offr., Ceylon, 1874; suptdng. offr., 1877; dist. engrnr., Feb., 1886; prov. engrnr. N. Prov., July, 1890; financial and office asst., Nov., 1894; prov. engrnr. E. Prov., Jan., 1895; ditto, Prov. Uva, May, 1895; on deputation to Seychelles, Nov. to Dec., 1896; prov. engrnr., Cent. Prov. Kandy, Sept., 1897; ag. dir. pub. wks., 15th Mar., 1903.
CREED, HENRY.—Apptd., after exam., 3rd class messenger in C.O., July, 1900; 2nd class, 1st Feb., 1901.

CRESWELL, MARGARET S.—Apptd. postmistress of Gibraltar and supt. of govt. telegraphs, Imp. P.O., Sept., 1877, as a mark of the estimation of services of her late father, the dep. postmstr.-gen. at Gib.; transf'd. to col. gov't. in Jan., 1886, when the col. assumed the control of the local P.O.

CRESWELL, CAPT. WILLIAM ROOKE, C.M.G. (1897).—Joined R.N., Dec., 1865; lieut., 1873; for service in action at Laroot River (wounded); engaged in suppression of slave trade on African coast, 1875-7; invalided, and ret. from Navy; apptd. to S. Australian naval force, 1885; comdr., 1891; post. capt., 1895; naval comdt., 1893-1900; commanded H.M.C.S. "Protector" to China during Boxer outbreak, 1900; naval comdt. of Queensland, 1900-4; dir. of Commonwealth naval forces, 1904.

CREWE, COL. CHAS. PRESTON, C.B. (1900).—B. 1855; served in Cape M.R., 1878-81; M.L.A. for E. Griqualand, 1899; M.L.A. for Alloa North and col. sec., Feb., 1904; raised and was major commanding Border Horse Regt., 1900; promoted to lieut.-col., May, 1900; colonel, 1901; in command of western div. of Cape, Nov., 1901, to termination of hostilities; ret. from Cape Colony forces, 31st Dec., 1902.

CROFTON, FRANCIS BLAKE.—B.A. Trin. Coll., Dublin, 1863; provincial librarian, Nova Scotia, Jan., 1883; author of "The Major's Big-Talk of Stories," "Haliburton, the Man and the Writer," "Hairbreadth Escapes of Major Mendax," "For Closer Union"; a vice-pres. of the internat. library conf., 1897.

CROFTON, RICHARD HAYES.—Ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A. class. tripos, 1901; clk., col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., 1st July, 1901; clk., audit dept., Hong Kong, 25th Aug., 1903; ag. local auditor, 14th Feb. to 22nd Dec., 1904; ch. clk., col. sec.'s off., 11th Mar., 1905; dir., widows and orphans' fund, 15th Feb., 1906.

CROOK, ALFRED H., B.A.—Ed. at Wesley Coll., Dublin; honorman and gold medallist. Trin. Coll., Dublin; M.A.; F.R.G.S.; asst. mast., Wesley Coll., Dublin; junr. asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, Oct., 1902; lecturer in biology, Hong Kong Coll. of Med.

CROOK, JOHN ROWLAND, A.M.I.C.E.—Entered Crystal Pal. Engng. Schl., 1881; asst. to F. F. Griffith, C.E. (consulting sanitary engr.), 1883; supt. of drawing office, C. P. Engng. Schl., 1888; gained 1st prize for civ. engng., City of Lon. Coll., 1889; asst. engr. water and drainage dept., Hong Kong, 1891; in charge of new sewerage wks., city of Victoria, 1891-94; sanitary survr., 1892; exec. engr. P.W.D., 1893; and water authority during absence of dir. of pub. wks., 1893-94.

CROSS, J. W.—Clk., Zulu interp., and sub-distributor of stamps in the res. mag.'s office, Ixopo, Natal, 23rd Oct., 1876; dep. clk. of the peace for Ixopo, 2nd Mar., 1877; and on the 3rd Mar., 1879, apptd. in command of the Ixopo, Natal, native foot contingent, 800 strong; and subsequently transf'd. to command of the mounted contingent during the Zulu war; acted on several occasions as res. mag. of Ixopo; clk. to R.M., Weenen, July, 1886; admtr. of native law, Jagadi's location, 1889; mag., Lower Tugela div., 1894; R.M., Alfred div., 1895; mag., Lions River div., 1898; Umvoti div., 1904.
CROSSKILL, HERBERT.—Dep. prov. sec., Nova Scotia, 1868-78; again since 1882.
CROUCHER, F., M.B.—House surg., gen. Hosp., Singapore, 1893; col. surg., Malacca, Jan.,

CROWTHER, FRANCIS G.—Apptd. draughtsman, P.W.D., G. Coast, July, 1888; inspr. of schls., Aug., 1901; mem. bd. of ed., Jan., 1902; trav. comsnr., Apr., 1902; compiled statement of govt. lands, July to Sept., 1902; ag. dir. of educ. and sec. to bd. of educ., Nov., 1902, to Apr., 1903; trav. comsnr., Dec., 1903; ag. regis. of deaths, Apr., 1904; trav. comsnr., July, 1904; ag. dir. of educ., June, 1904.

CRUISE, H. R.—Ag. 4th clk., col. sec.'s off., Leeward Is., Feb. to May, 1904; ag. overseer of roads, May, 1904; ag. 3rd outdoor offr., treasy., May, 1904, to Feb., 1905; ag. 1st clk., P.O., Feb. to Apr., 1905; apptd. 4th indoor offr., treasy., Apr., 1905; atty.-gen.'s clk., Aug., 1905.

CUDDDEFORD, WM.—Employed on financial mission in Bahamas, May, 1885, to May, 1886; supervisor of customs, G. Coast, Jan., 1887; local auditor, Lagos, under C. and A.G., London, Dec., 1889; ditto, S. Leone and Gambia, Sept., 1893; auditor, Windward Is., May, 1896.

CUMBERLAND, CHAS. RUSSELL, B.A. OXON.—Entd. Ceylon civ. ser., 4th Oct., 1889; attached to the secretariat, 1st Apr., 1891; pol. mag., Balapitiya, 29th Sept., 1893; ditto, Panadure, 16th Dec., 1895; offr. of cls. IV., 15th May, 1898; dist. judge, Kegalla, 13th Dec., 1899; asst. govt. agt., Matale, 15th Aug., 1900; on special duty, camp for prisoners of war, Diyatalawa, 3rd Dec., 1900; offr. of cls. III., 9th June, 1901; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1st July, 1902; asst. govt. agt., Matale, 30th Oct., 1903; has held numerous ag. appts.
CUNLIFFE-OWEN, EDWARD, C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1847; for services in connection with the Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886.

CUNNINGHAM, J. FRANCIS, F.R.G.S., F.Z.S.—Ed. King's Coll., Lond., and part.; sec. to B.C. Africa adminstr., 24th Sept., 1892; orgnsd. printg. dept., and was apptd. editor of offl. gazette, June, 1894; served as vol. in campaigns against Zaraifi, etc., 1895 (recd. Central African medal); won gold medal offered by Zombie rifle club, 1897, and the Edwards Cup, 1898; ch. Br. comsnr. for delimitation of Anglo-Portuguese boundary in B.C. Africa, 24th May, 1899; priv. sec. to Sir H. Johnston's spec. miss. to Uganda, 24th Aug., 1899; passed exams. at Mid. Temp. and admitted a law student, 29th June, 1901; admitted to Gray's Inn, 1st July, 1901; sec. to Uganda adminstr., 1st Apr., 1901; apptd. judl. offr., 19th July, 1900; carried on duties of ch. judl. offr. and regis. in Uganda, 10th Apr., 1900, to 19th Dec., 1900; acted at headquarters for spec. comsnr. during his tour of inspection, 28th May to 8th June, 1900; and during the dep. comsnr.'s absence with Nandi expdtn., 9th Jan. to 16th Feb., 1901; present post, sec. to Uganda adminstr.; registr.-gen. of marriages, 1st Nov., 1902; joined Uganda vol. reserve, 1903; elected sec. of corps (company comdr.), 24th Jan., 1905; won annual championship and cup, 24th Mar., 1905; author of "Uganda and its Peoples," "Notes on the Anthropology and Ethnology of Indigenous Races," 1905.

CUNYNGHAME, SIR PERCY F., BART.—B. 1867; cadet, Sarawak civ. ser., July, 1886; asst. res. of Trusan, May, 1890; res. (2nd class), Mar., 1896; lieut. 3rd battn. Middlesex Rifles, May, 1897; capt., Oct., 190; ag. res., Limbang, May, 1899; res., 2nd class, Upper Rejang, Aug., 1901; commdt., Sarawak Rangers, July, 1903; res., 1st div., and mem. of sup. coun., May, 1904.

CURLEWIS, JOHN STEPHEN, B.A., LL.B. (Cape).—Cape civ. serv., 1883-1888; advocate, sup. ct., Cape Colony, 12th Oct., 1887; advocate, high ct. (late S.A.R.), May, 1888; counsel for Imperial govt. in arbitn. with late S.A.R. re Asiatic question, 1895; ag. judge of high ct. (late S.A.R.), 15th Apr., 1899, to 31st Jan., 1901; pres. of crim. ct. for Witwatersrand, 2nd Nov., 1899, to 31st Jan., 1900; puisne judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, 1st Sept., 1903.
CURLING, C. NAPIER.—Apptd. res. just. and collr. of Elenthua, Bahamas, Aug., 1895; ch. registr., G. Coast, 12th Jan., 1899; dist. consmr., Mar., 1899; ch. registr. and sheriff, 1901.
CURMI, TANCRED.—Asst. supt. of pol., Malta, 1890; senr. asst., 1902; supt., 1903.

CURRIE, SIR DONALD, G.C.M.G. (1897), K.C.M.G. (1881), C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1825; chmn. of the Union-Castle Mail Packets Co. In July, 1876, at the time of the negotiations between the Earl of Carnarvon and Pres. Brand, he assisted by his good offices in bringing about the success of these negotiations, and thus ending the long dispute between the Br. Govt. and the Orange Free State in reference to the diamond fields; formerly M.P. for Perthshire.

CUSACK-SMITH, SIR BERRY, K.C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1859; ed. at Eton; barrister, Middle Temp., 1884; law scholarship, 1883; formerly lieut. 1st V.B. Essex regt.; apptd. H.M. Consul at Samoa, Jan., 1890, and H.M. dep. consmr. for the W. Pacific at Samoa, May, 1890; proceeded in H.M.S. "Curacoa" to Union Group to report on land claims, Aug., 1892; dep. consmr. for the Samoan and Union Groups, and authorised to perform the duties of res. in the Union Group, Oct., 1892; special judicial consmr., Apr., 1893; apptd. to license and superintend employment of Gilbert Is. natives in Samoa, Jan., 1894; consul-gen., Valparaiso, 1897.

CUSCADEN, W. A.—Sen. Sophister, Trin. Coll., Dub.; capt. 4th Roy. Dub. Fus.; asst. inspr. of constab., G. Coast, 1879; civil consmr., Tacquah, 1881; ch. inspr. of pol., Singapore, 1883; asst. supt. of pol., Penang, 1884; ag. pol. supt., Malacca, 1889, and July, 1897; Singapore, 1891, and Apr., 1893; asst. supt. of pol., P.W., 1893; supt. pol., Malacca, July, 1897; ag. supt. pol., Singapore, Apr., 1898; ag. inspr. gen. pol. S.S. Mar., 1901, to Aug., 1902, and Nov., 1903, to Mar., 1904; supt. of pol., Sing., Aug., 1902.
CUTHBERT, SIR HENRY, M.L.C., K.C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1829; min. of justice, Victoria, 1886-90; solr.-gen., 1894.

DA'ETT, JOHN.—Ed. at King's Coll., Lond.; asst. draughtsman, P.W.D., Jamaica, Jan., 1873; ch. ditto, Mar., 1875; dist. engnr., N. (now W.) Dist., Jan., 1883; engnr., Kingston Dist., 1886; asst. dir. pub. wks., Jamaica, 10th Mar., 1902.

DALE, CHARLES ERNEST.—Ed. at Greenwich and King's Coll., London; fell. soc. accnts. and auditors, fell. roy. statistical soc.; apptd. dir. customs Niger Coast Protec., Jan., 1895; asst. postmr.-gen., Apr., 1896; ag. dir.-gen. customs and postmr.-gen., Feb. to Sept., 1896, Oct., 1897, to June, 1898, Oct., 1899, to Feb., 1901; Dec., 1900, to July, 1901; treasr. Southern Nigeria, Feb., 1898; ag. sec., Feb. to April, 1900; Jan. to Apr., 1903, dir. pub. officers' guarantee fund, Apr., 1900; ag. treas., Lagos, Sept., 1904.

DALE, H. E.—Ed. at St. Paul's Schl. and at Oxford; class. schol., Balliol Coll., 1894; Hartford school, 1895; Craven schol., 1895; Ireland schol., 1896; 1st class mods., 1896; 1st class lit. hum., 1898; B.A., 1898; Fell. New Coll., 1898; Derby schol., 1899; M.A., 1901; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd class clk., C.O., Oct., 1898; sent on special mission to Br. Hond., Nov., 1903.

DALE, HAROLD ROSS.—Entered atty.-gen.'s office, Cape, Dec., 1879; 2nd class clk., July, 1883; ch. clk. to solr.-gen., Grahamstown, Mar., 1885; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., Nov., 1886; J.P. for dist. of Albany; reger. of sup. ct. and taxing offr., 1st June, 1901.

DALLAS, FRANCIS HENRY.—Ed. Univ. Coll. School; asst. sec. Penang municipality, 1895; ag. sec. May, 1896; Sarawak civ. sec.; ag. editor "Sarawak Gazette," and manager, Sarawak gov't. printing office, 1897; ag. treas. and clk., supreme coun., June, 1902; treas. and recorder of sup. coun., 1903; mem. of comte. of adminstr., 1903-4.

DALTON, THE REV. CANON JOHN NEALE, M.A., C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1839; was gov. to T.R.H. Princes Albert Victor and George of Wales, and ag. chaplain to H.M.S. "Bacchante"; chaplain in ord. to Her Majesty, 1886; canon of Windsor, 1885.
DALY, SIR MALACHY BOWES, K.C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1836; Lieut.-Governor of Nova Scotia, 1890.
DALY, THE HON. THOMAS MAYNE, K.C.—Mem. for Selkirk, Manitoba, in Dominion house of commons; min. of interior, Canada, 1892-4.

DANAVALL, JOHN MARTIN.—Barrister, Mid. Temp., 1877; ag. solr.-gen., Leeward Is., 1883; again in 1884; ag. mag. Dist. C, 1883, and again in 1885; 2nd puisne judge, Leeward Is., June, 1888; also dep.-judge of the vice-admiralty et al., 1889.

DANDY, JOSEPH HENRY.—Ed. Oakes Inst., Liverpool, certif. as inspr. of nuisances and sanitary inspr. from sanitary inst., 1896; 1st cl. advanced hygiene, S. Kensington, 1897; surveying (1st cl.) and certif. of sanitary knowledge, Victoria Univ., 1898; certif. schl. of hygiene, Univ. Coll., 1898; practical bacteriology, Univ. Coll., 1899; mem. of san. inst., 1900; san. inspr., Liverpool, chief sanitary inspr., Hong Kong, June, 1899.

DANE, ROBT., M.R.L.S., L.S.A. (1887), L.R.C.P. (1889).—House physician, Middlesex hosp., 1890; house surg., gen. hosp., Singapore, June, 1890; col. surg., Prov. Wellesley, Mar., 1893; col. surg., Sing., March, 1901; ag. col. surg. res., Sing., May, 1905; mem. of S. S. med. coun., July, 1905.

DANIEL, ROWLAND MORTIMER.—Ed. at St. Edmund's, Canterbury; 2nd Lieut., Glam. artil. militia, 1893; certif. of musk., Hythe; capt. and instr. of gunnery, 1898; seconded sub.-inspr., B.S.A. pol., Div. I. (now Bech. Prot. pol.), 1898; served through S. African war, 1900-02, including siege of Mafeking; ment. in desps.; brig. sig. offr. and staff capt., 1900-01; ag. asst. consmr., S. Bech. Prot., 1902; asst. res. mag. at Serowe, 1903; ag. asst. consmr., N. Prot., 1904.
DANIELL, HUMPHREY A.—Ed. at Eton; served with Hampshire regt. in S. Africa, 1901-02 (medal and 5 clasps); asst. dist. offr., Somaliland, Feb., 1906.

DANSEY, CLAUDE EDWARD MARJORIBANKS.—Ed. at Wellington; lieut., Lancas. Fusiliers; served during Matabeleland rebellion, 1896, with Matabele mounted pol. (medal); sub. comlt., B. N. Borneo constab., during operations against rebels under Mohamed Salleh, May, 1899, to Jan., 1900 (medal); commanded squadron 1st African Light Horse during South African War, 1900-2 (2 medals and 6 clasps); staff-lieut. for intell. and A.D.C. to G.O.C., Harrismith dist., S. Africa, 1902-4; specially mentd. by gov. of B. N. Borneo for services during expdh. in interior, and mentd. in desp., S. Africa, 1900; asst. dist. offr., Somaliland, 4th Nov., 1901.
DARLEY, Cecil W., I.S.O. (1903).—Engnr.-in-ch. for harbours and river navig. and water supply, N. S. Wales, 1891.

DARLEY, Sir Fred., Matthew, P.C. (1905), G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1897), K.T. Bach. (1887).—B. 1830; Ed. Dungannon Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A., Hon. LL.D., T.C., 1903; barrister, Ireland, 1853, N. S. Wales, 1862, Queensland, 1886; Q.C., 1878; M.L.C., N. S. Wales, 1868-1886; vice-pres., exec. coun., N. S. Wales, 1881-2-3; ch. just., N. S. Wales, 1886; lieut.-gov., N. S. Wales, 1891; acted as such six times for periods amounting in all to nearly 3 years, besides frequently as deputy for different governors; apptd. one of the comsrs. to inquire into South African War, 1902.
DARNLEY, E. R.—Scholar of Trin. Coll., Camb., 1893; of Lond. Univ., 1897; B.A. (sixth wrangler) and B.Sc., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd class elk., C.O., 11th Oct., 1898.
DARTNELL, Major-Gen. Sir J. G., K.C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1881).—B. 1838; late of the 27th and 86th regts.; comdt. of the volr. and mounted pol., Natal, 1874; ret., 1903.
DASHWOOD, Chas. James.—Govt. res. and judge of the N. territory of S. Australia, 1892-1895; mem. H. of Assem., S. Australia, 1887-92; crown solr., 1905.

DAVIDSON, Andrew, M.D., F.R.C.P. Edin.; M.R.C.S. Eng.; Fellow Royal Physical Soc.—Formerly physician to the ct. of Madagascar, visiting and suptndg. surg. civil hosp., Mauritius, Feb., 1877, and govt. analyst, Dec., 1877; acted as pol. and prison surg. in 1878; prof. of chem., Roy. Coll., Apr., 1879; supt. lun. asyl., Nov., 1879; author of numerous med. wks.; sec. to cattle plague comsn., etc.; medical adviser of the C.O., Edinburgh, 1897.

DAVIDSON, Walter Edward, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1859; scholar and exhibitor of Christ's Coll., Camb.; writer, Ceylon civ. ser., Aug., 1880; attached to Kandy Kachcheri, Dec., 1880; writer col. sec.'s office, Apr., 1881; transferred to Colombo Kachcheri, Sept., 1881; attached to the Negombo pol. ct., May, 1882; acted as asst. agt., Negombo, July to Oct., 1882; extra office asst., Colombo Kachcheri, May, 1883; and ag. office asst., Nov., 1884; sec. to Colon comsn., col. exhib., 1886; ag. asst. gov't. agt., Kegalla, Apl., 1888, Mar., 1892, and Feb., 1895; ag. pol. mag., Kalutara, Apl., 1899; Galle, Dec., 1890; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1887; Haldummulla, Oct., 1890; ag. dist. judge, Kalutara, Apl., 1899; ag. gov't. agt. and fiscal, prov. of Sabaragamuwa, July, 1896; asst. gov't. agt., Kegalla, Oct., 1896; N. Eliya, Feb., 1899; chmn. mun. coun., and mayor of Colombo, Apl., 1898; resig. as chmn. of mun. coun., going on leave; asst. sec. to administrator, Transvaal, 1901; col. sec., 1902-3; gov., Seychelles, 15th Apr., 1904.

DAVIDSON-HOUSTON, Lieut.-Col., Wilfred B.—Ed. at Corrig schl., Ireland, and St. Edward's, Oxford; 2nd lieut. 5th Royal Dublin Fus., Jan., 1887; capt., 1892; major, 1902; lieut.-col., 1906; B.S.A. Co. pol., and asst. comsnr., Rhodesia, 1890-92; certificates, R.I.C. and Dublin met. pol., 1895; asst. inspr., G. Coast Contab. (Hausas), Jan., 1894; inspr., Jan., 1898; capt., W. A. F. F., Jan., 1898; on spec. serv. Kwahu and Ashanti-Akim, Feb., 1894, to Mar., 1895; Ashanti expedn., 1895-6, commanded advance guard, main column (hon. ment. in desp., star); spec. miss. to N.W. Ashanti, Feb. to May, 1896; with Hausa detach. in London, Jubilee, 1897 (medal); operations in N. T., G. Coast, 1897-8 (medal and clasp); ag. res., Ashanti, May, 1899, to Apr., 1900; Ashanti campaign, 1900 (medal and clasp); S. African War, 1901-2, staff offr., Warrenton (medal and 5 clasps); adjutant 5th Roy. Dub. Fus., 1902; comsnr. of Ashanti, 1902; ag. ch. comsnr. of Ashanti, Mar. to May, 1903; and from July, 1904, to Mar., 1905; comsnr. of Montserrat, 25th Apr., 1906.

DAVIES, C. Rees.—Ed. at Eton; admitted solr. of sup. ct., Sept., 1891; under-sheriff, Pembrokeshire, 1897; called to the bar, Bahamas, July, 1901; ag. ch. just., Feb., 1903; ag. prosecuting coun. for the Crown, May, 1903; coroner, Nov., 1904; registr.-gen., Br. Hond., Apr., 1905; also registr., Vice-Admiralty ct.; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1905, to Jan., 1906, and mem. of ex. and leg. couns.; also mem. of bds. of educn. and quarantine; J.P. for col.; ag. atty.-gen., May, 1906.

DAVIES, Hon. John Mark.—M.L.C. for S. Yarra, Victoria, 1889; for Melbourne, 1894; min. for just., 1890-92; solr.-gen., 1893-1900, and 1902-3; min. of pub. instrn., 1903; atty.-gen. and solr.-gen., Victoria, 1903.

DAVIES, Hon. Sir Louis Henry, K.C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1845; called to the bar, 1866; Q.C. in 1880; was one of the Canadian counsel before the internat. fishery comsn. at Halifax in 1877; solr. gen. of P.E. Is., 1869, and again in 1872-3; led the opposition in P.E. Is. Legislature until Sept., 1876, when he became premier and atty.-gen.; resig. in Mar., 1879, and was defeated at the gen. elections which followed; was first returned to the House of Commons at the gen. elections in 1882, and has since sat for Queen's; min. of marine and fisheries in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896; mem. joint high comsn., Quebec and Washington, 1898-9; puisne judge of sup. court, 1901.

DAVIES, The Hon. Sir Matthew Henry, K.T. Bach. (1890).—B. 1851; solr.; late mayor of Prahran; mem. exec. coun. Victoria; min. without portfolio, Feb., 1886; speaker, legis. assem., 1887-92; vice-pres. and an exec. comsnr. for the centennial internat. exhibn., 1888.

DAVIES, Wm. Rees.—Ed. Eton and Trin. Hall, Camb.; B.A., 1885; called to bar (Inner Tem.), 1887; went South Wales circuit; J.P. and dep. lieut. Pembrokehire; M.P. for Pemb., 1892-3; asst. priv. sec. to chancellor of exchequer, 1893-5; atty.-gen., Bahamas, Dec., 1897; administered the govt., June, 1901; ag. ch. just., May to Aug., 1902; King's advocate, Cyprus, 1902.

DAVIS, Beccles.—Ed. C.M.S. grammar schl., Sierra Leone and Fourah Bay Coll. (Dunelm); 1st class customs officer, 1894; ag. statistical officer of customs, Niger Coast Prot., Apr. to Oct., 1894; P.C.O. at Opobo, Brass and Bonny, 1894 to 1899; supervisor of customs, Southern Nigeria, 1900; transferred to pub. wks. dep., 1903; accent., P.W.D., 1903.

DAVIS, C. G. H.—Ent. col. ser. of Br. Guiana as asst. clk. audit office, Nov., 1887; 5th cl. clk., Apr., 1889; 4th Sept., 1889; 3rd Jan., 1894; sec. to comtee. of combined court on exp. expenditure, 1893-4; 2nd cl. clk., Nov., 1896; ag. 1st cl. clk., 1895-6-7-8; ag. acct. to sup. ct., July, 1899, to Sept., 1900; 1st clk. and accent., post office, Sept., 1900.

DAVIS, C. T.—Ed. Christ's Coll., Brecon, and Oxford; classical scholar, Balliol coll., 1892-6; 1st class classical mods., 1894; Jenkyns exhib., 1896; 1st class lit. hum., 1896; appld. after compet. exam., 1st cl. clk., acct-gen.'s dept., admiralty, Oct. 5th, 1896; transfd. to sec.'s dept., inland rev., Mar., 1897; 2nd cl. clk., col. office, Apr. 15th, 1897; went to Gibraltar as sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir C. R. Thompson on a mission to inquire into the currency question, May and June, 1898; sec. Pacif. cable comtee., 1900; priv. sec., to Sir M. Ommannay, June, 1902; 1st cls. clk., 1st Apr., 1905.

DAVIS, L. A. R.—Permanent supernum. aid waiter, cust. dept., B. Guiana, Oct., 1882; 6th cl., Apr., 1883; 4th cl., Oct., 1884; 4th cl. offr. of cust., Jan., 1886; 3rd cl., Oct., 1887; 2nd cl., June, 1896; ag. 1st grade comsry. of taxation and offl. astt. to comptlr. of cust. in excise matters, July, 1898, to Mar., 1899; 1st cl. offr., 8th Sept., 1905; ag. survr. of cust., 1st Nov., 1905, to 28th Feb., 1906.

DAVIS, NICHOLAS DARNELL, C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1846; was for some years in Grenada, in the priv. sec.'s office; priv. sec. to Admstr. Baynes for a short time, In Br. Guiana served as clerk in several depts.; Aug., 1870, to Feb., 1871, sec. to the comsrs. of inquiry into the treatment of imgrts.; sec. to roy. comsrs. of inquiry into the treatment of imgrts. in Mauritius, Feb., 1872; comdt., Br. Sherbro, S. Leone, 1874; postmr.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1876; acted as rec-gen., Dec., 1880, to Sept., 1881; comptroller of customs, 1st Oct., 1881; ag. auditor-gen., mem. of the ct. of policy, and sen. comsrr. of the Vissengen estate, 1882; mem. ct. of policy again in 1886; auditor gen., 1st July, 1898; mem. exec. coun., Br. G., Feb., 1901; has acted as gov. sec. on eight occasions, and three times as dep. govr.
DAVIS, S. SPENCER.—Extra rev. offr., St. Kitts, 1893; 4th ditto, 1895; asst. treas., Gold Coast, 1901.

DAVSON, CHARLES SIMON, B.A., LL.D., K.C.—Ed. at Westminster and Trin. Hall, Camb.; called to the bar, Middle Tem., Jan., 1881; admitted to bar of Br. Guiana, 1882; acted as solr.-gen. on many occasions; stip. mag., 1888; solr.-gen., 1898; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1898, to Oct., 1899; again, Oct., 1900, to Apr., 1901; puisne judge, Mauritius, 1905.
DAWE, HON. CHAR.—Newfoundland: M.H.A., Harbour Grace, 1878-1889; for Port-de-Grave since 1898; mem. exec. coun. (without portfolio), 1894, and again in 1897.

DAWE, HON. E.—M.H.A. for Harbour Grace, Newfoundland, 1889-1905; rlwy. comsrr., 1890; financial sec., 1893; chrmn., bd. of wks., 1895; min. of agric. and mines, 1900; mem. of cabinet, 1903; min. of marine and fisheries, 1904.
DAWSON, J. B. H.—M.R.C.S. Eng., L.R.C.P. Lond., 1893, St. Mary's Hosp. med. schl., Lond.; dist. med. offr., Leeward Isl., Oct., 1894; dist. surg. of Sierra Leone Aug., 1893.
DAWSON, JOHN WILDING.—Asst. eng., drainage dept., Mauritius, 30th Nov., 1894; drainage authority, 15th August, 1898; asst. dir. of pub. wks. and surveys, 28th Dec., 1904.
DAWSON, SAMUEL EDWARD, C.M.G. (1906).—Queen's printer, Canada, and controller of stationery, Nov., 1891; doc. of letters, Leval. Univ., Quebec, and fell. of roy. soc. of Canada.

DAY, H. R. A.—Cadet, Sarawak civ. service, July, 1886; asst. res. Lundu, June, 1881; asst. res. Bitulu, June, 1893; res. 2nd cl., Mar., 1896; res. of Lundu, 1898; res., 2nd cl., Upper Sarawak, Jan., 1902.

DEACON, THOMAS.—Apptd., after open compet. exam., for the home civ. ser., to the Impl. Post Office, July, 1884; postmr.-gen., Gold Coast Colony, Sept. 2nd, 1899; lieut., G. C. Rifle vols., 1899; capt., 13th Oct., 1901; ag. adjutant, 11th Apr., 1903.

DEAKIN, THE HON. ALFRED.—Comsrr. of pub. wks. and min. of water supply, Victoria, Mar. 8th, 1883; and also vice-pres. bd. of land and works; solr.-gen. Nov. 13th, 1883; pres. of roy. coms. on water supply, Dec., 1884; ch. sec. and min. of water supply, 1886; resig., Nov., 1890; sen. deleg. to col. confce., 1887; one of the Austr. delegates sent to Eng. in connection with Austr. federation bill; joined 1st ministry of the Commonwealth as atty.-gen. and min. of justice, Jan., 1901; prime min. of Australia, 1903-4, again prime min., 1905.

DEALY, THOMAS KIRKMAN, F.R.G.S., M.R. San. I.—Ed. at St. Mary's Coll., Hammersmith; Marquis of Ripon's 1st prize and a double first (head of each list), 1880; matric. (1st div.), Lond. Univ., 1883; 1st B.A. (1st div.), 1884; Diplôme (degré supérieur) de l'Alliance Française (Paris), 1902; J.P., 1904; headmr. St. Mary's Derby, 1881-84; asst. master, govt. central schl., now Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1884; hon. examr. in physics to coll. of medicine for Chinese, 1888; passed 2nd standard higher grade Chinese, Aug., 1890; ag. 2nd master, Victoria Coll., Apr., 1890, to Sept., 1891, Apr., 1894, to Apr., 1895, Mar., 1899, to Apr., 1900, May, 1900, to Oct., 1901, and Mar. to Sept., 1904; apptd. master in charge, pol. schl., Sept. 1st, 1900; resig. pol. schl., 30th Apr., 1906; apptd. 2nd mast., Queen's Coll., 16th Mar., 1906.

DE BOISSIERE, ARNAULD.—Entd. civ. serv., Trinidad, 1891; trooper, light horse, 1897; sub-lieut., 1901; lieut., 1903; ag. chief of fire brigade, Mar., 1903; J.P. for colony, and inspr. of Indian immgrts.
DE CELLES, ALFRED DUCLOS.—Joint librarian of parlmt., Canada, 1885.

DE FREITAS, ANTHONY, B.A. (Cantab.).—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn; ag. pol. mag. and coroner for E. dist., Grenada, 1900 and 1901; ag. atty.-gen., St. Vincent, Nov., 1902; ag. pol. mag. and coroner, S. dist., Grenada, Nov., 1902; ag. regis., sup. ct., Grenada, Dec., 1902, to Jan., 1904; ag. pol. mag. and coroner, S. dist., Grenada, July, 1903; ag. ch. just., mem. ex. and leg. couns., and mem. bd. of educ., St. Vincent, May to Oct., 1904; pol. mag. and coroner, 1st dist., St. Lucia, Oct., 1904.
DE GRUCHY, A. NICOLLE, M.B., C.M., Edin.—Asst. med. offr., and J.P., Victoria, Seychelles, and outlying islands, 11th Sept., 1901; col.-surg., S. Nigeria, Apr., 1906.
DE KRETZER, EDWARD, I.S.O. (19·3).—B. 1854; employed in col. sec.'s off., Ceylon, 8th May, 1872; asst. aud.-gen., 1st Jan., 1901.

DE LA CORNILLIERE, HENRI GASTON ERNEST.—Draughtsman, P.W.D. of late O.F.S., Nov., 1892, to Mar., 1900; architectural asst., P.W.D., O.R.C., Mar., 1900, to July, 1902; head of architectural drawing off., ditto, July, 1902.

DELAFAYE, SIR LOUIS VICTOR, KT. BACH. (1898)—B. 1842; called to bar Mid. Tem., 1863; mun. councillor, St. Louis, 1870; prov. judge, sup. ct., Mauritius, Mar., 1879; mem. prison bd., Jan., 1884; mem. civ. ser. inquiry comsnn., Feb., 1884; mem. civ. ser. comsnn., 1884-95; ag. puisne judge, 1884-5, and May, 1890, to July, 1892; Q.C., Aug., 1892; puisne judge, Nov., 1894; ag. chief justice, June, 1898; chief judge, Dec., 1898.

DE SARAM, J.H., C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1844; writer to the govt. of Ceylon, 1865; pol. mag. of Balapiti-módara, 1866; dist. judge, Badulla, Oct., 1867; comsnr. of requests, Colombo, 1868; dist. judge, Kurunégala, 1875; regis.-gen., Colombo, 1886; dist. judge, Kalutara, Dec., 1883; registr.-gen., May, 1886; dist. judge, Jaffna, Aug., 1891; ditto, Galle, Dec., 1891; ditto, Kandy, Aug., 1893; ret.
DESCROIZILLES, FREDERIC VIVIAN.—Ed. Royal coll., Mauritius; inspr. of distill., Mauritius, May, 1874; asst. recvr.-gen., 16th June, 1897.

DESHON, EDWARD, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1836; mem. of land bd., Queensland; ensign in 68th L.I., June, 1854; lieut., Dec., 1854; served with 68th L.I. at siege and fall of Sebastopol, from Nov., 1854, to end of the war; medal and clasp, and Turkish medal; instructor of musketry to the 68th L.I. from Nov., 1857, to Oct., 1861; passed compet. exam. for admission to Staff Coll., in July, 1861; ret. by sale of comsn. in Oct., 1861; audit.-gen., Queensland, 1890; ret. 1901.

DES MARAIS, HENRY EUGENE.—Called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Apr., 1866; pol. mag., Rodrigues, June, 1875; dist. judge and stip. mag., Seychelles, Nov., 1879; ag. ch. civ. comsnr. on several occasions; ag. mag., Moka, Apr. to Sept., 1882; dist. mag., Moka, Aug., 1884; ag. pol. and additional mag., Port Louis, Nov., 1884; dist. mag., Moka, Dec., 1885; ag. jun. dist. mag., Port Louis, May to October, 1886; Mar., 1887, to Jan., 1888; dist. and stip. mag., Moka, July, 1889.

DE SMIDT, HENRY, C.M.G. (1901), B.A. (Cape Univ.), F.S.S.—B. 1845; entered the service in Mar., 1865; permanent under col. sec. of the col. of Cape of Good Hope, and perm. head of convicts and prisons dept., holding in addition the following offices: (1) accounting offrr., col. sec.'s dept.; (2) controller printg. and statnry.; (3) mem. civ. ser. comsn.; (4) mem. tender bd.; (5) custodian stamps; (6) admstr. food and drugs act; (7) inspr. emigrt. coolie ships. Was specially charged with the duties of dir. of the census of 1891; received congratulations of the govt., and was awarded an honorarium of 500L specially voted by parlmt.; elected fellow of roy. statistical soc., 1892; asst. treas., July, 1898; ret. 1904.

DEVILLIERS, RT. HON. SIR JOHN, P.C. (1897), K.C.M.G. (1882), KT. BACH. (1877).—B. 1842; ch. justice of the sup. ct., C. of Good Hope, 1874; atty-gen., from 1872 to 1874; for several years mem. of the legis. assem.; pres. of the legis. coun., and mem. of the coun. of the Univ. of C. of Good Hope in 1873; one of the roy. comsn. for the settlement of the affairs of the Transvaal, 1881; represented the Cape at the col. confece., Ottawa, 1894; mem. of judicial comte., H.M. privy coun., 1897.

DEW, ARTHUR TOMKYNES.—Naval cadet, 1867; midshipman, 1869; sub.-lieut., 1874; took part in the expdns. to the Linggi and Lukut rivers in 1874, and in suppression of piracy on Malayan coast (Indian medal, with Perak clasp); mag. and collr., Perak, 1881; sup. Br. territory of Dindings, 1884; dep. master attendant, shipping master, Singapore, 1886; is a J.P.; mag., Singapore and Penang; comsnr. ct. of requests; mag. and collr., dist. mag. and harbrmr., Matang, Perak, 1888; dist. mag., Krian, Apr., 1899; ag. dist. mag., Lower Perak, July, 1901; dist. offr., Batang Padang, Perak, Nov., 1902; dist. offr., Lower Perak, 14th Sept., 1904.
DEWAR, H. J.—Apptd. clk., repatriation, O.R.C., 23rd April, 1902; ditto, British refugee aid, 1st July, 1903; ditto, treasy., 1st July, 1903; examr. of acts., treasy., 4th Apr., 1906.
DEWDNEY, THE HON. EDGAR.—Lieut.-gov. of N.W. territories, 1881; min. of interior, Canada, 1888; lieut.-gov., Br. Columbia, 1892-7.

DE WET, THE HON. SIR JACOBUS ALBERTUS, K.C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1840; mem. Cape house of assem. and exec. coun.; min. for native affairs, 1884; Br. agent, S.A. Republic, June, 1890; ret. on pension, Sept., 1890.

DE WILTON, MAJOR ALBERT WALTER.—lieut., Lanarkshire militia, 1881; lieut., Connaught Rangers, 1883 to 1886; lieut., Indian staff corps, 1886 to 1894; capt., ditto, 10th Mar., 1894; major, ditto, 10th July, 1891; served in Burma campaign, 1885, 1887, 1889 (medal and two clasps); insp.-gen. of police, Mauritius, 29th Oct., 1898; insp.-gen. of pol. and prisons, Ceylon, 18th Aug., 1902.

DE WOLF, A.—Med. visitor of estates, Trinidad, 1st Apr., 1874; dist. med. offr., St. Joseph, 1st Jan., 1876; ditto, Port of Spain North, med. insp. of imigrts., and health offr. of shipping, Aug., 1890; ag. surg.-gen., on several occasions, 1884-1899; surg.-gen., 5th Nov., 1901.
DEWS, ALBERT, M.A.—Classical master, Codrington Coll., Barbados, 1893.
DIAS, FELIX REGINALD, M.A., LL.M. Trin. Hall, Camb.—Called to the bar, Inner Tem., May, 1887; pol. mag. and comsnr. of requests, Gampola, Ceylon, Oct., 1889; crown counsel for the island, July, 1893.
DICK, JOHN NORMAN.—Govt. engrnr. survr., Penang, 1888; ag. govt. marine survr., Singapore, June, 1895 to June, 1896; govt. mar. surv., Singapore, March, 1901.

DICKSON, THOMAS.—Ed. at Fettes Coll., Edin.; served in Boer war in Ist. cont., Ceylon M.I., Jan., 1900, to Jan., 1901 (medal and 3 clasps); and in S.A.C. from Jan., 1901, to June, 1902 (King's medal and 2 clasps); temp. apptd. ch. clk., land settm. dept., O.R.C., 10th Oct., 1901; asst. sec., land settm., 12th Aug., 1903; sec., land settm., 23rd Jan., 1905; ag. dir., land settm., 14th May to 14th Nov., 1906.

DILLON, A. BARROW.—Ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad; clk. to insp. of schls., B. Hond., 1893; ag. clk. to auditor and in treasy. and customs, 1893; clk. to col. engrnr., 1895; ag. insp. of schls., 1895-8; 2nd clk., treasy. and cust., 1897; insp. of schls., June, 1898; ag. supt. bot. station, 28th Sept. to 31st Dec., 1904; ag. ch. clk., C.S.O., and clk. of couns., 29th June, 1905.
DILLON, P. L., I.S.O. (1904).—B. 1846; 4th class landing waiter, Barbados, 1866; 3rd class, 1868; senior, 1880; landing survr., 1886; comptr. of customs, Dec., 1890.

DISCOMBE, JOHN.—Apptd., after compet. exam., 3rd class clk., sup. ct., Gibraltar; 2nd class clk. (supernumerary), Jan., 1890; ag. asst. registr., Nov., 1899; asst. registr. and dep. clk. of arraigns, Jan., 1900; ag. registr. and adminstr.-gen., July to Sept., 1901.

DOBIE, EDWARD DAVID.—Crown solr., Tasmania (also clk. of the peace and registr. of building socs.), May, 1887; barrister, etc., sup. ct., Tasmania, admitted July, 1882; joined civ. ser., Tasmania, Mar., 1883; was partly draftsman and afterwards solr.-gen., from Mar. to May, 1887.

DOBERCK, DR. W.—Astronomer in charge of Markree Observ., co. Sligo, 1874-1883; dir. Hong Kong Observ. since 1883; has published twenty volumes of observations and researches in Hong Kong, a pamphlet "On the Law of Storms in the Eastern Seas," and over 200 papers on scientific subjects in transactions of learned societies and scientific periodicals in Europe and America.

DOBSON, HON. ALFRED, C.M.G. (1904).—B. 1848; called to the bar, Inner Tem., Jan., 1875; mem. legis. assem., Tasmania, 1877-87; att.-gen. and mem. exec. coun., 1877; speaker legis. assem., July, 1885; solr.-gen., June, 1887; mem. of coun. of educn., and church advoc. for the diocese of Tasmania; agt.-gen. for Tasmania in London, 1901.

DODDS, THE HON. SIR JOHN STOKELL, K.C.M.G. (1901), KT. BACH., C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1848; att.-gen., Tasmania, Dec., 1878, to Oct., 1879, and in the Giblin ministry, Oct., 1879; col. treas. from Dec., 1881, to Aug., 1884; att.-gen. in the Douglas ministry from Aug., 1884, to Mar., 1886; also premier, Mar. to Dec., 1886; judge, sup. ct., 1886; deleg. col. confee., 1887; ch. just., 1898; admnstr. of govt., 1899, and again 1900-1; lt. gov., 3rd Aug., 1903.
DODDS, WILLIAM JOHN M.D. (Edin.), 1879, gold medallist, M.B. and C.M., 1876; 1st class honours, D.Sc., 1878.—Inspr. of asyls. and med. supt. of Valkenburg asyl., Cape, 1889.
DOHERTY, C. J.—Puiscne judge, super. ct., Quebec, 1891.
DONNAN, J., C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1837; mast. attendant, Colombo, Ceylon, 1863; was comdr. of govt. steamers, "Manchester" and "Pearl," from July 1859; retired, 1902.

DONNELLY, W. J. S.—Survr.-gen., Newfoundland, to 1882; rec.-gen., 1882; resig., 1889; M.L.A. for Placentia, 1893; rec.-gen. and mem. exec. coun., Apr. to Dec., 1894; M.H.A., Placentia, 1897; rec.-gen., 1898; inspir. of cust., 1902.

DONOVAN, JUSTIN FOLEY, M.D., M.CH.L.M.—Med. offr. and health offr., Killcare dispensary, co. Cork, 1876 to 1879; surg., R.N., 1879; received thanks of the admiralty, 1882 and 1888, "for zeal and devotion to duty" at R.N. hosps., Port Royal and Malta; and of the med. dir.-gen., R.N., for professional work, 1882-4-6; retired from the navy 1889; med. attendant, lepers' home, pub. gen. hosp. and constab. training depot, Spanish Town, Jamaica, 1889; med. examr., civ. ser. widows and orphans' pension fund, 1890.

DOUGLAS, ARCHIBALD CAMPBELL.—Ed. Radley Coll. and Oxford Military Coll.; apptd. a consular asst. on the Niger Coast Protec., Dec., 1894; asst. dist. comsnr., Feb., 1896; student, Lincoln's Inn, 1898; transport offr. in Qua Ibo dist. punitive exped., Feb. and Mar., 1899 (West African medal); promoted to rank of 2nd cl. offr., S. Nig., Jan., 1901; served as political offr. with Aro field force, Jan. to Feb., 1902 (medal); dist. comsnr. Brass, 1st Apr., 1904.
DOUGLAS, JAMES ARCHIBALD, M.A.—Director of education, So. Nigeria, Apr., 1903.
DOUGLAS, R. STAIR.—Cadet, Sarawak serv., Nov., 1896; asst. res., June, 1899; res. 2nd cl., Jan., 1902; 2nd cls. res., Baram dist., 1904.
DOUGLAS-DE-FENZI, C. W. P.—Clk. asst. and librarian, Natal; legis. coun., Feb., 1889; clk., legis. coun., 1893; ag. under-treas., Aug., 1905.

DOWNES, THE HON. SIR J. W., K.C.M.G. (1887), K.C.—B. 1844; att-gen., S. Australia, June, 1881, to June, 1884, and att-gen. and premier, 1885-7; del. to col. confee., 1887; ch. sec. and premier, 1892-3; mem. of senate, Australian parl., 1901-1903; mem. leg. coun., S. Aust., 1905.

DOWNES, MAJOR-GEN. M. FRANCIS, C.M.G. (1885), late R.A.—B. 1834; served in the Crimea till June, 1836, medal and clasp and Turkish medal; instructor of fortificn., R.M.Coll., Sandhurst, for two years; commanded artillery in Mauritius and St. Helena for four years; instructor of gunnery at Woolwich five years; col.-comdt., S. Australia, June, 1877; mem. of roy. coman. on defences, Sydney, 1881; sec. of defence, Victoria, April, 1885, to Mar., 1888; comdt., S. Australian forces, Apr., 1888-92; ret. 1902.

DOWSON, R. W.—Acentnt., Mauritius rly., Sept., 1867; jun. audit examr., June, 1878; ch. clk. rly. dept., Apr., 1882; ag. asst. man., July, 1884, to April, 1885; head accmnt., Sept., 1891; ag. gen. man., July to Sept., 1896; asst. gen. man., July, 1903.

DOYLE, E. A. GAYNES, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lond.; obstet. soc., Lond.; mem. Br. med. assoc.; late asst. house surg., Westminster hosp.; joined med. service, Trinidad, 1884; ag. dist. med. offr., 1886; res. surg., San Fernando hosp., Nov., 1892; ag. res. surg., col. hosp., Port of Spain, 1893.

DRAKE, THE HON. JAS. GEORGE.—Mem. leg. ass., Queensland, 1888, 1893, 1896, 1899; M.L.C., 12th Dec., 1899; sec. for pub. instn. and P.M.G., Queensland, Dec., 1899, to Feb., 1901; senator for Queensland, Commonwealth parl., Mar., 1901; P.M.G., Commonwealth, Feb., 1901, to Aug., 1903; min. of def., Aug.-Sept., 1903; atty.-gen., Sept., 1903; vice-pres. of ex. coun., 1903; ret. 1906.

DRAKE-BROCKMAN, RALPH EVELYN.—Ed. Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, Dulwich Coll., and St. George's Hosp.; scholarship, St. George's Hosp., 1st year's proficiency prize and Pollock prize; M.R.C.S. Eng., L.R.C.P. Lond.; apptd. surg.-capt., attached to railway pioneer regt., S.A.F.F., Dec., 1899 (medal and 2 clasps); med. offr., Uganda Prot., 26th Sept., 1900; Suk-Turkana expdttn., 1901 (ment. in desp., spec. ment. by Sir H. Johnston, G.C.M.G., K.C.B., recd. appreciation of serv. from S. of S. for Foreign Affairs); recd. appreciation from S. of S. for the Colonies for serv. during smallpox epidemic in Berbera, Somaliland Prot., 1904-5; med. offr., Somaliland Prot., 6th June, 1904.
