DRAYSON, MATTHEW JOHN.—Apptd., after compet. exam., a clerk in the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., May, 1883; asst. registr., May, 1892; promoted to higher grade 2nd div., July, 1896; seconded for service as chief clerk, col. sec.'s off., Hong Kong, 11th Jan., 1902; acted as clk. of coun., Aug., 1902; retd. to C.O. as deputy registr., 22nd July, 1905.

DRAYTON, EDWARD RAWLE, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1859; clk. col. sec.'s office, Barbados, Sept., 1878; 2nd clk. in office of gov.-in-chief of the Windward Is., July, 1879; ag. ch. clk., May, 1880, and May, 1881, to Jan., 1882; ch. clk., Jan., 1882; ag. col. sec., Grenada, Apr., 1883; priv. sec. to Maj.-gen. Browne, July to Oct., 1884; provost-marshal, ida., Apr., 1885; ag. col. registr., Sept., 1885; regist. sup. ct., J.P., July, 1886; mem. legis. coun., May, 1887; inspr. of prisons, Mar., 1888; mem. exec. coun., Nov., 1888; treas., May, 1890; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1890, to June, 1892; col. sec. and registr.-gen., June, 1892; admstr. govt. of Grenada whenever gov. absent, 1890-1900; govt. of St. Vincent, June, 1893, Feb. to Sept., 1897, and Feb., 1900, to April, 1901; and of Windward Is., Jan., 1897; delegate to W. Indian quarantine conf., Apl., 1904.

DRURY, EDWARD HERBERT MERIVALE.—Trinity Hall, Camb., 1890; called to the bar, Inner Temp., 1896; priv. sec. to Hon. Sir R. G. W. Herbert, G.C.B., Mar., 1899, to June, 1900; priv. sec. to Govr. Sir C. A. Moloney, K.C.M.G., Windward Is., July, 1900; ditto, Trinidad, Dec., 1900, to July, 1902; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, St. Helena, Oct., 1902; and clk. to ex. coun., Apl., 1903; J.P., Nov., 1902; asst. stip. mag., Nov., 1904.

DRURY, R. FREDERICK, A.M.I.C.E.—Asst. land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1887; asst. inspr. of bldgs. and asst. sanitary survr., 1888; ag. land survr. and asst. engrnr. on extraordinary pub. wks., 1890; annually recurrent wks., 1892 and 1894; duties in connection with black plague, 1894; ag. sanitary engrnr., 1895; ag. exec. engrnr. for annually recurrent wks., 1897.

DU BOULAY, ED.—Ag. clk. to audr. and treas., St. Lucia, Jan. to May, 1890; 4th clk., treas., Mar. to May, 1892; 3rd clk., May to Dec., 1892; 2nd clk., treas., and accnt., Jan., 1893, to Apr., 1894; ag. ch. clk., treas., Feb. to Nov., 1893; audit clk., Apr., 1894; ag. audr., St. Lucia, Mar. to Sept., 1901; attached to Somerset house for service in W. African colonies, Dec., 1901.

DUDER, T. C.—J.P. Newfoundland, 1874; M.H.A., Fogo, since 1893; financial sec., 1894; chmn. bd. of wks., 1894; survr.-gen. and chmn. bd. of agricul., 1897; min. agricul. and mines, 1898; stip. mag., Bonne Bay, 1901.

DUFF, HECTOR LIVINGSTONE KENNETH.—Asst. collr., B.C. Africa Prot., 1897; resig. from astntr. serv., Nov., 1903; re-apptd., Apr., 1904; collr., Mlanje dist., May, 1904; dist. res., 2nd class, Apr., 1906.

DUFF, GRANT, THE RT. HON. SIR M. E., P.C., G.C.S.I., C.I.E., F.R.S. (1880).—B. 1829; ed. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1850; under-sec. of state for India, 1868 to 1874; lord rector of Aberdeen Univ., 1866 to 1872; parly. under-sec. of state for the cols., Apr., 1880, to Aug., 1881; M.P. for Elgin dist., 1857 to 1881; gov. of Madras, 1881-6.

DUFF, ROBERT.—Record clk., imigrn. dept., Br. Guiana, Apr., 1883; 4th clk., Aug., 1884; 3rd clk., Oct., 1885; 1st class certificate in Hindustani, Mar., 1885; 2nd clk., Mar., 1893; ch. clk., Jan., 1894; has acted as sub.-imigrn. agt. on several occasions, and also as sen. sub.-imigrn. agt.; imigrn. agt. and ch. clk., July, 1898.

DUFFIELD, THOMAS.—Entered crown lands office, S. Australia, Mar., 1867; ch. clk. and imigrn. offr., crown lands and imigrn. dept., 1877; capt. mil. force, 1881; sec. crown lands dept.; mem. of central land and pastoral bds., 1896-1905; sec., crown lands dept., and inspr. of fisheries, Nov., 1901.

DUFFY, THE HON. JOHN GAVAN.—Eldest son of the late Sir Charles Gayan Duffy, Ed. at Stonyhurst. Vice-chancellor's prize, English essay, Melbourne Univ. Succeeded his father in representation of Dalhousie in legis. assem. of Victoria, 1874. Pres. of bd. of land and wks. of Victoria, 1889; postmr.-gen. of Victoria, 1890; also atty.-gen., 1892; mem. of cabinet without portfolio, 1892; resig. Jan. 1893; again postmr.-gen., 1894.

DUKE, MANSERGH PACE, M.R.C.P., and L.R.C.S.I., L.M. (1875).—Ed. Academic inst., and Meath hosp., Dub.; med. offr., dist. 2, Dominica, 1875 to 1880; asst. surg., Gold Coast, 1880-1; med. offr., dist. 2, Montserrat, May, 1886; offl. mem. legis. coun., Jan., 1888; med. offr., dist. 1, and med. offr. of health, Oct., 1889; mem. exec. coun., Sept., 1895; mem. pub. lib. comtee., Oct., 1896; ex officio mem. bd. of health, Jan., 1897; J.P., Apr., 1897; ag. escheator-gen., July, 1898; holds dormant comsn., July, 1898, to act as comsnr., and has so acted on numerous occasions.

DUKES, HUGH ROBSON.—Asst., Boys' Model Schl., Pietermaritzburg, Natal, 1st Feb., 1893; headmaster, Stanger schl., 1st July, 1895; ag. inspr. of schls., 15th May, 1902 to 30th June, 1903; headmaster, Richmond schl., 1st Oct., 1902; inspr. of schls., 1st July, 1903.

DUMARESQ, ROBT. HARE FITZROY.—Ed. at Haileybury Coll., 1885-1890; matric., New Coll., Oxford, 1890; farming in New Zealand, 1892-99; served in S. African war, 1899-1902 (Queen's medal and two clasps); inspr., land settm., O.R.C., 1902-1906; sec., dept. of agric., O.R.C., 17th Apr., 1906.

DUNBAR, ARTHUR DUNCAN, M.A. (Aberdeen).—Served during S. African war in S. African Light Horse (Queen's and King's medals and clasps); clk. to dir. of educn., Transvaal and O.R.C., 1902-3; priv. sec. to educn. adviser to high comsnr. for S. Africa, 1904-5; clk., educn. dept., O.R.C., 1905.

DUNCAN, ANDREW, H. F. (late lieut. R.N.).—Joined R.N., July, 1868; served in Mediterranean, S. America, Cape, India, Australia, and W. Coast of Africa; resig. Nov., 1883; passed exam. in theoretical survey, July, 1883, and practical survey, 1884, at the Cape Univ., in honours (first of his year on both occasions); from Apr., 1884, employed by the survr.-gen. of the Cape, and in priv. practice; mem. land comsn. of Br. Bechuanaland, Sept., 1885; survr.-gen. of that col., July, 1886; survr.-gen. to Br. S. Africa Co., 1891.

DUNCAN, PATRICK, C.M.G. (1904).—B. 1870; ed. at Oxford, schol. Ball. Coll.; 1st cls. class. mod., 1891; 1st cls. final class. school, 1893; Craven scholarship, 1890; clk., after compet. exam., int. rev., 1894; priv. sec. to Sir A. Milner; prin. clk., 1898; treas., Transvaal, 1901; col. sec., 1st Dec., 1903; mem. ex. and leg. couns.; mem. I.C.C.; ag. liet.-gov., Oct., 1906.

DUNCOMBE, HENRY FEVESHAM.—Entd. col. serv. as an acctnt., treasy., Bahamas, 1891; ag. mag. and coll.; in various dists., 1894-5-6; res. mag., Abaco, 1897; dist. comsnr. Lagos, 1898; has acted on various occasions as pol. mag., collr. of cust., and treas.; passed govt. exam. in Yoruba language, 1900.

DUNCOMBE, W. KELSALL.—2nd clk., tel. dept., Bahamas, Dec., 1905; rev. offr., Inagua, Mar., 1897; asst. res. just., Watlings Island, May, 1900; res. just., Cat Island, July, 1900; supervisor of cust., Lagos, Jan., 1905; ag. collr. of cust., Lagos, from Aug., 1906.

DUNEDIN, 2ND BISHOP OF (founded 1864), Rt. REV. SAMUEL T. NEVILLE, D.D.—Consec. 1871; Magdal. Coll., Camb., B.A. 1865; curate of Scarsbrick, Lancs., 1860-64; rector of Skelton, Staffs., 1864-71; is also primate of New Zealand.

DUNLOP, J. M. M., B.A., LL.D., D.C.L.—Ed. at Trin. Coll., Dublin; barrister-at-law, King's Inn, Dublin, and Inner Temple; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1900; ag. pol. mag., 1901-2-3; ag. atty.-gen., 1904; ag. ch. asst. col. sec., 1905; ag. atty.-gen., 1906; ditto, S. Nigeria, 1906; ag. judge of sup. ct., S. Nigeria, 1906.

DUNLOP, COLONEL SAMUEL, R.A., C.M.G. (1884)—B. 1838; ag. comsnr. of pol., S. Stiltns., Nov., 1870; ag. pol. mag. and comsnr. of ct. of requests, P.W., Apr., 1871; ag. dep. comsnr. of pol. and coroner, Apl., 1871; ag. dep. comsnr. of pol., Singapore, fire comsnr. and coroner, Aug., 1871; ag. inspr.-gen. of pol., Dec., 1872; comsnr. to Perak, Jan., 1874. In Nov., 1874, comsnr. with the forces sent to quell the disturbances in the native state of Sungai Ujong; inspr.-gen., Straits pol., May, 1875. In Nov., 1875, on the murder of Mr. Birch, apptd. special comsnr. (temporarily) for Perak affairs; organized the expdn. which captured the Passir Salak stockades, and was present at the capture, Nov., 1875; comsnr. to forces during operations in Perak, Dec., 1875; accompanied Gen. Colborne's force up the Perak river, and across country to Kinta; ag. res. coun., Penang, 1884 to 1885; pres. of Singapore mun. comsn., 1889; ret., 1890.

DUNN, ROBERT ERNST.—Clerk and interp. to the R.M., Umgeni div., Natal, 1880; to R.M., Lions River, Mar., 1883; to R.M., Alfred, Oct., 1887; to R.M., Upper Umkomanzi, 1894; mag. for Colony, mag., Weenen div., 1901.
DUNN, STEPHEN TROYTE, F.L.S.—Ed. Radley and Merton Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1889; asst. for India, Kew Gdns., 1899; sup., bot. and forestry dept., Hong Kong, 1903; J.P., 1906.
DUNSMUIR, JAMES.—B. 1851; elec. to legislature of B. Columbia, 1898; premier, 1900 to 1902, when he resig.; lieut.-gov. of B. Columbia, 11th May, 1906.

DUPIGNY, E. G. MORSON.—Asst. govt. off., Dominica, Feb., 1889; clk. comsnrs. of valuatin., 1890; clk. mag., dist. F., 1890; do., presit.'s off. Dom., 1890-92; do. regtr.'s off., 1892; do. treas., Antigua, 1896; stud. Mid. Temp., 1900.
DUPONT, LOUIS EVENOR.—4th clk., registry, sup. ct., Mauritius, 1st Sept., 1864; ch. clk., regsn. dept., 18th June, 1884; recvr. of regsn. dues and conservator of mortgages, 6th May, 1901.
DURMAN, F. J.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to C.O., 15th April, 1901.

DU VERGE, L. S. R., I.S.O. (1903).—Inspr. of distilleries, Mauritius, 1868; ag. registr.-gen., 1896-7; storekeeper-gen., June, 1897; ag. recvr.-gen., in 1901, 1902 and 1903; nom. mem. of coun. of govt.
DUVIVIER, AIMÉ.—Clk., archives office, Mauritius, 1st Nov., 1879; custodian of archives, 1st Jan., 1891.

DYER, JAMES HAROLD.—Ed. King William's Coll., I. of Man, King Edward VI. schl., Berkhamstead; recd. his comsn. in 3rd Batt. S. Lanc. regmt., 1889; obtained his compny., 1893; resig. comsn., 1894; joined Matabele relief force, 1896, and served through the campaign till the force was disbanded in 1897; joined C.M.R., 1897, and served through siege of Mafeking; resig. Cape Police in July, 1901; received medals and clasps; apptd. asst. dist. comsnr. S. Nigeria, 28th Dec., 1901; ag. dist. comsnr., Ediba, 1902.

DYER, THOS. T. R.—Entd. pub. ser. of St. Vincent as cust. offr., Jan., 1890; ag. clk., treasy., 1891; asst. clk., P.O., 2nd clk. gov. off. to Apl., 1892; ag. 2nd clk. in off. of gov., Windward Islds., May, 1892; transf. to St. Lucia; 4th clk., treasy., May, 1892; 3rd clk., Jan., 1893; ag. 2nd clk., Feb. to May, 1893; 2nd clk., govt. off., May, 1893; also supt. of govt. printing; ag. 2nd clk. in off. of adminstr., Wind. Islds., 1894; hurricane relic offr., St. Vincent, Sept. to Dec., 1898; ag. ch. clk. and clk. of couns., St. Lucia, Oct., 1900, to Mar., 1901; agptd. ch. clk. to gov. Windward Is. (Grenada), 1st Apr., 1901; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, and clk. of ex. and leg. couns., 16th Nov., 1902.

DYER, SIR WILLIAM T. THISELTON, M.A., F.R.S., K.C.M.G. (1899), C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1843; Chr. Ch., Oxon, 1873; asst. dir., Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1873; dir., 1885; ret., 1905; botan. adviser to S. of S. for Col.

DYETT, EDWARD F.—2nd treasy. offr., Montserrat, Aug., 1886; admeasurer of vessels, 1889; 1st treasy. offr., June, 1891; comsnr. oaths, Nov., 1895; clk. of waterworks comsnrs., 1896; sec. bd. of health, Jan., 1897; held a comsnr., Dec., 1897, to act as treas. of Montserrat in absence of comsnr., and so acted on numerous occasions until Jan., 1900, when authorised by law to perform duties of treas. in conjunction with those of 1st treasy. offr.; J.P., May, 1898; offl. rec. in bankruptcy, Aug., 1898; holds comsnr., dated Feb., 1899, to admnr. oaths of office to ag. comsnr. in absence of comsnr. and govrn.; mem. legis. coun., Mar., 1900; visiting just. of prison, Nov., 1900; ag. col. postmnr., Antigua, Nov., 1903.

DYETT, HENRY.—Served in P.O. and treasy., Montserrat, Mar., 1894, to Feb., 1896; sec. valuation comsnrs., 1895; clk. to Mr. E. Baynes, comsnr. and mag., Montserrat, and to exec. coun., Feb., 1896, also to Mr. F. H. Watkins, I.S.O., 1900, and to Lt.-Col.W.B.Davidson-Houston,1906; ag. 2nd treasy. offr., Apr., May and Sept., 1897; clk., legis. coun., Mar., 1898; ag. 2nd treasy. and quarantine offr., July, 1902, to Apr., 1903; ag. jun. audit clk., Antigua, Oct.-Nov., 1903; sec., valuation comsnrs., Montserrat, 1904; govt. offr., St. Kitts, Nov., 1906.

DYETT, RICHARD HENRY KORTRIGHT.—Served in pres.'s office, Virgin Is., 1879 to 1881, and 1882 to 1884; clk. regis.'s office, Nevis, 1886; 2nd clk. regis.'s office, Antigua, 1887; clk. to trustee, supt. ct. lib., July, 1888; comsnr. to admnr. oaths, 1889; marshal V.A. ct., L.I., Dec., 1889; clk. to mag., dist. A., 1890; dep. cor., dist. A., 1892; ag. postmnr., May to Sept., 1894; 1st clk. regis.'s offr., Jan., 1895; dep. cor., dist. B., Oct., 1896; ag. mag., dist. A., Jan., Feb., Apl., to Nov., 1897; sec. to H.M. Diamond Jubilee comte., June, 1897; ag. mag., dist. B., Apl., 1898, to Jan., 1899; ag. mag., dist. B., May to Sept., 1899; and 25th Oct., 1899; ag. mag., dist. A., Nov., 1899, to May, 1900; during which period (Jan. to Apl.) acted also as mag., dist. B.; ag. mag., dist. A., June, 1900, to Mar., 1901; and Apl. to Nov., 1901; also in Mar., 1902; mem. of "Coronation" comte., and hon. sec. to same, May, 1902; ag. mag., dist. A., Aug., 1902, to Feb., 1903; regis. and prov.-marshal and regis. of titles, St. Kitts-Nevis, 11th June, 1903; mem. of Basseterre town bd., June, 1903; regis. and prov.-marshal, Antigua, and ch. regis., Leeward Is., July, 1905; trustee, sup. ct. library, Aug., 1905.
EAKIN, JAS. W.—B.A., M.D., M. Ch. Univ., Dublin; govt. med. offr., Trinidad; dist. med. offr., Port of Spain, S., and consulting surg. Col. hosp., San Fernando; surg.-major, local force.
EARL, ROBT. STEPHEN, M.A., M.B., B.C. (Cantab., 1891).—Med. offr. Lasoyedist., Dominica, Jul., 1892; med. offr., No. 5 dist. Antigua, 1896; comsnr. Virgin Is.

EARNSHAW, ALBERT.—Ed. at Univ. Coll., Durham, and Pemb. Coll., Oxford; held foundtn.; Newby and Univ. class. schlrships., Durham, 1885-1887; 1st cls. classics, 1st year exam., 1886; 1st cls. class. final, 1887; B.A., 1887; M.A., 1890; elected fellow of Durham, 1889; open class. schlr., Pemb. Coll., Oxford, Jan., 1888; 1st cls. class. mods., 1889; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1891; B.A., Oxford, 1891; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1893; went N.E. circuit and W. Riding, Yorks, sessions, etc., 1893-1904; common law lecturer, Yorks. Coll., Leeds, 1899-1900; pol. mag. and coroner, W. div., Grenada, Feb., 1904; ditto S. (or 1st) div., and escheator-gen., Grenada, June, 1905.
EASTERBROOK, ARTHUR DOVE.—Asst. collr., B.Cent. Africa Prot., June, 1896; 2nd cls. asst., May, 1902; dist. mag. and collr., N. Nyasa, May, 1904.

EATON, HENRY FRANCIS.—Clk., col. storekeeper's office, Melbourne, Jan., 1853; transf. to civil commissariat, Feb., 1854; acctnt., govt. stores, Feb., 1855; transf. to treasy., Mar., 1865; ag. acctnt., treasy., 1869-70, 1876, 1886-7; acctnt. to treasy., 1887; under treas., Sept., 1889—1895; capt. volr. artillery, 1876; is in the commission of the peace of the Colony.

EDWARDS, COL. ALFRED HAMILTON MACKENZIE, C.B.—Served in 1st Dragoon Guards, Jan., 1883, to Apl., 1892; adjutant, Behar Light Horse Volunteers, India, Apl., 1892, to Apl., 1897; 1st and 5th Dragoon Guards and Imperial Light Horse, Apl., 1897, to Oct., 1900; Hazara (N.-W. Frontier, India), 1888, asst. prov. marshal (ment. in desp., medal and clasp); commanded Imperial Light Horse, S. African war, Nov., 1899, to June, 1900; dist. comsnr., Potchefstroom and Krugersdorp dists., June to Oct., 1900; commanded "A" division, S.A.C., Oct., 1900, to end of war (ment. in desp., Queen's medal, 4 clasps, King's medal, 2 clasps); substantive lt.-col. in the army, dated 29th Nov., 1900; commdt., Transvaal volunteer; left the service, 1906.

EDWARDS, CHAR. HENRY.—Ed. St. Bees gram. schl. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; exhibtn. of Queen's Coll., B.A., 1894; B.C.L., 1901; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1898; dist. comsnr., G. Coast, Dec., 1899; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius, 1904.
EGERTON, RICHARD.—Ed. Westminster and Chr. Ch., Oxford; B.A. 1867; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Nov., 1869; asst. res. mag., Kingston, May, 1892; res. mag., Jamaica, Feb., 1893.

EGERTON, SIR WALTER, K.C.M.G. (1905), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1858; ed. Tonbridge Schl.; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1880; asst. to the Chinese prot., Jan., 1881; mag., Singapore, Jan., 1881; passed final exam, Malay, May, 1882; colr. of land rev., Penang and P.W., Jan., 1883; 2nd mag. of pol., Penang, May, 1883; also official assignee under the Bankruptcy Ordinance (Sept., 1882); ag. sen. dist. offr. Butterworth, P.W., Apl., 1890; a J.P. and coroner for S. Sttlmts., and a visiting justice for Penang; offr. in char., Sungel Ujong, July, 1893; ag. res., Pahang, May to July, 1894; offl. assignee and regis. of deeds, Singapore, June, 1896; inspr. of prisons, S. Sttlmts., May, 1897; dep.-pres.of mun. comsrs., Singapore, Mar., 1897; 1st mag., Penang, Apr., 1897; ag. 1st mag., Singapore, Jan., 1898; ag. res. councillor, Malacca, Mar., 1898, and Mar., 1899; 1st mag., Singapore, Oct., 1898, but continued to act as res. coun.; ag. col. treas., S.S., Nov.-Dec., 1898; ag. col. sec., S.S., Apr., 1900; ag. res. councilr., Penang, Feb. to Apr., 1901; ag. col. treas., S.S., Aug.-Oct., 1902; res., Neg. Semb., Aug., 1902; high commr., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1903; and gov., Lagos, 30th July, 1904.

EGGETT, W. H.—Apptd., after a compet. exam., to be a clk. of the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O. Nov., 1880; promoted to higher grade, 2nd div., Apr., 1896; asst. accntnt., 6th Apr., 1899.

EHRHARDT, ALBERT.—Ed. King Edward's High Schl., B'ham, and Worc. Coll., Oxford; graduated with honours in mods. and lit. hum., 1886; Inner Tem. schrshp., common law, 1888; called to bar, 1889; dist. comsrs., Lagos, 1896; travelling comsrs. on the E. bndry., Aug., 1897; ag. res. of Ibadan, 1898; ag. treas. and mem. exec. and legis. couns., June, 1900; ag. att.-gen., Sept., 1900; rlyv. comsrs. at Aro, June, 1901; ag. treas., Aug., 1901, to Mar., 1902; ag. atty.-gen., Mar. to June, 1902; apptd. res., Ibadan, Feb., 1902; apptd. col. treas., May, 1902; atty.-gen., Fjiji, mem. ex. and leg. couns., May, 1903; ag. ch. just., May, 1904; apptd. as comsrs. to produce revised edition of ordinances of the colony, 1903; ag. ch. just., May to Oct., 1904.

ELCUM, JOHN BOWEN, B.A., Oxon.—Ed. Highgate Schl. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; scholar of Queen's Coll., 1878; cadet, S. Stltmsts., Oct., 1884; passed in Malay, 1886; ag. dist. offr., S. Malacca, 1887; dist. offr. Bukit Mertajam, P.W., Jan., 1890; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Dec., 1890; sheriff, Penang, Mar., 1892; ag. sheriff, Singapore, June, 1893; 2nd mag., Penang, Nov., 1893; ag. collr. land revenue, Singapore, Dec., 1893, to Feb., 1894; ag. inspr. of schls., S. Stltmsts., June, 1895; also ag. official assignee and regir. of deeds, Singapore, Mar. to June, 1896; collr. of land rev. and offr. in charge of treasy., July, 1897; ag. 1st mag., Singapore, Mar., 1898; inspr. of schls., Aug., 1898, but continued to act as first mag. till Feb., 1899; dir. pub. instrn., Aug., 1901; inspr. of prisons, July, 1904; fin. comsrs., F.M.S., Oct., 1904; dir. of pub. instruction, S. Stltmsts. and F.M.S., 1906.

ELDRIDGE, A. E. G.—Clk., P.O., Antigua, Mar., 1880; clk. to pres. and isld. sec. and clk. to exec. coun., 1882; postmr. of Antigua, Aug., 1891; ag. mag. and coroner, Dist. B., 1894, 1896, and 1897; ag. mag. and coroner, Dist. H., Nevis, Apr., 1896, to Feb., 1900; mem. St. Kitts-Nevis legis. coun., and gen. legis. coun., 1899; ag. comsrs., of Virgin Is., and ag. asst. col. sec., Leeward Is., 1903.

ELFFERS, PIETER.—T. ii. (Holland), T. i. (O.F.S.); ed. at Normal Training Coll., Nymeyer, Holland; Dutch mast., pub. schl., Stellenbosch, 1882-84; ditto, Grey Coll. Schl., 1884-93; prin., govt. schl., Fauresmith, 1893-1901; Dutch mast., boy's high schl., Wynberg, Cape Colony, 1901-03; ditto, pub. schl., Bethulie, 1903-04; ditto, pub. schl., Winburg, 1904-05; inspr. of schls., O.R.C., 1st July 1905.

ELGEE, CAPT. C. H.—B. 1871; ed. Lancing and Sandhurst; 1st coms., 16th Foot, Feb., 1892; capt., Oct., 1899; active service, Chitra, 1896 (medal with clasp); Ashanti campaign, relief of Kumasi, 1900 (medal); adjutant, Lagos Haussa Force, 1899; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to Sir W. Macgregor, gov. of Lagos, 1900 to 1902; and clk. of ex. coun.; ag. insp.-gen., 1902; trav. comsrs., Lagos, 1902; ag. col. treas., mem. of ex. and leg. couns., 1902; resident, Ibadan, 1st Jan., 1903; author of "Memo. on Negro Education."

ELGIN AND KINCARDINE, 9th Earl of (creat. 1633), VICTOR ALEXANDER BRUCE, K.G. (creat. 1899); G.C.S.I., G.C.LE., P.C., LL.D., D.C.L.; Baron Bruce, 1603; Earl of Kincardine and Baron Bruce of Torry, 1647; Baron Elgin (U.K.), 1849.—B. 1849; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford, M.A.; treas. of Household and 1st comsrs. of wks., 1886; Lord-Lieut. of Fifeshire since 1885; Viceroy of India, 1894-99; chrmn. Roy. Coms. on S. African War, 1892; chrmn., Roy. Coms. on Free Churches Controversy in Scotland, 1904; chrmn. Roy. Coms. to administer Scottish Churches Act, 1905; sec. of state for the Colonies, Dec., 1905.

ELIOT, EDWARD CARLYON.—Ed. Bradfield Coll. and Uppingham; apptd. 5th class clk., govt. secretariat, British Guiana, 1895; seconded ag. clk. to govt. agent, N.-W. dist., Br. Guiana, for periods 1896 to 1899; 4th class clk., govt. secretariat, 1899; pay and quartermr. to Hausa force, Gold Coast Col., 1900; apptd. senior ast. treas., Oct., 1902; dist. comsrs., G. Coast, 5th May, 1905.

ELLIOT, LAURENCE S., I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1845; clk. to mags., W. Australia; landing and tide waiter at Bunbury, 1863; also postmr. and ast. dist. registr.; clk. in col. sec.'s office, 1872; sec. to central bd. of educn. and ast. clk. in legia. coun., 1873; registr.-gen., registr. of deeds, and registr. of brands, 1876; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s dept., Jan., 1878, to Jan., 1880; 1st clk. and registr.-gen., col. sec.'s dept., Jan., 1880; apptd. to take the census, Apr., 1881, and to superintend its compilation; ch. clk. and accntnt., treasy., Apl., 1881; ag. astt., col. sec., Dec., 1889, to Oct., 1890; under treas., Jan., 1891; is a J.P.

ELLENBERGER, JULES.—Clk. to ast. comsrs., Bech. Prot., 1890; interpreter to concessions coman., Bech. Prot., 1893; ditto to Sir S. Shippard's boundy. comsn., 1894; J.P., 1893; astt. mag., 1898; served in S. African war, including relief of Mafeking; spec. comsrs. for oaths of allegiance in So. dist. of Bech. Prot. to burghers of late S.A.R. and O.F.S.; astt. comsrs., So. dist., Bech. Prot., 11th July, 1902.
ELLERY, R. L. J., F.R.S., C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1827; astronomer, Victoria, 1853.
ELLIOT, MAJOR SIR HENRY GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1899), C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1826; ch. mag. of Tembuland, C. of Good Hope; in command of Tembu levies during Basuto war; ret., 1904.

ELLIOTT, SIR CHARLES BLETTERTAN, LL.B., K.C.M.G. (1901), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1841; clk., col. sec.'s office, Cape, 1859; clk. to Justice Watermeyer, 1863; sec. to examrs. in literature and science; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1867; ch. clk. dept. of lands and wks., Dec., 1872; advoc.sup., 1873; astt. comsrs., crown lands and pub. wks., 1876; gen. manager, rlys., Dec., 1880; is mem. of coun. of Univ., Cape; examr. and moderator of examrs. in science; ret., 1904.

ELLIOTT, COL. JOHN, R.M.L.I., C.B. (1877), C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1824; entered R.N. in 1838; 2nd lieut., June, 1846; col., 1869; served in the China expdn., 1842 (medal); served in H.M.S. "Fox" during the Burmese war, 1852 (officially thanked); mentioned in despatches; commd. the Royal Marines in the steam flotilla on the Irrawaddy (mentioned in gov.gen.'s despatch; medal with clasp for Pegu). Served at the bombardment and surrender of the forts of Bomarsund, in Aug., 1854 (Baltic medal), and during the siege of Sebastopol in 1855, the expdn. to Kertch and Yeni-Kale (medal with clasp, 5th class of the Medjidie, and Turkish medal); has also the medal for the Kaffir war. Served as maj. of brigade to the force of Royal Marines landed in Mexico in 1863; lieut.-col., Devonshire Rifle Volr. Corps, 1870; in 1871 J.P. for Plymouth; in 1874 called to the bar, Mid. Temp., and joined the western circuit; in 1876 inspr. of prisons and stip. J.P., Br. Guiana; inspr.-gen. of pol., Barbados, 1881; adminstd. govt. of St. Vincent, 1884 and 1889; acted as col. sec., Barbados, 1886 and 1894; ret. 1895.

ELLIS, FRANCIS ROBT., C.M.G.—B. 1849; writer in the Ceylon civ. ser., July, 1871; additional pol. mag., Kurunegala, 1874; office asst. to gov't. agt. N. Prov., 1876; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, 1878; ag. asst. agt. Matale, 1879; ditto, Mullaitivu, 1880; dist. judge, Ratnapura, 1st Apr., 1883; pol. mag., Colombo, 1885; dir. and inspr.-gen. of prisons, 1891-6; ag. inspr.-gen. pol., May to Nov., 1893; ag. prin. collr. of cust., Jan., 1896; gov't. agt., S. prov., Jan. to Mar., 1897; gov't. agt., W. prov., Sept., 1897; aud.-gen., Mar., 1902; ag. col. sec., 19th Nov., 1903; ret.
ELLIS, FRANK THOS.—Cadet, S. Sttlmts., 27th Nov., 1903.

ELLIS, WALTER DEVONSHIRE.—Ed. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxon; scholar, 1890-1895; prox. acc. Hertford Univ. scholarship, 1891; 1st class classical mods., 1892; Univ. jun. Greek testament prize, 1893; 1st class literae humaniores, 1894; B.A., 1894; M.A., 1897; chancellor's prize, Latin essay, 1895; apptd., after compet. exam., to be a clk. in the C.O., Apr., 1895; priv. sec. to Lord Selborne, June, 1897; 1st class clk., 1899.

ELLIS, WILLIAM GILMORE, M.D., M.R.C.S.—Med. supt., lunatic asylum, Singapore, 1888; ag. also as municipal health offr., Singapore, May, 1889, to Feb., 1892, again 1894; ag. col. res. surg. in 1901, 1902 and 1903.

ELY, HERBERT WILLIAM.—Clk. in dept. of agt.-gen. for Tasmania; ch.clk., 1895; sec., 1899; acted as priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir Edward Braddon, P.C., K.C.M.G., ex-premier of Tasmania, during his visit to England for the Diamond Jubilee celebration, 1897.

EMERSON, Geo. H.Y. K.C.—Speaker, house of assem., Newfoundland, 1890-94; mem. of official delegation to London, 1890, and of delegation from legislature in 1891 on the fisheries question; mem. exec. coun., 1895; asst. judge, sup. ct., Nov., 1896.
EMMENS, JOHN HOWARD.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., 11th Mar., 1901.
EMMS, C. B.—Clk., G.P.O., O.R.C., Aug., 1902; clk., treasy., Oct., 1903; asst. acctnt., funds dept., July, 1905.

EMTAGE, O. DE C., M.A.—Ed. Harr. Coll., Barbados; Barbados scholar, 1886; scholar, Worc. Coll., Oxon, 1887; 1st class math. mods., 2nd cls. finals, 1890; hon. nat. sc. schools, 1891; asst. mast., Harr. Coll., 1891; examiner, 2nd grade schools, Barbados, 1891-1902; ditto, gram. school, St. Lucia, 1894-95; ditto, Grenada, 1897-98; head master. The Lodge school, 1899.

EMTAGE, WM. THOS. ALDER, M.A., Oxford; Fell. of Phys. Soc., Lond.; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; scholar, Pembroke Coll., Oxford, 1880; 1st cls. math. mods., 1881; 1st cls. final maths., 1883; 1st cls. nat. sci., 1885; University Coll., Nottingham, math. lecturer, 1885; asst. prof. math. and phys., 1889; prof. maths. and phys., 1891; principal, tech. inst., Wandsworth (L.C.C.), 1895; director of pub. instn., Mauritius, 1900; examnr., Oxford Locals, maths., 1888, 1889; examnr., final hon. school nat. sci., Oxford, 1890, 1891; author of "Mathematics of Electricity" (Oxford Press), and other works.

ENGELBACH, A. H. H., I.S.O. (1902).—B. 1850; apptd., 29th Dec., 1869, after passing an exam. before the civ. ser. comsrs., clk.in the acctns. branch in the office of the sec. of state for the colo., having acted in that capacity since Mar., 1868; 1st class asst. clk., 1st Sept., 1879; bookkeeper and sen. asst. to the financial clk., 1884; acted for financial clk., June, 1886, to Apr., 1887; accountant, Apr., 1896.

ENNIS, GEORGE FRANCIS MACDANIEL.—Ed. at King's Coll. school and privately; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Jan., 1892; sec. to gov', B.N. Borneo, 13th Nov., 1894; session judge, B.N. Borneo and Labuan, 24th Dec., 1894; judge of general ct., Labuan, 1895; judge of chief ct., B.N. Borneo, 14th Aug., 1895; ag. res., Province Alcock, B.N. Borneo, 13th Dec., 1895; resig., Oct., 1897; ag. asst. judge, Zanzibar, 18th May to 7th July, and 1st Aug. to 24th Oct., 1899; registr., E. Africa Prot., 1st Oct., 1899; town mag., Mombasa, 20th July, 1900; ag. judge, E. Africa Prot., 6th Sept., 1900; ag. asst. judge, Zanzibar, 25th Jan., 1901; legal vice-consul, Uganda Prot., 1st Jan., 1902; judge of high ct. of Uganda, 11th Aug., 1902, and judge of H.B.M. Court of Appeal for Eastern Africa.

ENRAGHT-MOONY, F.—Sub-inspr., Basutoland police, Mar., 1884; acted as gov't. sec., Basutoland, 1893-1895; commanded Bech. Prot. native police with rank of capt., 1896; asst. comsrr., Basutoland, 1897; acted as gov't. sec., 1900-1901; mentioned in com.-in-chief's desps., 1901; native comsrr., Zoutpansberg, Aug., 1901; commanded Ft. Edward and Spelonken dist., Northern Transvaal, and acted as intell. offr. until close of war; special comsrr., Swaziland, June, 1902; res. mag. and native comsrr., Swazi-land, 1905.

ESNOUF, E. AMAND.—Dist. mag., Seychelles, 1870; Grand Port, Mauritius, 15 May, 1878; dist. mag., Moka, 1883; jun. dist. mag., Port Louis, Aug., 1884; ag. master sup. ct., May, 1886; and Dec., 1887, to Jan., 1888; ag. sen. dist. mag., Pt. Louis, Mar., 1890, to Mar., 1892; Dec., 1892, to Feb., 1893; May to Aug., 1896; ag. dist. mag., Pt. Louis, 3rd div., Feb., 1898; ag. dist. and stip. mag. Riv. du Rempart, Mar., 1898; dist. mag., Pt. Louis, 3rd div., Mar., 1899; ag. substitute mast. sup. ct., Aug., 1901.
EVANS, SIR DAVID, K.C.M.G. (1892).—B.1849; Lord Mayor of London, 1891-2; raised funds for relief of distress caused by the Mauritius hurricane and the fire at St. John's, Newfoundland.

EVANS, FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1881), C.V.O. (1903).—B. 1849; entered the civ. ser., Admiralty, Somerset house, 13th Dec., 1867; transf'd. to the sec.'s dept. at Whitehall, Jan., 1872; asst. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, May, 1878; priv. sec. to Sir S. Rowe, K.C.M.G., Feb., 1879, to Feb., 1881; ag. clk. of legis. coun., Sept., 1879; acted as col. sec., Dec., 1879, to Feb., 1880; ch. sec. and ch. of the staff to Sir S. Rowe during threatened hostilities with Ashanti, 1881; asst. col. sec., G. Coast, 1882; ag. clk. of customs and treas., Oct., 1882, to Jan., 1883; ag. col. sec., Aug. to Dec., 1883, and on other occasions; clk. of legis. coun., Feb., 1882, to Apr., 1884; dep. gov', Lagos, July to Aug., 1883, and May, 1885, to Jan., 1886; dep. gov', G. Coast, Sept., 1883, and Apr., 1885; col. sec., Lagos, Jan., 1886; ag. admnstr., July, 1886; col. sec., G. Coast, 1887; Leeward Is., 1888; Jamaica, 1896; ag. 1st cl. clk., C.O., Feb., 1900; col. sec., Gibraltar, 1901.
EVANS, HON. JOHN WM., C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1855; M.L.A. for Kingborough, Tasmania, since 1896; warden and master warden of marine bd.of Hobart for many years; premier of Tasmania, July, 1904.

EVANS, WILLIAM.—Cadet, S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1882; ed. King's Coll., Camb.; ag. astt. protector of Chinese, Singapore, Dec., 1885; ag. collr. of land rev., Malacca, May, 1886; 2nd astt. prot. of Chinese, May, 1887; ag. astt. prot. Chinese, Singapore, Feb., 1888; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1888; passed exams. in Chinese and Malay; J.P.; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, 1888; astt. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, June, 1890; ag. prot. of Chinese, Penang, July, 1890; ditto, S. Sttlmts., Nov. and Dec., 1891, to June, 1893; astt. prot. Chinese, Penang, 1893; prot. of Chinese, S. Sttlmts., Apr., 1895; ag. audr.-gen., S.S., May, 1901, to July, 1902; pres., municipal comsrs., Singapore, Sept., 1903; seconded for 6 mos. spec. serv. in Transvaal as prot. of Chinese, 25th Dec., 1903; ag. res. coun., Malacca, Mar., 1906.
EVELYN, EDWARD A.—2nd clk. to regtr., St. Kitts, 1899; govt. offr., Sandy Point, Apr., 1903; govt. offr., Dieppe Bay, St. Kitts, June, 1905; clk., regtr.'s off., May, 1906.

EVELYN, EDWARD ERNEST.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; ent. col. sec.'s off., Barbados, 1883; astt. clk., govt. savings bk., 1883; ch. clk., 1884; rev. offr., St. Lucia, Jan., 1885, to Nov., 1890; accnt., P.W.D., Nov., 1890, to Jan., 1893; audit clk., Jan., 1893, to Mar., 1894; ch. clk., govt. off., and clk. of coun., Apl., 1894; astt. col. sec., S. Leone, Nov., 1900.
EVELYN, E. F.—Acentnt. of post office, Barbados, Sept., 1880.

EVELYN, MERRIT.—2nd clk. to pres., St. Kitts, Dec., 1878; ch. clk., treasy., Nevis, 1881; ag. treas., Nevis, July, 1882-1883; rev. offr. in charge, Nevis, treasy., 1883; prot. of imgrts., Nevis, 1883; land and house tax comsrs., 1892; ag. mag., Nevis, 1894-5, 1895-6; water comsrs., Nevis, 1901; mem. quarantine bd., Nevis, 1903.

EVERARD, W.—Supernum. col. sec.'s office, Barbados, Sept., 1868; landing waiter, customs, Dec., 1868; ag. inspr. of inl. rev. offrs., Jan., 1884, to Dec., 1885; ch. clk., P.O., Mar., 1886; sen. landing waiter, customs, June, 1886.
EVERETT, COL. SIR W., K.C.M.G.—B. 1844; apptd. one of Brit. comsrs. in Paris for settlement of W. African affairs, 1895-1898.
EVERY, THE REV. EDWARD FRANCIS, M.A.—Bishop of the Falklands, 1902.

EWING, HON. THOMAS THOMSON.—M.L.A. for Richmond, N.S. Wales, 1885-94; ditto for Lismore, 1894-1901; some time chrmn. of parl'y. pub. wks. comttee., N.S. Wales; retd. to 1st house of rep., C. of Aust., Mar., 1901; re-elected Dec., 1903; vice-pres. of exec. coun., July, 1905; min. of home affairs, 13th Oct., 1906.
EYLES, C. H.—Asst. col. surg., G. Coast Col., 1883; col. surg., Br. Honduras, 1887.

EZECHIEL, P. H.—B.A., Bombay, 1889 (at 14); first in Woolwich competn., 1893; B.A., London, 1894 (2nd exhibn. at matric., 1892); major schlr., Trin. Coll., Camb., 1896; 4th wrangler and B.A., Camb., 1897; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., 11th Oct., 1898; passed final exam. for Bar, 1902; sec. to Crown agts. for the Colonies, May, 1905.
FABRE, HECTOR, C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1834; formerly senator, Canada; hon. comsrs. for Canada, Ind. and Col. exhibn.; now Canadian agt., Paris.
FACEY, LUTHER HERBERT.—3rd cl. clk., prisons dept., Jamaica, June, 1877; 2nd cl. clk., June, 1879; 1st cl. clk., police and prisons dept., Nov., 1892.

FAGAN, JOSEPH PATRICK.—L.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., L.M., Ireland; dip. trop. med.; dist. med. offr., Niger Coast Prot., 13th Apl., 1894; served in operations agst. Chief Nana, Benin River, 1894, (spec. ment. in desps., medal and clasp); served in operations agst. King of Benin, 1897, and Aro field force 1901 (medal and clasp); senior med. offr., 29th May, 1903; ag. dep. P.M.O., 1905.

FAIRBAIRN, JOHN.—Temporary clk. in leg. coun. off., Cape Town, Apr., 1881 to 30th Apr., 1889; crown lands off., 31st May, 1890, to 31st July, 1891; clk., to regtr. of mines, Prince Albert, 1st Aug., 1891, to 31st May, 1892; leg. coun. off., 1st June, 1892, to 30th Sept., 1893, and 1st Jan. to 31st May, 1899; clk. of the papers and comtce. clk., 1st July, 1899; clk. astt. and taxing offr., 1st Jan., 1904; hon. sec., Central Good Hope Red Cross comttee., Victoria League for concentration camp relief, etc., etc.; is an esquire of the order of St. John of Jerusalem, in England, 1901; S. African War medal and clasp, 1899-1901.

FAIRBAIRN, ROBERT, I.S.O. (1906).—Second master, boys' schl., Perth, W. Australia, 1859; clk. to bench of mags., landing waiter, and postmr., Vasse, 1862; res. mag., mag. of local ct., and dist. regisr., 1875; Newcastle, 1875; Vasse, 1880; Kimberley, 1883; Fremantle, 1886.

FALCK, DAVID GEORGE ANOSI.—Joined post and tel. dept., O.F.S., Nov., 1881; postmr. and telegraphist, Winburg, Nov., 1881; landdrost's clk., Bethlehem, Aug., 1889; ag. landdrost, Heilbron, Apl., 1890; sec., G.P.O., O.F.S., Aug., 1892; landdrost, Ficksburg, 1894 (not assumed); postmr.-gen., O.F.S., 1st Jan., 1895; ditto, O.R.C., Mar., 1900.
FALSHAW, PERCY SCOTT.—M.R.C.V.S., Govt. vet. surgeon, Singapore, S. Sttlmts., 15th June, 1893.
FARRAR, NICHOLAS.—Supernum., P.W. dept., Br. Guiana, 1882; 3rd cls. clk., G.P.O., 1893; 2nd cls. clk., audit dept., 1900; postmr.-gen., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., Jan., 1905.

FARRER, ROLAND J.—Ed. at Eton, and Balliol Coll., Oxon.; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1896; ag. dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, Mar., 1897, to Sep., 1898, Dec., 1898, to Mar., 1900; ag. collr. land rev., Penang, Mar., 1900; ag dist. offr., Dindings, May, 1900; ag. supt. educ., Penang, June to Aug., 1900; head of Malay Coll., Malacca, May, 1901; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec., June, 1901; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, May, 1902; confirmed, July, 1904; ag. dep. pub. proser., July, 1904; resumed duty as dist. offr., B. Mertajam, Feb., 1905.
FAULKNER, E.—Ed. at the gram. schl., Freestown; brigade office clk., S. Leone, 1876 to 1883; ch. clk. col. secretariat, 1883; astt. col. sec., 1889.

FAURE, HON. SIR P. H., K.C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1848; sec. for native affairs, Cape, July, 1890; col. sec., May, 1893; sec. for agricult., Jan., 1896, to Oct., 1898; col. sec., 1902-04; M.L.A. for Namaqualand, 1904.
FAWCETT, WILLIAM, B.Sc. (LOND.), F.L.S.—Asst. botanical dept., Br. Museum, 1880; dir. pub. gardens and plantations, Jamaica, 1886; mem. bd. of govs., Jamaica institute, 1887.

FAWKES, ARCHIBALD WALTER, K.C.—Ed. Repton schl.; B.A., St. John's Coll., Camb.; called to bar, Inner Temp., Jan., 1879; registr., sup. ct., comsrr. of ct. of requests and of stamps, Malacca, 1884; ag. registr., sup. ct., Penang, 1885; pol. mag. and coroner, Gibraltar, 1886; acted as atty.-gen., 1890 and 1891; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, 1892; puisne judge, O.R.C., 1901.
FELDEN, Capt. R. B.—Capt., R.A.; ed. Wellington Coll.; lieut. R.A., July, 1884; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., Barbados, May, 1890; to high comsrr., Cyprus, 1892; and to gov., Br. Guiana, 1898.

FELL, T. E., B.A., Cantab., 1897—Apptd. ch. clk., col. sec.'s off., G. Coast, 2nd Oct., 1897; ag. astt. col. sec., 14th Sep., 1899; inspr., preventive services, 4th Dec., 1899; ag. trav. comsrr., 1900-1901; trav. comsrr., 16th June, 1902; ag. comsrr. for nat. affairs, 26th June, 1902.

FERGUSON, DONALD.—Elected to provincial legislature, Pr. Ed. Is., 1878; apptd. to provl. cabinet in 1879, with the portfolio of pub. wks.; resig. the latter office in 1880, when he was apptd. provl. sec. and comsrr. of pub. lands; served as deleg. to Downing st. in 1886, regarding communication between P. E. Is. and mainland.

FERGUSSON, CLAUD.—Served during S. African war in I.Y. (King's and Queen's medals and clasps, D.C.M.); asst. sec., repatriation comsrr., Philippolis, Aug., 1902; sec., repat. coman., Bethulie, July, 1903; admstr., govt. relief dept., Senekal, Nov., 1903; inspr. of accts., govt. relief dept., O.R.C., May, 1904; astt. acctnt., educn. dept., O.R.C., Sept., 1904; ag. acctnt., ditto, 23rd June to 31st Oct., 1906.

FERGUSSON, RIGHT HON. SR JAMES, BART., P.C., M.P., G.C.S.I. (1885), K.C.M.G. (1874), C.I.E. (1880).—B. 1832; ed. at Rugby and Univ. Coll., Oxford; was lieut. and capt. of the Grenadier Guards; served in the Crimean campaign, including the battles of Alma and Inkerman (wounded) and siege of Sebastopol; was M.P. for Ayrshire, 1854-57 and 1859-68; unsuccessfully contested Sandwich, 1859, Frome, 1875, Greenock, 1878; was under-sec. of state for India and the home depts. in Lord Derby's 3rd, and Mr. Disraeli's 1st admstns.; gov. S. Australia, 1868; and gov. of N. Zealand, 1873; resig. 1874; gov. of Bombay, Mar., 1880, to Mar., 1885; M.P. for Manchester, 1885 and 1886; under-sec. of state foreign affairs 3rd Aug., 1886; postm. gen., 1891-92; d. 1907.
FERNANDEZ, HENRY.—Clerical astt. treas., Br. Guiana, 1887; 5th class clk., 1889; 4th class, 1891.

FERNANDO, MARCUS H.—Ed. Royal Coll., Colombo, and Univ. Coll., Lond.; Ceylon Govt. Univ. and Gilchrist scholar, 1883; B.Sc., 1886; M.B., 1888; gold medallist in med. and forensic med.; M.D., 1889; fellow of Univ. Coll., Lond., 1890; col. surg., civil med. dept., Ceylon; registr. of Ceylon Med. Coll., lecturer on pathology, 1890, and analyst to govt. of Ceylon; reported on epidemics of malarial fever, 1895; also in Bombay, 1897, on epidemics of plague; physician, gen. hosp., Colombo, 1897.
FRERREIRA, P. J., C.M.G. (1880).—Was comdt. of local cavalry in the Transvaal against Sikukuni.
FFOLIOTT, Lieut.-Col. O. HUMAN. Served in Imp. army, 1858-81; mil. sec. col. forces, Cape, June, 1882.
FFRENCH-MULLEN, VINCENT, L.R.C.S.I.—Dist. med. offr., Port Maria, Jamaica, Apr., 1881.

FIDDIES, GEORGE VANDELEUR, C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1905), B.A.—B. 1858; ed. at Dulwich Coll. and late scholar of Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 2nd class mods., 1879; apptd., after compet., be a clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., 25th Mar., 1881; priv. sec. to Earl of Onslow, Feb., 1887; and to Baron H. de Worms (afterwards Lord Pirbright), Feb., 1888, to Aug., 1892; priv. sec. to Sir R. Meade, 11th Mar., 1896; 1st class clk., 1896; imperial sec. and acctnt., to Sir A. Milner, high comsrr. for S. Africa, Sept., 1897; political sec. to Lord Roberts at Pretoria, June, 1900; sec. to the Transvaal administration, Dec., 1900; returned to C. O., 1902, as prin. clk.

FIDDIAN, ALEXANDER.—Ed. Univ. Coll., Cardiff, and Oxford; schlr. Pembroke Coll., 1893; 1st class classical honours, 1895; Goldsmiths co. exhibt., 1895; 1st class lit. hum., 1897; B.A., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd class clk., C.O., Oct. 12th, 1897; astt. priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Alfred Lyttelton, S. of S. for the colonies, 1st Apl., 1905.

FIELDING, HON. WILLIAM STEVENS.—Mem. for Halifax, Nova Scotia assem., 1882; declined premiership, but entered govt. without office in that year, and continued in that position till May, 1884, when he resig.; premier, provincial sec., and treas., Aug., 1884; min. of finance in Sir W. Laurier's admstn., June, 1896.
FINDLAY, HON. J. G., LL.D.—Atty.-gen. and col. sec., New Zealand.

FIRMSTONE, H. W.—Ed. at Rugby and Hertford Coll., Oxon (scholar); cadet, S. Sttlmts., 1890; passed final exam. in Chinese, Dec., 1893; ag. dist. offr., South Malacca, May, 1894; ag. astt. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Jan., 1896; dist. offr. P.W., Oct., 1896; astt. protec. Chinese, Singapore, Feb., 1897; ag. astt. protec. Chinese, supt. Indian imgrts., and mun. comsrr. for Penang, Aug., 1898, to Feb., 1902, and from Aug., 1902; ag. prot. of Chinese, S.S., Feb. to July, 1902; ag. astt. ditto, Penang, Aug., 1902, to Feb., 1903, and from Aug., 1903; collr. of land rev. and offr. in charge of treas., Malacca, Jan., 1905.

FISHER, HON. SIDNEY ARTHUR.—B.A. of Trin. Coll., Camb. He first contested Brome unsuccessfully in 1880. In 1887 he was elected, but was defeated in 1891. Re-elected, in 1896, and became min. of agricul. in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.

FISHER, STANLEY.—Ed. at Westminster and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; M.A., 1891; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple and Lincoln's Inn (called 1890); apptd. pres. of dist. ct. of Kyrena, Cyprus, May, 1902; acted as King's advocate, May to Oct., 1902.

FISHER, WILLIAM WOODHOUSE.—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1877; ag. crown counsel, N. circuit, Ceylon, Jan., 1885, to Feb., 1886; ag. dist. judge, Matare, June, 1886, to Feb., 1887; ag. crown counsel, Kandy, Feb., 1887; crown counsel, N.W. prov., Ceylon, Mar., 1887; sec. to comtce. for drafting code of civ. procedure, June, 1887, to May, 1888; additional crown counsel, N. circuit, Jan., 1890; pres. dist. ct., Kyrena, Cyprus, 1891; ag. puisne judge, 1894; mag. Jamaica, 1895; puisne judge, S. Sttlmts., Apl., 1905.
FITZGERALD, THE HON. G. P.—Mem. of cabinet, Tasmania, without portfolio, 1888-92.

FITZPATRICK, HON. CHARLES.—Born in Quebec, 1853; Dufferin medallist, law faculty, Laval Univ., 1876; one of the counsel for the defence of Louis Riel at Regina in 1888; first returned to the Quebec legis. assem. at the gen. election of 1894, and again in 1896; solr. gen. in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.

FLEISCHER, THODORE T. C.—Out-door offr. of customs and treasy. dept., 1st Aug., 1878; 4th clk., customs and treasy. dept., 25th Oct., 1878; 3rd clk., 22nd June, 1879; 2nd clk., 16th May, 1882; apptd. 1st class clk. in the treas. dept. in 1885, on separation of the customs from treas.; ag. chief clk. and book-keeper, treas., 9th March, 1890, to 10th Jan., 1900; ag. ch. clk. and cashier, 5th June, 1900, to 31st May, 1901.

FLEMING, Sir Francis, K.C.M.G. (1892), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1842; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Nov., 1866; crown solr. for Mauritius, 1869; dist. and stip. mag., 1872; acted as dist. judge, Seychelles, 1874; dist. judge, Jamaica, 1876; atty.-gen., Barbados, 1878; acted as ch. just., Sept., 1878, to Mar., 1879; acted as ch. just., St. Lucia, July, 1879; priv. sec. to Sir G. C. Strahan, administering the govt. of Canada, in 1880; puisne judge, Br. Guiana, 1881; acted as atty.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1882; Queen's advoc., Ceylon, 1883; atty.-gen., 1884; acted as ch. just., Mar. to Dee., 1885; col. sec., Natal, July, 1886, but did not take up apptn., being apptd. col. sec. of Mauritius, Dec., 1886; administd. govt., July, 1887, to Dee., 1888; col. sec. Hong Kong, 1889; administd. the govt., Feb. to Dec., 1890; gov. S. Leone, 1892; ditto, Leeward Is., 1895; ret. 1901.

FLEMING, Sir Sandford, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.E., C.M.G. (1877), LL.D. (St. Andrews, 1884)—B. 1827; engnr.-in-ch., Northern Railway, Canada, 1836 to 1863; deleg. to Canadian and Br. govt. from Red River settlers, 1863, in reference to Pacific Rly.; nominated by provs. of Canada, N. Brunswick and N. Scotia; gov't. engnr.-in-ch. inter-col. rly., 1863; engnr.-in-ch., Canadian Pacific rly., 1871 to 1880; elected chancellor, Queen's Univ., Canada, 1880; re-elected 1883, and again 1886; deleg. from Canadian inst. and Amer. meteorological soc., N. York, to internat. geograph. congress at Venice, 1881; representing the Dominion of Canada at internat. prime meridian confce., Washington, 1884; dir. Hudson Bay Co., Canadian Pacific rly., etc.; author of "The Intercol., an Historical Sketch," 1876, "Old to New Westminster," 1884, memoirs on "A Prime Meridian for all Nations," "Universal Time," and various other wks.; deleg. to col. confce., 1887; representative of Canada, col. confce., Ottawa, 1894; on special mission to Hawaii, 1894, with Mr. Mercer of the C.O., negotiating for landing place for Pacific cable.
FLETCHER, A. G. M.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Trin. Coll., Oxford; cadet, Hong Kong, 1901; ag. asst. registr. gen., 1903-4; ag. asst. col. sec., 1905-6.
FLETCHER, Geo. Wm.—Temporarily employed in Imp. commissariat, Hobart, 1851; clk., col. treasv., Hobart, July, 1852; receiver and paymr., Jan., 1886.

FLETCHER, Stanley Hewitt.—Mem. Inst. Chartd. Acctnts., 1892; 2nd acctnt., Br. C. Africa Prot., June, 1893; collnr., Zomba (dist.), Apl., 1900; British vice-consul and admnstr. agt., Chinde, Jan., 1901 (Cent. Africa medal and clasp).

FLETCHER, Wm., D.S.O., Surgeon, capt. militia med. staff corps; 6 mos. in 1898 attached to regulars at R.A.M.C. depot, Aldershot; med. offr., Niger Coast Prot. (now So. Nigeria), 1898; served with Ashanti expdn., 1900 (medal and clasp); Aro expdn., 1901-2 (medal and clasp).

FLINT, Thomas Barnard, M.A., LL.B., D.C.L.—B. 1847; ed. at Mt. Allison Coll., Sackville, New Brunswick; B.A., 1867; M.A., 1872, and Harvard Univ., Boston; LL.B., 1871; barrister-at-law, 1872; sheriff, Yarmouth Co., 1883-7; asst. clk., House of Assembly, Nova Scotia, 1887 to 1891; elected to H. of C., Canada, 1891; re-elected, 1896 and 1900; clk. of H. of C., Canada, 11th Nov., 1902; recd. degree of D.C.L. from Mt. Allison Univ., 1903.

FLYNN, Edmund James, K.C., LL.D.—Born 1847, at Percé, Gaspé; ed. Quebece Seminary and Laval Univ. (L.L.D. with honours, 1873), LL.D. (Lav., 1878); Q.C. 1887; called to the bar 1873; prof. of Roman law, Laval Univ., since 1874; mem. legis. assem., Quebec, Apr., 1878; mem. exec. coun. and comsnr. crown lands in Chapleau govt., Oct., 1879, to July, 1882; comsnr. of rlys. in Ross govt., Feb., 1884, and solr.-gen., May, 1885.

FORD, Henry Francis.—Entered G.P.O., Cape, 1868; promoted to deeds office, 1872; apptd. to inaugurate deeds office at Kokstad, and served as clk. to ch. mag. Transkei; in charge of deeds office, King William's Town, 1884; ch. clk., insolveney branch, master's office, Capetown, Jan., 1888; registr. and mstr. high ct., Griqualand, July, 1890.

FORD, Joseph Charles.—Ed. in France (Académie de Douai), and by private tuition by Dr. Langley at Wolverhampton; sec. inst. of Jamaica, 1889; one of the comsnrs. of Jamaica internat. exhib., 1891, and spec. comsnr. for Bahamas at that exhib.; apptd. superint. of govt. printing off., Feb., 1891; co-ed. and compiler of "Handbook of Jamaica."

FORD, Sir Theodore Thomas, KT. Bach. (1888).—B. 1829; called to the bar, Middle Temp., 1866; puisne judge, S. Sttlmts., Mar., 1874; ag. judge of Penang, 1874, to Apr., 1876; resig. and returned to England; re-apptd. senior puisne judge, 1876; ch. justice, 1886; ret., 1889.

FORDE, Robert Michael.—Asst. col. surg., Gold Coast, Nov., 1891; ag. dist. comsnr., Axim, 1892; on special service on Anglo-French bndy., comsnr., Feb. to July, 1892; med. offr. special mission to Kumasi, Dec., 1894, to Jan., 1895; col. surg., Gambia, Feb., 1895.

FOREMAN, John.—Mont., France, L.K.Q.C.P.I. and L.M.; med. offr., Virgin Is., 1878; ag. mag., Virgin Is., 1880; M.L.C., 1880, and mem. ex. coun., 1885; ag. med. offr., Dominica, 1882-1884; med. offr., Dieppe Bay, St. Kitts, 1889-1893; med. supt. leper asylum and Pogson hosp., and med. offr., dist. 5, Sandy Point, St. Kitts, 1893; surg.-capt., St. Kitts-Nevis def. force.

FORESTIER-WALKER, Gen. Sir Frederic William Edward Forestier, G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.B. (1894), C.B. (1878), C.M.G. (1886), Knight of Grace of St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1844; ed. at Sandhurst; entd. Scots. Guards, 1862; capt. and lieut.-col., 1873; col., 1878; maj.-gen., 1887; lieut.-gen., 1895; gen., 1902; served on staff in Kafr war, 1877-8; mil. sec. to Sir Bartle Frere, 1878-9; in Zulu war, 1879 (ment. in desps.); A.A. and Q.M.G., home district during war in Egypt, 1882; A.A. and Q.M.G., Bechuanaland, 1884; commdd. 2nd brig. at Aldershot, 1889-90; commdd. troops in Egypt, 1890-95; western dist., 1895-99; and troops in S. Africa, 1899; commdd. lines of communication in S. African war, 1899-1901; and troops in Cape Colony, 1900-1; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Gibraltar 1st Aug., 1905.

FORGET, Amédée Emmanuel.—Ed. Marieville Coll., Canada; called to the bar, 1871; served as sec. to council of the bar, Quebec; clk. of coun. and priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., N.W. Territ., 1876; asst. comsnr. of Indian affairs for Manitoba and N.W.T., 1888; mem. of coun. of pub. instn., 1893; Indian comsnr., 1895; lieut.-gov., N.W.T., 11th Oct., 1898; lieut.-gov. of Saskatchewan, 1st Sept., 1905.

FORREST, THE RIGHT HON. SIR JOHN, P.C., G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1891), LL.D. (Camb.), F.R.G.S., F.G.S., F.L.S., C.M.G. (1882).—B.1847; Hon. Fellow of the Italian geog. socy.; Hon. Fellow of the imp. geog. societies of Vienna and St. Petersburg; knight of the Italian crown; entered the survey dept. of W. Australia, 1865; commanded exploring expeds. in 1869 into the interior in search of the remains of Dr. Leichardt; in 1870 from Perth to Adelaide along the S. Coast; and in 1874 from Champion Bay, on the W. Coast; to the overland telegraph between Adelaide and Port Darwin, a journey of nearly 2,000 miles. For these services received thanks of gov. and legis. couns., and gold medal of the Roy. Geog. Socy. of London, 22nd May, 1876, and a grant in fee of 5,000 acres of crown land; dep. survr.-gen. of W. Australia, 1876; in 1878 and 1882 conducted the trigonometrical surveys of the Nichol Bay district, and the Gascoyne and Lyons District, in N.W. Australia, 1878; acted as comsnr. of crown lands and survr.-gen.; comsnr. of crown lands and survr.-gen. of W. Australia, 1883 to Dec., 1890, during which time was mem. of the exec. and legis. couns.; in Mar., 1883, reported on the Kimberley dist.; in Dec., 1890, was sent for to form first ministry under responsible govt., in which he took the position of premier and treas., which he continuously held for over 10 yrs., resigning on 13th Feb., 1902, to join the 1st Commonwealth Govt. of Australia; was one of the representatives of W. Australia at the National Australian federation convention, held at Sydney, 1891; author of "Explorations in Australia," 1865; "Notes on Western Australia," 1883, 1884, and 1885; deleg. to col. confce., 1887; represented W. Australia at the federal convention at Adelaide, Mar., 1897, and in June of the same year was present as rep. of the col. in London at H.M.'s Diam. Jub.; again rep. W. Australia at fed. convention, Sydney, 1897; Melbourne, 1898; joined 1st Commonwealth ministry as postmaster-general, Jan., 1901; defence minister, 1901 to 1903; and min. for home affairs until Apr., 1904; treas. of Commonwealth, July, 1905.

FORTH, J. OGLIVIE.—Audit clk., Fiji, Aug., 1883; acctntt. native taxes and clk. nativ. accts., June, 1889; ch. clk. audit office, Oct., 1890; col. auditor, Nov., 1891; Oct., 1890, proceeded to Tonga to audit accts. of Tongan govt., for which he received thanks of King George Tubou's cabinet.

FOSBERY, WIDENHAM FRANCIS WIDENHAM, C.M.G. (1905).—B. 1869; cons. agt., Niger Coast Prot., 1893; ag. vice-cons., 1894-5; dist. comsnr., 1896; ag. div. comsnr., 1897-8; pol. offr., central division expdn., 1898 (severely wounded); res., Benin City, 1898; pol. offr., Bonin Terr. expdn., 1899 (medal with clasp); ag. div. comsnr., 1900; pol. offr., Ishan expdn., 1901 (medal with clasp); -div. comsnr., 1902; pol. offr., Asaba Hint. expdn., 1902; senior div. comsnr., 1903; pol. offr., Igarra expdn., 1903 (clasp); ag. high comsnr., 1903-4; ag. sec. to adminsn., 1904; dep. high comsnr., Sept., 1904, to Feb., 1905, and again in Sept., 1905; prov. comsnr., 1906; ag. col. sec., S. Nigeria, May to June, 1906; ag. gov., June to Aug., 1906; dep. gov. and ag. col. sec., Aug. to Oct., 1906.

FOSTER, E. ALEXANDER, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1846; ed. at Kingswood Coll., Bath, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; clk., audit office, Jamaica, Mar., 1866; 2nd clk., May, 1868; ch. clk., island med. office, June, 1882; sec. of cent. bds. of health and leg. couns., Antigua; J.P., ag. col. sec., Leeward Is., Sept., 1897, Sept. to Oct., 1899, Nov., 1899, to Apr., 1900, and Aug. to Oct., 1901; admntr., Antigua, in Jan., 1898, Dec., 1899, and in Jan., Feb., Mar., 1900, also in June, July, Aug. and Sept., 1905, under dormant comsn.; holds dormant comsnr. to administer govt. of Leeward Is. in certain events.

FOSTER, THE HON. GEO. E., B.A., D.C.L.—Ed. common and superior schls., King's County, New Bruns.; Douglas gold medallist, Univ. N.B., 1866; B.A., 1868; medallist, ancient rhetoric, Edin., 1873; prof. classics, Univ. N.B., 1873-9; returned to parlmt., 1882; min. of marine and fisheries for Dominion, Dec., 1888; min. of finance, 1888 to 1896; represented Canada at col. confce., Ottawa, 1894.
FOSTER, RICHARD WITTY, M.L.A., S. Aust., 1893.—Comsnr. of pub. wks., 1899; ditto, and min. of industry, 1902-1904; ditto, and min. of agric., 1st March to 26th July, 1905.
FOSTON, EDMUND CHRISTOPHER.—L.R.C.P. (Edin.), L.F.P.S. (Glas.); col. surg., prov. Wellesley (South), S. Sttlmts., 26th Aug., 1891; supt. S. S. emig. depot, Negapatam, 17th Nov., 1900.
FOWLER, CHAS. W., C.M.G. (1890).—Late R.N.; asst. collr., Uganda Prot., 7th July, 1896; supt. of marine, 1st Apr., 1898; sub-comsnr., 1st Apr., 1904.

FOWLER, FRANK.—Asst. clk., pub. works dept., Brit. Guiana, Feb., 1878; 3rd clk., Jan., 1881; 2nd clk., Apl., 1884; 1st clk. and draftsman, gov't. ld. dept., Nov., 1884; 3rd gov't. survr., May, 1886; senr. gov't. survr., Apl., 1896; apptd. J.Y., Nov., 1900; asst. comsnr. of lands and mines, Apl., 1902; comsnr. of lands and mines, 1st Apl., 1903; has held numerous acting appointments.

FOWLER, GEO. MERRICK, C.M.G. (1905).—Ed. Chelt. Coll.; writer, Ceylon service, Jan., 1874; pol. mag., Galagedara, Sept., 1876; landing survr., Galle, Feb., 1878; pol. mag., Navalapitiya, Jan., 1880; asst. collr. customs, Galle, June, 1880; dist. judge, Matara, May, 1886; asst. gov't. agt., Matara, 1887; ditto, Kalutara, 1888; ditto, N'Elhiya, 1891; ditto, Trincomalee, 1895; apptnt. confirmed, Dec., 1895; ag. dist. judge, Kalutara, May, 1897, Negombo, Oct., 1897; ag. prin. asst. col. sec., July, 1897; gov't. agt., Ratnapura, June, 1898; ag. gov't. agt., W. prov., June, 1899; gov't. agt., S. prov., Feb., 1900; gov't. agt., and fiscal, W. prov., Mar., 1902; ag. aud.-gen., Apr., 1905; ag. col. sec., Sept., 1905; ag. aud.-gen., Dec., 1905.
FOWLIS, HENRY GEORGE.—Entd. judicial dept., Gambia, 1890; gov't.'s off., 1893-1901; col. sec.'s off., 1902-05; ch. clk. and acctnt., P.W.D., 1906.

FOX, HENRY WILSON, B.A.—Ed. Charterhouse, Marlborough Coll., Univ. Coll., Lond., Trin. Coll., Cam.; exhibtn., Trin. Coll., 1882; foundation scholar, 1883; natural sci. trip.; exhibtn., Lincoln's Inn, 1888; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1888; priv. sec. to Sir C. Mills, K.C.M.G., 1887 to 1889; admitted to practise in sup. ct., Cape Colony, and high ct., So. Rhodesia, 1894; pub. prosecutor, So. Rhodesia, 1894-1897; manager B.S.A. Co., head office, London, 1898; served during 1896-97 in the Matabeleland and Mashona land rebellion (medal and clasp and ment. in deeps.).
FOX, WALTER.—Asst. supt., botanic gardens, Singapore, 16th June, 1879; supt., forests and gardens, Penang, 7th Dec., 1903.

FOXON, Cuthbert Colenso.—Cik. and Zulu interp. to asst. comsnr. and res. mag. Ndandwe dist., Zululand, Sept., 1887; transfd. to Entonjaneni dist., Apl., 1891; to Lower Umfolosi dist., Feb., 1892; ag. res. mag. there Mar., 1892; ag. sub-inspr. Z'land. pol. for six months, May, 1892, and Apl., 1893; transfd. as cik. and Zulu interp. to Nkandhla dist. Nov., 1892; sub-inspr. Z'land. pol., Apl., 1893; commanded detachment Z'land. pol. with the special comsn. annexation of Sambanans and adjacent territories, May, 1895; Br. res. Amatongaland, Br. Protectorate, June, 1896; deputy comsnr., Sept., 1896; res. mag., Z'land., Dec., 1897; mag., Nkandhla, 1901; J.P. for colony, 1904; mag., Umlalazi, 1905.

FOXON, Frank Ernest.—3rd class clk. civ. serv., Natal, and 2nd clk. and Zulu interp., Umgeni div., Nov., 1880; ag. clk. of ct., regisr. circuit ct., and sub-distributor of stamps, Weenen country, 1886; clk. of ct., dep.clk. of peace, and sub-distributor of stamps, UpperUmkomangti div., May, 1887; admstr. native law, Impendlele, Mar., 1889; ditto, Inanda Location, Jan., 1890; ag. mag., Lower Tugela div., Apr. to June, 1890; ag. R.M. at Verulam and Stanger, 1890 to 1894; R.M. for col., and mag., Ndwedwe div., July, 1894; J.P. for col., 1893; served at commencement of Zulu war, 1879, in imp. transport dept., afterwards as lieut. Natal N. pioneers (medal and clasp); served as levy leader in command of Sibepeu's contingent and Yamela's mounted men against Dinuzulu in Zululand, 1888; lieut., Natal carabiners, Apr., 1889, capt., Dec., 1897; mag. Ixopo div., 1897; served through the Boer war, 1899-1900, including the siege of Ladysmith.

FRANCE, Henry Dunlop.—Rev. offr., Anguilla, 1896; govt. offr., St. Kitts, 1897; rev. offr., Nevis, 1899; tariff clk., treasy., St. Kitts, 1901; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1902; supervisor of cust., G. Coast, Sept., 1904.

FRANCIS, Col. Claude Augustus.—Ed. Craven Coll. and Vienna; entd. Impl. Yeo., 1875; C.M.R., 1876; S. African War (medal with clasp) 1877-8; inspr. constab., and armed pol. and J.P., Br. Guiana, 1880; higher grade, 1883; county inspr., 1891; acted as dep. inspr-gen., 1892, and from Feb., 1895, to Apr., 1896; ag. inspr-gen., May to Oct., 1896; sup't fire brig., 1895-6; mem. excise, quarantine, and health bds.; author of "A Manual for the Use of Police and Constabulary," "A Criminal Code," 1895, and "A Police Catechism," 1898; sheriff-commdt., armed constab., sup't civ. pol. and J.P., Fiji, 1897; marshal, V.-A. ct.; commdt., European vol. force, which he raised; localcol., 1898; M.L.C., 1905; inspr.-gen. of constab., 1906.

FRANCIS, Cyril Gerard Brooke.—Cik., G.P.O., Fiji, 1898; ditto, treasy., 1899; lieut., armed constab., 1901; in command detachment, Nadarivatu, 1902; ag. adjut., A.N.C., and inspr. pol., 1903; P.S. certif., schl. instruc., Chelsea bks.; passed course musketry and Maxim gun, Hythe, 1905.

FRANKLIN, William.—Asst. regisr., natives, Kimberley and De Beers, Mar., 1877; regisr. deaths, Kimberley and De Beers, 1878; clk. to civil comsnr. and res. mag., Barkly West, Jan., 1879; issuer of passes, Mar., 1879; inspr. of claims, Barkly West, June, 1879; inspr. of Sivonell's diggings, May-June, 1882; from Oct., 1882, to Aug., 1883, off. valuator of all diamonds exported from the dist. of Barkly West; apptd. regisr. of claims, Barkly West, Sept., 1883; inspr. and regisr. mines and diggings, Barkly West.

FRASER, Charles A.—Ed. at Wellington Coll.; entd. 54th regt., Jan., 1875; resigned, 1877; in colonial forces in S. Africa, 1878 (medal and clasp); asst. inspr., G. Coast constab., 1882; dist. comsnr., 1889; treas., etc., Falkland Is., 1891; col. sec., Falkland Is., 1893; comdt. and prov.-marshal, Bahamas, 1894.

FRASER, Duncan Cameron.—B. 1845; B.A., 1872; called to the bar, 1873; mem. legis. coun., Nova Scotia, 1878; resig. in same year; gov't. leader in legis. coun., and mem. of exec. coun., 1888; elected to H.of C.as mem. for Guysborough, 1891; re-elected 1896; lieut.-gov. of Nova Scotia, 27th Mar., 1906.

FRASER, F. Blache.—2nd clk. atty.-gen.'s office, Trinidad, Jan., 1865; promoted to col. sec.'s dept. as 3rd clk., Sept., 1867; 3rd landing waiter, customs dept., Jan., 1875; principal landing waiter, Jan., 1883; ch. clk. customs dept., Apr., 1894.
FRASER, J. J., K.C.—Atty.-gen. and premier, N. Brunswick; ret., 1882; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1883.

FRASER, Malcolm A. C.—Ed. at King Edward's schl., Bromsgrove; probation clk. in col. sec.'s office, W. Australia, Apr., 1876; 2nd clk., land and survey dept., June, 1876; transfd. to col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1877; clk. and assist. priv. sec. to gov', July, 1878; also asst. clk. to the exec. coun.; clk. to gov', Sir W. C. F. Robinson, K.C.M.G., Apr., 1880; corresp. clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1881; also meteorological reporter; regisr.-gen., 1891; also regisr. of patents, trade mks., etc.; represented the gov't. of W. Aust. at the conference of gov't. statisticians apptd. to arrange for a uniform census of Australia, Sydney, Jan., 1900; supt. of census, Western Australia, 1900; gov't. statistician, 1901; represented W.A. at conference of statisticians convened to arrange for uniformity in the collection and compilation of statistics throughout the Commonwealth of Aust.

FRASER, Major-Gen. Sir Thomas, K.C.B. (1900), R.E., C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1840; apptd. C.M.G. for civ. services in connection with the settlement of the Transvaal question; served in expdrny. force in Egypt, 1882, and in the Nile expdrny. force, 1884, with brevet rank 4th class Osmanieh, and 3rd class of col.; Medjidie.

FRASER, William.—King's student, Aberdeen Univ., 1899-1901; M.A., ditto, 1901; privy coun. certif., 1903; ass't mast., Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, 1901-3; prin., govt. schl., Senekal, 1903-4; inspr. of schl., O.R.C., 1st July, 1904.

FREER, Gerald Dudley, L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), 1888. M.B. (Lond.), 1898.—House surg. gen. hosp., Birmingham; house surg. gen. hosp., Singapore, July, 1890; col. surg. Malacca, Feb., 1893; col. surg. res. Penang, Jan., 1897; ag. col. surg., Penang, June, 1900; col. surg., Singapore, 1st Mar., 1901; principal, med. schl., S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1905; mem. S.S. med. coun., July, 1905.

FREMANTLE, Admiral the Hon. Edmund Robert, R.N., Sir, G.C.B., C.M.G. (1874).—B. 1836; apptd. to the Order of St. M. and St. G. for service during the Ashanti war, 1873-74, having been some time sen. naval offr. on the station.

FRENCH, Maj.-Gen., Sir George Arthur, R.A., K.C.M.G. (1902), C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1841; ed. at Sandhurst and Woolwich; joined R.A. as lieut. in 1860; proceeded to North America in Dec., 1861, with expeditionary force sent out in consequence of the "Trent Affair"; adjt. R.A., Kingston, from 1862 to 1866; qualified as 1st class gunnery instructor in 1867; 1st class inspr. of warlike stores in 1868; apptd. I.S.W. at Quebec in 1869 on the withdrawal of the Imp. troops; inspr. of artillery, with rank of lieut.-col., in 1870; organised the permanent batteries of artillery in 1871; comsnr. N.W.M.P., and stip. mag. for the territories, in Dec., 1873; raised, organised, and equipped the force; commanded the expdn. sent from the Red River to the base of the Rocky Mountains in 1874; inspr. of warlike stores, Devonport, 1878 to 1883; comdt. Queensland forces, with rank of col., 1883-91; comdt., N. S. Wales forces, 1896.

FRENCH, JOHN K.—Cltk., poor law bd., Sandy Point, Mar., 1894; clk., P.O., St. Kitts, Nov., 1897; clk. to registr. and prov.-marshal, Nevis, Sept., 1901; govt. offr., sub-treas., Sandy Point, St. Kitts, June, 1905.
FRENCH, STIR S. R., K.C.M.G. (1901), C.M.G., (1896).—B. 1849; sec. and acctnt., G.P.O., Cape, Aug., 1880; controller P.O. savings bk., 1884; postmr.-gen., 1892.
FRERE, AUBREY TEMPLE.—Cadet, Sarawak service, Oct., 1886; asst. res., May, 1890; res., 2nd class, 4th div., Jan., 1894.

FRERE, BARTLE HENRY TEMPLE, LL.B.—Ed. at Charterhouse school and Trin. Coll., Cam.; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1887; pres. dist. court, Cyprus, 1897; transfld. to Gibraltar as pol. mag. and coroner, 1902.

FRERE, HAROLD ARTHUR.—Cadet, Br. North Borneo Co.'s serv., Nov., 1897; asst. supt. of goals, 2nd May, 1898; ag. supt. of pub. wks., 2nd May, 1898; ag. P.M.G., 16th Sept., 1898; sec. to the comsrs., 2nd Aug., 1899; ag. supt. of goals, 22nd Jan., 1900; editor "British North Borneo Herald" and manager, govt. printing off., 8th May, 1900; inspr. of prisons, 1st Dec., 1901; resig., to take up apptmnt. as supt., Georgetown prison, Br. Guiana, June, 1906.

FRETTZ, W. H.—L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., Edin.: med. offr., dist. 2, Nevis, Feb., 1882; dist. 3, S. Kitts, May, 1886; dist. 1, S. Kitts, Aug., 1896; official mem. legis. coun., S. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1896; offr. mem., leg. coun., St. K.-N., Nov., 1896, to Dec., 1900; senior med. offr., health offr. and analyser of vital statistics, Feb., 1903; mem., Basseterre town bd., 1903; offr. M.L.C., 1903.

FROST, THE HON. SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1904); C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1828; M.L.A. C. of G. H., field comdt. of Cape vols., and late of C.M.R.; sec. for agriculture, Cape Col., 1893-6; ditto, 1902-1904; M.L.A. for Queenstown, 1904.

FROUDE, ASHLEY A., B.A., C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1863; ed. Westminster and Oriel Coll., Oxon; priv. sec. (unpaid) to Sir Robert Herbert at the C.O., Nov., 1886; sec. to roy. comsn. for the division of Malta into electoral dists., Jan., 1888; sec. to the Behring Sea comsn., June, 1891, and to the joint Behring Sea comsn. at Washington, Jan., 1892; on the staff of the Br. agt., Behring Sea arbitration, 1892-3.

FRY, R. S.—Trigonometrical asst., survey dept., S. Sttlmtns, Jan., 1881; res. Aug., 1884; rejoined July, 1885; dist. survr., Sept., 1885; ch. survr., Dec., 1888; observer for time balls and dep. registr. of shipping, Jan., 1894.

FRY, WM. HERBERT, L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.).—House surgeon, S. Sttlmtns, 13th Aug., 1896; supernumerary col. surg., Siug., 1st Apr., 1900; col. surg., prov. Wellesley, South, 17th Nov., 1900; ditto, prov. Wellesley, North, 1st Mar., 1901; state surg., Pahang, F.M.S., June, 1905.
FULLARTON, R.—Harbourmr., Melbourne, 1877; ch. harbourmr., pres. pilot bd., and chmn. steam navig. bd., 1882; capt. comdg. Victorian naval brigade, 1871; mem. of coun. of defence, 1884.
FULLER, A. J.—M.L.A. for Tembulan, Cape Colony, since 1898; sec. for agric., Feb., 1904.

FULLER, F. C. F. D., C.M.G. (1906).—Cadet, Fiji, 1884; res. comsnr., Rotuman, 1889; dis. comsnr., Lagos, 1892; res. of Ibadan, 1897; col. treas., Lagos, 1901; asst. sec. to govt., Malta 1902; mem. exec. coun.; ch. comsnr., Ashanti, 1905.

FULLER, SIR THOS. EKINS, K.C.M.G. (1904); C.M.G. (1903).—B. 1831; emigtn. agt. in Lond. for Cape of Good Hope, 1872; resigned, 1876; served as mem. of house of ass. for 23 years; agent-gen. in Lond. for Cape of Good Hope, 1st Jan., 1902; J.P., Cape Town; has served on various councils and comtees, in Cape Colony.
FURLONG, L. O'B. M.H.A., St. John's, E. Newfild., 1893; mem. exec. coun. and chmn. bd. of wks., Apr. to Dec., 1894; M.H.A., St. John's, E., 1897; cashier, govt. savings bank, 1905.

FYSH, THE HON. SIR PHILIP OAKLEY, K.C.M.G., (1896).—B. 1833; treas. in Mr. Kenmersley's ministry, Tasmania, 1873-5, and without portfolio to July, 1876; premier without office, Aug., 1877, to Mar., 1878, and member without office of Mr. Giblin's 1st administration; maj. comdg. Tasmanian volr. rifle regt. 1880-4; again premier, Mar., 1887, to Aug., 1892; treas. in the Braddon Ministry, 1894-9; agt.-gen. for the col. in London, 1899-1900; elected to 1st H. of R. for Tasmania, 1901, and for Denison div., 1903; min. without portfolio in 1st Commonwealth govt., 1901-3; and postmr.-gen. till Apr., 1904.
FYSON, PERCIVAL WILLIFRID.—Asst. govt. printer, Perak, 1896-99; head printer, Br. Cent. Africa Prot., July, 1899.
GABRIEL, J. S., M.R.C.S.E.—Med. offr., Antigua, dist. 6, 1881.

GAGNON, CHARLES ANTOINE ERNEST.—Ed. St. Ann's Coll.; notary public, 1869; since 1885 pres. of Quebec board of notaries; elected to Quebec legislature, 1878-81, and since 1883; prov. sec., 1887; sheriff of Quebec, 1890.

GALE, CHARLES HENRY.—Assoc. M.I.C.E.; articled pupil, 1882-5; asst. and resident engr., 1890; ch. draughtsman P.W. dept., Hong Kong, Sept., 1890; asst. engr., Jan., 1900, and ag. exec. engr., May, 1900; exec. engr., Feb., 1901; J.P., 1914.

GALE, WALTER A.—Ed. high schl., Perth, St. Peter's Coll., Adelaide, and Exeter Coll., Oxon; 3rd master, high schl., Perth, 1885; asst. registr., col. sec.'s office, W. Australia, 1886; sec. to cent. bd. of educn., 1888; registr.-gen.; registr. of patents, etc., Sept., 1890; in charge of census for 1891; clk. of legis. assen., 1891; transfld. to Commonwealth govt., 1901.
GALEA, HENRI.—Master, sup. ct., and registr., Mauritius, 4th Feb., 1899.

GALLWEY, LT.-COL. HENRY LIONEL, C.M.G. (1890), D.S.O. (1896).—B. 1859; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sandhurst; entered army, May, 1878; promoted lieut., Mar., 1881; capt., Oct., 1887; brevet-maj., May, 1897; maj., Mar., 1899; and lt.-col., Apr., 1901; apptd. dep. commr. and consul, Niger Rivers prot., Mar., 1891; dep. commr. and consul, Niger Coast prot., Jan., 1897; and divn. commr. Niger Coast prot. (now South. Nigeria), Apr., 1899; ag. consul-gen. Niger Coast prot., Aug. to Nov., 1896; Jan., 1897; Feb., 1898, to Jan., 1899; ag. high comsnr., South Nigeria, Mar. to Dec., 1900; Brass exped., 1895 (deeps. medal with clasp, D.S.O.); Benin exped., 1897 (deeps., clasp, brevet majority); Aro exped. as chief political officer, 1901-2 (deeps.); gov., St. Helena, 1902.

GALLWEY, Sir Michael Henry, K.C.M.G. (1883), C.M.G. (1883).—B. 1826; ed. Trin. Coll., Dub.; B.A.; barrister, King's Inns, Dub., Trin. term, 1855; Munster circuit; atty.-gen., Natal, 1857; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; pres. Transvaal and Zululand bdry. comsn., 1878; received the thanks of the sec. of state; ch. just., Natal, 1890; ret., 1902.

GARDINER, John.—Entd. govt. ser., S. Austral., in ch. sec.'s off., 1866; treas., 1871; clk., engrn.-in-chief's dept., 1874; acctnt., 1876; ch. acctnt., 1888; sec. comsrs. P.W., and chrmn. supply and tender bd., 1899; transf. to serv. of Commonwealth of Aust. as pub. serv. inspr., 1902.

GARDNER, Harry.—Major (ret.), Roy. Scots; served in Bechuanaland expdn., 1884, and Zululand, 1888; ag. col. sec. and rec. gen., St. Helena, 1886-7; ag. treas., Zululand, 1890; confirmed, 1891; acctnt. offr. for prov. of Zululand, 1891; inspr., audit dept., 1899.

GARLAND, Patrick Joseph.—Apptd. a sen. med. offr., Northern Territories, 1899; served on Fra Fra expdn., under Captain Donald Stewart, June and July, 1899; specially mentioned in despatches; served on Neutral Zone expdn., Feb., 1900; mentioned in despatches; served on Fra Fra expdn., Mar., 1900; served during Ashanti rebellion and siege of Kumasi; acted as P.M.O. to column during retirement of Sir F. M. Hodgson, governor; specially mentioned in despatches; apptd. dist. comsnr. of Axim Dist., 1901.
GARLING, Henry Chas.—Supt.'s asst., Skerrett's farm and school, Antigua, 18th Apr., 1894; ag. asst. supt. of agric., Feb., 1904; 2nd outdoor offr., treas., Feb., 1905.

GARNEAU, Pierre.—Merchant and mem. of bd. of trade, Quebec; late gov't. dir. of North Shore railway; mayor, 1870-73; M.L.A., Quebec, 1873-78 and 1881-86; mem. of prov. exec. coun. and ministry as comsnr. of agricult., pub. wks., and crown lands, 1874-78; M.L.C., Quebec, 1887, and again comsnr. of agricult., pub. wks., and crown lands to 1891.

GARRARD, C. G.—Ed. Haileybury; dep. regir. sup. ct., Penang, Jan., 1892; ag. dep. regir. sup. ct., Singapore, Feb. to Oct., 1893; ag. regir. sup. ct., Penang, July, 1894; ag. dep. regir. sup. ct., and mag., Malacca, Apr., 1895; also ag. colfr., land rev., and offr. in charge of treas., Malacca, Aug., 1895; ag. dep. regir. sup. ct., Penang, Mar., 1896; asst. regir. and mag., Malacca, 1896.

GARRAWAY, David G., I.S.O. (1906).—Entered the provost-marshal's office, Grenada, in Jan., 1867; ch. elk., col. sec.'s office, 1868; elk. of couns., 1868 to 1878; dep. sec., regir., and elk. of the crown, and sec. bd. of educn., 1871 to 1878; comsnr. of census, 1871; ag. auditor, 1871 to 1872, and 1875; sec. to exec. comtees, 1875-6; ag. treas., 1876-7-9, 1880-1-2; and ex officio a mem. of the legislature; auditor, 1878; notary public, 1880; compiler of census, 1881; ag. pol. mag., St. Vincent, 1883-4; treas., St. Lucia, July, 1884; mem. ex. and leg. couns.; performed duties of col. sec. in conjunction with his own during visit of gov.-in-ch., 1889 and 1896; admstrd. govt., part of Feb., 1899; comp. of customs; Br. Guiana, Sept., 1900; mem. of excise bd.; cent. bd. of hth. and quar. bd., div. pub. offrs. guar. fund.

GARRAWAY, D. Julian.—Entd. pub. serv., St. Lucia, Mar., 1892, as supernumy. clk., treasy.; ag. 4th clk., Nov., 1892; clk. P.O., June, 1893; ag. clk. to adminstr.-in-chief, Windward Is., Aug., 1893; 2nd clk. registr.'s off., June, 1895; ch. clk., Jan., 1898; ag. registr., sheriff and adminstr.-gen., on 3 occasions, 1901-2-3; rev.offr., Grenada, June, 1903; ag. pol. mag., West Dist., Feb., 1904; additional coroner, West Dist., Mar., 1904; ag. pol. mag., North Dist., for spec. purpose of hearing appeals agst. town bd. assessmnts for 1904; asst. tax offr. in connectn. with hearing appeals agst. assessmnts. for land and house taxes for 1904; ag. pol. mag., West Dist., May and June, 1905; ag. comsnr., Carriacou, Aug., Sept., 1906; ag. road surv., West Dist., Oct., 1906, in addition to duties of substantive office.

GARRAWAY, E. C. F., L.R.C.S.I.—Dist. surg., Millwood, Knysna, Cape Colony, 1888; ditto, Kuruman, Brch., 1891; surg., Bech. Bord., pol., 1892; divsln. med. offr., S.A.C., with rank of major, 1901; prin. med. offr., ditto, Mar., 1905; served in Mataele wars and Boer war (1899-1902).
GARRAWAY, R. F.—Postmr., Dominica, June, 1881; sec. bd. of hth. and quar. bd., Sept., 1881; ag. dist. mag., dist. G., Apr., 1893.

GARRETT, Herbert Leonard Offley.—B. 1881; Ed. at Charterhouse and St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1902 (2nd cls. hons., class. tripods); ast. mast., The Lodge, Barbados, 1903-4; jun. ast. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1904.

GARRETT, Mark.—In F.A.M.P., Cape, July, 1876, to Nov., 1879; clk. to C.C. and R.M. King William's Town, Sept., 1880; clk. col. sec.'s office, Capetown, May, 1884; sec. civ. ser. coman., Mar., 1886; prin. clk., atty.-gen.'s office, 1895; sub-comsnr., war losses compensation comsn., Mar. to Sept., 1903; census staff, col. sec.'s office, prin. clk., pol. branch, atty.-gen.'s office, Aug., 1904.

GARRICK, The Hon. Sir J. F., K.C., K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1836; sec. for public lands and mines, Queensland, 6th Feb., 1878; atty.-gen., Dec., 1878, to Jan., 1879; col. treas. and postmr.-gen., 13th Nov. to Dec., 1883, when he resig. the former office; agt.-gen. for col. in London, June, 1884, to June, 1888; was deleg. to col. confce., 1887; again agt.-gen., 1890-5; d. 1907.

GATT, C.—Supt. govt. printing office, Malta, Feb., 1882; was priv. sec. to Sir Richard Wood, H.M.'s diplomatic agt. and consul-gen., Tunis, Sept., 1868, to May, 1871; ast. sec., Gozo, 1892; rec.-gen., 1899; audr.-gen., 1902; mem. of ex. coun. and of coun. of govt., 1903.

GATT, L., C.M.G. (1901), C.E.—Entered the Malta ser., Mar., 1883, as land survr.; rly. inspr., 1884; ast. engrn., waterwks., 1885; survr. P.W.D., 1888; manager and engrn., Malta rly., 1895; ch. engrn., waterwks., and elect. lighting dept., 1896; supt. of P.W., with a seat in exec. coun., and a seat in coun. of govt., 1897.

GATTY, Sir Stephen Herbert, Kt., Bach. (1904), K.C.—Scholar Winchester schl. and New Coll., Oxon.; called to bar, Mid. Tem., Nov., 1874; went the N.E. circuit; atty.-gen. Lee-ward Islands, June, 1883; ag. ch. justice and local comsnr., W. Ind. incumbered estates ct. for Antigua, June to Oct., 1884; chancellor of the diocese of Antigua, July, 1884; local comsnr. incumbered estates ct. for St. Kitts, Oct., 1884; atty.-gen., Trinidad, Dec., 1885; chancellor of diocese and admntg. advoc., 1887; chmn. roy. comsn. on franchise and electoral dists., 1888, and of royal comsn. on Metayer system in Tobago, 1890; puisne judge, S. Stltns., 1892; chief just., Gibraltar, 1895; resig., Mar., 1905.
GAVIN, J. C.—Sec. to treasy., rec.gen., and paymr.gen., New Zealand, Mar., 1860; asst. comptroller and audr.gen., 1890.
GEAR, HON. HENRY.—M.H.A. for dist. of Burin, Newfoundland, 1900; again elected, 1904; mem. exec. com., 1903.

GEBERS, H. L.—Student Int. S.N.A. Dep., 1894; clk., Zulu and D. Int. mag., ct., Dundee, 1895; 3rd cls. clk., 1895; 2nd cls. clk., 1900; lent to Transvaal, 1901; clk. of ct. P. P. Vryheid, 1901; J.P.; Vryheid, 1901; A. A. M., Vryheid, 1902; reverted to Natal service, 1903; transf'd to mag. ct., Dundee, 1904, senior clk. and A. A. M.
GEBERS, WILHELM FRIEDRICH.—Asst. inspr. of native educ., Natal, 1st Mar., 1903; inspr., ditto, 1st July, 1904; sub-inspr. of schls., 1st July, 1906.

GEIKIE, R.—Ed. at Harrow and Cambridge; undergraduate scholarship, King's Coll., 1895; Winchester prizeman, 1896; 1st class hist. tripos, 1896; elected fellow King's Coll., 1900; app'td. after open compet. exam., 2nd class clk. Local Govt. Bd., Nov., 1897; 2nd class clk., C.O., Mar., 1899.
GEMMELL, GEORGE.—Clk. treasy. dept., Seychelles, Feb., 1887; clk. to admr., Feb., 1889; clk. of councils, Jan., 1897; 1st customs off'r., May, 1900; postmr., June, 1905.
GENNADIUS, P.—Dir. of agricul., Cyprus, 1896.
GEORGE, WM. JAMES, J.P.—Ed. Midland Institute, Birmingham; M.L.A., W. Aust., 1895 to 1902; comsnr. of rlwys., July, 1902.

GIBBES, REGINALD PRESCOTT.—Cadet, S.S., Nov. 1889; passed final exam., Tamil, Jan., 1892; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Aug. to Nov., 1892; ag. dist. off'r., Nebong Tebal, P. W., Mar., 1894; passed exam. in Malay, 1894; 2nd ast. prot. of imigrts., Penang, May, 1896; ag. dist. off'r., Dindungs, Aug., 1897; transf'd to Calcutta as (temp.) ast. emigtn. agt. for Trinidad, Mauritius, Fiji, Jamaica, &c., Aug., 1900; app't confirmed, Aug., 1901; passed exam. in Hindustan, May, 1901; ag. emigtn. agt., 19th Feb. to 12th Oct., 1902; emigtn. agt., 14th Sept., 1903.
