GIBBS, LAWRENCE.—A.M.I.C.E., grad. assoc., and county engrn.; ast. engrn. P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1890; ag. exec. engrn., 1896.

GIBRALTAR, LORD BISHOP OF, RT. REV. WM. ED. COLLINS.—Late Exhib. of Selw. Coll., Camb.; B.A. (Jun. Opt.), 1887; Lightfoot schl., 1889; Prince Consort pri., 1890; M.A., 1891; B.D., 1902; D.D., 1903; F.C. of All Hallow's, Barking, Lond., 1890-1; lectr. of Selw. Coll., 1891-3; lectr. of St. John's Coll., Camb., 1891-4; exam. hist. trip., 1894-5; select jr., Camb., 1899-1902; prof. of eccles. hist., King's Coll., Lond., 1893-1904; C. of All Hallows, Barking, 1894; exam. chap. to Bp. of St. Albans (Festing), 1897-1903; Bishop of Gibraltar, 25th Jan., 1904.

GIBSON, FRED. ALLAN, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1860; clk. Mauritius civ. ser. after exam., Oct., 1875; clk. despatch branch, Sept., 1878; ag. précis writer, Nov., 1882, to June, 1883; ag. ch. clk. and sec. to exec. and legis. couns., Mar., 1890; clk. exec. and legis. couns., Sept., 1892; ag. jun. ast. col. sec., Sept., 1892; ch. clk., Mar., 1894; ag. ast. col. sec., June, 1894, to Jan., 1895; June to Dec., 1895, Mar. to Sept., 1896, Jan. to Nov., 1897; ag. registr.-gen., Jan. to Sept., 1899; nom. mem. coun. of govt., Apl. to Sept., 1899; ag. ast. col. sec., July, 1900; ast. col. sec., Apr., 1901; collr. of cust., 9th Nov., 1903; ag. col. sec., 9th Nov., 1903, to 19th Aug., 1904; ditto, 19th Sept. to 18th Dec., 1904.

GIBSON, HENRY JAMES, C.B. (1902), B.A.—B. 1869; ed. Rossall and St. John's Coll., Oxford; Casherd scholar; 1st cls. class. mods.; clk. War Office, 1885; princ. clk., 1897; ast. acctnt.-gen., 1900; dep. acctnt.-gen., 1903; ast. dir. of Army finance, 1904; priv. sec. to Mr. Woodall, 1894-95; to Hon. St. John Brodrick, 1895-6; ast. compt. and audr., 1905; is hon. ast. audr. for certain Crown Colonies and Prots., ag. on behalf of the S. of S. for the Colonies

GIBSON, JAS. YOUNG.—Clk. and Zulu interp. to ct. of R.M.; sub-distr. of stamps, and regisr. circuit ct., Newcastle, Natal, Apr., 1882; also dep. clk. of peace, Mar., 1887; ast. comrs. and R.M., Nqutu dist., Zululand, Feb., 1889; ditto, Ndawande dist., May, 1889; mag. for colonv.; mag., Umvoti div., 1900; J.P. for colony, 1902; ag. mast., sup. ct., 1904; mag., Up. Umkomazi, 1904.

GILES, MORTIMER.—Entl. survey dept., S. Australia, 1864; draughtsmen, 1875; clk. and ast. draughtsmen, lands titles off., 1876; draughtsmen, 1889; draughtsmen and 3rd dep. regisr.-gen. of deeds, 1901; regisr.-gen. of deeds and regisr. of buildg. socs., 1904.

GILL, THOMAS, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1849; entered col. service, S. Australia, Feb., 1865; acctnt., treasy., Jan., 1883; under treas., 1894; also regisr. of inscribed stock in Adelaide, 1895, and mem. of supply and tender bd., 1903.
GILL, WALTER, F.L.S., F.R.H.S.—Conservator of forests, S. Australia, July, 1890.
GILLIGAN, GEOFFREY G.—Capt., 4th Battn. A. and S. Highrs.; served in S. Africa, 1900-01; (medal with 3 clasps); inspr. of pol., Br. Guiana, 1904; ast. polit. offr., Somaliland, Jan., 1906.

GILLOTT, HON. SIR SAMUEL KT. BACH (1901).—B. 1838; M.L.A. for East Melbourne, Victoria, since Feb., 1899; min. without portfolio 1900-01; atty.-gen., 1901-02; Mayor of Melbourne, 1900-02; first Lord Mayor, 1902-3; chief sec. and min. of labour, Victoria, Feb., 1904.

GILMAN, E.W.F., B.A., Oxon. Cadet, S.S., Nov., 1899; ag. 4th magrs., Sing., Nov., 1901; passed final exam. in Tamil, June, 1902; ag. ast. supt. of Indian immigrants, Penang, Apl., 1902; confirmed, July, 1904.

GILMORE, HON. GEORGE CROSLER.—B., 1864; B.A., LL.M.; barrister-at-law, Inner Tem.; barrister, solr., attorney and proctor of Tasmania; M.L.A for George Town, Tasmania, 1893-1900; comsr. of ct. of requests and stip. mag., West Coast, 1900-1903; M.L.A. for Waratah since 1903; atty.-gen. of Tasmania, July, 1904.
GIROUARD, DESIRÉ, K.C.—Ed. Montreal Coll.; called to the bar, Canada, 1860; K.C., 1876; represented Jacques Cartier co., 1878 to 1895; judge sup. ct., Canada, 1895.

GIROUARD, LT.-COL. SIR EDWARD PERCY CRANWILL, K.C.M.G. (1900), D.S.O., R.E.—Served with Dongola expeditionary force, 1896 (desp.), brevet-major; British medal, Khedive's medal, 2 clasps); Nile expdn., 1897 (desp., clasp); rlwy. traff. man., Woolwich Arsenal, 1890-5; dir. of Sudan rlwys., 1896-8; pres. Egyptian rlwy., bd., 1898-9 (2nd cls. Modjide); S. African war, 1899-1902 (desp.); comsnr. of rlwys., Transvaal and O.R.C., 1902; mem. I.C.C.; resig. 1904; high comsnr., N. Nigeria, 1907.

GLASGOW, 7TH EARL OF, THE RIGHT HON. SIR DAVID BOYLE, G.C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1833; capt. (retired) R.N.; served in the Crimean and China wars, 1857 (medal and clasp); J.P. for Avrshire, and chmn. of co. coun.; gov. of N.Z., 1892-97.

GLASIER, F. BEDFORD.—Ast. civ. engrn., Bermuda harbr. wks., June, 1894; engrn.in charge, Coatracoalcos harbr. wks., Mexico, May, 1896; dist. engrnr., S. Leone govt. rly., June, 1897; dist. engrnr., Tarkwa rly., Gold Coast, Oct. 1898; and gen. manager, S. Leone govt. rly., May, 1899; promoted gen. manager, Lagos govt. rly., Apr., 1900.

GLYDE, ADOLPHUS YEOVIL.—Apptd. clk. in land titles dept., Perth, W. Australia, 1882; dep. regir. of titles, ag. regir. of deeds, sub. collr. of internal rev., asst. regir. of titles, 1893; ag. regir. of deeds, Nov. 1896, to Jan., 1897.
GOBEIL, ANTOINE, I.S.O. (1904).—Law clk., dept. of pub. wks., Canada, 1879 to 1883; then sec., dep. min. of pub. wks., Jan., 1891.

GODBOLT, THOMAS.—Entd. N. S. Wales public serv., July, 1871, as jun. clk., govt. printing off.; asst. paymaster of imperial pensions, 1877; bd. of trade clk. and cashier, govt. shipping office, 1881; emergency offr., col. treas., 1891; paymaster of imperial pensions and allowances, 1893; paymaster, N. S. Wales Imperial Bushmen's contingent (local pay and allowances), 1900, and chief paymaster (for local pay and allowances), N. S. Wales Naval Contingent to China, 1900.

GODFREY, JOSEPH ED.—M.B. and C.M., Edin., 1882; gov. med. offr., B. Guiana, Feb., 1883; med. inspr. of estates hospitals, May, 1898; deputy regir. of births, deaths and marriages, 1898; deputy chmn., cent. bd. of health, 1902; acted on sev. occasions as surg.-gen., regir.-gen., and chmn. of cent. bd. of health; apptd. surg.-gen., regir.-gen. of b., d. and m., chmn. cent. bd. of health, and offl. mem. of ct. of policy, July, 1904.
GODFREY, LEOPOLD HENRY.—Active serv. volunteer in 1st batt., Royal Sussex regt., Feb., 1901, to May, 1902; architectural draughtsmn, pub. wks. dept., O.R.C., June, 1902.

GOLDIE, CHARLES.—Apptd., after compet. exam., an examg. offr., H.M. customs, Greenock, Sept., 1863; inspr. of invoices and ch. landing survr. H.M. customs, Jamaica, Oct., 1869; one of the comsrs. to inquire into the pilot service of Jamaica, Apr., 1870; ag. collr. of customs, Kingston, 1873, and 1875 to 1876; acted in 1874 as island treas., and as collr.-gen. of customs, 1875 and 1885; collr. of customs and shipping master, Kingston, 1883.

GOLDNEY, SIR JOHN TANKERVILLE, Kt. BACH. (1893).—B. 1846; ed. Harrow, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1869, N. circuit; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Apr., 1880; ag. ch. just., May, 1881; puisne judge, Br. Guiana, June, 1883; judge sup. ct., S. Stltnts., Mar., 1887; ch. just., Trinidad, 1892; retired, 1902.

GOLLAN, H. C.—Ed. at Charterhouse; M.A., Edin. (1887); called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Jan., 1891, N. circuit; priv. sec. to Col. Lugard, comsrr. and comdt., W.A.F.F., Sept. 1st, 1899; priv. sec. to H.C. of Nor. Nig., 1st Jan.-21st Sept., 1900; ag. sec. to the admintr., 1st June-31st Aug., 1900; atty.-gen. of Nor. Nig., 21st Sept., 1900; ag. ch. just., 16th Nov., 1900-4th Apr., 1901; ch. just., 4th Nov., 1901; ch. just., Bermuda, 1904.

GOMPERTZ, H. H. J.—Ed. at Bedford schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxon (scholar); 2nd cl. lit. hum., B.A. (1890); cadet, S. Stltnts., 1890; passed final exam. Chinese, Dec., 1893; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Aug., 1894, to Dec., 1896; passed final exam. in Cantonese, 1895; agt. deputy-registr., sup. ct., Penang, Dec., 1896; passed final exam. in Hokkien, 1897; 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, 1st Jan., 1897; transf. to Hong Kong as asst. registr.-gen., 1897; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Easter, 1899; actg. sen. mag., June, 1899, to June, 1900; asst. col. sec. Jan. 1st, 1900; mem. land ct., 1st June, 1900; pres. land ct. 10th Jan., 1902; ag. pol. mag., 1904-5; ag. sen. mag., June, 1906.
GOODMAN, CLIFFORD E.—Entered pub. library, Barbados, Sept., 1890; 5th clk., audr.-gen.'s office, Apr., 1891; ch. clk., inspr.-gen.'s office, July, 1892; also regir. of births, dist. A.

GOODMAN, GERALD AUBRET.—Ed. at Lodge-schl. and Harrison Coll., Barbados, and Univ. Coll., Lond.; 1st com. law schlr., Mid. Temp., Trinity term, 1885; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., June, 1885; ag. judge of petty debt ct., and of asst. ct. of appeal, Barbados, 1889; J.P., 1889; ag. solr.-gen. in 1890-1-2; M.H.A. since Oct., 1889; mem. bd. of educ. since Oct., 1891; solr.-gen., Mar., 1896; ag. atty.-gen., 1891, 1898, 1900-1-2-3-4; K.C. for Barbados, 1903; mem. of quar. bd. and gen. bd. of health, 1902.

GOODMAN, SIR WILLIAM MEIGH, KT. BACH. (1902).—B. 1847; ed. at Univ. Coll.; graduated B.A. (honours) at the Univ. of Lond., 1867; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Nov., 1870; went S.E. circ. and Surrey sessions; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, 1883; ch. just., 1886; comsrr. to revise and consolidate the laws of the col., 1886; atty.-gen. Hong Kong, 1889; ag. col. sec., May, 1891, to Mar., 1892; ag. ch. just., Mar. to Aug., 1895, Jan. to May, 1896, and Apr., 1899, to Feb., 1900; K.C., 1900; ch. just., Hong Kong, 1st April, 1902; ret., 1905.

GOODRIDGE, HON. A. F.—Mem. of house of assem. for Ferryland, Newfoundland, 1880 to 1882, and 1882 to 1885; for Twillingate, 1885 to 1889; returned for same dist., 1893; mem. exec. coun., 1885 (without portfolio) to 1889, and again in 1894; after the resign. of the Whiteway ministry in Apr., 1894, he was called upon to form a ministry, and became premier of the col., which office he held till Dec., 1894; was a mem. of the bd. of wks. and rly. comsrs. under the Thorburn admnstr. (1885-9); he is also a mem. of the Newfoundland chamb. of com., and has been its pres. and vice-pres.
GOOLD, ALBERT WM. FISHER.—Supernum. inspr. of pol., Mauritius, 19th Dec., 1881; inspr., 16th Sept., 1894; ch. offr., 1st Nov., 1898; inspr.-gen., 17th Aug., 1902.

GOOLD-ADAMS, MAJOR SIR H. J., K.C.M.G. (1902), C.B. (1898), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1858; Roy. Scots fus.; comdg. a troop Bechuanaland border police, Aug., 1885; comdt., 1888; major, 1889; again comdt., 1893-5; commanded field force against Matabele, 1893; res. comsrr. for Bechuanaland Protectorate; dep. admnr., Orange River Col., Jan., 1901; lt.-gov., 7th Aug., 1901.
GORDON, ARTHUR HENRY WYNNDHAM.—Supt. of police, Mauritius, 28th Nov., 1889; ag. inspr.-gen., 2nd Aug., 1892; supt. of prisons and reformatory, 25th Oct., 1892.

GORDON, ARTHUR JOHN LEWIS, C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1847; priv. sec. to the gov. of Trinidad, 1866 to 1870; ag. col. sec., Trinidad, Apr., 1870; priv. sec. to gov. of Mauritius, 1870; priv. sec. to the gov. of Fiji, 1875-80; priv. sec. to gov.-gen. of Canada (Earl of Aberdeen), 1893.

GORDON, J. G. W.—Ed. at King Edward's Schl., Birmingham; attached to headcrs. staff, Natal Army, South African War (Queen's medal and six clasps, King's medal); cont., stationery dept., O.R.C., 17th Nov., 1900.

GORDON, BRIG.-GEN., J.M., C.B. (1901).—B. 1856; Lieut. R.A., from the R.M. Acad., Woolwich, 1875; lieut. instr. R.V. force, S. Australia, Dec., 1881; on the formation of a permanent artillery force in S. Australia, apptd. lieut.-in-comd., Sept., 1882; capt., Aug., 1883; maj., May, 1885; ag. D.A.A.G., Dec., 1885; and hon. A.D.C., May, 1886; comdt. S. Australia mil. forces, 1893; lieut.-col., 1892; col., 1895; brig.-gen., 1901; inspr. mil. stores for Australian cols., 1898-9; transf'd, with mil. def. dept., to serv. of Commonwealth of Aust., 1st Mar., 1901.
GORDON, JAMES.—Admitted to S. Aust. bar, 1881; stip. mag., Adelaide, 1892; pol. mag., Adelaide, 1902; mem. of Adelaide licensing bench, 1892; chrmn. ditto, 1901; mem. central bd. of health, 1901.

GORDON, THE HON JOHN HANNAH, K.C.—Admitted to S. Aust. bar, 1876; M.L.C., 1888; min. of ed., 1889-90 and 1892; ch. sec., 1893-6; atty.-gen., 1899-1902; atty.-gen. and min. of ed., 1902-3; 3rd judge of sup. ct., 1903.
GORDON, RICHARD WOLF.—Clk. in supreme ct., England, 1892; 3rd asst., B.C. Africa Prot., Apr., 1900; ag. collr., Angoniland, 1904; 2nd cls. dis. res., Apr., 1906.

GORDON, WILLIAM MONTGOMERIE.—Ed. Edin. Acad.; clk. to consul-gen. of W. Pacific, Aug., 1879; clk. of exec. coun. and ch. clk. of crown lands in col. of Fiji, May, 1880; also acted as sec. to lands comm.; res. commr. and stip. mag. for the island of Rotumah, May, 1882; commr. to inquire into claims of Europeans to land, Nov., 1882; stip. mag., Fiji, Apr., 1889; priv. sec. to gov., Ceylon, June, 1887; ch. clk. col. secy.'s off., Leewards and clk. of fed., exec. and legis. couns., and of the local legis. coun. of Antigua, Apr., 1889; ag. commr., Montserrat, Dec., 1891, to Mar., 1892, Mar., 1894, to June, 1895, and July to Dec., 1899; ag. col. sec., Leeward Islds., Aug. to Nov., 1900; asst. col. sec., Trinidad, July, 1901; ag. col. sec., Trinidad, Aug.-Sept., 1903, Mar.-Aug., 1904, Apr.-Sept., 1905; admstl. govt., 25th-28th Aug., 1905.
GOBE, LIEUT.-COL. JAMES C.—Audit.-gen. W. Africa Sttlmnts. from 1882 to 1887; apptd. to admstl. govt. of the Gambia, June to Nov., 1894; col. sec. S. Leone, 1894; rec.-gen., Cyprus, 1901.

GOTTLIEB, F. H. VALENTINE.—Entered gov't. service, S. Sttlmnts., Oct., 1880; ch. clk. G.P.O., Singapore, 1888; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1892; dep. regis. sup. ct., Singapore, Aug., 1895; ag. asst. regis. sup. ct. and mag., Macceca, Mar., 1898; also ag. sheriff, Sep., 1898, to Nov., 1899; asst. regis., sup. ct., Penang, July, 1904.
GOUGE, HERBERT DILLON.—Assessor, land tax dept., S. Australia, Apr., 1886; pub. actuary, Mar., 1894.

GOUGH, FREDERIC HARRISON.—Ed. at Oriel Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. class. mods., 1884; B.A. (2nd cls. final class. sch.), 1886; M.A., 1889; barrister-at-law, Inner Tem., 1894; N.E. Circuit; pol. mag. and coroner, Freetown, S. Leone, July, 1903; solr.-gen., Oct., 1903; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions, 1903-1906.

GOUGH, FREDERIC.—Messenger in office of Woods and Forests, 12th Apr., 1869; apptd., after exam., mess. C.O., 20th Jan., 1871; Queen's mess., Mar., 1880; sent three times on foreign service; acted as Queen's page-in-waiting on col. premiers during diam. jub., 1897; read. address of thanks, and jub. medal, 1897; res. off. keeper, C.O., Mar., 1898; apptd. by the S. of S. to be in attendance on col. premiers during their Coronation visit, 1902; (received joint letter of thanks, and Coronation medal).

GOULBURN, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. CHRISTOPHER GEORGE BARLOW.—D.D., 1891; ordained by Bishop of N. Queensland, 1881; curate of Mackay, 1881-2; vicar of St. Paul's, Charters Towers, 1882-5; mission chaplain to the bishop, 1885-6; vicar of St. James's pro-cathedral, Townsville, 1886-91; hon. canon, 1887-91; vicar-gen. 1887-8; consecrated 2nd bishop of N. Queensland, 1889; installed Bishop of Goulburn, 23rd Apr., 1902.
GOULD, JULIAN BARING.—Cadet, Sarawak serv., Mar. 1897; asst. res., Nov., 1900; res., 2nd cls., Jan., 1902.

GOURDEAU DE BEAULIEU, MAJOR FRANCOIS FRÉDÉRIC.—Priv. sec. to Hon. J. Cauchon; mem. of Mackenzie admin., 1870; ch. clk. and acctnt. dept. of marine and fisheries; dep. min. of marine and fisheries of Canada, May, 1896.

GOWAN, SIR JAMES ROBERT, K.C.M.G. (1905), C.M.G. (1893), LL.D.—B. 1815; lieut., 4th regt., North York, 1838; called to Canadian bar, 1839; judge of Simcoe, 1842-83; chairman of bd. of pub. instruction, 1844 to 1871; commr. for consolidating statute law of Canada, 1858-59; chmn. of bd. of judges, 1869-86; engaged in consolidation of the crim. law for Dominion, 1869; mem. of "The Law Reform Comm." 1871; roy. commr. to inquire into charges against the ministry in reference to the Pacific rly. contract, 1873; commr. for consolidating the statutes of Ontario, 1876-77; apptd. to high ct. of just., 1882; a senator of the Dominion, 1885; clmm. of Barrie Collegiate Inst., by annual election, 1881-87; called to Irish bar, 1889.

GOWDEY, WM. WALLACE.—Solr., Barbados, 1881; mem. house of assessees, 1883-93; mem. of comte of inquiry into Dodd's reformatory and industrial schl., 1884; mem. bd. of health, 1884-92; J.P., 1885; mem. of financial inquiry comm., 1885; of general hosp. bd., 1889-92; pol. mag. and judge petty debt ct., Dist. E., Barbados, 1892; receiver of wrecks, Dist. E., July, 1897; pol. mag., Dist. A. Oct., 1901; visiting just., Lazaretto, Jan., 1902; receiver of wrecks, Dist. A., May, 1902; ag. judge, Bridgetown petty debt ct., May and June, 1902; ag. judge, asst. ct. of appeal, July, 1902, to Feb., 1903; commr. of probates, Aug., 1902; judge, Bridgetown petty debt ct., Dec., 1902; ag. coroner, Dist. A., March to May, 1903; ag. judge, asst. ct. of appeal, April, 1903; ag. inspr. of prisons, May, 1903.

GRAHAM, MICHAEL, M.R.C.S., Eng., L.R.C.P. Lond., M.A., M.B., B.C., Camb., 1891.—Ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; St Thomas's Hosp., Lon.; B.A. Camb., 1887; 1st cl. honours nat. sciences, tripus and exhibitioner, St. John's Coll.; asst. med. offr., city asylum, Birmingham, 1891; surg. to the lying-in hospital and general penitentiary, Kingston, Jamaica, 1892; late sec. med. council of Jamaica, 1897; mem. bd. of governors, Institute of Jamaica, 1902; joint author, with F. V. Theobald, S.E. Agric. Coll., "A Monograph on the Mosquitoes of Jamaica," 1905.

GRAHAM, SIR FREDK., K.C.B. (1907), C.B. (1899).—B. 1848; apptd. clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., after compet. exam., 30th May, 1870; 2nd class clk., 30th Sept., 1872; priv. sec. to Mr. Herbert, 1st Oct., 1872; to the Rt. Hon. M. E. Grant-Duff, M.P., 8th Jan., 1881, to 5th Aug., 1881; to Mr. L. H. Courtney, M.P., 6th Aug., 1881; to the Hon. Evelyn Ashley, 12th May, 1882; and to the Earl of Dunraven, 24th June, 1885; to Sir R. Herbert, 3rd Feb., 1886; and again to the Earl of Dunraven, 3rd Aug., 1886, to Feb., 1887; 1st class clk., Dec., 1887; ag. prin. clk., Feb., 1892; prin. clk., 11th Mar., 1896; asst. under sec. of state, 1st Mar., 1897; ret., 1907.

GRAHAM, SIR JOHN JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1905), C.M.G. (1899).—B. 1847; clk. to C.C. and R.M., Albany, 1864; clk. to regis. E. dists. ct., May, 1865; asst. regisr., Jan., 1868; asst. regisr. sup. ct., Mar., 1872; regisr. and master, E. dists. ct., Feb., 1875; ch. clk. to atty-gen. and clk. of peace, Capetown, Jan., 1878; sec. law dept., July, 1882; high sheriff and taxing offr., sup. ct., May, 1884; mem. of the col. tender bd.; a J.P. for the col.; sec. to law dept., 1889.
GRAINGER, HENRY ALLERDALE.—Mem. H. of Ass. S. Austr., 1884, 1890 and 1901; state agent in London for S. Austr., 1901; ag.-gen., 1902-1905.
GRANGE, HERBERT CHARLES.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to bd. of educ., Aug., 1901; transfd. to C.O., Feb., 1902.

GRANNUM, CLIFTON.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; 2nd clk. to gov. Windward Is., Sept., 1891; ditto St. Vincent, Apr. 1892; supervisor of customs, G. Coast Coll., Oct., 1893; asst. local audr., G. Coast and Lagos, May, 1895; local audr., S. Leone, June, 1896; J.P., S. Leone, 1897; ch. asst. treas., G. Coast, Apl., 1903; ag. treas. and mem. ex. and leg. couns., mem. bd. of educ., July, 1903-June, 1904.

GRANNUM, EDWARD ALLAN.—Passed Barbados, W. I., civ. ser. exam., 1886; 3rd clk. regi-strar's off., Jan. to Oct., 1885; 2nd clk., Oct., 1885, to Nov., 1887; clk. master in chancery off., Nov., 1887, to June, 1888; honours graduate, diploma Ontario Com. Coll., Canada, affiliated institute chartered accountants, Canada, Dec., 1889; practised public accountant, Barbados, W. I., to Aug., 1897; asst. auditor, Sierra Leone and Gambia, 25th Sept., 1897, to 15th Jan., 1900; acted auditor May to Nov., 1898 asst. auditor, Gold Coast and Lagos, Jan. to March, 1900; auditor, March, 1900; introduced system double entry accounts Gold Coast P.O., July, 1901; prepared scheme for introduction of double entry system of accounts for Accra town council, Aug., 1901.

GRANNUM, EDWARD T.—Representative of Bridgetown, Barbados Assem., 1883; J.P., 1886; mem. exec. comtee., 1885-89, and 1890-94; mem. gen. bd. of health, 1885; deleg. to Washington as to tariff arrangements under McKinley act, 1891; audr.-gen., Barbados, 1894; ag. col. sec. and mem. exec. coun., Aug. to Dec., 1895, and July to Nov., 1896; ag. col. sec., mem. of exec. coun., May to Sept., 1897, also in 1905, and June to Sept., 1906.

GRANT, CHARLES CAMERON.—King's student, Aberdeen Univ., 1879-8; M.A., ditto, 1899; 2nd asst., Fraserburgh pub. schl., Aberdeenshire, 1890-1901; 1st asst., Aberlour pub. schl., Banffshire, 1901-2; priv. coun. certif., Scotch educn. dept., 1901; teacher, refugee camp, Springfontein, O.R.C., Nov., 1902; priv., govt. schl., Winburg, Jan., 1903; corrprnde. clk., educn. dept., Sept., 1904; admstve. clk., 1905; sec., Grey Coll. coun., 1906.
GRANT, COLIN.—Apptd. 3rd asst., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., 1897; 2nd cls. dist. res., Oct., 1905.

GRANT, H. E. W.—Sec. to the educn. bd., Barbados, 1882; priv. sec. to Sir W. Robinson, 1883; and at Trinidad, 1885 to 1891; went on mission to Venezuela, 1887; sec. to special pub. wks. and road loan bd., Trinidad, 1890-1; priv. sec. to Sir W. B. Griffith, gov. of G. Coast, 1891-2; called to the bar, Inner Tem., Jan., 1896 res. just., Harbour Is., Bahamas, Apr., 1897; ag. clk. col. sec.'s office, and ag. clk. of councils, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1899; appt. confirmed, 1901; ag.-atty.-gen., B. Hond., Mar., 1902, to Oct., 1903; ag. col. sec., 14th Dec., 1904, to 21st July, 1905; admstvl. govt., 22nd July to 6th Aug., 1905; ag. col. sec., 7th Aug. to 9th Nov., 1905; col. sec., Falklands, 16th Nov., 1905; assumed office, 14th Feb., 1906.
GRAVES, F. HELY-HUTCHINSON.—Cadet, H.M.S. "Conway," 1889; clk., Imperial Ottoman Bank, Alexandria, 1894; clk., crown agent's off., 1899; acctnt., P.W.D., Northern Nigeria, 1905.
GRAVES, THE HON. J. H.—M.L.A. for Delatite, Victoria; consnr. of trade and customs, Victoria, 9th July, 1881, to Mar., 1883.
GRAVES, SILAS.—Clk., procureur-genrl.'s dept., Mauritius, 2nd May, 1859; deputy collr. of cust., 2nd Mar., 1896; ag. collr. in 1899, 1900, 1903 and 1905.
GRAY, E. A. S.—Denny, Magd. Coll., Oxon; B.A. 1892; asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1896; asst. dist. commr., Niger Coast prot., Sept., 1899; dist. commr., Feb., 1902.

GRAY, GEORGE SMITH BOOTH ERSKINE.—Conway, 1879-80; midshipman, R.N.R., Dec., 1880; mercantile marine offr., 1894 to 1894; sub-lieut., R.N.R., 1887-1894; Lieut., R.N.R., 1894; H.M.S. "Anson" and naval employment, 1894-1897; joined So. Nigeria marine, Jan., 1900; served Ubium expdn. and Aro campaign (medal); gov. of gaols, 1902.

GRAY, JOHN.—Served during S. African campaign as offr. comdg. cyclist corps, and staff offr. to Col. Barker's force; prin., E. London refugee camp schl., June, 1902; asst. sup't., Brandport refugee camp, July, 1902; sup't., ditto, Sept., 1902; prin., Hoopstad schl., June, 1903; corrprnde.clk., educn. dept., Mar., 1904; statistics clk., ditto, Sept., 1904.

GRAY, REGINALD.—Called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1875; compiled Bermuda laws, 1690-1883, in 1884; revising offr., Bermuda, 1889-93; counsel to statute law consolidation comtee., Bermuda, 1898-1900; atty.-gen., 1900.

GRAY, ST. GEORGE.—B.A., M.B., B.Ch. (Univ. Dub.), L.M. (Rotunda); ag. col. ass't surg., St. Lucia, 1892; supernumerary ditto, 1894; col. ass't surg. and med. offr. in charge of Yaws hosp., 1898; med. offr. in charge of agric. schl., 1901; med. offr., W. Africa med. staff, Sierra Leone, 1903.

GRAY, SIR S. BROWNLLOW, KT. BACH. (1901), C.M.G. (1888).—B. 1823; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 1847; inspr. of schls., Bermuda, 1848; atty.-gen. and advoc.-gen., 1861; ch. justice, May, 1900, ret., 1904.

GRAY, THOS. ALEXANDER.—Served in R.I.C., June, 1882, to Jan., 1899; sergt.-major, Leeward Is. pol. and jailer, Montserrat, Jan., 1899; mem. of hurricane distress relief comtee., and survr. of roads, Northern dist., 1900; inspr. of pol., Dominica, Feb., 1902; J.P. and mem. of telephone bd., Dominica, May, 1902; lieut., Roseau volr. fire brigade, July, 1903; ag. sub-inspr. of schls., Dominica, June to Nov., 1903, and again from Apr. to Oct., 1904; mil. instr., Dominica defence reserve, 1st Apr., 1904; mem. of Roseau town bd., Jan., 1906; dep. supt. of jail at Mombasa, Br. E. Africa, Jan., 1907.
GREAVES, DUDLEY, A., M.R.C.S., Edin., L.R.C.P., Edin.—Asst. med. offr., Virgin Is., March, 1901; deputy cornh.

GREAVES, Lieut.-Gen. Sir GEORGE RICHARD, K.C.B. (1885), K.C.M.G. (1881), C.B.—B. 1831; has held several important posts; ch. sec. to gov't in Cyprus, July, 1878, to July, 1879; adjt.-gen. in India, Oct., 1879, to Oct., 1884; ch. of the staff at Suakin, 1885; in command of Meerut div. of Indian army to 1892.

GREAVES, KENNETH J.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; called to bar, Mid. Temple, 26th Jan., 1900; ag. deputy clk., house of assem., Barbados, May-Sept., 1900; ag. pol. mag., Bridgetown, Oct., 1900, to Feb., 1901; ditto, April, 1901; ditto, St. Thomas and St. James, Nov., 1901, to Aug. 1902; apptd. pol. mag., 1st Sept., 1902.

GREAVES, WILLIAM HERBERT, K.C.—B.A., Oxon, honours, juris., 1879; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., 1880; ag. solr.-gen., Barbados, Aug., 1884, to July, 1886, and July, 1886, to Jan., 1887, when confirmed; J.P., Sept., 1886; escheator-gen., 1887; Q.C. for Barbados, 1890; mem. exec. comn., 1890; acted as atty.-gen., July to Dec., 1887, and June to Dec., 1891; mem. of house of assem. since 1882, and of bd. of educn.; atty.-gen., Mar., 1896; now ch. just.

GREEN, AMAZIAH.—Entd. audit dept., N.S. Wales, 1877; regis. of adjustments, audit dept., for two years; bookkeeper, post and telegraph dept., for eight years; acctnt., dept. of justice, 1887; senior inspr., treasy. dept., 1896; military staff paymr., 1900; Comwlth. pub. serv. inspr. for W. Australia, 1902; is also comwlth. electoral offr., and registr. for comwlth. pub. wks., for W. Australia; J.P. for W. Australia and N.S.Wales.
GREEN, A. PIERCE—Surg., A.M.D., 1878; med. offr., col. forces in Basuto war, 1881; regtl. surg., left wing, C.M.R., 1881-4; med. offr., Cape rlys., 1888; ditto, Bechuana land border pol., 1886.

GREEN, CHARLES FRANCIS J.—Cadet, Sts. Sttlmts., Nov., 1898; ag. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Mar., 1900; ag. 4th mag., Sing., June, 1901; passed final exam. in Malay, Oct., 1901; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Feb., 1902; confirmed, July, 1903; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., July, 1906.
GREEN, GEORGE C.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil serv., and assigned to C.O., 26th Aug., 1901.

GREEN, JOHN FREDERICK NORMAN, B.A.—Scholar, Emman. Coll., Camb., 1890; bracketed 15th wrangler, 1893; B.A., 1893; 1st class, Part II, nat. science tripus, 1894; 2nd class clk. C.O. after compet. exam., Feb., 1896; priv. sec. to Sir E. Wingfield, Dec., 1898; to Sir M. Ommanney, 1900; 1st class clk., 16th June, 1902.
GREENE, GEOFFREY PHILIP.—Gen. mag. Ceylon rlyws., 11th Oct., 1901.

GREENE, GEORGE BALL.—Ed. in England; jun. clk., id. dept., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1887; 5th class clk., Feb., 1888; 4th cl., secretariat, 1891; sec. to comte. for inquiring into post-office, 1893; sec. pilotage comsn., and 3rd class, 1895; 2nd class, and despatch clk., 1899; 1st class clk., 1901; ag. prin. clk., 1902-4 and 1906; priv. sec. to gov. and ag. gov., 1905 and 1906.

GREENE, JOHN LE SUEUR.—Ed. Roy. Coll.; entered col. sec. office, Mauritius, Jan., 1886; jun. copyist, procur.-gen.'s office, Mar., 1887; sen. ditto, Feb., 1888; govt. translator of laws, Nov., 1888; 5th class clk., July, 1890; 4th class clk., Mar., 1896.

GREENE, SIR WILLIAM CONYNGHAM, K.C.B. (1900), C.B. (1897)—B. 1854; ed. Harrow, and Pemb. Coll., Ox.; M.A. 1890; entrd. F.O. 1877; ag. 3rd sec. legation at Athens, 1890; ditto Stuttgart, 1883; ag. chargé d'affaires Stuttgart and Darmstadt at various times, 1883-9; 2nd sec. diplomatic service, 1887; 2nd sec. Hague, 1889-91; Brussels, 1891-3; sec. of legation and chargé d'affaires, Teheran, 1893-6; H.M. agt. at Pretoria, S.A. Republic, with rank of chargé d'affaires, 25th Aug., 1896, to 1899.
GREENE, W. H., M.I.C.E. (London.)—Asst. engrn., Victorian rlyws., 1885; dist. engrn., 1885; res. engrn., 1868; ch. engrn. of existing lines, 1882; Victorian rly. comsnr., 1887-92.

GREENER, HERBERT.—D.S.O., clk., sec.'s dept., G.P.O., Cape, 1884; in charge of money order dept., 1886; 1st clk., admstr.'s office, B. Bechuana land, 1888; recvr. of revenue, Vryburg, and P.M.G., Bechuana land, 1889; examnr. of acctns., acctnt. to high comsnr., Cape-town, 1890; paymr. (rank captain), Bech. Br. pol., 1891, combined with prin. cust. offr., for Bech. Prot., 1895; specially employed in charge of finances, Mafeking siege, and financial asst. to mil.-govr., Bloemfontein, 1899-1900; transf'd. to S.A.C. as ch. paymr. and acctnt.-gen. (rank major), Oct., 1900; promoted lt.-col., 1901.

GREENLEES, THOMAS DUNCAN, M.B. Edin.—Surg.-capt., Graham's Town 1st city volrs.; late asst. med. offr., counties asyl., Carlisle, and City of London asyl.; prizeman medico-psychological assn.; med. supt., Graham's Town asyl., and visiting surg. to the chronic sick hospital, Aug., 1890.
GREENWAY, THE HON. THOMAS.—Mem. of legis. assem., Manitoba; formerly pres. of exec. comn.; premier; min. of agric. and immigr. and rly. comsnr.

GREENWOOD, ALFRED CRAVEN.—Exec. staff, inventions exhibn., 1885, and Col. and Ind. exhibn., 1886, and Royal Jubilee exhibn., Manchester, 1887; sec. to various charitable instittns., 1887 to 1890; priv. sec. to Sir A. Shea, gov't Bahamas, and clk. to counr., 1891-4; treas. of Gibraltar, 1897; J.P. and mem. of sanitary comsn., chrmn. at various times, 1898-1904, also contractg. offr., 1897; ag. capt. of port, July to Oct., 1901-1903; hosp. comsnr., 1903; chrmn. of comte. on wharfage tolls and storage waterfront wharf, 1902; chrmn. of comte. on management, working and revenue of new comml. mole, 1904.

GREENWOOD, THOMAS J.—Inspr. Cyprus pol., Feb., 1887; ag. local comdt., Limasol, Oct. to Dec., 1894, and Apr. to July, 1895, bronze medal, roy. hum. soc., for saving and attempting to save life during Limasol floods, Nov. 12th, 1894; locl. comdt., 1896.

GREGOROWSKI, REGINALD ADDINGTON.—2nd clk., govt. sec.'s office, O.F.S. Dec., 1895; priv. sec. to state pres., 5th Mar., 1896; landdrost clk., Bethlehem, Dec., 1897; priv. sec. to state pres., July, 1898; landdrost clk., Wepener, Jan., 1899; priv., govt. schl., Reddersburg, O.R.C., Ist Oct., 1900; asst. clk., educn. dept., 1st Mar., 1901; ch. clk. and registr., educn. dept., 1st July, 1901.
GREGORY, HON. HENRY.—B. 1860; M.L.A. for N. Coolgardie, W. Australia, 1897; ditto for Menzies since 1901; min. for mines, 1901-2, and 1902-4; min. for mines and rlyws., Aug., 1905.

GRENFELL, 1ST BARON OF KILVEY (creat. 1902), GENERAL SIR FRANCIS WALLACE, G.C.B., G.C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1841; gov't and comdr-in-chief of Malta, 1898; A.D.C. to Sir A. Cunynghame; also staff offr. to Col. Glyn in the Transkei, 1887-8; dep. asst. adjt. and qrtmr.-gen. in the Kaffir war, 1878; the same in the Zulu war, 1879; asst. qrtmr.-gen. in the Boer war, 1881; served in the Egyptian campaign of 1882, and the Nile expdn. of 1884; was Sirdar of the Egyptian army from 1885 to 1892; commanded the troops employed at Sukam against the Dervishes; after filling various posts at home, went again to Egypt in 1897 to command the British troops; with Lord Kitchener in the Nile campaign, 1898; gov. of Malta, 1899 to 1903; commd. 4th Army Corps, 1903-4; G.O.C. forces in Ireland, 1904.

GRENIER, GEORGE.—Employed in Jaffra Kachcheri and registn. dept., 1861-1872; in Queen's advocate's dept., April, 1872, to 1878; second dep. registr. sup. ct., 9th Apr., 1878; dep. registr., 15th June, 1883; registr., 1st May, 1894.
GRENIER, JOSEPH.—Dist. judge, Colombo, Ceylon, 23rd April, 1903; has acted on various occasions since 1897 as sol.-gen., comsnr. of assize, and puisne justice.

GREY, 4TH EARL OF (creat. 1806), VISCOUNT HOWICK, BARON GREY, BART.—G.C.M.G. (1904), Ed. Harrow, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; M.P., 1880-6; dir. B.S.A. Co.; admstr. of Rhodesia, 1896 to 1898; gov.-gen. of Canada, 26th Sept., 1904.

GREY, ROBERT, CAMBELL.—Junior offr. treas., Selangor, 1st Aug., 1888; asst. in land off., Krian, 14th June, 1889; passed final exam. in Malay, 6th Dec., 1890; asst. mag., Kinta, 24th Dec., 1890; ag. supt. of Penghulus, Lower Perak, 14th June, 1891; supt. ditto, 1st Nov., 1891; asst. mag. and collr. of land rev., Lower Perak, 1st Apr., 1893; ag. dist. mag., Kuala Kangsar, 1st Jan., 1894; asst. sec. to govt., Selangor, 28th Feb., 1895; ag. dist. mag., Krian, 9th Apr., 1896; dist. offr., Ulun Selangor, 1st May, 1897; ditto, Kuala Selangor, 1st Jan., 1899; ditto, Ulu Pahang, 1st Nov., 1899; dist. mag., Kuala Kangsar, 19th Jan., 1900; ag. sec. to govt., Selangor, 1st Oct. to 30th Dec., 1900; ag. dist. mag., Kinta, 10th Oct., 1901; resumed duties as dist. mag., Kuala Kangsar, 21st Nov., 1901; sec. to res., Selangor, 1903.

GREY-WILSON, SIR WM., K.C.M.G. (1904), C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1852; ed. at Chelt. Coll.; priv. sec. to Sir William Grey, gov. of Jamaica, 1874; to Lieut.-Gov. Edw. E. Rushworth, Mar., 1877; admstr., Major-Gen. J. R. Mann, R.E., Aug., 1877; to Lieut.-Gov. F. P. Barlee, Br. Honduras, Sept., 1877; and clk. of the exec. and legis. couns., June, 1878; ag. mag., Orange Walk, in charge of frontier scouts, Dec., 1879, to Apr., 1880, and June, 1880, to Mar., 1881; asst. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, 1883; special comsnr., Sulymah, Dec., 1883, to June, 1884; 4th asst. col. sec., G. Coast Col., 1884; col. sec., St. Helena, July, 1886; ag. gov., 1887; gov., 1890; gov., Falklands, 1897; gov., Bahamas, 5th May, 1904.

GRIFFIN, CHAS. JAMES.—B.A. (Hons.) Royal Univ., Ireland; 1st schlr. in mod. liter.; triple exhibnr.; chancellor's gold medallist; called to the bar, Ireland, June, 1898; apptd. crn. prosr., B. Cent. Africa Prot., Aug., 1901; ag. chief judicial offr., and H.B.M. vice-consul, Nov., 1901 to June, 1902; ag. judge of high ct., Feb. to Oct., 1904; apptd. registr.-gen.of births, deaths, marriages, and banking, under various local ordinances, and is also registr. of deeds and admstr. of deceased estates under the high ct., judge of high ct., B.C. Africa, 17th Feb., 1906.

GRIFFIN, C. T.—M.R.C.S., Eng.; L.R.C.P., Edin.; L.S.A., Lond.; suptgd. med. offr., Dikoya, Ceylon, 1st Jan., 1883; dist. med. offr., 17th Jan., 1888; govt. med. offr., 1st Sept., 1892; col. surg., prov. of Uva, 21st Sept., 1898; asst. prin. civ. med. offr. and insp.-gen. of hospitals, 17th Jan., 1901.

GRIFFIN, EUGENE PATRICK.—Third clk., treas., Gibraltar (after compet. exam.), Feb., 1883; pol. clk., 1883; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, 1886; 2nd class clk., Jan., 1890; 1st class clk., Jan., 1893; ch. clk. and cashier, P.O., Nov., 1893; transf'd. to col. sec.'s office, July, 1895.

GRIFFIN, MARTIN J.—Called to the bar in 1868 from offices of Hon. James McDonald (now ch. just. of Nova Scotia) and of Hon. Wm. Miller (speaker of Senate of Canada); edited Halifax "Express" till 1874; contested Halifax co. for local legislature in 1874; edited Halifax "Herald" till 1878; apptd. asst. on fishery comsn. under Washington Treaty in 1873; priv. sec. to min. of just. in 1878; sec. of civ. serv. comsn. in 1881; edited Toronto "Mail" till Aug. 6, 1885; when apptd. joint partly. librarian.
GRIFFIN, REGINALD HERBERT.—Ed. Marlborough and C. C. Coll., Oxford; 1st mods., 1892; 2nd lit. hum., 1901; clk., higher div. W. O., Oct., 1901; 2nd cls. clk., C. O., Feb., 1903.

GRIFFITH, CHARLES DUNCAN, C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1830; was lieut. Uitenhage Hottentot levy in Kaffir war of 1846-47; lieut. Kaffir pol., Jan., 1848, to Dec., 1850; capt. commanding the Albany Hottentot levy in Kaffir war of 1851-52 (medal); several times mentioned in gen. orders; comdt. of the 2nd div. of the armed mounted pol. force, Nov., 1852; 2nd in command during the operations in 1857-58 against the Tembo chief Fadana, and the Galeka chief Kreli; captured the chief Fadana; C.C. and R.M., Albert, 1858; Queenstown, June, 1859; Albany, Nov., 1868; King William's Town, July, 1869; gov.'s agt. and ch. mag., Br. Basutoland, July, 1871; comdt. of the F.A.M.P. upon the outbreak of the Galeka tribe under the chief Kreli, Sept., 1877; Jan., 1878, comdt.-gen. of col. forces, and from that date in command of the col. forces in the col. until the end of the Galeka war and Gaika rebellion; in July, 1878, received thanks of houses of parlmt. for his services; Aug., 1878, re-apptd. gov.'s agt., in, and ch. mag. for, Br. Basutoland; ret. 1882.

GRIFFITH, F.W.—Matric. Lond. Univ., 1885; supernum. P.O. St. Vincent, Sept., 1885; elk. P.O., Jan., 1886; clk. to col. registr., June, 1886; ag. clk. to registr. sup. ct., Jan., 1887; marshal V.-A.-ct., Nov., 1887; ag. registr. sup. ct., 1888; 2nd clk. govt. office, Apr., 1889; ag. col. postmr., Nov., 1889, to Oct., 1890, Dec., 1891, to Jan., 1892, and May to June, 1892; escheator-gen. and casual revenue, Nov., 1890; inspr. of schls., Dec., 1890; ch. clk. govt. office and clk. of couns., June, 1893; J.P., Apr., 1894; filled combined offices of inspr. of schls. and local audit clk., Jan.-Dec., 1897; med. registr., Jan., 1898; spec. ment. in desps. and commended by S. of S. for services rendered in connection with hurricane relief work, 1898; sec. of hurricane loan bd., June, 1899; mag. 3rd dist. and mag. 1st dist. (crim. jurisdn.), Oct.-Nov., 1899; has acted as mag. and coroner, 1st dist., on several occasions; offl. visitor and visiting just., Kingstown prison, June, 1900; nominated offl. mem., Kingstown bd., 30th June, 1900; acted as regir., sup. ct., and registr.-gen. for short period; sec. to hosp. and asylums inquiry comsn., 1900; performed duties of warden, Leeward dist., Nov., 1900, to Feb., 1901, and July-Aug., 1901; supvr. of cust. and port offr., Apr.-May, 1901; supt. of Crown lands, land comsnr., and in charge of survey dept., May, 1903, to Jan., 1904; chmn. of Kingstown bd., Jan., 1904; chief of pol., chief of excise, supt. of pris., port offr., supvr. of cust., and in charge of fire brig., May to Oct., 1904.

GRIFITH, HORACE MAJOR BRANDFORD, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1863; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; confidential clk. to admtr. of Lagos, Oct., 1880; priv. sec. to lient.-gov., 1880, and clk. of legis. coun., 1882; 1st clk. and warehouse keeper at Lagos, 1883; attached to audit office, Accra, in 1884; priv. sec. to gov., G. Coast, 1885 to 1886; controller of customs, Lagos, 1889; treas., Gambia, 1894; ag. asst. col. sec. on several occasions; treas., 1891; ag. col. sec. 1894; has admstd. gov't. on various occasions between 1896 and 1899.

GRIFITH, HON. SIR SAMUEL WALKER, P.C. (1901). G.C.M.G. (1895), K.C.M.G. (1886), K.C.—B. 1845; ed. at Univ., Sydney, N.S.W.; B.A., 1868; Mort Travelling Fellowship, 1865; M.A., 1870; called to the Queensland bar, 1867; Q.C. 1876; entered the legis. assem., 1872; atty.-gen., 1874 to 1878; sec. for pub. instruction, 1876 to 1878; sec. for pub. wks., 1878 to 1879; led the opposition in the assem., 1879 to 1883; premier, col. sec., and sec. for pub. instruction, Nov., 1883; deleg. to the inter-col. convention held at Sydney, Dec., 1883; resig. office of sec. for pub. instruction, Jan., 1886; mem. of the federal coun. of Australasia, and chmn. of the standing comttee. of the fed. coun., 1886; resig. office of col. sec., Apr., 1886, and accepted newly-created office of ch. sec.; deleg. to col. counce., 1887; ch. sec. and treas., Aug., 1887; pres. of fed. coun., 1888; resig. office after gen. election, June, 1888, and became leader of opposition in assem.; again premier, and ch. sec., 1890; ch. just. of Queensland, 1893; lient.-gov., Sep., 1899; chief just. of high ct. of Australia, Sep., 1903.
GRIFITH, W. L.—Act. of the Canadian govt. in Wales for some years; sec. to high comsnr., Mar., 1903.

GRIFITH, SIR WM. BRANDFORD, KT. BACH. (1898).—Gilchrist scholar, 1877; B.A. (Lond.), 1880; called to the bar, Mid. Term., June, 1881; ag. Queen's advoc., G. Coast Col., Apr. to Oct., 1884; dist. comsnr., 1885; ag. puisne judge, Lagos, Jan., and Cape Coast, May, 1885; ag. Queen's advoc., 1886 and 1887; compiled ordinances of G. Coast Col., 1887; res. mag., Jamaica, 1888; R.M., St. Catherine, Feb., 1893; ag. atty.-gen., May to Dec., 1893; ch. just. G. Coast Col., May, 1895; admstd. gov't. of Lagos, July to Aug., 1896; dep. for gov. G. Coast, Aug. to Nov., 1897; revised ordinances of G. Coast Col., 1898.

GRIFITHS, GEORGE HOLLIER, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1837; clk. col. sec. off., Mauritius, 1860; clk. pc.w. dept., Mauritius, 1861; ch. offr. pol. and inspr. of liberated Africans, Seychelles, 1881; ag. ch. civ. comsnr., 1884-87; inspr. of pol., Maur., 1887; ret., 1888; ag. ch. offr. pol., Sey., 1889; inspr. of rds. and jnt. conservator of crown lds., 1898; town surv. and dist. valuator for loc. bd. of hth., cent. dist., 1900; treasur. and collr. of cust., 23rd Dec., 1901; ag. crown prosecutor, Oct., 1906.

GRINDLE, G. E. A.—Scholar of Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; 1st class classical mods., 1889; 1st class in final classical schl., 1891; B.A., 1891; elected fellow of C.C.C., 1891; Chancellor's English Essay, 1892; M.A., 1894; 2nd class clk. in local govt. board, Feb., 1893; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1895; 2nd class clk., after compet. exam., in office of S. of S. forCols., 10th Feb., 1896; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Nov., 1898; 1st class clk., 30th June, 1900.

GRINLINTON, F. H., C.M.G. (1903). B. 1853, F.R.G.S., A.I.C.E., F.R. Met. S.—Apptd. to Ceylon survey dept., Apr., 1870; dist. survr., Jan., 1877; ch. survr., Nov., 1881; ag. survr.-gen., Aug., 1894; survr.-gen., Dec., 1896; ret. June, 1904; proceeded to F.M.S. to advise govt. on reorganizn. of survey dept., Oct., 1904.

GRINLINTON, SIR J. J., KT. BACH. (1894). F.R.G.S., and A.I.C.E.—B. 1828; was employed for seven years on the Ordnance Survey of England; gazetted ensign in the 65th Regt.; apptd. depot adjt.; served in the Crimean campaign of 1855-6 as liet. 4th King's Own Regt., and asst. engrnr. during the siege of Sebastopol; was also engaged making a mil. survey of the allied positions; was mentioned in despatches on the fall of Sebastopol, 8th Sept., 1855; Crimean medal with clasps; 5th class of the Order of Medjidie, and the Turkish Medal; asst. survr.-gen. of Ceylon, Sept., 1857; retd. from the army by the sale of his comsrs. Oct., 1858; apptd. an offl. councillor of the municipality of Colombo at its formation at 1866, and served for six years; resig. the public service, Oct., 1872, and subsequently followed mercantile pursuits; mem. of the central irrign. bd., July, 1887; a councillor of municipality of Colombo, 1887, and mem. legis. coun., Ceylon (to represent the gen. European community), Nov., 1888; comsnr. for Ceylon, Chicago Exhibn., 1893.

GROOM, HON. LITTLETON ERNEST, M.A., LL.M.—B. 1867; M.P. for Darling Downs, Queensland; elected to 1st lse. of rep., C. of Aust., 1901; re-elected, Dec., 1903; mem. of royal comsns. on Iron Bonus, 1902, and Navigation Bills, 1904-5; several times Crown prosecutor, and twice ag. judge of dist. ct., Queensland; min. for home affairs, C. of Aust., 5th July, 1905; atty.-gen., 13th Oct., 1906.
GROVE, WM. ED. URE.—Suptdt. and inspr. of pol., India, 1892 to 1902; supt. of pol., Malacca, S. Stitmts., 1st Aug., 1902.

GRUBER, JASPER WM.—Employed under vestry of St. Ann, Jamaica, in 1854; collr., 3rd class customs and internal rev., Feb., 1868; parochial treas., May, 1870; barhrmr., St. Anns' Bay, Aug., 1872; 1st class collr., Montego Bay, Oct., 1875; is also treas. parish of St. James.

GUERITZ, EDWARD PEREGRINE.—Cadet and dist. offr. and res., Sarawak govt., 1874 to 1877; asst. pres., Br. N. Borneo, 1882-84; collr. of land rev., Sungei Ujong, 1885; collr. and mag., Jelebu, June, 1885; ag. res. Labuan, May, 1890; asst. govt. sec., Br. N. Borneo, 1891; res. Labuan, 1893; seas. judge, Br. N. Borneo, 1894; mem. coun., 1894; judge of chief ct., 1895; govt. sec., 1898; apptd. one of the comsners. to admnstr. the govt., Br. N. Borneo and Labuan.
GULLICK, JOSEPH WM., C.M.G. (1906).—Clk., examiner's office, Imp. customs, Dec., 1862; transfd. to E. and A. dept., Somerset House, Feb., 1873; supt. col. audit branch of that dept., Feb., 1892.

GUNN, HUGH.—Sub.-inspr. of schls. under Scotch educn. dept., June, 1899; inspr. of schls., O.R.C., 29th Mar., 1902; ch. ditto, 1st July, 1903; ag. asst. dir. of educn., 17th Aug., 1903; dir. of educn., 1st Feb., 1904; M.L.C., June, 1904.

GUPPY, ROBERT.—Imperial postal serv., 31st Mar., 1892; ch. clk. and acctnt., post and tel. dept., G. Coast, 1900; ag. dir. of telegrapha, 2nd June to 26th July, 1901; acctnt., 1902; prepared system of accounting passed in Council and brought into force 1st Sept., 1902.

GURDON, Sir W. BRAMPTON, C.B., K.C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1840; ed. at Eton and at Trin. Coll., Camb., B.A. (1863); auptd. clk.in the treasy., 1875; and received the K.C.M.G. for services on special missions in 1879 and 1881 to S. Africa for the settlement of financial questions; resig. 1885.
GURNER, JOHN AUGUSTUS.—Ed. at Chelt. Coll., and Jesus Coll. (Camb.), LL.B.; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1877; parly. draughtsman, Victoria, May, 1882, now one of the crown prosecutors.
GUSHUE, GEO. W.—M.H.A. for Trinity Dist., Newfoundland, 1894; again elected, 1900; re-elected, 1904; min. of pub. wks., 1900.
GUTTERON, W.B.—Rev. offr., Br. Honduras, Apr., 1883; keeper, Queen's warehouse, Jan., 1888; ch. clk. treasy., customs, and P.O., Feb., 1891; postmnr., 1897.

HADDON-SMITH, GEO. BASIL, C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1861; lieut. Royal Guernsey militia, 1879; attached 32nd (D.C.L.I.) regt., 1880; attached 107th (Royal Sussex) regt., 1881; asst. inspr., Lagos constaby., 1886; adjt., 1887; priv. sec. to ag. gov. and clk. of couns., 1887, 1889, and 1893; ag. inspr.-gen. and sheriff of Lagos, 1888, 1890, 1891, and 1893; ag. dist. comsnt., 1887, 1888, 1889, and 1891; took part in Jebu expedition, 1892, mentioned in despatches (medal and clasp); political offr. on Sir G. Carter's mission to Yorubaland, 1893; asst. col. sec., Lagos, 1894; ag. col. sec., 1895; priv. sec. to Sir F. Scott in Ashanti expdn., 1896; ch. ass. col. sec., G. Coast, 1896; ag. col. sec., 1896-7; mem. of exec. and legis. coun., G. Coast coll.; political offr. to Col. Sir J. Willcocks in the Ashanti expdn., 1900; took part in relief of Kumasi, spec. ment. in desps. (Ashanti medal and clasp); ag. gov. Gambia, July to Oct., 1901; col. sec. S. Leone, Oct., 1901.

HADEN, FRANCIS SEYMOUR, B.A., C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1850; ed. at Westminster schl., and at Chr. Ch., Oxford; priv. sec. to Sir Henry Bulwer, K.C.M.G., lieut.-gov. of Natal, Mar., 1877; ag. ch. clk., exec. of coll.'s office, Mar., 1880; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1881; acted as col. treas. and also as col. sec., 1885; col. sec., 1887; was chmn. of Natal trade comsnt., 1885-6; deleg. to customs and rly. confee., Cape Town, 1888; dep. gov., Natal and Zululand in Dec., 1890; dep. gov. in Aug., 1891; ret., 1893, on conclusion of responsible gov.
HAGGART, THE HON. JOHN G.—Postmnr.-gen., Canada, 1888; min. of rlys. and canals, 1892-6.

HAHN, D. M., A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Realschol, Hamburg; apprenticeship under J. A. Biernatzki, architect, Altona; theoretically instructed at the technological instrn., Hamburg; in charge govt. saw mill, Trinidad, Jan., 1887; foreman, pub. wks., Jan., 1888; ag. asst. engr., N. Div. in 1889, 1890-1; asst. engr., N. Div., 1891; asst. engr. of works and buildings, Feb. 1894; asst. engr. office and wks. Nov., 1899.

HAINES, REV. FRANK WM., B.A. (Oxon).—Ed. Christ's Coll., Erceknock, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; govt. tutor and chaplain, Selangor, 1890; inspr. of schls., Selangor, 1891; col. chaplain, Malacca, 1899; also ag. headmr., High sch., Malacca, 1899-1900; col. chap., Penang, Apr., 1901; (Surrogate), hon. chaplain, Penang volunteer corps.

HALCOMB, FRED., M.A. (Wadham Coll., Oxon).—Early librarian, S. Australia, Jan., 1870; clk., asst. and serg.-at-arms, legis. coun., Apr., 1874; clk. house of assem., May, 1887; clk., legis. coun., 1901.

HALE, ABRAHAM.—Ag. mag., Matang, 13th Dec., 1884; insp. of mines and asst. mag., Kinta, Feb., 1885; collector and mag., Tampin, 17th Oct., 1887; ag. sup. of Negri Sembilan, Mar., 1888; clk. of coun., 20th Oct., 1889; asst. Indian immigr. agent, Dec., 1889; ag. for resident, Feb. to Mar., 1900; offr. in charge, Mar. to Nov., 1894; dist. offr., Tampin, 1st Jan., 1896; dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, 26th Jan., 1897; passed higher standard in Malay, Apl., 1897; examiner in Malay, 1898; dist. offr., Klang, 9th Mar., 1899; collector of land rev., Kuala Lumpur, and regisr. of titles, Selangor; chmn., pub. gardens comtee., Kuala Lumpur, and of museum comtee., 21st Nov., 1899; vice-chmn., Malay agric. sttlmt., 23rd Sep., 1900; dir. of W. and O. fund, 19th Apl., 1901; dist. offr., U. Selangor, Jan., 1903; ditto, Larut and Krian, and regisr. of titles, North Mar., 1904.

HALL, CECIL G.—Volr. col. sec.'s office, Mauritius, Nov., 1896; clk., gen. bd. of health, June, 1897; clk. med. dept., Nov., 1898; acmnt., imigrn. dept., June, 1872; senior clk. Oct., 1880; and ag. ch. clk. several times from 1880 to 1886; inspr. of imigrts., 1886; ag. storekr.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1898; do., Sept., 1901; ditto, 12th Aug., 1903 to 19th Aug., 1904.

HALL, GILBERT AMOS.—Ed. at Harrow schl.; cadet, Straits, 1888; passed final exam. in Malay, Apr., 1891; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, July, 1891; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July to Sept., 1893; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Mar., 1894; supt. of educn., Penang, Oct., 1894; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Nov., 1896; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, May, 1897; sheriff and dep. regisr., sup. et., Singapore, July, 1897; ag. collr. ld. rev., Penang, Mar., 1898; 2nd asst. col. sec., S. Sttlmts., Aug., 1898, but continued to act as collr. ld. rev., Penang, till Mar., 1900; ag. collr. ld. rev. and offr. in charge of treasy., Malacca, Sept., 1900, and confirmed, Aug., 1902; sen. dist. offr., July, 1904; ag. P.M.G., Aug., 1904; ag. inspr. of prisons, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1905.

HALL, THE HON. SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1824; emigrated to N.Z., 1852; mem. legis. assems., 1855; col. sec., 1856; mem. legis. coun., 1862-6; postmnr.-gen., 1866-9; col. sec., 1872-3; premier, 1879-82.
HALLETT, W. CARDY.—Ed. at Gordon's Coll., Bermuda, Aug., 1881; asst. supt. pub. wks., Jan., 1887; supt., July, 1887; col. survr., Oct., 1887.

HALLEY, JOHN.—Ed. at Gordon's Coll., Marischal Coll., and Univ. of Aberdeen, M.B., C.M., D.Ph.; civ. surg. attached to R.A.M.C., Sierra Leone, 1900-2; ditto, S. Africa, 1902; dist. med. offr., Fiji, 1903; health offr. and local authority, Suva, 1903; supt. of Muana leper asylum, Bega, 1904; dist. med. offr., Navua, 1905; stip. mag. of the colony, 1906; govt. med. offr., Levuka, 1906.
HALLEY, ROBT. HENRY.—Ed. Dollar Academy, Scotland; clk., central repatriation bd. Bloemfontein, Apl., 1902, to May, 1903; clk. to col. sec., O.R.C., May, 1903.

HALLIFAX, EDWIN RICHARD.—Ed. Blundell's schl.; Blundell's scholar, Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1896; cadet, Hong Kong, 1897; passed cadet, 1900; ag. police mag., New Territory, 1899; J.P., 1900; asst. sup. of pol. and pol. mag., N.T., 1901; ag. regisr.-genl., May-June, 1902; ag. deputy sup. of police, 1903-4.

HALLIFAX, F. J.—Cadet, S. Sttlmts., Mar., 1893; passed final exam. in Malay, June, 1894; ag. dist. offr. Penang, Apr., 1896, and Malacca, Nov., 1896; supt. of educn., Penang, July, 1897; dist. offr., June, 1898; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, June, 1901; ag. coll. of land revenue, Penang, Apr., 1902; also ag. regisr. of deeds and regisr. of bills of sale, Penang, May, 1902; asst. P.M.G., Penang, Aug., 1902, but continued to act as coll. of land rev.; ag. collr. of land rev., and offr. in charge of Treasury, Malacca, Apr., 1903; 2nd mag., Penang, Jan., 1905; ag. sen. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Mar., 1906; pres. mun. comsrs., Penang, 1st Sept., 1906.
HALL-JONES, Hon. WM.—Min. for rlys. and pub. wks., New Zealand.
HAMBLIN, R.A.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser. and temporarily employed in the science and art dept. from Mar. 21st to May 26th, 1899; assigned to C.O. July, 1899.

HAMILTON, CHARLES BOUGHTON, C.M.G. (1896).—B. 1850; entered the service, 1865; 2nd clk. rec.-gen.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1873, 1877; col. book-keeper, 1879; ag. rec.-gen. 1881; and ag. aud.-gen., 1883 to 1886; received thanks of coun. on leaving for post of rec.-gen., &c., Trinidad, 1886; J.P.; ag. audr.-gen., M.E.C., and M.L.C., 1886; mem. of bd. of educn., 1887; of bd. of health, 1888; M.L.C., 1889; chmn. of comttee. for widows' fund, 1889; ag. col. sec., 1890; chmn. hrbr. improvement comttee., and ag. audr.-gen. and col. sec., 1891; mem. coll. coun., 1891; rec.-gen., Br. Guiana, Dec., 1892; ag. aud. gen. and mem. ct. of policy, May, 1893, to May, 1896, and again Oct., 1895; sen. comsrs., Vlissingen estate; chmn. spirits comn., "Trotman," trust comttee.; dir. widows' and orphans' fund, &c.; mem. exec. coun., Dec., 1895; off. mem. ct. of policy, July, 1896; chrmn., excise bd., 1901; chrmn., educn. comsrs., 1902; vote of thanks of comt. for spec. serv. gratuitously rendered in public roads and bridges admnstr., 1894 to 1905.
HAMILTON, CHARLES EDWARD—Called to the bar, Ontario, 1865; elected mayor of Winnipeg, 1885; elected to Manitoba legislature, 1885; atty.-gen., Manitoba, Feb., 1887-8.
HAMILTON, R. W.—Ed. St. Paul's schol. and Trin. Hall, Camb.; B.A., 1889; M.A., 1892; sec. coms. of inquiry, Dominica, 1893-4; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1895; dist. comsrs., Lagos, 1895.

HAMILTON, Sir W. A. B., K.C.M.G. (1897), C.B. (1892), C.M.G. (1887)—B. 1844; ed. at Harrow; apptd. a jun. clk. in the C.O., May, 1864, after compet. exam.; sent on secret service to N. America, 1867; 2nd class clk., 30th Sept., 1st class clk., July, 1879; and priv. sec. to Mr. Knatchbull-Hugessen (now Lord Brougham), M.P., 9th Oct., 1872; called to the bar (Inner Tem.), 18th Nov., 1872; priv. sec. to Mr. James Lowther, M.P., under sec. of state for the cols., 25th Feb., 1874, to 14th Feb., 1878; priv. sec. to the Right Hon. James Lowther, M.P., ch. sec. for Ireland, 15th Feb., 1878; capt in the E. Lothian yeomanry cavalry, 5th June, 1878; major, 18th June, 1883; hon. lieut.-col. and comdg. regt., 1894; comdg. 12th yeomanry brigade, 1895; author of "Mr. Montenello: a Romance of the Civil Service"; priv. sec. to Mr. Stanhope, 3rd Aug., 1886; and to Sir H. Holland (now Lord Knutsford), Jan., 1887, to Aug., 1892; sec. to col. confee., 1887; prin. clk., 1st Jan., 1894; ch. clk., Mar., 1896; apptd. offr. of arms of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1901.

HAMILTON, WILLIAM WYMOND, M.A., F.R.M.S.—Clare Coll., Camb. (hon. classics, 1873); 1st class prof., Roy. Coll., Mauritius, Jan., 1879; sen. prof., May, 1887; ag. rector, May, 1891, to July, 1892, and Apr. to Nov., 1894, again May, 1900; rector, 1901; ag. director of pub. instn. in 19, 2, 19 and 19.

HANCOCK, HERBERT HENRY, M.A.—Scholar John's Coll., Oxon; 2nd class classical moda., 1876; 2nd class classical finals, 1878; B.A., 1879; M.A., 1883; asst. master Cheltenham Coll., 1879; 2nd master Bosworth schl., 1880; prof. classics and moral philos., Codrington Coll., Barbados, 1883; ag. prin., 1889-91; 2nd master, Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, 1892; ag. prinl., Apr. to Dec., 1894; clk. to coll. coun., 1892; lieut. Trinidad field artillery.

HANCOCK, WILLIAM J., M.I.C.E., M.I.E.E.—Ed. Glasgow Univ.; supt. of telephones, W. Australia, 1885; supt. of telegraphs and telephones, 1890; hon. local sec. and treas. for W. Australia of the institn. of electrical engineers of London; govt. electrical engineer, 1894.

HAND, JOHN ST. VINCENT.—1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s off., Lagos, Oct., 1898; ch. clk., gen. man.'s off., Lagos govt. rlyw., Apr., 1901; asst. sec., sec.'s off., S. Nigeria, Feb., 1904; priv. sec. to J. Th. Jorburn, Esq., ag. gov. of Lagos, July, 1905; and clk. to exec. coun.
HANITSCH, KARL RICHARD, PH.D. (Jena).—Demonstrator of Zoology, Univ. Coll., Liverpool, 1887; curator and librarian, Raffles library and museum, Singapore, 1895.

HANLEY, ALLAN HASTINGS, C.M.G. (1903); F.R.C.S.—Med. offr., Niger Coast Prot. (now S. Nigeria), May, 1893; sen. med. offr., Apl., 1901; dep. prin. med. offr., May, 1903; ag. prin. med. offr., Dec., 1903 to Apl., 1904.

HANLEY, GEORGE A.—Joined R.I.C., June, 1886; Leeward Is. pol. force, Dec., 1889; lieut. and adjt., Sept., 1899; Hythe musk. certif., including machine guns, June, 1898; inspr. of pol., Antigua, Apr., 1902; lieut. and adjt., Antigua def. force, 1902; capt., Mar., 1904; visiting just., cent. prison, Dec., 1903, to July, 1905; inspr. of pol., St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1905; capt. and adjt., St. Kitts def. force, July, 1905; J.P. for Antigua and St. Kitts-Nevis.

HANNAM, WILLOUGHBY, M.I.C.E.—Apprentice to M. Du Bays, C.E., Reading; ast. survr., Melb. and River Murray rly., Victoria, 1860; engnr. Moreton Bay tramway co., Brisbane, 1861-63; dist. engineer (S. dist.) Queensland govt. rlys., 1863-8; contractor's engnr. on same, 1868-83; dist. engnr. and in charge of surveys cent. div., Queensland rlys., 1872-85; ch. engnr., N. div., 1885; has been engaged on survey of 1,060 miles of rly., and directed survey of 250 miles more.

HANNYNGTON, JOHN ARTHUR.—Capt., Indian army; temp. maj., King's African Rifles; ed. at Unit. Serv. Coll., Westward Ho!; passed in Hindustani, higher standard, Kiswahili, govt. test. exam. for promotion to field offr. and staff employ; gazetted 8th June, 1889; apptd. comdt., Jubaland Camel Corps, 15th Dec., 1901; comdt., 6th King's African Rifles, Somaliland Prot., 28th Feb., 1905.

HARBIN, JOHN ALPHONSE.—Ch. clk. to regisr., Grenada, July, 1884; ditto to regisr. sup. et., July, 1886; marshal V.-A. ct., Mar., 1887; ag. regisr. sup. et., and ag. regisr. in V.-A. ct., May, 1888, to Apr., 1890; acctnt., treasy., July, 1890; sec. to comsrs. pub. wks. dept. inquiry, Dec., 1890; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1892 to Feb., 1893; ag. inspr. of schools Feb., 1893, to Mar., 1894; inspr. of schools Mar., 1894; ag. col. postmaster, Aug.-Sept., 1894; mem. bd. of ed. Apr., 1895; ag. regisr. sup. et., Aug.-Sept., 1901; ag. inspr. of schools, St. Vincent, Sept.-Nov., 1901; mem. bd. of ed., St. Vincent, Sept.-Nov., 1901; ag. pol. mag. and assts. treas., Carriacou, Dec., 1901, to Jan., 1902.
HARBOUR, E. W. H.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to bd. of trade, 22nd Jan., 1901; transf'd to C.O., 22nd July, 1901.
HARBY, F. N.—Apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., 1st May, 1906.

HARCOURT, ALGERNON BERNARD.—Apptd. cons. agnt. in Oil R. Protect., Oct., 1892; ag. v-cons., Bonny dist., June, 1894, to July, 1895; dist. comsnr., Bonny, 1st Apr., 1896; ag. v-cons. cent. div. on various occasions; transf'd to Cross Riv. div., Dec., 1899; travllg. comsnr., S. Nig., 1st Apr., 1900; ag. divsl. comsnr., Cross Riv. div., May-Dec., 1900; ag. res. Benin City Territories, 1901 and 1902; divl. comsnr., Cross River, 17th Jan., 1903; polit. offr., M'Kpani expdn., 1903 (medal and clasp); ag. sec. and dep. high comsnr., 24th Feb. to 3rd Apr., 1905; asst. prov. comsnr., E. Prov., 1st May, 1906.

HARDING, ALFRED J.—Ed. Christ's Coll., Brecon, and St. John's Camb.; foundtn. schlr., 1899; 1st class nat. sci. trip. part I., 1900; B.A., 1900; apptd. after compet. exam. as 2nd cl. clk., Oct., 1901.

HARDING, EDWARD J.—Ed. Dulwich Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1899); 1st cls. class. mods., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1903; B.A., 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., marine dept., bd. of trade, 30th Nov., 1903; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 31st May, 1904.

HARDY, WENTWORTH MARMDUKE.—Entered survey dept., S. Australia, Oct., 1885; accompanied the survr.-gen. as survr. and draughtsman to Port Darwin, Dec., 1888; rose step by step, till apptd. Jan., 1878, trigonometrical survr. In 1884, at the recommendation of the survr.-gen. of Victoria, and to carry out his suggested reformation, appointed inspr. of surveys and prof. adviser, survey dept., Tasmania.

HARE, FREDERICK ARTHUR.—Ed. St. Columba's Coll., Wicklow; clk. to mags., Vasse, W. Australia, 1880; clk. and asst. priv. sec. to Sir William Robinson, during various periods from 1880 to 1882; inspr. of pol. for the S. dists. of the col., 1882; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to Sir F. N. Broome; ag. govt. res., Wyndham, Cambridge Gulf, 1866-7; ag. govt. res., Albany, 1887-8; res. mag. York, 1888-97; warden and res. mag. East Coolgardie Goldfield, 1897-1900; comsnr. of police, 1900.

HARE, REGINALD CHARLES.—Clk. treasry., W. Australia, Apr., 1881; pol. inspr. S. dists., June, 1886; ag. govt. res., Wyndham, May, 1888; ditto, Roebourne, June, 1889; sec., W. Australian govt. agency, London, May, 1890.

HARMAN, WM. HENRY.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv. and assigned to science and art dept., 10th Apr., 1900; and to bd. of agric., 6th June, 1901; transf'd to C.O., 27th Jan., 1902.
HARPER, FRANCIS HENRY.—Transferred from the service of the Royal Niger Co. as supplementary clk. to C.O., 1st Jan., 1900.

HARRIOTT, ALEXIS WYNNS.—Ed. at Yale; P.A. 1856; M.A. and B.Ph., 1859; ag. col. survr. and inspr., P.W., Turks and Caicos Islds., Feb. to Apl., 1864; J.P., 1879; unoffl. mem. legis. bd., 1881 to 1888, when apptd. asst. comsnr. Grand Turk; ag. comsnr., Apl. to July, 1888; manager savings bk., 1889; mem. bd. of educn., 1891; coroner, 1893; ag. auditor on various occasions, 1888-1906; ag. offl. mem. of legis. bd., 1904 and 1905.

HARRIOTT, EDMUND COWLES.—Clk. to comsnr., and to legis. bd., Turks and Caicos Islds. Nov., 1887; inspected govt. schls., 1890, 1891, and 1902; ag. registr. sup. ct., and of deeds, 1891 and 1894; ag. asst. comsnr., dist. mag., registr. sup. ct., man. savings bank and inspr. of pol. and prisons, G. Turk, May to July, 1896, Aug. to Nov., 1901, and May to Aug., 1906; ag. aud., Aug. and Sept., 1901, and June to Aug., 1906; ag. dist. mag., visiting Caicos Is., July, 1902; ag. acctnt. and clk. (collr. of cust., postmtr., cashier and regisr. shipping); comsnr.'s office, Apl. to June, 1903; confirmed July, 1903; ag. offl. mem., legis. bd., 1906; is a J.P.

HARRIS, CHARLES ALEXANDER, C.B. (1904); C.M.G. (1900); B.A.—B. 1855; scholar, prizeman, and Porteus medallist of Christ's Coll., Camb.; Tancred student in com. law, Linc.'s Inn, 1877; 12th classic, 1878; apptd. 2nd class clk., C.O. after open competn., 1st July, 1879; sec. to W. Indies Finance Comms., Dec., 1882; asst. sec. Sugar Bounties Comsce., 1889; priv. sec. to Mr. Buxton, M.P., Jan., 1894, to June, 1895; and to the Earl of Selborne, June, 1895; 1st class clk., 1896; specially engaged in work connected with Venezuelan bndry. arbitn., 1896-8; prin. clk., Nov., 1898; sent to Paris, 1899, in connection with the Venez. bndry. arbitn.; conducted British case in Brazilian-B. Guiana bndry. arbitn., 1901-4; author of numerous articles in Dict. of Pol. Econ. on economic and statistical subjects, and of lives of various colonial govs. and statesmen in Dict. of Nat. Biog.

HARRISON, CUTHBERT WOODVILLE.—B. 1874; ed. at Sherborne Schlr., Clifton Coll., Balliol Coll., Oxford, and with Walter Wren, Esq.; 3rd cls. class. mods., 1894; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1897; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, May, 1899; also ag. harb. mast., Klang, Feb., 1901; passed cadet, Apr., 1901; asst. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, continued also as ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang and harb. mast. at Port Swettenham, Oct., 1901; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Dec., 1903; ditto, Tampin, Mar., 1904; also asst. dist. offr., Matang, Oct., 1904; ag. asst. sec. to res., Mar., 1905; ag. sec. to res., Aug., 1906; ag. asst. sec. to res., Dec., 1906.

HARRISON, JAMES HERBERT HUGH.—Ed. Regent's Park Coll., and New College (Lond.), and at University Coll. and Hospital; F.R.C.P. (Lond.); M.R.C.S. (Eng.); dist. mag. and dist. surg., Cayo, Br. Honduras, 1890; ag. dist. surg., Orange Walk, 1891, 1892; dist. comsnr. and dist. surg., Orange Walk, June, 1892.

HARRISON, JOHN B., C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1856; Nat. Sc. scholar, Christ's Coll., Camb., B.A. (Nat. Sc. Tripos), 1887; M.A., 1887; prof. of chemistry and agricul. science, Barbados, Sept., 1879; govt. analyst and prof. of chem., Br. Guiana, Dec., 1889; is F.C.S., F.G.S., and F.I.C., and author of several papers relating to the agriculture and geology of the W. Indies.

HARRISSON, SYDNEY THIRLWALL.—Ed. Blackheath; apptd. asst. acct., P.W.D., G. C. Col., 9th Nov., 1894; ch. acct., 2nd June, 1897; asst. acct., W.A.F.F., 26th Feb., 1898; ch. acct., 8th Nov., 1899; treas. N. Nig., 1st Jan., 1900.

HART, JOHN HINCHLEY.—Supt. King's house garden; and grounds, Jamaica, during their formation, Nov., 1875; supt. govt. cinchona plantations, Oct., 1881; ag. dir. pub. gardens and plantations, July, 1886; ch. botanical dept., Trinidad, Mar., 1887; is a fellow Linn. Soc.; author of a "Botanist's Ramble in Cent. America," a work on cacao culture, curing, and fermenting (1891), a report on forest conservation (1891), and pamphlets relating to the cultivation of W. Indian products; engaged by the govt. of the Windward Is. to inspect and report on the working of the botanical stations, St. Vincent and Grenada (1890); lieut. Trinidad rifle volrs., Mar., 1889; capt., 1897; capt. reserve, 1898; govt. member, Trinidad public library; ex. off. mem. agric. soc., Trinidad; supt. botanic station, Tobago; author of paper on rubber cultivation in West Indies.
HART, W. EDWARDS.—Librarian, public library and museum, Mauritius, 1st Oct., 1903.

HARTE, LOVELACE FRED. EDWARD RALPH.—First class certif. at I.C. Coll. in Dec., 1875; articled law student, 3rd Jan., 1876; ag. clk. of the peace the same year; Jan., 1877, clk. in customs dept., Trinidad; elk. in audit office, Sept., 1878; 7th elk., Jan., 1883, June, 1887; 2nd elk., regiar, sup. ct., 1887; ch. elk., 1897; also comsrr. of oaths for whole isld.; ag. dep. registr. on occasions in 1896-7-8.
HARTLEY, CHAS.—M.A., Cantab.; lecturer in English and modern languages, Royal Coll., Ceylon, 11th Dec., 1896; principal, Royal Coll., 23rd June, 1903.

HARTLEY, E. B., C.M.G. (1901).—Surg.-Lieut.-Col., C. M. R.; created V.C. for gallantry in the operations against the stronghold of the Basuto chief Morosi; prin. med. offr., Cape col. forces, 1878; served through the Gaika, Morosi, and Basuto wars, 1878-81; govt. med. offr., Basutoland, 1874-7; ret., 1904.

HARVEY, GEORGE LEONARD, C.E.—Asst. dir. of wks., So. Nigeria, June, 1900; asst. dir. of pub. wks., 1901; deputy dir. of pub. wks., 1901; ag. dir. of pub. wks., Mar. to Nov., 1902, and Apl. to Nov., 1904.
HARWARD, JOHN.—Principal, Royal Coll., Ceylon, 1st Apl., 1892; dir. of pub. instrn., 23rd June, 1903.

HASTINGS, W. C. HOLLAND.—H.M.'s navy, 1865-81; retired with rank of commodr.; inspr. of imgrts., Jamaica, Nov., 1881, to May, 1889; coroner, Nov., 1886, to May, 1888; ch. of pol. and ag. town warden, St. Lucia, May to Dec., 1889; also for two months harbrmr. and pilot; asst. harbrmr., Hong Kong, Dec., 1889, to Feb., 1899; ag. harbrmr., Feb., 1891, to Apr., 1892; ag. pol. mag., Apr., 1892, to May, 1895; ag. capt. supl. of pol. and supt. fire brigade, May, 1895, to Apr., 1896; ag. harbrmr., June to Dec., 1895; ag. pol. mag., Apr., 1896, to Apr., 1897, and Feb., 1898, to Feb., 1899; postmnr.-gen., Feb., 1899; seconded to bury the dead in plague epidemic, 1894, June to Aug., inclusive; mem. legis. coun., Nov., 1891, to Apr., 1892, and again from June, 1895, to Oct., 1896; mem. of various comms. and comtees.
HATCHER, C. M.—Apptd., after compet. exam., elk. 2nd div. civ. ser., and assigned to W.O., 11 Sept., 1893; transfed. to C.O., 18 Mar., 1896; minor staff offr., Feb., 1902.
HATHERLEY, GEORGE MOOR.—2nd lieut. roy. artily., Nov., 1890; lieut., Nov., 1893; apptd. with local rank of capt. to comm. of permanent force at Albany, W. Australia, July, 1899.
HATHERTON, BARON.—Col. the Hon. Edward George Percy Littleton, C.M.G. (1880); b. 1842; late mil. sec. to the gov.-gen. of Canada.

HAZELOCK, SIR ARTHUR ELIBANK, G.C.M.G. (1895), G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E. (1896), K.C.M.G. (1884).—B. 1844; entered the army, 32nd lt. infy., Jan., 1862; capt., 1873; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to the offr. admnstng. the govt. of Mauritius from July, 1873, to Jan., 1874; ag. ch. civ. comsrr., Seychelles Is., from Apr., 1874, until Feb., 1875; mem. of the exec. and legis. couns. of Fiji, May, 1875; apptd. (provisionally) col. sec. and rec.-gen., Fiji, 1st Sept., 1875; pres. of Nevis, 1877; admnstg. the govt. of St. Lucia from June, 1878, to Aug., 1879; ch. civ. comsrr., Seychelles, Oct., 1879; gov. W. Africa Sttlmnts., 1881; Apr., 1881, sent on a mission to Paris in conjunction with Mr. (now Sir A.) Hemming, of the C.O., to assist in negotiating the settlement of certain questions at issue between Great Britain and France, with regard to territory on the W. Coast of Africa; apptd. H.M.'s consul for Liberia, Dec., 1881; negotiated the sttlmnt. of a coterminous boundary between S. Leone and Liberia, and inquired into the claims of Br. subjects against the Liberian Govt., Mar., 1882; gov. of Trinidad, Dec., 1884; gov. of Natal and special comsrr. for Zulu affairs, 1885; also gov. of Zululand, 1887; attended internat. confce. at Brussels on the suppression of the slave trade, 1889; gov. of Ceylon, 1890; of Madras, 1895 to 1900; of Tasmania, 1901 to 1904.

HAWKINS, ISAAC THOMAS, M.I.C.E.—Mem. of incorp. assoc. of mun. and county engrns.; diplomas in engineering science and construction, sanitary science and pub. health law; articled and served through shops; held several apptmts. on mun. engineering govt.; col. survr., Lagos, 19th June, 1897; title altered to dep. dir. of pub. wks., 1st Jan., 1898; prov. engrnr., S. Nigeria, 1st May, 1906.

HAY, SIR JAMES SHAW, K.C.M.G. (1889), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1839; joined 89th (Princess Victoria's) regt. in 1858; served in India during the latter part of the Mutiny; was adjt. for some time, and afterwards instructor of musketry; ag. adjt. to the provisional depot batt. at Dum Dum in 1859; adjt. to the Glasgow Highland volrs. in 1868 at their formation; asst. inspr., G. Coast armed native pol., 1875; dist. comsrr. of Accra, Oct., 1875; inspr.-gen., G. Coast constab., Apr., 1877; asst. col. sec., G. Coast col., 1878; had charge of several special missions to the tribes in the interior; ag. col. sec. from Oct., 1878, to Jan., 1880, and again, Aug., 1880; inspr.-gen. of pol. Mauritius; admstr. of the Gambia, 1885; adminstr. the govt. of the W. Africa Sttlmnts., from July, 1886, to Dec., 1887; gov. Sierra Leone, Oct., 1888; served in the Yomnie expedn., 1887-8 (medal and clasp); ag. admstr., Barbados, 1891; gov., Barbados, 1892 to 1900.

HAYCRAFT, THOS. WAGSTAFFE.—B.A., St. John's Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Tem., 1885; practised in S.E. circuit and Sussex sessions; exams. of the ct., 1889 to 1899; apptd. arbitrator on bd. of Lon. chan. of arbitration, 1897; pres. dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, 1899; ag. Queen's advocate, Oct., 1900, to Feb., 1901; author of "Executive Powers in relation to Crime and Disorder."

HAYNES, THOS. BENJAMIN.—Apptd. dispenser, penal settlement, Massaruni, B. Guiana, 6th June, 1870; 1st cl. prison warder, 12th July, 1877; schlstr., to penal settmts., 1st Sept., 1878; dispenser in charge of leper asylum, Kaow Is., 27th Oct., 1879; elk. to penal settmts., 27th Oct., 1883; keeper, New Amsterdam prison, Berbice, 5th July, 1887; supt., Georgetown prison, 15th June, 1894.
HAYNES, W. LINDSAY H.—Clk. to ch. just. Barbados, 1874-8; elk. to comsrrs. apptd. to investigate offences against rioters, 1876; int. rev. offr., dist. B, 1878; now inspr. of int. rev. offr. regis.

HAZELAND, Francis Arthur.—Cllk. to puisne judge, Hong Kong, Nov., 1878; ag. dep. regisr., Mar. to Nov., 1882; clk. to ch. just., Sept., 1883; ag. ch. clk. col. sec. office, Mar. to Oct., 1890; ag. clk. of coun., Mar. to Dec., 1890; dep. regisr. and acctnt., Jan., 1896; J.P., 1894; ag. regisr., sup. ct., offl. trustee, regisr. of companies and land offr., Oct., 1895, to Feb., 1896; has passed in Chinese (higher standard); called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Hilary, 1900; ag. pol. mag., June, 1900, to June, 1901; 2nd pol. mag., 7th June, 1901.
HEALY, P. J.—Crown prosecutor, S. dist., N.S. Wales, Jan., 1878; ditto, S.W. dist., 1879; crown prosecutor, Sydney, Jan., 1882.
HEAN, Hon. Alexander.—B. 1859; warden and coun. of Sorrell mun., Tasmania, since 1886; M.L.A. for Sorrell since 1903; min. of lands and wks., agric. and rlwys., and comsnr. of main roads, July, 1904.
HEARSEY, Herbert Hyde Young.—Apptd. med. offr., B. Cent. Africa Prot., 6th June, 1896; principal officer, April, 1902.
HECTOR, Sir James, M.D., F.R.S., K.C.M.G. (1887), C.M.G. (1875).—B. 1834; dir. of geological surveys and curator of the col. museum, N. Zealand.
HEIDENSTAM, Dr. F. C., C.M.G. (1884).—Ch. med. offr., Cyprus, 1882.

HEIDENSTAM, O. C.—Ed. at King's Schl., Warwick; vol. clk., col. sec.'s off., Cyprus, 5th Feb., 1897; 3rd div. clk., ditto, 16th Jan., 1899; inspr. of pol., 12th Jan., 1904; sent to Egypt for training with H.M.'s troops, May, June, and July, 1904; in command of depot, 8th Aug., 1904; in command of Kyrena div., gov. of prison and dep. coroner, 15th July, 1905; in command of Papho div., gov. of prison and dep. coroner, 4th Aug., 1906; acted as comsnr. for Papho on several occasions; passed in mod. Greek, lower standard, Apr., 1906.

HELY-HUTCHINSON, The Hon. Sir Walter Francis, G.C.M.G. (1897), K.C.M.G. (1888), C.M.G. (1883).—B.1849; B.A. of Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar by the soc. of the Inner Tem.; accompanied Sir Hercules Robinson to Fiji as special attacheé on the occasion of the cession of the islands to Gr. Britain, in Sept.-Oct., 1874; priv. sec. to Sir Hercules Robinson, 10th Oct., 1874, for Fiji affairs; priv. sec. for N.S. Wales affairs, Jan., 1875; col. sec. of Barbados, Nov., 1877; ch. sec. to gov't. of Malta, 1883; and lieutenant-gov. and ch. sec. to gov't., 1884; gov'f. of Windward Is., 1889; gov'f. of Natal, 1893; gov'f. Cape, 1901.

HEMERY, Percy.—1st clk., gov't. savings bank, Br. Guiana, July, 1880; 3rd clk., treas., Jan., 1881; cashier, Aug., 1881; ag. ch. clk., 1884 and 1885; acctnt., govt. savings bank, Apr., 1888; ag. col. book-keeper, July, 1888, and Aug., 1890; ag. asst. rec-gen., and ag. sub-admstr., Berbice, Feb., 1891; ag. dep. rec-gen., Apr., 1891, May to Dec., 1892, May, 1893-1894, twice in 1895-8; sec. cattle diseases coman., Oct., 1891; asst. rec-gen., Berbice, June, 1897; J.P., and comsnr. of oaths and affid., June, 1897; sub-admstr.gen., Berbice, Nov., 1897 (additional); ag. compt. of cust., 22nd Feb., 1904; asst. recvr-gen.; ag. recvr-gen., July, 1906.

HEMING, Sir A. W. L., G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.M.G.(1890), C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1841; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., Feb., 1860; promoted Oct., 1864, 1st junior class; priv. sec. to Sir F. Rogers (Lord Blachford), 20th May, 1867, till 20th May, 1871; priv. sec. to Mr. Herbert, 21st May, 1871, till 29th Sept., 1872; apptd. a 2nd class clk., 30th Sept., 1872; 1st class clk., Nov., 1874; priv. sec. to the Earl of Cadogan, 2nd March, 1878, to 30th June, 1879; sent on a special mission to Paris, Apr., 1879; prin. clk., 1st July, 1879; again proceeded on special service to Paris, May, 1881; Br. deleg. to the W. African confce. at Berlin, 1884; on special service at Paris, 1889-90 and 1894, in connection with the delimitation of French and English possessions on the W. coast of Africa; gov'f. of Br. Guiana, Mar., 1896; gov'f. of Jamaica, 1898 to 1904.
HENDERSON, A.—Apptd.clk., refugee camps, O.R.C., 1st Nov., 1901; clk. attached to audit dept., April, 1902; clk., treasy., 20th Aug., 1902.
HENDERSON, J. T.—Shorthand reporter of debates, legis. coun., Natal, 1883.

HENDERSON, W. R., C.M.G. (1901), M.D., and M.Ch., Dublin (1878).—B. 1855; entered army med. staff, Mar., 1880; served in India, and in Egypt during Soudan and Nile campaigns; senr. med. offr., imperial troops, Cape Coast Castle, 1886, and at S. Leone, 1888; ret. from army as capt., Feb., 1889; now on reserve of officers; entered Lagos med. serv., Oct., 1889; prin. med. offr. of expedit. against the Jebus, 1892, ment. in desps. and name brought to the notice of S. of S., received medal and clasp; during serv. in Lagos was in charge of secretariat, ag. trav. comsnr., and polit. offr. at Jebu Ode; prin. med. offr. of col. troops, Ashanti expedit., 1895-6 (ment. in desps. and received star); apptd. prin. med. offr., G. coast, June, 1897; accompanied late Sir W. Maxwell on expeditn. to N. Territories, July to Nov., 1897, and received thanks of H.M.'s govt. for services; mem. of comtee. at C.O. for re-organisation of W. African med. staff, Oct., 1901, and received thanks of S. of S.

HENDY, Charles Lowick.—Sen. clerical asst. inspr. prisons office, Br. Guiana, 1881; ag. clk. of supplies and sec. to the bd. of prisons, July to Oct., 1885; July to Sept., 1888; and June, 1890, to Jan., 1891; issuer to convict prison, Rio Massaruni, Nov., 1890; clk. of supplies and sec. to bd. of prisons, June, 1892; ag. sup't. orphan asyl., Nov. to Dec., 1895, and Feb. to July, 1896; ag. man. dir. orphan asyl., in addition to duties as clk. of supplies, from July to Nov., 1898, May, 1899, to Jan., 1900, Nov. to Dec., 1900; ag. sup't. orphan asyl., July, 1901, to Jan., 1903; ag. sup't., almshouse, and sec., poor law comsurs., June, 1903.
HENKEL, C. C. H.—Conservator of forests, Transkeian territories, Cape Col., July, 1889.
HERBERT, Chan. Edward.—M.H.A., South Australia, 1900-05; govt. res. and judge of N. Territory, 1905.

HERCHENRODER, Furcy Alfred, K.C.—Ed. Roy. Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Mid. Tem., June, 1888; ag. crown pros., Mauritius, 1893-8; leg. adviser, pol. mag., crown pros., conservr. of mortgages, and curator of vacant estates, Seychelles, May, 1898; judge Seychelles, 1900; proc. and advoc. gen., Mauritius, 3rd Apr., 1905.

HEREFORD, George A.—Cadet, S. Stltms., Nov., 1898; ag. dist. officer, Balik Pulau, Penang, Apr., 1899; passed final exam. in Malay, Oct., 1901; supt. of educn., Penang, Nov., 1902; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Jan., 1904; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, May, 1906.

HERN, W. Stanley.—Lieut., Duke of Edinburgh's Wiltshire Regt.; served in Mashonaland rebellion, 1896-1897; in S. Africa, 1900-1902; with W.A.F.F., 1904-05; offr.comdg.preventive service, Lagos, 1906; dist. comsnr., June, 1906; offr. comdg.ljebu Ode, 1906.

HERVEY, DUDLEY FRANCIS AMELIUS, C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1849; ed. at Marlborough; cadet, S. Sttlmnts., May, 1867; passed in Malay in Nov., 1868; ag. priv. sec. to gov. in Mar., 1869; ch. clk. and interp. to the lieut.-gov., Penang, Jan., 1870, in which year he accompanied H.M.S. "Algerine" to inquire into various cases of piracy on the coast of Acheen; sen. sworn clk., sup. ct., Singapore, Jan., 1871, also clk. in bankruptcy, and J.P. and ag. mag., Sept., 1871; Aug., 1878, pol. mag., Malacca; has accompanied various expdts. to the different native states of the peninsula; J.P.; res. councillor, Malacca, 1882; also supt. of Negri Sembilan, 1883-6; ret., Dec., 1893.
HESKETH, GILBERT WOOD.—Ed. Birkenhead, and Caugton, Ches.; engineer, corporation, City of Cape Town engine works; joined service of P.W.D., O.R.C., 2nd July, 1903.

HEWICK, JOHN EDWIN.—Ed. Highgate Schl.; asst. supt. of pol., P.W., 1872; during the Parak war commanded an expdtn. across the country in pursuit of Sultan Ismail. Called to the bar of the Mid.
