ROGER, CAPT. ARCHIBALD.—Ensign 2nd roy. Lanark militia, Aug., 1859; gazetted to the 2nd W. I. regt., Nov., 1860; lieut., Aug., 1861; capt., Oct., 1866; exchanged to the 1st batt., 17th regt., Feb., 1867; sold out of the service, Jan., 1870; was priv. sec. to Mr. Bayley, gov. of the Bahamas, Dec., 1860, to July, 1863, and fort adjt., Jamaica, Dec., 1863, to Sept., 1864; pol. mag. of the rural dist., St. Christopher, June, 1873; dist. mag., May, 1874.

ROGERS, C. S.—Obtnd. higher standard certif. at Imperial Forest Schl., Dehra Dun, India, with medals for forestry and forest engnrng., 1893; apptd. to Indian forest dept. as forest ranger, 26th May, 1893; promoted to extra asst. conserv. of forests, 4th grade, 26th Mar., 1894; passed exam. in Burmese language, higher standard, 1897; promoted to 3rd grade, 3rd Sept., 1897; 2nd grade, 27th Nov., 1899; forest officer, Trinidad, 24th Mar., 1901.

ROGERS, JAMES LUKE.—Joined the Leeward Is. pol., Nov., 1877; served in Antigua, Dominica, and Montserrat; corporal, Jan., 1888; ret., Nov., 1889; 3rd cl. warder, Montserrat gaol, Dec., 1889; ag. gaoler, June, 1898.

ROGERS, JOHN ARTHUR.—Entd. P.W.D., Gold Coast, 4th Nov., 1893; offi. mem., Accra town coun., 12th Feb., 1901; appraiser for town coun., Cape Coast, July, 1905; served in Ashanti expdn., 1895-96; recd. thanks of headqrs. staff; 2nd lieut., G. Coast vols., 28th July, 1899; lieut., 17th Aug., 1900; capt., 30th Oct., 1904; certif. army form E. 511, Chelsea barracks, Mar., 1900.

ROGERS, THOMAS.—Employed by Cape govt., 1874 to 1889; by Main Reef G.M.C., Transvaal, 1889-90; in naval dockyard, Simon's Town, 1890-91; re-apptd. by Cape govt., Feb., 1891, to Mar., 1897; inspr. of drills and diamond settler, late O.F.S., Mar., 1897, to Oct., 1899; ditto, Imperial military govt., 1st Oct., 1900; ditto, civil govt., O.R.C., 1st July, 1902.

ROHRWEGGER, FRANK, C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1859; ed. at Radley and Brasenose; called to the bar, Inn. Tem., hilly, 1885; joint author of "Parochial Assessment Rules"; a dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1894; ag. Queen's advoc., 1894; D.C., E. dist., 1895; pol. mag., 1886; resigned.
ROLANDO, FRANÇOIS ADOLPHE.—Regis. and offr. of the civil status, Seychelles, 6th Jan., 1890; crown atty., Mauritius, 10th Sept., 1897; ag. recvr. of registn. dues, 1st Oct., 1905.

ROLLESTON, THE HON. WILLIAM.—Ed. at Rossall; grad. at Emmanuel Coll., Camb.; 2nd cl. in the classical tripes in 1855; was apptd. a mem. of an educn. comsnr. which framed the educational system adopted in the prov. of Canterbury, N.Z., 1863; prov. sec. of that prov. and mem. of the bd. of educn., 1864; under-sec., native dept., and inspr. of native schls., 1865; elected supt. of the prov. of Canterbury, 1868; was again elected supt. at two consecutive elections, and held the office at the time of the abolition of the prov., 1876; sat in the col. parl. from the year 1868 to 1887; is a mem. of the senate of the Univ. of N. Zealand; min. for educn., lands, and immigr., 1880; also, temporarily, min. for native affairs, 1881; min. for lands, mines, and immigr., 1882 and 1883, and till the accession of the Stout ministry in 1884; leader of the Opposition, 1891-93.

RONAYNE, THOMAS.—Ed. Wakefield, Yorks; apprentice at Inchicore works, Dublin, of Gt. S. and W. rly. of Ireland; employed subsequently at Atlas loco. wks., Manchester; emig. to N. Z., 1875; employed in various capacities on N. Z. rlys. up to 1886, when apptd. res. and loco. engnr. on Wellington section, where he effected improvements on the Rimutaka incline; subsequently transfld. as loco. engnr. to Addington, and later to Greymouth, to put the Grey-Brunner line into working order. Is now gen. man., N. Z. rlys.

ROPER, H. B., I.S.O. (1903).—Clk. in treasy., and sec. to govt. office, Griqualand W., 1873; ag. postmr.-gen., 1874; C.C. and R.M., Hay, 1876; ditto, Herbert, 1878; ag. R.M., Kimberley, 1881; ag. C.C. and registr. of deeds, 1882; chmn. of municipal and mining areas comsnr., 1882; inspr. of Sihunel's Diggings, 1883; held inquiry into detective dept., of Kimberley, 1884; ch. of detective dept., and comsnr. of pol., Kimberley, 1885; comsnr. of pol., 1888; inspr.-gen. of gaols and convict stations, 1888.

ROSBOROUGH, J.—Ed. at Trin. Coll., Dublin; B.A., Univ. of Dublin, 1893; capt. 6th R.I. Rifles, 1896; served with Gambia expdn., '01; Somaliland, 1902-1904 (medal with two clasps); capt. 1st Batt., King's African Rifles, British Central African Prot.

ROSS, A. E. CLARENCE.—2nd clk., surg.-gen.'s offr., Trinidad, 1875; clk. to dir. pub. wks., Feb., 1876; savings bk. clk., recvr.-gen.'s dept., Nov., 1880; acctnt., savings bk., Oct., 1882; sec. to trade and taxes comsnr., 1886; acctnt., recvr.-gen.'s dept., May, 1889; ch. mag., savings bk., Jan., 1901; P.M.G., Trinidad and Tobago, Sept., 1903.
ROSS, DAVID ALEXANDER.—Admitted to the bar, Quebec, 1884; Q.C., 1873; in 1878-9 mem. legis. assem. for co. of Quebec; mem. of the exec. coun., atty.-gen., and in 1887-8, mem. legis. coun.

ROSE, JAMES FREDERIC.—1st cls. certd. schl. mast., B. Guiana; ass't mast., normal schl. for 2 years; clk. to inspr. of schl., July, 1873, to May, 1877; jun. educntl. dist. offr., May, 1877, to 1882; sen. educntl. offr., 1882 to 1891; 2nd asst. inspr. of schl., 1891 to 1903; acted as 1st asst. ditto on various occasions, 1892-1903; 1st asst. ditto, July, 1903.

ROSS, G. W., LL.D.—Held seat in house of commons, Ontario, 1872 to 1883; min. of educn. in 1883; was inspr. of pub. schls. from 1871 to 1883; comsnr. for Ontario and also for the Dominion to the Ind. and Col. exhib., 1886.

ROSS, JOHN KENNETH MURRAY.—Clerical asst. native dept., Fiji, Sept., 1879; stip. mag., Aug., 1880; ag. res. comsnr., Colo West, Oct., 1882; mag., Tallevu and Ra., Apr., 1887; called to the bar, Fiji, Mar., 1888; ag. res. comsnr., Lomaiviti, Aug., 1888; proceeded in July, 1893, to Rarotonga and Penrhyn Is. as special judicial comsnr., under the W.P. high comsnr.; collr. of customs, ch. inspr. of distilleries etc., Dec., 1896; ag. atty.-gen., May to Sept., 1897; called to the bar, Inner Tem., Jan., 1900; ag. ch. just., June, 1901, to Jan., 1902, and from May to Nov., 1902; ag. atty.-gen., Jan. to May, 1903; mem. exec. coun., Aug., 1903; M.L.C., 1906.

ROSS, REGINALD JAMES BLAIR, B.A.—Ed. at Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inn. Temp., Apr., 1894; dist. comsnr., Lagos, Mar., 1898; ag. pol. mag. on various occasions in 1898, 1899 and 1900; ag. asst. col. sec., 1900; ag. res., Ibadan, Oct., 1301, to Sept., 1902; pol. mag., Jan., 1903; ag. atty.-gen., 1903, 1904 and 1905; ag. judge of sup. ct., Feb. to May, 1905.

ROSS, S. B. C.—Ed. Uppingham Schl. (1889-93); Owens Coll., 1893-96; B.A., 1896; (Eng. lang., lit. honours); univ. schlr., 1896-7; prize for Roman Law Constitutl., Law, 1898; cadet, F.M.S., 1899; sent to Canton to study Chinese, 1900; transfd. to Hong Kong, 1901; ag. registr., land ct., 1902 and 1903; ag. asst. postm.-gen., 1903; sec. to comsnr. of inquiry into sup. ct. regys., 1903; ag. asst. P.M.G., 1903; ag. P.M.G., 1903-4; ag. asst. col. sec., and clk. of couns., 1904; ag. pol. mag., New Territory, 1905.

ROUILLARD, JEAN EDMOND.—Ed. Roy. Coll., Maur., and King Henry VIII.'s Schl., Coventry; called to bar, Mid. Tenn., 1891; licent. laws, Paris, 1891; ag. dist. mag., Plaines Wilhelms, Maur., Oct., 1899; ag. crown pros., Feb., 1900; crown pros., leg. advr., pol. mag., etc., Seychelles, 4th Sept., 1900; mag. Rodrigues, July, 1902.
ROULEAU, CHARLES B.—Ed. at Laval normal schl., Quebec; advoc., Quebec, 1868; dist. mag., Ottawa, 1876; stip. mag., N.W. Territories, 1883; judge, N.W. Territories, 1887.

ROUND, FRANCIS R., M.A., C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1845; ed. at Marl., and Balliol Coll., Oxford, where he obtained a double 2nd class in mods., and a 2nd in classics in final exam., 1868; apptd., after a compet. exam., clk. in the office of the S. of S. for the Cols., Feb., 1869; 2nd class clk., Sept., 1872; accompanied Sir P. Juliany to Mauritius in 1873 as sec. on special mission of inquiry into the civil estabmt. and gen. expenditure of that col.; astt. priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon Sir Michael Hicks Beach, Mar., 1878; priv. sec., 1st Apr., to 27th Apr., 1880; priv. sec. to Mr. Herbert, Jan., 1881; 1st class clk., and financial clk., Dec., 1881; on special service to Br. Bechuanaland and Mauritius, 1886; acted as col. sec., Mauritius, 1886-7; prin. clk., C.O., Mar., 1896; ret., 1905.

ROUPELL, ERNEST P.S., D.S.O.—B. 1870; ed. at Marlbg. Coll.; 2nd lieut. R. Welsh Fus., 1888; transf. to Milford Haven div. R. Engnr. Militia, 1891; cons. agt., Oil Rivers Prot., 1893; transf. to Oil River Irreg., 1894; served in Benin reg., 1894 (mentd. in desp.), and in minor operations; astt. comsnr., Anglo-German Bound. Comsnr., 1896; political offr., 1897-8; divisional comsnr., Brass River div., So. Nig., 1898-1900; Ashanti expdn., 1900-1901; temporary 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 15th July, 1902; astt. sec. to gov't of Malta, 17th Apr., 1905.

ROUSSEAU, JAMES TOD, M.A., Edin.—Ent. civ. ser., 1882; astt. sub-intendant of crown lands, Trinidad, 1897; magistrate and warden of Tobago, 1899; sub-intendant of crown lands, 1903; mag. and warden, Tobago, 1904.

ROUTH, REGINALD LAMY.—Ed. St. Mary's Hall, Southsea and Cadet School; frigate, H.M.S. "Conway"; extra master's certif., 1893; master of govt. vessels, Lagos, Apr., 1897; astt. marine supt., Lagos, Dec., 1902; comdr., H.M.C.S.Y., "Ivy," So. Nigeria, June, 1906.

ROWDEN, ERNEST GEORGE.—Special mast., roy coll., Mauritius, Jan., 1898; head-master, Victoria schl., Seychelles, 27th Dec., 1901; inspr. of schl., Seychelles, 1st Mar., 1902; ditto, Gold Coast, 17th Dec., 1904.

ROWELL, THOMAS IRVINE, C.M.G. (1890), M.D., C.M., Aberdeen.—B. 1841; prin. civ. med. offr., S. Stiltsm., July, 1877; and health offr., port of Singapore, J.P.; acted as col. surg. on several occasions from May, 1868, to Mar., 1876; accompanied Sir W. Robinson on mission to invest king of Siam with G.C.M.G., Nov., 1878; registr. of births and deaths, Dec., 1882, and of Mohammedan marriages, Dec., 1883; pres. fisheries inquiry coman., 1887; pres. Singapore mun. coun., 1888-9; ret., 1890.
ROWLAND, J. W., C.M.G. (1897)—B. 1852; astt. col. surg., G. Coast Col., May, 1880; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1887; col. surg., 1887; retired, 1897.

ROWLEY, THOS. WM.—Ed. Eden Coll.; 2nd lieut., 2nd L.I. Regt., Roy. Guernsey Militia; attached to 105th Regt., L.I., at Guernsey, for two trainings, and passed school; lieut., Mar., 1879; passed prelim. exam., R.M.C., Sandhurst, Apr., 1880, and qualifying exam. for line, July, 1881; joined armed constabty., N.Z., 1885; transf. to mtd. pol., Jan., 1886; and to N.Z. Artill., June, 1886; resig., Aug., 1888; inspr. of roads, Perak, Nov., 1889; astt. collr. land rev., mag. and J.P., Perak, Dec., 1890.

ROXBURGH, T. LAWRENCE.—Ed. Eden Univ.; clk. petty sess., parish of St. Elizabeth, and astt. clk. to dist. ct., Jamaica, Jan., 1882; clk. of cts., St. Eliz., Apr., 1888; 1st class clk., col. sec.'s office, Feb., 1890; astt. col. sec., 2nd Jan., 1902; ag. col. sec., July to Nov., 1902; admstr., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1906.
ROZAN, JULES.—Spcl. clk., audit. off., Mauritius, 1st Jan., 1870; ch. clk., ditto, 19th June, 1897.
RUSHMER, JAMES.—Entered C.O., Oct., 1893; apptd., after exam., library attendant, 1st July, 1896.
RUSHTON, CHARLES BERKELEY.—C.I.K., comsnr. of rlyws. dept., W. Aust., Apr., 1896; ch. clk., July, 1896; ag. sec. to comsnr. of rlyws., 23rd Aug., 1901; sec. to ditto, 18th Dec., 1901.

RUSSELL, Lieut.-Gen. Sir BAKER CREED, G.C.B., K.C.M.G. (1880), 13th Hussars.—B.1837; was in command of the troops employed against Sikukuni in S. Africa; A.D.C. to Her Majesty Queen Victoria, 1880; astt. adjt.-gen. for cavalry in the expdn. to Egypt, 1882, where he also commanded the 1st Cavalry brigade.

RUSSELL, CONRAD.—Ed. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. astt. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, S. of S. for the Colonies, Apr., 1903; ditto to Hon. Alfred Lyttelton, Oct., 1903; ditto to Lord Elgin, Dec., 1905.

RUSSELL, H.C., B.A., F.R.S., C.M.G. (1890).—B.1836; govt. astronomer, N.S. Wales, July, 1870-1905; author of memoirs on Australian eclipse expdn., 1875, and of numerous other papers on astronomical subjects, and has designed a variety of instruments for use in the observatory.

RUSSELL, JAMES GEORGE, I.S.O. (1903).—Master of the sup. ct., S. Australia, Dec., 1878; regis. of V.-A.ct., of companies, of trades unions, 1878; comsnr. of inland rev. and ag. regis. of probates, 1884; comsnr. of taxes, 1884; of stamps, 1886; comsnr. of insolvency, 1889; special mag. local ct., Adelaide, 1889; pres. of state bd. of conciliation, 1895; ag. judge of sup. ct., Mar. to Oct., 1897.
RUSSELL, JOHN WILLIAM PEMBERTON.—Astt. transport offr., Uganda Prot., 21st Aug., 1899; chief of customs, Apr., 1904; transport offr., 1905.

RUSSELL, ROBERT, JUN.—Natal exhibn. from Pietermaritzburg high schl. to Merton Coll., Oxford, 1885; B.A., M.A., and B.C.L., 1893; called to the bar by the In. Tem., 1893; acted as clk. in atty.-gen.'s office, Natal, 1891; astt. inspr. of schl., 1892; sec. to the agt.-gen. for Natal in London, Dec., 1893; acted as priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon. H. Escombe, ex-premier of Natal, during his visit to England, 1897.

RUSSELL, WESTERN FRANCIS, B.A.—Ed. at Cranbrook schl. and Christ Church, Oxford; 2nd cls. math. mods.; 1st cls. math. finals, 1884; 1st cls. prof. (math.) Royal coll., Mauritius, Aug., 1886; ag. sen. prof., 12th Apl. to 28th Nov., 1894; and 1st Aug., 1901, to 17th Oct., 1902; ag. rector, 18th Oct. to 30th Nov., 1902; ag. sen. prof., 27th Mar., 1903, to 8th Feb., 1904; ag. astt. col. sec., 24th Feb., 1904.
RUTLEDGE, THE HON. SIR A., KT. BACH. (1902).—B. 1843; barrister, Queensland, 1878; mem. of legis. assem. since 1878; atty.-gen. from 1883 to 1888; dist. ct. judge, Queensland, 1906.

RYAN, Sir CHARLES LISTER, K.C.B. (1887), C.B. (1881), J.P., Berks.—B.1831; apptd. astt. examr., impl. audit office, Mar., 1851; jun. clk. in H.M.'s treasy., Whitehall, Feb., 1852; was priv. sec. to Mr. Disraeli, Sir Stafford Northcote, and Mr. Gladstone; apptd. to audit office as sec., May, 1865; astt. comptroller and auditor, Mar., 1873; comptroller and audr.-gen., June, 1888; was hon. auditor of certain crown cols., ag. on behalf of H.M.'s S. of S. for the cols.; ret., 1896.

SABBEN, FREDERICK.—Ed. Royal Naval Schl.; govt. agt., Fiji, July, 1882; inspr. of imgrts., Jan., 1883; boarding offr., Nov., 1883; bondkeeper, 2nd class clk., and magazine-keeper, Jan., 1884; inspr. of pol., and offr. in charge of gaol, May, 1886; gaoler, Aug., 1887; supt., prisons, Aug., 1903.
SACHSE, HON. ARTHUR OTTO, C.E.—M.L.C. for N.E. Prov., Victoria; min. of pub. instr. since Sept., 1903.

SADLER, Lieut.-Col. JAMES HAYES, C.B. (1902).—Late Indian Staff Corps; 1st coman., 6th July, 1870; served with 61st Foot in Canada and Ireland, and 40th Foot and 33rd Bengal Native Infantry in India; apptd. to polit. dept. in India, July, 1877; asst. agt. to gov. gen. at Baroda, May, 1881; asst. sec., legis. dept., July to Oct., 1886; Aug. to Oct., 1888, and Apr., 1889, to Dec., 1890; polit. agt. with the ex-Amir of Afghanistan, Apr., 1891; polit. agt., Kotah and Jhalawar, July, 1891; on spec. duty with Prince Damrong of Siam, Feb., 1892; polit. agt. and consul of Muscat, Nov., 1892; ag. res. and consul-gen., Persian Gulf, June, 1893; asst. sec., foreign dept., govt. of India, Apr., 1895; polit. agt. and consul, Somaliland Prot., Aug., 1898; retired from Indian serv., Mar., 1899; degree of honour and gold medal of Indian govt. in Persian, Arabic, and Urdu; continued to be employed as consul-gen., Somaliland, till Dec., 1901; comsnr., Uganda, Dec., 1901; comsnr. and comdr-in-chief, Brit. East Africa Prot., Dec., 1905.
SALOMONS, Sir Julian Emmanuel, K.C., Kt. Bach. (1891).—B. 1834; vice-pres., exec. coun., N.S. Wales, 1891; resig., 1893; mem. of legis. coun.; London agent-gen., N.S.W., 1899-1900.
SAMPSON, Victor, K.C., B.A., LL.B.—Obtained Cape Univ. Chanc. gold medal for essay on the native question, 1877; M.L.A., Cape Colony, since 1893; atty.-gen., Feb., 1904.

SAMUEL, Silveric, L.—Ed. at St. Gregory's Rom. Cath. gram. schl., Lagos; messr. and copyt., educ. off., Oct., 1897; clk. to inspr. of schls., Mar., 1899; sec. to commrs. for exhibtn. of schl. wks., 1902 and 1903; clk. and interpreter to trav. comsnr., Ilesha interior dept., Feb., 1904; 3rd cls. clk., govr.'s off., Aug., 1904.

SAMUELSON, S. O.—Ag. regisr. native high ct., Natal, Feb., 1881, to Apr., 1882; ag. Zulu interp., sup. ct., May to June, 1882; clk. and Zulu interp. to R.M., Umlazi div., Oct., 1882; Zulu interp., sup. ct., Feb., 1888; under-sec. for native affairs, 1893; mem. of civ. ser. bd.; 1894-1901; J.P. for colony, 1894.
SANDERS, Wm. RUTHERFORD BOGLE.—Cadet, Ceylon, 4th Oct., 1879; pol. mag., 1st Jan., 1888; dist. judge, 1st May, 1896; ag. govt. agt., N. prov., 21st Apl., 1902; officer of cls. II., 18th July, 1902.

SANDERSON, James.—Clk. to ch. just., Barbados, 1867; clk. to gen. bd. of health, Barbados, Apr., 1871, also clk. to quarantine bd. in 1880; solr., atty., and proctor, Barbados, in 1873; was deleg. for Barbados to quarantine confce. at Demerara in 1888; govt. dir. on bd. of Barbados rly. co. in 1891.

SANGSTER, G. H.—Served in 2nd Dragoons and 91st Highlanders; asst. supt. pol., Gambia, 1897; ag. supt. of pol., inspr. of prisons, Gambia, 1897; asst. inspr., S. Leone frontier force, 1898; Mendi exped., 1898, mentioned in despatches; ag. dist. comsnr., Karene, 1899; inspr., S. Leone F.F., June, 1899; W. Africa medal and clasp, S. Leone, 1898-9; Royal Humane Soc.'s bronze medal, 1898; asst. dist. comsnr., S. Leone, Dec., 1899-1900; travelling comsnr., Gambia, Nov., 1900; field treas., Gambia field force, 1901; special mention in despatches; priv. sec. to ag. govr. of Gambia.

SANKEY, John.—Customs clk., England, Dec., 1886; excise offr., Jan., 1887; 2nd div. clk., June, 1887; ag. asst. P.O. survr., 1897; acctnt., dept. of posts and telegraphs, O.R.C., Sept., 1901; ch. acctnt., ditto, July, 1903.

SARTORIUS, Maj.-Gen. REGINALD WILLIAM, C.M.G., V.C. (1874).—B. 1841; 6th regt. of Bengal cavalry; apptd. to the order of St. M. and St. G. for service in the Ashanti war, 1873-4, under Sir J. H. Glover, and received the V.C. for an act of conspicuous gallantry in saving a wounded sergt.-maj. of Houssas.

SARWAR, HAFIZ GHULAM.—Cadet, S. S., Sept., 1897; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Jan. to June, 1900; and from Sept., 1900, to Nov., 1901; passed final exam., Chinese, July, 1900; 3rd mag., Sing., 29th Apl., 1902; ag. sheriff, deputy-registr., and asst. official assignee, Penang, May, 1902; dep. registr., sup. ct., Penang, Dec., 1905.
SASSE, HAROLD ST. JOHN.—Clk. col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., 1st Aug., 1902; clk., audit off., Hong Kong, 21st June, 1905.

SAUER, THE HON. J. W.—Sec. for native affairs in Scanlen ministry, Cape col., May, 1881; ret. 1884; col. sec., July, 1890, to Jan., 1896; comsnr. of pub. wks. in the Schreiner ministry, Oct., 1898, to 1900; mem. for George, 1904.

SAUNDERS, CHARLES J., B.A.—Ed. Merchant Taylors, and St. John's Coll., Oxon; cadet, S. S., 1891; passed final exam. in Chinese, Feb., 1895; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Feb., 1895; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, May, 1895; ag. dist. offr., Penang, Mar., 1897; Malacca, May, 1897; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Aug., 1897; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, and supt. of Ind. immgr., Penang, June, 1898; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Aug., 1898; also ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Sept., 1898; 2nd ast. prot. of Chinese, Penang, Aug., 1898; still ag. at Singapore, till Feb., 1899; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, May, 1900; ag. offl. assignee, S.S., Jan., 1901; sheriff, dep. regisr., supreme court, and ast. official assignee, Penang, May, 1902; munici. comsrr. for Singapore, Sep., 1903; ast. prot. of Chin., Penang, but cont. to act as offl. assignee, Sing. May, 1904.

SAUNDERS, Sir C.J.R., K.C.M.G. (1906). C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1857; olk. of the ct. and Zulu interp. to the R.M., Alexandra co., Natal, Nov., 1876; to the R.M., Durban, 1878; to the R.M., Alexandra, Nov., 1879; ag. ch. clk., office of sec. for native affairs during Zulu war, for the purpose of registering native levies, Apr., 1883; Zulu interp., sup. and circuit cts., Dec., 1883; ag. regisr. to the native high ct., Apr., 1884; ast. admstr. native law, Umlazi, Jan., 1886; ditto Inanda, June, 1886; proceeded to Amatongaland to ratify treaty with Queen of that country, Sept., 1887 (mentioned in despatches); R.M., Eshowe, Zululand, Jan., 1888; apptd. to define bdry. between Amatongaland and Zululand (again mentioned in despatches), July, 1889; ast. comsrr. and R.M., Lubombo, Aug., 1890; R.M., Eshowe, 1891; special comsrr. to territories of Sambaan, Umbegiza, and Mohlalein, N. of Zululand, Apr., 1895; ch. mag. and civil comsrr., prov. of Zululand, 1897; col. govt. representative, Zululand delim. comsrr., 1902; comsrr. for nat. affairs, Zululand, 1906.

SAUNDERS, Sir Frederick Richard, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1838; ast. survr., Ceylon, 1887; ast. agt., Trincomalee, July, 1882; ast. to govt. agt. at Kandy, Aug., 1885; dist. judge, etc., Ratnapura, Nov., 1885; ast. to the govt. agt., W. Prov., at Ratnapura, Oct., 1887; inspr.-gen. of prisons, 1874; agt., E.Prov., 1878; agt. W. Prov., 1879; M.L.C., 1879; ag. audr.-gen., 1890; treas. and comsrr. of stamps, Oct., 1890; ag. audr.-gen., 1896; ret., 1898.

SAUNDERS, James B.—Harbrmr., Trinidad, Sept., 1894; also J.P. and mag. and regisr. of births and deaths for waters of the col.; coroner for Gulf of Paria; mem. of quarantine bd., and shipping master, etc.
SAUVAGE, Daniel.—Govt. pilot and ast. port offr., Seychelles, Feb., 1894, and port offr., May, 1897.

SAXTON, George Shadwell.—Ed. at Clifton Coll.; writer, Ceylon civ. ser., 1878; pol. mag. Haputale, 1887; apptd. govt. agent, Mannar, 1887; Chilaw, 1888; dist. judge, Tangalla, 1890; apptd. govt. agent, Matale, 1890; Matara, 1899; Kalutara, 1902; govt. agent, Sabaragamawwa, 1902; dist. judge, Matara, Nov., 1906.

SAYER, W.F.—Ed. Univ. Coll. Schl., Lond.; solr., 1879; bar. and solr. in W. Australia, 1891; sec. law dept., Mar., 1897; ag. comsrr. of titles, Mar., 1899; atty.-gen., 1901; partly, draftsmn., 1902; crown solr., Dec., 1902; atty.-gen., 1904.

SCANLEN, The Hon. Sir Thomas Charles, K.C.M.G. (1884).—B. 1834; mem. house of asmem., Cape, since 1869; dep. sheriff for Cradock, 1857-66; J.P. for Cradock, May, 1869; mem. of the divisional coun. of Cradock, 1867-77; comsrr. and chmn. of the municipality of Cradock, 1870-77; apptd. by sup. ct. examr. for admission of attorneys and notaries, May, 1887; mem. of the exec. coun., May, 1881; atty.-gen. and premier, May, 1881, to June, 1882; col. sec. and premier, July, 1882, to May, 1884; proceeded to England to negotiate loan for pub. wks., Oct., 1883; as a priv. mem., carried through the Executors and Trustees Accounts Act, 1873; Titles Registration and Derelict Lands Act, 1881; prepared and carried during term of office, General Loans Act, 1881; Postal Act, 1882; Lands Clauses Act, 1882; Municipal Act, 1882; Police Regulation Act, 1882; Aliens' Naturalisation Act, 1883; Public Health Act, 1883; Liquor Licensing Act, 1883; legal adviser, Rhodesia, 1894; legal adviser, Rhodesia Administrative Staff, 1895.

SCHOLTZ, Clerke Burton.—Comsrr., Lichtenburg, Transvaal, 1877; lieut., Bechuanaland field force on Sir C. Warren's staff; mem. of comsrr. on Stellaland claims, 1885; ch. clk. and ast. mag., Vrijburg, 1885; R.M., Kuruman, 1889.

SCHOMBURGK, Otto Heinrich.—Clerk, engrnr.-in-chief's office, S. Aust., Jan., 1874; clk., ch. sec.'s office, Aug., 1877; clk., regisr.-gen. of deeds office, July, 1880; clk., sheriff's office, Nov., 1882; dep. sheriff, 1891; also returning offr. W. Adelaide, Jan., 1893, to June, 1902; sheriff, July, 1903; also Commonwealth electoral offr., for the state of S. Aust., Aug., 1903; major, comdg. field batt. of artillery (retired).

SCHOOLES, Henry Rawlins Pipon.—Ed. Marl., called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Hilary, 1873; admitted to bar, Leeward Is., May, 1873; mem. for Sandy Point legis. assem. of St. Kitts, Mar., 1874, to Apl., 1876; ag. atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Apl. to Sept., 1876; re-elected mem. for Sandy Point, Jan., 1877; mem. of new legis. coun. of St. Kitts, 1879, and elected by the legis. coun. as sen. mem. for St. Kitts of fed. coun. of the Leeward Is.; June, 1880, atty.-gen., Br. Honduras; June, 1881, apptd. provisionally as ch. just. of Br. Honduras; atty.-gen., Grenada, Apl., 1883; ditto, Jamaica, 1896; ch. just., Gibraltar, 1905.

SCHREIBER, Collingwood, C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1831; emigrated from England, 1852; entered service of Hamilton and Toronto Rly., and became astt. engrnr., 1852; suptding. engrnr. of wks., N. Ry. of Canada, 1860-64; div. engrnr., Nova Scotia govt. service, on Truro and Pictou Rly., 1864-5; survey engrnr., Dominion govt. service, on inter-col. rly., 1886; suptding. engrnr., N. Brunswick E. Rly., 1869-71; ditto, inter-col. railway, 1871-3; ch. engrnr. of govt. railways in operation, 1873-8; also gen. man., 1878-80; also engrnr.-in-chief, Canadian Pacific Rly., 1880; now engrnr.-in-chief, govt. rlys. and dep. min. of rlys. and canals.

SCHREINER, The Hon. W.P., C.M.G. (1890), K.C.—B. 1857; ed. S. A. Coll., Capetown, Camb. Univ., and London; advoc. of sup. ct., Cape, 1882; legal adviser to high comsrr., 1887-93; atty.-gen. in Mr. Rhodes's ministry, 1893; mem. of Janesons Raid Comte.; premier and col. sec., 1898-1900.
SCOFFHAM, W.H.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to census office, 20th Feb., 1901; transfd. to C.O., 17th Dec., 1902.

SCONCE, Herbert W.—Ed. at Hurst Coll., Sussex; Gram. Schl., Bristol; Trim Coll., Camb.; B.A. math. tripos, 1886; M.A. 1897; astt. master, Faversham Gram. Schl.; Godolphin Schl., Hammersmith, and Grocers' Co.'s Schl., Hackney, 1887 to 1893; astt. mr., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, Apr., 1893; astt. inspr. of schls., July, 1895; ag. inspr., May to Nov., 1898; June to Nov., 1900; July, 1902, to Apl., 1903; inspr. of schls. and offl. visitor of Onderneeming Indust. Schl., Apl., 1903; mem. bd. of agric. for educatl. matters; examr. for prelim. exam. for govt. clerical serv.

SCOTT, J.H.—Mag., Tembuland, Aug., 1876; capt. in Tembu levies, Sept., 1877; ag. ch. mag., Tembuland, Apr. to July, 1878; ch. clk., native affairs dept., Oct., 1878; sec. to atty.-gen., on special duty to N. Border during war there, Feb. to Apr., 1879; ag. special comsrr., N. Border, May to July, 1879; special comsrr. and special mag. of N. Border, July, 1879; res. comsrr., Pondo-land, July, 1888.

SCOTT, RALPH.—B.A. Lond. Univ.; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1895; ag. collr. of land rev., Singapore, Mar., 1897; passed final exam. in Malay, Sept., 1897; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Sept., 1897; confirmed, Aug., 1898; apptd. to Cls. IV., Jan., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Apl., 1903; attached to land off., Sing., on spec. duty, July, 1905.

SCOTT, THE HON. RICHARD WILLIAM, K.C.—Called to the bar of Upper Canada, 1884; created a Q.C., 1867; sat in the legis. assem. of Canada from 1867 to 1873, and while there carried the Separate School Act of Upper Canada; returned to the Ontario assem. at confedn., 1867; ret. on his entrance into the Dominion Govt., Nov., 1873; called to the senate, Mar., 1874; was speaker of the Ontario assem. from 7th to 21st Dec., 1871; mem. of exec. coun. and comsrr. of crown lands for same prov., from later date until Nov., 1873, when sworn of the Queen's privy coun.; S. of S. of Canada, Jan., 1874; ret. on resignation of Mackenzie govt., Oct., 1878; is now a senator; was the framer of the Canada Temperance Act, known as the Scott Act; joined Sir W. Laurier's govt. as S. of S., June, 1896.
SCOTT, ROBERT.—Acctnt. to director, army telegraphs, O.R.C., July, 1900; asst. acctnt., dept. of posts and telegraphs, Aug., 1902; ag. comsrr., ct. of requests, Singapore, July, 1906.
SCOTT, WALTER.—Apptd., after comp. exam., clk. of the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., 22nd Jan., 1883; promoted to higher grade, 2nd div., 3rd Nov., 1896.
SCROGGS, LIEUT. HAROLD CHAS., R.N.—Harbour mast. and supt. of merc. marine, Mauritius, 23rd June, 1903.

SCRUNBY, CHARLES BURDNETT, B.A.—Ed. priv. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb., and in France; asst. collr., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., 1899; ag. vice-consul, Fort Johnston, 1901; dist. comsrr., Lagos, 1902; ag. collr. of cust., 1904; asst. to comsrr. for Egba bdry. delimitation, 1904-5; certif. from schl. of mil. engnr., Chatham, in mil. topography and astronomy, 1905.
SEALY, A.—Pol. mag. of dist. B, Barbados, Sept., 1873.

SEALY, FRANK DEAN.—Supernum. clk., imigr. dept., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1884; 4th class clk., Apr., 1891; 3rd class, Jan., 1893; has acted as ch. clk.; 2nd class clk., Apr., 1895; ag. imigr. agt., 1896 and 1898.

SEALY, GORDON COURLAND MILLER.—Clk. to the atty.-gen., Tobago, Jan., 1882; clk. in col. sec.'s dept., 1883; acted as confid. clk. to the admstr. and clk. of couns., 1883 to 1886; 3rd clk., surg.-gen.'s dept., Br. Guiana, 1887; 2nd clk., 1887; has acted as ch. clk.

SEALY, J. ERNEST CAMERON.—2nd clk., audit and registry dept., Tobago, Feb., 1888; clk. to comsrr. and financial bd., Nov., 1890, and Oct., 1893; sec. to bd. of educn., Sept., 1892; ch. clk. treas., June, 1893; postm. of Tobago, Dec., 1895.

SEARCY, ARTHUR.—Boarding offr., cust., S. Aust., 1873; clk., cust., 1874; clk., audit offr., 1875; clk., cust., 1876; tide inspr. and measurer of ships, cust., 1889; correspondg. clk., cust., and sec., marine bd., 1890; ag. dep. comsrr. of taxes and stamps, 1891; correspondg. clk., cust., and sec., 2nd bd., 1891; sec., cust., and sec., marine bd., 1894; clk. astt. and sergeant-at-arms, H. of A., 1894; dep. comsrr. of taxes and stamps, 1896; comsrr. of ditto, 1897; president, marine bd., 1902; also inspr. of kerosine, 1902; also comsrr. of trade marks and regisr. of copyrights, 1904.
SEATON, G. L.—Apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. mess., C.O., 17th May, 1899; 2nd cl., 18th Feb., 1900; King's mess., 10th June, 1904.

SECCOMBE, G. S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), 1874.—Mem. Medico-Psychological Assoc.; sen. astt. med. offr. and dep. med. supt. of Caterham Asylum, England, 1874; med. supt. of lun. asyl., and med. offr., roy. gaol, Trinidad, Sept., 1882.
SEDGEWICK, ROBERT, K.C.—Dep. min. of just. and solr. of Indian affairs, Canada, 1888; now a puisne judge, sup. ct.
SEE, THE HON. SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1845; mem. legis. assem., N.S.W.; treas. in Gibb's ministry, 1891-4; ch. sec. in Sir W. Lyne's ministry, 1899; premier, 1901; now M.L.C.

SEIGNORET, C. A.—Supernumerary treas., Dominica, 1890; sec. to special yaws comsrr., Mar., 1891, to Sept., 1892; ch. clk., regisr.'s office and clk. pub. market, 1892; asst. sec. to Sir R. Hamilton during his inquiry in Dominica, 1893; govt. offr., treasy., 1895; sec. bd. of health and quarantine, 1895; ag. clk., admstr.'s office and exec. coun., 1895; ag. treas., Dominica, July, 1901.
SEIGNORET, G. B.—Clk. to postmtr., Dominica, 1899; govt. offr., treasy., 1893; ch. clk. registr.'s off., 1895; ag. postmtr., 1896-7; clk. govt. offr., Roseau, 1897; govt. offr., treasy., 1901.

SELBORNE, 2ND EARL OF (creat. 1882), VIS-COUNT WOLMER, BARON SELBORNE (U.K.), P.C. (1900), G.C.M.G. (1905), THE RT. HON. WM. WALDEGRAVE PALMER.—B. 1859; ed. at Winchester and at Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1st cls. hist.), 1882; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Childers, Chanc. of Excheq., 1882-85; was mem. of parlmt. for E. Hants, 1885-92, and for W. Edinburgh City, 1892-95; succeeded his father, 1895; Under S. of S. for Cola., June, 1895; chmn., Pacific cable comtee., 1896; mem. of Pacific cable comtee., 1899; first lord of the admiralty, Nov., 12th, 1900; high comsrr. for S. Africa, and gov. and comdr-in-chief of Transvaal and O.R.C., 15th Mar., 1905.

SELBY-LOWNDES, R. C. W.—Ed. at I. of Wight Coll. and Tamworth Coll.; served in B.S.A. pol. during Mashonaland rebellion, 1896-7; chartered company's civil serv., 1898-1900; served during S. Africa war, Rhodesia regt., Rhodesian field force, and French's scouts, 1901-1902 (medals, Mashonaland, 1897, Queen's medal, S. A. war, 4 clasps, King's medal, 2 clasps).

SELLS, H. C., B.A.(Oxon.).—Cadet, S. Sttlmnts, Oct., 1897; ag. priv. sec. to gov., Nov., 1898, to Nov., 1899; ag. 4th mag., Singapore, Jan., 1900; passed fin. exam. in Malay, Apr., 1900; ag. dist. offr., prov. Well., May, 1901; supt., money order branch and govt. savings bank, Sing., May, 1902; head of Malay coll., Malacca, July, 1904; ag. dist. offr., Nibong Tetal, Apr., 1905.

SEMPER, D. H.—Clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, 1893; dep. coroner for dist. B., Oct., 1898; ag. Federal treas., Oct., 1902; fed. treas., Mar., 1903; acted as clk., leg. coun., Antigua, Dec., 1902; ditto, ex. coun., Montserrat, Mar., 1903; ag. ch. regist., Leeward Is., and regist. and prov. marshal, admstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank, regist. of titles, regist. of friendly societies, and regist. gen. of births, deaths and marriages, Antigua, Feb. to July, 1905; apptd. regist. and prov.-marshal, admstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank, and regisr. of titles, St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1905; J.P., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1905.
SENEVIRATNE, ALEXANDER DE ALWIS.—Mem. of legis. coun., Ceylon; advoc. of the sup. ct.; mem. mun. coun., Colombo, and coun. of legal educn.; chmn. Ceylon national assoc.

SENIOR, BERNARD, I.S.O. (1906).—Clk. to the bndry. comsn. (Redistribution of Seats Act), 1884; attached to Scotch educn. dept., 1885, and again 1888; priv. sec. to Sir F. R. Sandford, under-sec. for Scotland, 1887; 1st class clk., col. sec.'s office, G. Coast, 1888; local audtor, Lagos, Aug., 1889; ditto, G. Coast, Dec., 1889; ditto, Br. Bechuanaland, Feb., 1894; ditto, Cyprus, 1895; and-gen., O.R.C., Sep., 1902; is also chmn. of tender bd.; mem. of comsn. to inquire into law relating to mining for diamonds and precious stones in O.R.C., 1903; ag. col. treas., 12th Aug., 1903, to 15th Feb., 1904; M.L.C., May, 194/; deleg. for O.R.C. at S. African freights confee., 1904-5; mem. of O.R.C. civ. ser. comsn., 1905; author of "A Guide to the examination of Government Accounts in Cyprus."

SETH, ARATHOON, I.S.O. (1905).—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, June, 1893; Hindustani interp., magistracy, Hong Kong, Feb., 1888, to Dec., 1869 (in the serv. of the P. & O. Co. in the interim); re-entered magistracy as 3rd clk., Sept., 1872; 2nd clk., Sept., 1873; 1st clk., Feb., 1875; clk. of couns., and ch. clk., C.S.O., Feb., 1881; valuer under the "Rating Ord.", 1875," 25th June, 1881, to 19th Mar., 1883; J.P., Jan., 1882; supt. of the opium revenue, Aug., 1883, to Feb., 1885; ditto, imports and exports, June to Oct., 1887; sec. to bd. under "Taipingshan resumption ordce.", 1894-1895 (recd. thanks of govt.), sec. to insaniary properties comsn., July, 1896; ag. deputy land offr., Nov., 1894, to Jan., 1895; ditto, Mar., 1895, to Nov., 1896; dep. regisr. and appraiser, sup. ct., and comsnr. for oaths, Mar., 1895; comsnr. for taking acknowledgments of married women, Oct., 1896; sec. squatter's bd.; offr. recvr. in bankruptcy, Mar., 1895 to Nov., 1896; ag. aast. regisr. gen., July to Sept., 1897; ag. regisr., sup. ct.; ag. land offr.; ag. regisr. of companies; offr. admstr., and offl. trustee, 13th Dec., 1898, to 26th Jan., 1900; mem. bd. examrs., 1902; ag. regisr., sup. ct., offr. admstr., offl. trustee and regisr. of companies, 1903-'4; confirmed, 1904.

SETH, G. G.—B.A., Calcutta; cadet, S. S., Nov., 1901; ag. sheriff, Singapore, Jan., 1903; passed final exam. in Malay, Jan., 1903; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, 5th Jan., 1904; ag. dep. regisr., sup. ct., Singapore, July, 1904; ag. dist. offr., N. Tebal, June, 1906; ag. dep. regisr., sup. ct., Penang, Sept., 1906.

SHAND, CHAS. ARTHUR.—Mem. local assemby, Antigua, 1876-1891; ditto, federal coun., 1877-1891; J.P., Antigua, 1885; mem. ex. coun., Antigua, 1887; chmn. P.L. guardians and of several local boards, Antigua; dist. mag., Nevis, Feb., 1903; and offl. mem. leg. coun., St. Kitts-Nevis; mem. exec. coun., St. Kitts-Nevis, Oct., 1904; J.P., Nov., 1904.

SHARE, HAMNET HOLDSHIT.—Entered Royal Navy, 1880; served in H.M.S. "Tourmaline" and numerous other ships (Egypt medal, 1882, and Khedive's Bronze Star); sec. to comdr.-in-chief, Pacific station, 1899-1900; ditto, Australia station, 1901-1903; priv. sec. to gov. of N. S. Wales, May, 1903; priv. sec. to gov.-gen. of Australia, 22nd Nov., 1904.

SHARPE, SIR ALFRED, K.C.M.G. (1903), C.B. (1897).—B. 1853; H.M.'s vice-consul, Nyasaland, 14th Feb., 1891; ditto, for territories under British influence North of the Zambesi, 18th Feb., 1893; consul for same territories, 1st Feb., 1894; ag. comsnr. and consul-gen. at Zomba, 7th May, 1894, to 3rd May, 1895; given rank of deputy comsnr., 1st Apl., 1896; and again acted as comsnr. and consul-gen., 16th Apl., 1896, to 14th July, 1897; comsnr. and consul-gen., for territories under Brit. inf. N. of Zambesi, 15th July, 1897; recd. Jubilee medal, 1897; comsnr., commdr.-in-chief, and consul-gen., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., 1st Jan., 1902; recd. Coronation medal, 1902.

SHARPE, W. S., C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1860; capt. 4th batt. R.I. Rifles, 1893; major, Oct., 1903; msptr., Sierra Leone frontier force, Mar., 1894; 3rd comsnr. to Anglo-French bndry. comsnr. and delimited frontier from Atlantic coast to Little Mola River, 1896; dist. comsnr., Karene dist., Jan., 1897; F.R.G.S., and J.P. for the col.; transf'd. to N. Nigeria as asts. res., Dec., 1900; apptd. 2nd class res., Oct., 1901.

SHAW, CAPT. BERNARD VIDAL.—Late capt. rifle brigade; priv. sec. to dir. of crim. investig., London; comsnr. of pol., Capetown, 1882 to 1888 (office abolished); visiting mag., Breakwater convit station (Cape); author of "Epitome of the Criminal Law of the Cape"; sub-imigrn. agt., Br. Guiana, 1889; supt. H.M.'s penal stltmnt., Massaruni, Jan., 1894; stip. mag., 1897.

SHAW, COURTENAY.—Clk. in off. of sec. to Transvaal administn., Mar., 1901; ag. priv. sec. to ditto, Mar. to June, 1902; priv. sec. to col. sec., Transvaal, July, 1902; ag. sec., Transvaal imigrn. off., May, 1904, to June, 1906; J.P., Witwatersrand, May, 1906; priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., O.R.C., 1st July, 1906.

SHAWE, HENRY BENJAMIN.—Clk. at Clanwilliam, Cape Colony, 12th May, 1883; col. secy.'s off., Cape Town, Dec., 1883; sen. examnr. of acctns., 14th Apl., 1890; chief ditto, 1st July, 1894; acctnt., 10th July, 1895; acctntg. offr., 31st Apl., 1899; ch. clk. and acctng. offr., 1st Mar., 1901; ag. under col. sec., 1st Apl. to 30th Sept., 1901; ast. under col. sec., 1st Apl., 1902.
SHEA, THE HON. SIR E. D., KT. BACH. (1902).—Col. sec. and clk. of coun., Newfoundland, Jan., 1874; cashier of savings bank, and pres. of legis. coun., Feb., 1886.
SHEA, THE HON. GEORGE.—M.H.A., Ferryland (Newfild.), 1889-93; again in 1897; mem. exec. coun. (without portfolio), 1897; mem. of treasy. bd., 1898; mayor of St. John's, 1902; mem. exec. coun., 1904.

SHENTON, THE HON. SIR GEORGE, KT. BACH. (1893), J.P.—B. 1842; mem. of the legislature of W. Australia, 1870-74, 1875-90; on the introduction of respon. govt. col. sec. in the first ministry formed by Sir John Forrest; pres. of the legis. coun., 1892-1906; is senior mem. of the metropolitan prov.; has been mayor of Perth on eleven different occasions.

SHEPPARD, WALTER SYDNEY.—B.A., M.B., B. Ch., Cantab., civil surg. in charge of H.M.'s troops, N.E. dist., Eng., May, 1893, to May, 1899; spec. plague med. offr., India, May, 1899, to Nov., 1900; house surg., gen. hosp., Singapore, 19th Nov., 1900; super. col. surg., S. Sttlmts., 1st Mar., 1901; col. surg., Prov. Wellesley, Aug., 1905.

SHEPSTONE, H. C., C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1840; priv. sec. to the lieut.-gov., Natal, 1857; clk. to exec. coun., 1863; Indian imigrn. agt. and J.P., 1864; R.M. and admstr. of native law, Alfred co., 1870; ditto, Durban co., 1878; acted as sec. to Mr. Justice Phillips, C.M.G., on special service to Barbados, 1876; served on the staff of Sir Theophilus Shepstone, K.C.M.G., H.M.'s special comsnr. in S. Africa, 1877; sec. for native affairs, Transvaal, July, 1877; attended Cetwayo on his visit to England, 1882; sec. for native affairs, Natal, 1884; ret., 1893.

SHEPSTONE, JOHN WESLEY, C.M.G. (1888).—B. 1827; July, 1846, govt. interp., Natal; July, 1847, accompanied Capt. Kyle on special mission to Zulu king; Dec., 1850, accompanied Capt. Gordon on special mission to Pondos; Oct., 1851, interp. to recorder and mag.'s offices; Dec., 1851, asst. R.M., Pietermaritzburg co.; Aug., 1852, R.M., Lower Umvoti, and govt. agt., Noniti country, and J.P.; 1857, commanded native forces against Chief Usidlo and tribe, and against Chief Matyana and tribe; Nov., 1858, supt. of the cultivation of cotton; Apr., 1861, ag. sec. for native affairs and judicial assessor; July, 1861, commanded a native force in the Fort Buckingham expedn.; May, 1862, ag. R.M., Inanda div.; Aug., 1863, asst. R.M., Pietermaritzburg co.; ag. R.M., Umvoti co., Sept., 1867; May, 1874, comsnr., Klip River and Weenen counties; July, 1874, ag. sec. for native affairs and judicial assessor; Oct., 1874, J.P. for the col.; Sept., 1876, sec. for native affairs and mem. exec. and legia. couns.; Mar., 1878, mem. of the bdry. comsnr.; Dec., 1878, delivered ultimatum to Zulu delegs. at Lower Drift, Tugela; Aug., 1879, accompanied Sir Garnet Wolseley to arrange the settlement of Zululand; mentioned in despatches; Dec., 1882, Br. comsnr., Zululand; Jan., 1884, judge, native high ct.; ret., 1896.
SHEPSTONE, THEOPHILUS, C.M.G. (1880).—B. 1843; was in command of a Basuto regt. in the Zulu war; mem. of Natal legis. coun. until 1887; counsellor with Umbeandes, King of Swaziland, 1887-91.

SHERIFF, PERCY MUSGRAVE CRESSWELL.—Called to the bar, Mid. Temple, June, 1892; admitted to the bar of B. Hond., 1893; of Jamaica, 1895; ag. dist. comsnr., Belize, for six months in 1898; apptd. mag. and coroner, So. dist., Grenada, 1900; visiting just. of prisons, 1900; chairman of St. George's Road Board, 1901; ag. mem. legis. coun., June, 1902; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, Dec., 1902, to Dec., 1903, when confirmed; admtd. govt. and ag. col. sec. and treas., Aug., 1903; mem. bd. of educn.; prov. mem. of exec. coun., and legal adviser to admtr., and draughtsmen of govt. bills, Dec., 1902, to Apr., 1906; M.L.C., 1904; ch. just., St. Lucia, 1906; compiled "Index of Laws of Grenada and St. Vincent," to Dec., 1905."
SHIPWAY, WALTER ROBERT.—Apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd div. clk., and assigned to C.O., Dec. 1898.

SHOLL, L. H., I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1844; clk., P.W. office, S. Australia, July, 1858; clk., waterworks dept., Jan., 1859; clk., audit office, Sept., 1868; ch. clk., Jan., 1872; ch. clk. and acctnt., treasy., July, 1874; acctnt. and receiver of rev., treasy., July, 1876; cashier and acctnt., treasy., July, 1879; under-treasyr., July, 1883; under-secr. and govt. statist., May, 1890; also clk. of exec. coun., July, 1894.
SHORT, EDWARD AWDRY.—Ed. at Clifton, and Cent. Tech. Coll., London; ch. engrg. dept. of posts and telegraphs, O.R.C., 9th May, 1904.

SHORT, EDWARD MORRISON DE COURT.—Ceylon writer, 1878; pol. mag., 1883; asst. to govt. agt., S. prov., 1886; asst. collr. of cust., and landing survr., cust., Galle, May, 1886; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Jan., 1889; Hambantoto, Sept., 1889; Kegalla, May, 1890; Vavuniya and Mullaitivu, Dec., 1890; Kalutara, Apr., 1894; Chilaw, Dec., 1895; Vavuniya, Apr., 1896; Matara, Nov., 1897; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Jan., 1900; Nuwara Eliya, 15th Aug., 1900; ag. pol. mag., Galle, Oct., 1890; dist. judge, Matara, Feb., 1892; asst. govt. agt., Matale, 16th Jan., 1900; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, 15th Aug., 1900; ag. govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Oct., 1904; asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Dec., 1904; chrmn., mun. council, and mayor of Colombo, Feb., 1905.
SHOWERS, EDWARD MELLAN.—Served in Bengal police, Feb., 1867, to Sept., 1901; comsnr. of pol., Transvaal, 8th Nov., 1901.
SIDNEY, T. S., M.A.—Ed. Harvard, and Trin. Hall, Camb.; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., June, 1888; enrolled adv. to high ct. of Madras, 1894; apptd. dist. comsnr., Epe, Lagos, Jan., 1901.

SIERRA LEONE, BISHOP OF, RT. REV. EDMUND HENRY ELWIN, M.A., Merton Coll., Oxon; cur. of St. Peter-le-Bailey, Oxford, 1894-6; chaplain at Fourah Bay Coll., S. Leone, 1896-8; principal, 1898-1901; chapln. to Bp. of S. Leone, 1898-1901; Bishop of Sierra Leone, 1901.
SIMPSON, GEORGE.—Inspr. of schls., Bermuda, 1880; also inspr. of dockyard schls.

SIMPSON, SAMUEL.—Ed. at Owen's Coll., and Edin. Univ.; B.Sc.; Stevens scholar; sec. to the Union; sen. pres. of students' representative coun.; Highland and agric. soc'y.'s prizeman; life mem. and silver medallist of roy. agric. soc'y.; holder of nat. diploma in agric.; sen. lecturer in agric. to Govt. Agric. Coll., Ghizeh, Egypt; cotton expert to B.C.A. Prot., Mar., 1905.

SINCKLER, E. G.—Entered col. sec.'s office, Barbados, Nov. 1874; acted as clk. to lieut.-gov., 1878; 2nd clk. corrpndece branch, col. sec.'s office, 1879; ag. asst. clk. to Gov. W. Robinson, 1880; 1st clk., record branch, col. sec.'s office, and clk., courts of ordinary and error, Jan., 1883; ag. ch. clk., 1886; comsnr. of census, 1891; ch. clk. to judges, 1892; J.P., Oct., 1892; ag. registr., friendly socs., June, 1893; ag. sen. pol. mag., Bridgetown, and dist. A, Aug. to Oct., 1893; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. B, Sept. to Oct., 1894; has acted several times as coroner, dist. A; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. F, July, 1896, to Jan., 1897; ag. pol. mag., judge, and coroner, dist. B, Sept. to Oct., 1897; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. D, Mar. to Apr., 1898; ag. coroner, St. Michael, 1898 (at time of hurricane), and has repeatedly acted as such; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. B, 1899; ag. pol. mag., dist. A, Feb., 1900; ag. comsnr. of probate, May to Nov., 1900; ag. pol. mag., dist. A, July, 1900, to Sept., 1901; pol. mag. and judge, dist. E, Oct., 1901; discharged duties of pol. mag. and judge, dist. D, in addition to own duties, Sept., 1905; ag. pol. mag., dist. A, 18th July to 23rd Nov., 1906; took active part in promotion of celebration of tercentenary of Barbados, 1905.

SINCLAIR, WM.—Ck., stores dept., L. and N.W. rly., Jan., 1863; stores auditor, July, 1875; rly. storekeeper, Midland system, Cape govt. rly., Aug., 1878; ch. rly. storekeeper, Jan., 1886; storekeeper, C.S.A.R., 1904.

SINGAPORE, LABUAN, AND SARAWAK, 3rd BISHOP OF LABUAN, THE RIGHT REV. GEORGE FREDERICK HOPE, D.D.—Ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1861; M.A. 1867; D.D., 1881; curate of Roxton with Great Barford, Beds, 1861-66; curate of Trinity, St. Marylebone, London, 1866-67; col. chaplain, Malacca, Nov., 1867; ditto, Singapore, Feb., 1874; regisr. of the diocese of Labuan, Apr., 1874; archdeacon of Singapore, Dec., 1874; bishop of Singapore, Labuan and Sarawak, 1881.

SIVEWRIGHT, THE HON. SIR JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1892), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1848; gen. man. of telegraphy, C. of Good Hope, Apr., 1878; ret., 1885; M.L.A., 1889; mem. of Rhodes's ministry without portfolio, July, 1900, and subsequently as comn. of crown lands and pub. wks.; resig., 1893; resumed office, Jan., 1896.

SKEETE, ELLIOT L., B.A., Durham, 1888.—Asst. to supt. bot. station, Barbados, Sept., 1890; ag. supt., Dec., 1890, to Jan., 1891; ch. offr. reformatory, May, 1892; ag. supt. reformatory and bot. station, July to Dec., 1894, and Apr. to May, 1895, and since 1899.

SKERRETT, CHRISTOPHER MARTIN.—Supernumerary clk., regis. and provost marshal's office, Dominica, July to Dec., 1891, and Feb. to Sept., 1892; asst. clk. to gov't. offr., Roseau, Sept., 1892, to Mar., 1893; aid waiter, treasy., Aug. to Oct., 1893; tax collr., treasy., Nov., 1893, to Apr., 1894; asst. clk., tax dept., treasy., 1895; asst. to admstr., Jan. to Mar., 1896; 2nd clk., regis. and prov. marshal's off., Dominica, Mar., 1898, to Dec., 1899; 1st clk., Jan., 1901; is a comn. to admstr. oaths, Dominica; and supt. of pub. cemetry; has held numerous "acting" appointments.

SKINNER, WM.—Ed. Bedford Gram. Schl.; open scholar, St. Catherine's Coll., Camb., B.A. (math. tripos), June, 1882, and bracketed 10th sen. optime; M.A., Oct., 1886; headm. gram. schl., Dominica, 1st Jan., 1883; J.P. and visiting just., Roseau prison, Dec., 1886; nom. mem. of Roseau town bd., 1899; sub-inspr. of schls., Dominica, 12th June, 1901; inspr. of weights and measures, Dominica, 1st Feb., 1902; ag. inspr. of schls., Leeward Islands, June to Nov., 1903; ag. treas., Dominica, Dec., 1903; chmn. of Roseau town bd., May to Dec., 1904; ag. inspr. of schls., Leeward Islands, Sept., 1905; offr. in charge of prison discipline, Nov., 1905.

SKIRVING, JOHN MACKENZIE.—Served in 91st Highlanders; on Boer prisoners of war staff, St. Helens, Nov., 1899 to Oct., 1902; inspr. of pol. gaoler and mag.'s clk., St. Helena, Mar., 1903; sanitary inspr., Apr., 1906.

SLADER, C. H. YORKE.—Jun. clk., treasy., Jamaica, 1883; sen. asst. clk. cts., Clarendon, 1889; dep. clk. cts., Westmoreland, 1892; 1st cls. clk., sup. cls., 1894; ag. regis., 1895; lieut., Jamaica militia, 1895; offr. with Jubilee contingent (medal), 1897; ag. regis., 1898; capt., 1899; passed exam. before judges of sup. ct. equal to solrs. final, 1899; clk. of cts., Clarendon, 1900; joined hon. socy. Grays' Inn, 1904; passed final, 1905; ag. comsnr. and judge of grand court, Cayman Islands, 1906.

SLATER, A. R.—Ed. at King Ed. schl., Birmingham, and Emm. coll., Camb. (B.A. 1897); cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1898; extra offr., asst. govt. agent, W. P., Aug., 1899; 2nd asst., P.M.G., Mar., 1900; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Dec., 1901; on spec. duty at camp for prisoners of war Diyatatalawa, July, 1902; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1903; clk. leg. coun., May, 1904.

SLOLEY, HERBERT CECIL, C.M.G. (1905).—Served in C.M.R. in campaigns against Griquas and Moirosi (medal with clasp, 1877-8-9); capt. in native contingent, Basuto war, 1880-1; mentioned in despatches; sub-inspr., Cape police, 1883; ditto, Basutoland, 1884; inspr., 1886; asst. comsnr., Basutoland, 1889; ag. res. comsnr., July to Dec., 1895; govt. sec., 1898; ag. res. comsnr., Dec., 1900; res. comsnr., Sept., 1901.

SLYNE, D.—Barrister-at-law; Lord O'Hagan medallist and David Lynch medallist, King's Inns, Ireland; entered Imperial civ. serv., open compet., 1880; until 1900 attached successively to Inland Rev. Dept. at Dublin, Sligo, Chester, Oxford, Leicester, Birmingham, Dublin, Dundee, and Birmingham; apptd. supervising excise offr., Trinidad, 1900; ag. receiver-gen., May, 1902; receiver-gen., Feb., 1903.

SMALLWOOD, HENRY ARMSTRONG.—Entered navy, 1885; on board H.M.S. "Victoria," at time of disaster, June, 1893; asst. comsnr., Colo West, and stip. mag., Apr., 1894; ag. stip. mag., Ba and Yasawa, Apr., 1896; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, and priv. sec. to gov. Fiji, Apr., 1896; ag. sec. to W. Pac. high comsnr., May, 1897, to May, 1898; stip. mag., Savu Savu and Tavuni, Jan., 1899; island treas., Cyprus, Mar., 1900; passed lower standard Greek, Dec., 1900; ag. comsnr., Nicosia, June-Sept., 1904.

SMART, ARCHIBALD WM.—Clk., dept. of trade and cust., Melbourne, 1863; landing waiter, 1868; landing survr., 1890; senior ditto, 1893; collr. of cust., Victoria, and regis. of shipping, Melbourne, 1903; ch. Commonwealth survr., 1903.

SMARTT, JAMES A.—Alderman of Brandon, Manitoba, 1882-84; mayor, 1885-87; mem. judicial bd., 1886; mem. of prov. legislature, 1886; min. of pub. wks., 1888; dep. min. of the interior of Canada, Apr., 1897; dept. supt.-gen. of Indian affairs, July, 1897.

SMARTT, CAPT. J. P. (3rd Batt., South Lancs. Regt.).—Public wks. and roads dept., G. Coast, 1893-96; local auditor, N. C. Prot. (now S. Nigeria), Oct., 1896; chief ast. treas., Dec., 1901; ag. treas., Dec., 1901, to June, 1902, and June to Dec., 1903; lieut. G. C. rifle volrs., May, 1893; capt., Nov., 1893; ag. adjt., May to Nov., 1893; ag. comdt., Oct., 1895, to Mar., 1896; attached for duty with Niger C. Protec. force, May to July, 1897, May and Aug., 1900; ag. treas., So. Nigeria, June to Dec., 1903, and in Sept., 1904; served with expdt. Benin territories, Apr. and May, 1899; mentd. in despatches, medal and clasp; bronze medal R. Hum. Soc., 1887.

SMARTT, DR. THOS. WM.—L.R.C.S., Ireland, 1878; L.K.Q.C.P.I., 1880; M.L.A. for Wodehouse, Cape Colony, 1894; col. sec., 1898; comsnr. of pub. wks., 1900-02, and from Feb., 1904; ag. prime minister during periods of 1904 and 1905; holds Cape Colony general service medal, 1879, with clasp, "Transkei" having served as surg. with P.A.O.C.V.A.; also holds medal and clasp for Kimberley siege, 1899-1900.

SMEETON, S. P., I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1842; extra clk. in office of S. of S. forCols., June, 1862; 1st class clk. in col. sec.'s office, Jamaica, June, 1870; regis.-gen. of births, deaths and marriages, 8th Feb., 1878; suptl. the taking of census of Jamaica, 1881 and 1891; dep. keeper of records (on amalgamation of registration and record depts.), May, 1899; nom. mem. of comsn. to inquire into marriage and regis. laws, May, 1903.

SMITH, ALLAN F., I.S.O. (1906).—Clk. in P.O., Bermuda, 1879; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1886; asst. col. sec., 1895; col. postmtr., 1896; ag. col. sec., July and Aug., 1899, and from May, 1900, to July, 1901.

SMITH, SIR C. ABERCROMBIE, KT. BACH. (19 '3).—M.A. (Camb. and Glas.), fellow of St. Peter's Coll., Camb., 2nd wrangler and 2nd Smith's prize-man, 1858, &c.; mem. of the house of assem., Cape, 1866-75; mem. of the exec. coun., 1872; comsnr. of crown lands and pub. works, 1872-75; mem. of comttee. under Ordinance 97, 1872-5; comsnr. of the sinking fund, 1872-4; mem. of the univ. coun., 1873; chmn. of the meteorol. comsn., 1874; comptroller and audr.-gen., 1875; chmn. of the tender bd., 1876; mem. of the surety bd., 1875-86; vice-chancellor, Cape Univ., 1877-9; comsnr., civ. ser. exams bd., 1886; chairman, 1887; ret., 1904.

SMITH, C. A. JEFFREY.—Apprentice, pub. wks. dept., Jamaica, Aug., 1870; ast. to ch. supt., 1875; 3rd class ditto, 1879; 2nd class ditto, 1881; ag. dist. engnr., W. dist., 1886; ditto, Kingston dist., 1888-9; overseer of wks., prisons dept., 1889; 1st cls. supt., pub. wks., and supt., St. Catherine waterwks., 1896; also acted on several occasions as govt. assessor, and supt. irrigation wks.

SMITH, SIR CECIL CLEMENTI, P.C. (1906), G.C.M.G. (1892), K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1880).—B. 1841; ed. at St. Paul's Schl. and Corp. Chr. Coll., Camb.; B.A. 1862, M.A. 1868; elected student interp., Hong Kong, Mar., 1862; regar.-gen., Mar., 1865; ag. pol. mag., June, 1865; ag. col. sec., 1867; mem. of exec. coun., 1869; ag. col. sec., Feb., 1872, to Dec., 1873; treas., 1871; acted as col. sec., Oct., 1874, and also from Mar., 1875; col. sec., S. Stltms., July, 1878; apptd. under the F.O. to be Br. comsnr. to settle certain marine claims at Manila, Sept., 1878, and received the thanks of H.M.'s govt.; also for services in connection with the "Niseru" case, 1884; administered govt. S.S., Mar., 1884, to Nov., 1886; lieut.-gov. and col. sec., Ceylon, Nov., 1885; gov. of S.S., 1887; also consul-gen. and high comsnr., for Borneo and Sarawak, 1889; ret., 1893; pres. of comsnr. to inquire into Trinidad riota, 1903.

SMITH, SIR CHARLES HOLLED, K.C.M.G., C.B.—B. 1846; ensign, 1863; lieut., 1869; capt., 1877; S. African war, 1879-1881; Zulu campaign; Transvaal cam., battles of Laing's Nek, Ingogo, and Majuba Mountain; despatches, Lon. Gaz. May 3, 1881; Egypt. expedit., 1882; at Ramleh, Tel-el-Mahuta; action at Kassassin; battle of Tel-el-Kebir, Lon. Gaz., Nov. 2, 1882; major (brevet), Nov., 1882; with Egypt. army, Jan., 1883, to Aug., 1892; Soudan expedit., 1884-5; lieut.-col., June, 1885; col., Dec., 1888; gov.-gen., Red Sea littoral, and comdt., Suakin, Sept., 1888, to Aug., 1892; Soudan, 1888-91; action of Gamaizah, Lon. Gaz., Jan. 11, 1889; occupation of Handoul, Jan., 1891; major-gen. comding Victorian mil. forces, Dec., 1894; several medals, clasps to bronze stars, and mentioned in numerous despatches.

SMITH, EDWIN MITCHELL.—Entered survey, crown lands dept., S. Australia, as ast., June, 1862; cadet and jun. survr.; survr., Jan., 1869; draftsman, Jan., 1874; steward and survr. of educn. lands, Feb., 1882; ch. clk., land office, Jan., 1886; dep. survr.-gen., July, 1894; mem. of central pastoral and central land bds.
SMITH, FRANCIS.—Ch. mag., Gambia, 1879; puisne judge, G. Coast Col., 1887.

SMITH, FRANCIS JAGOR.—M.A. Oxon.; ed. Merchants Tylors schl.; scholar, St. John's Coll., Oxford, 1892; 2nd cls. class. mods., 1894; 2nd math. mods., 1894; 2nd Lit. Hum., 1896; B.A., 1896; M.A., 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1896; attached to col. sec.'s off., 1896; office ast. to govt. ast., and deputy fiscal, Cent. Prov., Apr., 1898; comsnr. of requests and pol. mag. Balantiti, Aug., 1900; ditto, Galle, June, 1901; customs survr., customs, Colombo, Dec., 1901; col. sec., Sept., 1905.

SMITH, THE HON. SIR FRANCIS, K.N.T. (creat. 1862).—B. 1819; B.A. Lond. Univ., 1840; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., May, 1842; solr.-gen., Tasmania, 1840; atty.-gen., 1854; mem. of the legis. assem., 1849-60, and of the exec. coun., 1855-60; atty.-gen. upon the introduction of responsible govt. in 1856; prime min., 1857; raised to the bench as a puisne judge of the sup. ct., 1860; ch. just., May, 1870; was admstr. of the govt., 1874; again, 1880; ret., 1885.
SMITH, FRANK BRAYBROOKE.—Director of agric., Transvaal, and agric. adviser to the govt., Apl., 1902; M.L.C.

SMITH, FREDERICK BONHAM, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1837; entered col. sec.'s office, Barbados, 1859; in office of govt.-in-chief of the Windward Is., 1860 to 1866; pol. mag. of Christchurch, Barbados, 1866; sen. pol. mag. of Bridgetown and St. Michael, 1873; ag. judge of the ast. ct. of appeal, 1878 to 1880; inspr. of prisons, 1878, held with office of provost-marshal from 1882; ag. col. sec., 1879.

SMITH, FREDERICK STANLEY.—Ed. at Sherborne Coll., Eng.; called to the bar, In. Temp., Jan., 1892; ag. pol. mag., dist. "B," Barbados, Aug. to Oct., 1893; ag. pol. mag., dist. "D," Apr., 1894, to July, 1895; and from Dec., 1895, to Oct., 1896; ag. pol. mag., dist. "C," Jan. to Feb., 1897; J.P., Apr., 1897; ag. judge-asst., ct. of appeal, Apr. to Sept., 1897; ag. pol. mag., dist. "E," Jan., 1898; ag. pol. mag., Bridgetown and dist. "A," Feb. to May, 1898; ag. pol. mag., dist. "F," Sept. to Oct., 1898; apptd. pol. mag., dist. "D," Jan., 1899; ag. pol. mag., Bridgetown and dist. "A," Nov., 1901, to Apr., 1902; mag., dist. "A," 1902; ag. judge. ast. ct. of appeal on several occasions.

SMITH, GEORGE.—Transf. from W.O. to ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, Nov., 1879; ch. clk., 1881; ast. to ch. sec., 1883 to 1891; clk., legis. coun., 1886 to 1891; ag. ch. sec., May to Oct., 1888; comsnr. of Papho, 1891; regia.-gen. and mem. legis. coun., 1893; British delegate of Evcaf., 1905; ag. rec.-gen., 1905 and 1906.

SMITH, GEORGE.—Ed. at Galway House Schl., Leyton, Essex, and at Morden House, Greenwich; ag. dist. comsnr., Anamaboe, G. Coast, 1877; dist. comsnr. Dexovoe, 1878; ditto, Palma and Leckie, Lagos, 1880; sub-collr. customs, Palma and Leckie, 1883; ch. clk. and warehouse keeper, customs, Lagos, Aug., 1889; ag. dist. comsnr., E. dist., Lagos, Mar., 1892.
SMITH, GEORGE DOUGLAS, C.M.G. (1905).—Joined Imperial Brit. East Africa Co., 1890; treas., Uganda Prot., Mar., 1894.

SMITH, CAPT. GEORGE ECKLIN.—Ed. Rathmines Schl. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; joined 5th Batt., Royal Irish Regt., 1891; capt., 1895; hon. capt. in army, 1900; ast. inspr., G. Coast constab., 1897; sub-inspr., B. Guiana police, 1898; dist. inspr., 1900; A.D.C. to Sir F. Hodgson, K.C.M.G., gov. of B. Guiana.

SMITH, GEORGE WHITFIELD.—F.L.S., Clk., pub. library, Barbados, Sept., 1879; 3rd treasy. clk., St. Lucia, 1882; sub-collr. of taxes, 1st dist., St. Lucia, Aug., 1882; rev. offr., Leeward dist., St. Vincent, Feb., 1885 (resigned); curator, botanic gardens, Grenada, Dec., 1890, resigned, 16th Dec., 1893; trav. supt., Imperial dept. of agric., Dec., 1898; pol. mag. and ast. treas., N. dist. Grenada, and pol. mag., 3rd dist., St. Vincent, Apr., 1904; comsnr., Carriacou, Oct., 1904; was engaged as ast. botanist to W. India exploration comttee, of Royal Soc. during scientific exploration of St. Vincent, 1888-89; is a corrpdg. mem. of Royal Hort. Soc. of England.

SMITH, GERALD STANLEY WELLS.—Graduate Ontario business coll., Canada; supery. clk., regisr.'s office, Grenada, Jan., 1890; asst. clk., post office, Mar., 1890; 4th clk., treasy., June, 1890; asst. clk., col. sec.'s office, Oct., 1890; 2nd clk., treasy., Nov., 1891; audit clk., Nov., 1897; has held numerous acting appointments, including ag. aud. of Windward Islands, 4th June to 31st Dec., 1906.

SMITH, Lieut.-Col. Sir Gerard, K.C.M.G. (1895).—B.1839; late lieut.-col. Roy. Scots Guards; groom-in-waiting to Her Majesty, 1883-5; M.P. for High Wycombe, 1883-5; J.P. for E. Riding of Yorkshire; gov. of W. Australia, 1896 to 1900.
SMITH, Henry J.—Apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. messenger C.O., 12th Nov., 1883; 2nd cl. ditto, 1st June, 1897; Queen's mess., 1st Apr., 1898.
SMITH, James Alfred.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1891; promoted to higher grade, 1902.

SMITH, Jas. C.—Ch. clk. and storekr., Imperial treasy. and commissariat depts., Bahamas, June, 1876, to Aug., 1889; mem. bd. of educ., 1886 to 1892; postmr., 1889; J.P., 1890; elected mem. of Bahamas House of Assembly in 1882-89 and 1896; resigned 1897; asst. postmr. gen., Sierra Leone, Dec., 1896; J.P., 1897; ag. postmr. gen., 1897, 1899, and 1900; ag. curator of intestates, S. L., from Jan., 1899, to Jan., 1900.

SMITH, James Landreth.—Draftman, P.W.D., St. Lucia, 13th July, 1891; draftsman, survey off., Sept., 1893; wardens, and supt. of water-wks. under St. George's town bd., Apr., 1895, to Aug., 1901; chief overseer of roads and works, Grenada, 19th Aug., 1901; asst. supt. of wks., 1st Apr., 1902; comsnr. of crown lands, 28th June, 1904.

SMITH, James Parker, M.P.; P.C.(1904).—Ed. Winchester, and Trin. Coll., Camb. (4th wrangler and 2nd Smith's prizeman, 1877; fellow of Winchester and late fellow Trin. Coll.) called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1881; J.P. and D.L., Renfrewshire and co. of city of Glasgow; J.P., Lanarkshire; dir. of Union Bank of Scotland; elected for Partick div. of Lanarkshire, 1890; partly priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, 1901-3.

SMITH, Sir John Smalman, Kt. Bach. (1896).—M.A., St. John's Coll., Camb.; b. 1847; called to the bar, In. Tem., 1872; special pleader Oxford circuit; puisne judge, G. Coast Col., 1883; judge, Lagos, 1886; ch. just., Lagos, 1889; ret., 1896.

SMITH, Lindsay Lea.—Acting clk. to comsnr., Turks Is., 1894, 1896, 1900, and 1903; confirmed 1st July, 1903; ag. acctnt. and clk. (collr. of customs, postmr., cashier and regisr. of shipping) comsnr.'s office, June to Sept., 1904, and May to Aug., 1906.

SMITH, Richard William, A.M.I.C.E., B.A., B.A.I.—P. W. Dept., Ceylon; ed. at the Grammar Schl., Chard, Somerset, 1874, and Dublin Univ. (Trinity College), 1875-79; employed in engineer's off., D.W.W. Rlyw., 1879; res. engrnr., Wicklow Harb. works, 1880-83; asst. to Mr. J. Lanyon, Belfast (rlyws., waterwks., drainage, etc.), 1883-85; res. engr., Cleggan Harb. Works, 1885-88; asst. res. engrnr., St. Helier's Harb. Works (Jersey), 1888-89; sup't. P.W., Pahang, S.S., 1889-92; D.E., P.W.D., Ceylon, 1892-94; ag. prov. engrnr., N.W.P., 1894-97; dist. engrnr. and ag. prov. engrnr., S.P., 1898; seconded to railway extensions staff, 1898; seconded to "Water Supply Schemes," 1898-1900; charge of Boer Camp Works, 1900; "Water Supply Schemes," 1901-02; seconded as res. engrnr., proposed convict prison works, Seychelles, 1902; returned to Ceylon 13th Aug., 1902.
SMITH, Robert Murray, C.M.G. (1884).—B. 1831; agt.-gen. for Victoria in United Kingdom, 1881-86.
SMITH, Hon. Sydney.—M.P. for Macquarie, New South Wales; postmr.-gen., Commonwealth of Australia, in Reid-Turner ministry, 1904.

SMITH, Thomas Sercombe, B.A., LL.B. (Lond.).—Hong Kong cadet, 1882; attached to C.O., 1883; passed cadet, 1886; ag. asst. registr.-gen., 1886-90, except for five months as ag. pol. mag.; asst. registr.-gen., 1891; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Nov., 1893; ag. puisne judge, various dates, 1890-7, and in 1900, 1901, 1902, 1904 and 1905; treas. Dec., 1896; pol. mag., July, 1898; ag. col. sec., 1898-9, for 3 months in 1901, and from Aug., 1905, to Dec., 1906.

SMITH, Wm. Edward.—Employed on L. & N.W. Rly., 1872-82; asst. traffic man., Indian guaranteed rlys. (Seinde, Punjab, and Delhi), 1883-86; ag. district traffic man., Lahore and Delhi div.; gen. man., Barbados Rly., 1889; gen. and traffic man., Trinidad and Govt. Rly., Feb., 1894; comsnr., Colonial and Indian Exhib., London, 1905; is a J.P.

SMITH, Sir William Frederick Haynes, K.C.M.G. (1890), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1839; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., June, 1863; solr.-gen., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1865; atty.-gen., Oct., 1874; admstd. the govt., Apr. to Sept., 1884, and in 1887; gov., Leeward Is., Nov., 1888; gov., Bahamas, 1895; high comsnr., Cyprus, 1898-1904.

SMITH, Sir William James, Kt. Bach. (1896).—M.A., LL.M. Trinity Hall, Camb.; b.1853; acted as puisne judge of the G. Coast Col. from Dec. 1880, to July, 1881; puisne judge of the sup. ct. of Cyprus, 1882; ch. just., 1892; ch. just., Br. Guiana, 1898; puisne judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, 1st Mar., 1902.
SMITH, William Ramsay.—B.Sc., M.B., M.S., Edin.; chmn. cent. bd. of health, coroner, vaccination offr. and inspr. of anatomy, S. Australia, 1899-1903; chmn. cent. bd. of health, and coroner, 1903.

SMITH-STEINMETZ, Gerard Archibald.—Ed. at Stonyhurst coll.; cadet, Straits Settlements, Oct., 1902; in charge of S.S. emig. depot, S. India, June, 1904; passed final exam. in Tamil, July, 1904; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Feb., 1905.

SMUTS, Johannes.—Clk., treasy., Cape, 1882; acted as priv. sec. to the Hon. J. Gordon Sprigg, 1885-6; to Sir H. A. Smyth, 1889; to Lieut.-Gen. H. E. Cameron, and to Sir H. Loch, 1891; sec. to Hon. J. H. Hofmeyer (Swaziland Convention), 1890; asst. dispatch clk., govt. house, 1886; 2nd class clk., 1887; ag. priv. sec. to Lieut.-Gen. Cameron and to Sir H. B. Loch, 1891 to 1895; ch. clk. to high commr., 1891; acted as clk. of exec. coun., 1891 and 1895; Br. consul in Swaziland, Aug., 1899-9; served on staff of Sir C. Warren during part of S. African war; registr. of deeds, Apr., 1902; chmn. of Swaziland concessions comsn., 1904.
SMYLY, Philip Crampton.—Ed. at Trin. Coll., Dub., B.A., LL.B.; called to the bar, King's Inns, Dub., 1888; LL.D., 1891; Queen's advoc., S. Leone, 1895; atty.-gen., 1896.
SMYTH, C. E. Owen, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1851; ed. at Erasmus Smith Schl., Dub.; specification clk., S. Australia, 1876; profnl. clk., 3rd class, 1878; 1st class, 1883; supt. of pub. bldgs., 1886.

SMYTH, H. Warington.—Ed. at Westmstr. and Trin. Coll., Camb., M.A., L.L.M., F.G.S., F.R.G.S., barr.-at-law; unpaid asst. to mineral adviser to office of woods, 1891; sec., dept. of mines, Siam, 1891; director-gen. of mines and geological survey, Siam, 1895; sec., Siamese Legation, 1897 (order of White Elephant, 3rd cls.); sec. to mines dept., Transvaal, 1901; M.L.C., Transvaal, 1906; mem. exec. coun., while ag. comsnr. of mines, 1906; author of “Five Years in Siam,” “Mast and Sail in Europe and Asia,” papers on Indo-China, etc., etc.

SMYTH, J. L.—Served in R.I.C. from 1889 to 1897; transf. from detec. dept., Belfast, to Jamaica constab., Sept., 1897; ag. supt., M. and S. co. gaol, Sept., 1900, to Feb., 1901; asst. supt. of pol., Br. Hond., July, 1906; J.P. for colony.
SNOWBALL, Jabez Bunting.—Lieut.-gov., New Brunswick, Canada, 5th Feb., 1902.

SOLOMON, John.—2nd class asst. at excise, England, Apr., 1868; 1st class asst., June, 1870; ride offr., June, 1871; resig., Jan., 1876; ag. 3rd clk., immgr. dept., Br. Guiana, July, 1877; ag. cashier in rec-gen.’s office, 1878; comsry. of taxation, 1879.
SOLOMON, Michael Claude.—Ag. 3rd clk., island med. dept., Jamaica, Mar., 1885; 3rd clk., Oct., 1888; 2nd clk., July, 1891.

SOLOMON, Hon. Sir Richard K.C.B. (1905), K.C.M.G. (1901), C.B. (1903), K.C.—B.1850; ed. S. African Coll., Capetown; St. Peter’s Coll., Camb., 23rd wrangler; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1879; legal adviser to late Lord Rosmead on his mission to Mauritius, 1886; mem. of native law coms.; chrmn. of mining coms.; mem. of House of Ass. for division of Tembuland, Cape; atty.-gen., Cape, 1898-1900; legal adviser to Transvaal adminstr., 1901-1902; atty.-gen., Transvaal, 19th June, 1902; mem. ex. and leg. couns.; mem. I.C.C.; ag. lieut.-gov., Transvaal, 1905.

SOLOMON, Wm. Henry.—B.A., Cantab; admitted to the bar, Inner Temple, 1877; practised in Cape Colony; asst. legal adviser, Cape, 1883; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1887; pres., spec. treason court, 1900; 1st puisne judge, Transvaal, 1902.

SOUZA, Simon I. de.—Ed. at the R. Catholic gram. schl., Lagos; 1st despatch clk. and shorthand writer, col. sec.’s office, Lagos, June, 1895; acted as clk. of the legis. coun., 1900; regis. of correspondence, June, 1900; conf. clk. to gov. and clk. of leg. coun., July, 1901; ag. priv. sec. to gov., and clk. to exec. coun., Feb., 1902; in charge of Ibadan residency, Nov., 1903.
SORZANO, Tildeber.—Draughtsman, crown lands office, Trinidad, 1874; first ditto, survey dept., Aug., 1881.
SPALDING, Lieut.-Col. Warner Wright, C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1844; N. S. Wales Artill., 1876; in command of the detachment which served in the Soudan, 1884; res. mag. Norfolk Is., 1896.

SPEED, Edwin Arney.—M.A., LL.B.; ed. at Rugby, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; major scholar and senior exhibtnr., Rugby schl.; scholar and prize-man of Trin. Coll., Camb.; 1st cls. class. tripes, 1890; 2nd cls. law tripes, Part I., 1891; called to the bar, Inner Temple, June, 1893; dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 1899; atty.-gen., Lagos, 1900; edited a revised edition of Lagos laws, 1901; has acted on numerous occasions as ch. just. and also as col. sec., and has held a comsn. as dep. gov.

SPENCE, Frank.—3rd clk. col. sec.’s office, Fiji, 1880; clk. to commrs. of wks., Oct., 1884; ag. priv. sec. to gov., 1884, 1886, and 1887 to 1896; sec. to consul-gen. for W. Pacific, 1889 to 1896; stip. mag., Cakandrove and Bua, 1896 to 1898; provincial inspr. and stip. mag., Cakandrove and Bua, 1898; ditto, Cakandrove, 1901; stip. mag., Navua, Jan., 1904.
SPENCE, J. B.—Med. supt. of Colombo lun. asyl., Ceylon, 1886.

SPENCE, R. O. H.—Cler. asst., P.W. dept., Br. Guiana, 16th Aug., 1886; ag. 2nd clk., P.W. dept., Aug., 1889; ag. 3rd cl. clk., treasy., Jan., 1890; gov. offr., govt. ld. dept., Apr., 1890; clk., govt. ld. dept., May, 1890; sec., Bartika comsnr., June, 1891 to May, 1893; clk., dept. mines, Oct., 1892; ag. 3rd cl. clk., dept. mines, Apr., 1893; gov’t. offr., dept. mines, July, 1893; J.P., May, 1894; comsnr. to admr. oaths, June, 1894; dist. govt. offr., dept. mines, Aug., 1896; warden, dept. mines, Nov., 1896; obt’d certif. as sworn land surv., June, 1898; ag. ch. clk., dept. mines, Feb., 1900.

SPENCER, Cyril E.—Clk., ch. sec.’s office, Cyprus, 1st Aug., 1892; passed in Mod. Greek, lower standard, Nov., 1893; clk., legis. coun., Mar., 1894; ag. priv. sec. to O.A.G., Jan.-Apr., 1898; inspr. of pol., May, 1898; ag. priv. sec. and clk. to ex. coun., Feb.-May, 1899, and Dec., 1899, to May, 1900; ag. local comd. of police, Papho, July, 1900, to June, 1901; Limasol, from June, 1901.

SPICER, Quinten Herbert.—2nd clk., col. sec.’s dept., Tobago, 1884; 1st clk., audit and registry dept., 1884; 2nd rev. offr., 1885; harbrmr. and landing waiter, 1880; supervisor of excise 1892; inspr. of pol., 1896; in charge of prison, 1897; asst. harbrmr., Trinidad, 1899.
SPIRE, John.—Apptd., after exam., 3rd class messenger, C.O., Jan., 1883; 2nd class, July, 1896; Queen’s mess., Nov., 1899.

SPRIGG, The Rt. Hon. Sir John Gordon, P.C. (1897), G.C.M.G. (1902), K.C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1830; represented E. London in Cape assm. 1869-1904; col. sec. and premier, Feb., 1878, to May, 1881; col. treas., May, 1884, to Nov., 1886; premier, Nov., 1886; apptd. comdr. legion of honour for services in Paris Exhib., 1889; resig. office, July, 1890; again treas., 1893, and also premier, 1896; represented the col. in London at H.M.’s Diam. Jub.; resig. premiership, Oct., 1898; again premier 1900-04.

SPROULE, Percy Julian, B.A., Camb.—Bar-rister-at-law (Mid. Temp.); cadet, S. S., Nov., 1895; ag. dep. regist., sup. ct., Penang, Mar., 1897; passed final in Malay, Mar., 1899; ag 3rd mag., Penang, June, 1899; dep. regist., sup. ct., Penang, Sept., 1899; ag. asst. regist., sup. ct., Penang, May, 1903; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., Sept., 1904; ag. dep. pub. pros., May, 1905; ag. sol.-gen., Penang, May-Aug., 1906.
STAGG, Sidney.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. 2nd div. civil ser., and assigned to board of agriculture, Dec., 1897; transf. to C.O., Mar., 1899; regist. of col. laws, Jan., 1906.

STANFORD, Walter Ernest Mortimer, C.B. (1900), C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1850; mag.’s clk., Cape Col., 1863; mag., 1876; capt. of levies in Gcaleka war, 1877-8 (medal); mem. native laws and customs coman., 1880-2; comdt. in war of 1880-1; on special service to Pondoland, 1884; ch. mag. Griqualand E., 1885; negotiated treaty with Pondos, 1886; sec. nat. affairs dept., 1898; ditto and ch. mag., July, 1904; mem. of S. African native affairs comsn., Sept., 1903, to Jan., 1905; holds rank of colonel in Cape colonial forces.

STANMORE, The Right Hon. Sir Arthur Hamilton Gordon, 1st Baron (creat. 1893), G.C.M.G. (1878), K.C.M.G. (1871), C.M.G. (1859). —B. 1829; priv. sec. to his father, Earl of Aberdeen, when 1st lord of the treas., 1832 to 1855; M.P. for Beverley from July, 1854, till Mar., 1857; accompanied Mr. Gladstone on his special mission as lord high comsnr. extraord. to the Ionian Is., Nov., 1858; capt. comdt. of the 1st Aberdeenshire R.V., Feb., 1860; lieut.-gov. of N. Brunswick, Oct., 1861; gov. of Trinidad, Nov., 1866; gov. of Mauritius, 1870; ret., 1874; gov. of the new col. of Fiji, Jan., 1875; H.M.'s high comsnr. and consulgen. for the W. Pacific, 1877; gov. of N. Zealand, 1880; gov. of Ceylon, 1883; ret., 1890.
STANTON, LIONEL WM.—Inspr. of schls., S. Aust., 1876; asst. inspr.-gen., 1892; chmn. of bd. of inspr., 1896; inspr.-gen. of schls., 1902; sec. to min. of educ. and sec. of educn. dept.,

STEELE, T. M.—B.A. Roy. Univ., Dublin; ed. at Wesley Coll., Dublin; 2nd div. clk., nat. debt. off., Dec., 1894; transf. to P.W.D., Ireland, 1896-1904; examr. of acct., audit dept., O.R.C., Sept., 1904; dist. examr. of acct., July, 1906.

STEELE, WALTER MATTHEW.—Clerical asst., treasy., Br. Guiana, Feb., 1884; 5th class clk., Feb., 1886; 4th class, July, 1888; 3rd class, Apr., 1890; ag. clk-in-charge, sub-treasy. and savings bk., Dec., 1891, to June, 1893; 2nd class clk. (acctnt.), govt. land dept., April, 1896; asst. treas., So. Nigeria, July, 1904; ag. treas., Cent. or Niger Prov., 1st May, 1906.

STEEN, DAVID MILLER, B.A.—Scholar, Queen's Coll., Belfast; 1st class exhibitioner, Roy. Univ.; B.A. 1896; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 1887; pol. mag., Balapitiya, 1889; asst. collr., customs, Trincomalee, 1890; ditto, Galle, 1892; pol. mag., Haldemulla, 1893; ag. dist. judge, Badulla, 1893; ag. dist. judge, Tangalla, 1895.

STEERE, C. J. Lee.—Probation clk., col. sec.'s off., W. Aust., Feb., 1885; jun. clk., Aug., 1885; ag. clk., gov.'s off., 1889-1890; clk. of leg. coun. and clk. of parlts., Dec., 1890, to June, 1901; clk. of leg. assem., June, 1901.

STEIN, JOHN.—Ed. at Christ's Coll., Finchley, and at Neuwied, Germany; sec. to consulates for Germany, Sweden and Norway, and Denmark, in Mauritius, May, 1889, to Dec., 1896; apptd. by S. of S., F.O., an asst. dist. comsnr. in H.B.M.'s Niger Coast Protectorate, April, 1897; ag. dist. comsnr., Oppo, June, 1897, to June, 1898; Bonny, Jan., 1899, to Jan., 1900; promoted dist. comsnr., Jan., 1900; asst. sec. to gov't., Southern Nigeria, June, 1900; ag. sec. to gov't., Sept., 1900, to May, 1901, and from June to Nov., 1901; West African medal with clasp, 1899; ment. in desps. for services in connection with Aro expedition, 1901-2; ret., 1904.
STEPHEN, CHELVUM.—Ind. interp. to res. mag., Durban, Natal, Jan., 1875; clk. and Ind. interp. to res. mag., Umlazi div., co. of Durban, Mar., 1875; Tamil interp., sup. ct., 1895.

STEPHEN, GUY NEVILLE.—Ed. Paris, Marseilles, London; M.R.C.S., Eng., 1881; licentiate of medicine, France, 1882; ag. house surg., Lincoln co. hosp.; ditto, asst. med. offr., Middlesex co. asylum (Colney Hatch); surg. to the consulate of Norway and Sweden, Marseilles, 1881; surg. to the Br. Consulate and Seamen's Home, Marseilles, 1882; dist. med. offr., Nicosia, Cyprus, 1883; med. offr., central prison, gen. and ophthalmic hosp.; med. offr. of health, Nicosia; pres. of the municipality, Nicosia, 1889; ag. ch. med. offr., 1885 and 1890; surg. to Smyrna Hosp., 1894.
STEPHEN, SIR MATTHEW HENRY, KT. BACH. (1904).—B. 1828; mem. legis. assem., N.S.W., 1869-71; judge, sup. ct., May, 1887; ret. from bench, 1903.
STEPHENS, WILLIAM WILBERFORCE.—Clk., col. secretariat, N. S. Wales, 1852; priv. sec. to various premiers, 1856-58; clk., land dept., 1858; undersec. for lands, 1870; sec., atty.-gen.'s dept., 1880.

STEPHENS, THOMAS NOAKES.—Jun. offr., S. Australia; customs, 1864; clk., marine bd., 1865; boarding offr., customs, 1866; asst. landing waiter, 1867; confid. clk., 1870; ch. clk., treasy., 1875; sec. marine bd., 1877; sec. of customs, 1889; undertreas., 1890; collr. of customs, regis. of shipping, ch. inspr. of distilleries and excise, pres. of marine bd., 1894; is a J.P.; transf. with cust. dept. to serv. of Commonwealth of Aust., 1st Jan., STEPHENSON ALBERT EDWARD.—Clk. E. and A. dept., Feb., 1884; local auditor, Lagos, Aug., 1888, also ag. local auditor, G. Coast, Dec., 1888, to Aug., 1889; returned to E. and A. depts., and apptd. to col. audit branch, Jan., 1891; clk. in charge of acct., May, 1893; ast. supt., Aug.,

STEVENS, FRED. G.—B.A. Oxon; ed. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; cadet, Straits Settlements, Oct., 1902; demarcation offr., Sing., 15th June, 1903; dep. coll. of land rev. and regis. of deeds, Sing., in addtn. to other duties, Oct., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, June, 1904; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1904; ag. head, Malay Coll., Dec., 1905.

STEVENS, PERCIVAL.—Asst. M.I.C.E.; ast. engr., govt. rly., Trinidad, 1874; dist. engr., Couva extension rly., 1878; res. engr. in charge of construction, San Fernando and Guaiara rlys., 1879; 1st ast. engr., P.W. dept., Jan., 1885; has acted as ast. dir. of P.W., 1885, 6, 8, 9, and 90; engr., N. div., P.W.D., Jan., 1892; 1st engr. in charge of rds. and bdges., P.W.D., Jan., 1894; engr. in charge of rds. and bdges, rds. and bdges. dept., Jan., 1895; ag. dir. pub. wks., with seat in legis. coun., May, 1894, to Feb., 1895.

STEWARD, MAJ.-GEN. EDWARD HARDING, C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1835; entered army, Roy. Engrns., Oct., 1854; employed from 1865 to 1879 in the fortification branch of the W.O.; services lent in 1866 to 1869, and in 1877 to the S. Australian govt., and in 1877 till 1879 to the govt. of Victoria. In charge of the defence of the Natal frontier in 1879, and comdt. of Natal and mem. of exec. coun. of the col. in 1880; services lent to govts. of Victoria and N. S. Wales in 1883; and in 1884, on retirement from the army, became military adviser to the agts.-gen. of Victoria, N. S. Wales, Queensland, S. Australia, N. Zealand, and Tasmania.
